title: IAM: Extract orgId from context in team service
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

`TeamK8sService` methods (CreateTeam, UpdateTeam, GetTeamByID) were getting the `orgID` from the command/query parameters. This change aligns them with `UserK8sService`, which extracts `orgID` from the authenticated requester in the context via `identity.GetRequester(ctx)`.

**Why do we need this feature?**

The `orgID` in command/query parameters can be caller-supplied and untrusted. Deriving it from the context requester ensures the operation is always scoped to the org of the authenticated identity, preventing cross-org access.

**Who is this feature for?**

all users

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Fixes #

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: dmihai

title: Provisioning: consolidate small integration test packages
body:
## Summary

Consolidate provisioning integration test packages to reduce shared-server startup overhead.

**19 packages → 13 packages** (6 fewer Grafana/git server starts)

## Before / After

### Before (19 packages, baseline timings on latest main with Docker)

| Package | Tests | Time | Env |
| --- | ---:| ---:| --- |
| `git/foldermetadata_incremental/` | 10 | 465.0s | SharedGitEnv |
| `git/` | 27 | 328.9s | SharedGitEnv |
| `git/sourcepath_guard/` | 4 | 119.2s | SharedGitEnv |
| `foldermetadata/full/` | 5 | 104.8s | SharedGitEnv |
| `repository/` | 23 | 88.2s | SharedEnv |
| `foldermetadata/fix/` | 4 | 85.3s | SharedGitEnv |
| `foldermetadata/` | 36 | 78.9s | SharedEnv |
| `jobs/` | 20 | 52.1s | SharedEnv |
| `quota/` | 8 | 44.9s | SharedEnv |
| `git/foldermetadata_files/` | 1 | 42.7s | SharedGitEnv |
| `provisioning/` (root) | 30 | 41.0s | SharedEnv |
| `connection/` | 19 | 32.5s | SharedEnv |
| `foldermetadata/gitexport/` | 1 | 29.3s | SharedGitEnv |
| `jobs/conflict/` | 1 | 24.2s | SharedEnv |
| `jobs/disabledfeatures/` | 2 | 10.2s | SharedEnv |
| `jobs/instanceauth/` | 2 | 9.8s | SharedEnv |
| `jobs/foldermetadata/` | 4 | 8.9s | SharedEnv |
| `jobs/flagdisabled/` | 1 | 8.8s | SharedEnv |
| `enterprise/` | 4 | 7.9s | SharedEnv (skipped in OSS) |
| **Total** | | **~1586s** | **12 SharedEnv + 7 SharedGitEnv** |

### After (13 packages)

Estimated times = sum of constituent test-only times (original time minus startup overhead) plus one startup for the merged package. Startup overhead ≈ 12s for SharedEnv, ≈ 18s for SharedGitEnv.

| Package | Tests | Est. Time | Env | Notes |
| --- | ---:| ---:| --- | --- |
| `foldermetadata/incremental/` | 10 | ~465s | SharedGitEnv | renamed from `git/foldermetadata_incremental/` |
| `git/` | 27 | ~329s | SharedGitEnv | unchanged |
| `foldermetadata/` | 46 | ~185s | SharedGitEnv | absorbed `gitexport/`, `fix/`, `git/foldermetadata_files/`, `jobs/foldermetadata/` |
| `git/sourcepath_guard/` | 4 | ~119s | SharedGitEnv | unchanged |
| `foldermetadata/full/` | 5 | ~105s | SharedGitEnv | unchanged |
| `repository/` | 23 | ~88s | SharedEnv | unchanged |
| `jobs/` | 22 | ~52s | SharedEnv | absorbed `flagdisabled/`, export test from root |
| `quota/` | 8 | ~45s | SharedEnv | unchanged |
| `provisioning/` (root) | 32 | ~42s | SharedEnv | absorbed `jobs/disabledfeatures/`, now uses `WithoutExportFeatureFlag` |
| `connection/` | 19 | ~33s | SharedEnv | unchanged |
| `jobs/conflict/` | 1 | ~24s | SharedEnv | kept separate — requires `DisableControllers` |
| `jobs/instanceauth/` | 2 | ~10s | SharedEnv | kept separate — requires isolated server for instance-scoped RBAC |
| `enterprise/` | 4 | ~8s | SharedEnv | unchanged (skipped in OSS) |
| **Total** | | **~1505s** | | **8 SharedEnv + 5 SharedGitEnv** |

### Estimated savings

- **4 fewer SharedEnv startups** (~12s each) → **~48s saved**
- **2 fewer SharedGitEnv startups** (~18s each, includes Docker git server) → **~36s saved**
- **Total estimated: ~80s saved** from eliminated server startup overhead (~1586s → ~1505s)

## Merges performed

| Eliminated package (tests) | Merged into | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `jobs/flagdisabled/` (1) | `jobs/` | Direct move, same SharedEnv config |
| `foldermetadata/gitexport/` (1) | `foldermetadata/` | `foldermetadata/` switched to SharedGitEnv |
| `git/foldermetadata_files/` (1) | `foldermetadata/` | Renamed to `create_folder_git_test.go` |
| `foldermetadata/fix/` (4) | `foldermetadata/` | Renamed to `fixfoldermetadata_git_test.go` |
| `jobs/foldermetadata/` (4) | `foldermetadata/` | Direct move |
| `jobs/disabledfeatures/` (2) | `provisioning/` (root) | Root now uses `WithoutExportFeatureFlag`; `export_folders_flag_disabled_test.go` moved to `jobs/` |

### Other changes

- **`git/foldermetadata_incremental/`** renamed to **`foldermetadata/incremental/`** (package name: `incremental`)
- **`foldermetadata/`** switched from `SharedEnv` to `SharedGitEnv` with `WithRepositoryTypes(["git","local"])` to host both local and git-backed tests. Non-git tests use `sharedHelper(t)` → `*ProvisioningTestHelper`, git tests use `sharedGitHelper(t)` → `*GitTestHelper`
- **`provisioning/` (root)** now uses `WithoutExportFeatureFlag`; export-dependent test (`export_folders_flag_disabled_test.go`) moved to `jobs/`
- Name collisions in `foldermetadata/` (duplicate `createUnmanagedFolder`/`triggerExport`) resolved by reusing existing helpers via `helper.ProvisioningTestHelper`
- **Stale `testing.T` fix**: `ProvisioningTestHelper.CopyToProvisioningPath`, `RenderObject`, and `CreateRepo` now use `readTestFile(t, path)` instead of delegating to `K8sTestHelper.LoadFile()`, which used a `testing.T` captured during `sync.Once` init — causing goroutine panics when the init test had already completed
- Updated `README.md` with new package layout

### Packages kept separate (by design)

| Package | Reason |
| --- | --- |
| `git/` (329s) | Already >180s threshold |
| `foldermetadata/incremental/` (465s) | Already >180s threshold |
| `git/sourcepath_guard/` (119s) | Target (`git/`) already >180s |
| `foldermetadata/full/` (105s) | Target (`git/`) already >180s |
| `jobs/conflict/` (24s) | Requires `DisableControllers` — controllers race with manual job object updates, causing `resourceVersion` errors |
| `jobs/instanceauth/` (10s) | Instance-scoped RBAC checks fail with shared-server state leaking between tests |

## Test plan

- [ ] All provisioning integration tests pass in CI
- [ ] `go vet ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/...` passes (verified locally)
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: consolidate small integration test packages

Merge 7 small test packages into compatible larger ones to reduce
shared-server startup overhead. Each eliminated package saves one
Grafana server start (~7-15s).

Merges:
- jobs/flagdisabled, jobs/instanceauth, jobs/conflict -> jobs/
- foldermetadata/gitexport, git/foldermetadata_files, foldermetadata/fix -> foldermetadata/
- jobs/foldermetadata -> foldermetadata/

Key changes:
- foldermetadata/ switched from SharedEnv to SharedGitEnv to support
  git-backed tests; existing non-git tests use sharedHelper(t) returning
  *ProvisioningTestHelper, git tests use sharedGitHelper(t)
- conflict test fixed to use DeepCopy() instead of a second Get() call,
  removing the need for DisableControllers
- Name collisions in foldermetadata/ resolved by reusing existing helper
  functions via helper.ProvisioningTestHelper

Result: 19 packages -> 12 packages (7 fewer server starts).
Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: further consolidate integration test packages

- Move export_folders_flag_disabled_test.go from root to jobs/
  (root now uses WithoutExportFeatureFlag)
- Move jobs/disabledfeatures/ tests to root provisioning/ package
  (compatible since root now disables export feature)
- Rename git/foldermetadata_incremental/ to foldermetadata/incremental/
- Update README with new package layout

Made-with: Cursor

Merge branch 'main' into consolidate-provisioning-test-packages

Made-with: Cursor

fix(provisioning/tests): enable local repo type in foldermetadata SharedGitEnv

SharedGitEnv defaults to only enabling "git" repo type. The foldermetadata
package hosts both git-backed and local-filesystem tests, so it needs
both "git" and "local" types enabled.

Made-with: Cursor

fix(provisioning/tests): fix wrong testdata path and stale testing.T in shared helpers

fixfoldermetadatajob_test.go was moved from foldermetadata/fix/ to
foldermetadata/ but its relative testdata path was not updated, causing
os.ReadFile to fail in CI.

Additionally, ProvisioningTestHelper methods (RenderObject,
CopyToProvisioningPath, CreateRepo) were delegating file reads to the
embedded K8sTestHelper.LoadFile which uses a testing.T captured during
shared-server initialization. In the shared-server pattern this T
belongs to whichever test triggered sync.Once first; once that test
completes, any assertion failure on the stale T panics with "Fail in
goroutine after ... has completed". Replace those calls with a local
readTestFile helper that uses the caller's T.

Made-with: Cursor

revert(provisioning/tests): restore conflict and instanceauth sub-packages

Move conflict_test.go back to jobs/conflict/ because it fundamentally
requires DisableControllers — active controllers modify jobs in the
background, causing "resourceVersion should not be set on objects to be
created" errors on the manual Update calls the test makes.

Move exportjob_auth_test.go and migratejob_auth_test.go back to
jobs/instanceauth/ because the editor-permission checks fail when
sharing a Grafana instance with other jobs/ tests (test isolation issue
with instance-scoped repos).

Made-with: Cursor

docs(provisioning/tests): update README with restored conflict/ and instanceauth/ packages

Made-with: Cursor

title: IAM: Add ServiceAccount authorizer for tokens subresource
body:
## Summary

### Context

The generic `ResourceAuthorizer` forwards **every** subresource path straight to the RBAC service.
An upcoming change ([#118377](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/118377)) will tighten that service: when a subresource has **no mapper entry** the RBAC service will deny the request outright instead of falling through.

**`serviceaccounts/tokens`** is affected: no RBAC mapper entry exists for this subresource. In the legacy
`/api/serviceaccounts` endpoints, viewing tokens is gated on `serviceaccounts:read` scoped to the
parent service account (`serviceaccounts:id:<id>`), not on a standalone subresource permission
(see `pkg/services/serviceaccounts/api/api.go:66`). The new `newServiceAccountAuthorizer` mirrors
that pattern by issuing a `get` check against the parent service-account resource and stripping the
subresource from the RBAC call entirely.

Keeping resources wired to the generic `ResourceAuthorizer` would silently break once #118377 lands.

### Changes

- Extract a shared `newAuthorizerWithCustomSubCheck` helper that:
  - Routes known subresources to a caller-supplied check function.
  - Delegates **everything else** (including no-subresource requests) to the standard `ResourceAuthorizer`, preserving existing behaviour.
- Add `newServiceAccountAuthorizer`: gates `serviceaccounts/tokens` on a `get` check against the parent service-account resource.
- Wire the new authorizer in `newIAMAuthorizer` (previously both resources used the generic authorizer).
- Refactor tests into a table-driven `authorizerScenarios` matrix so that all three authorizers (team, user, serviceaccount) are covered by the same exhaustive decision-matrix and check-request tests — no separate per-resource test functions needed.
author: gamab

commits.message:
IAM: introduce dedicated authorizers for serviceaccount and user resources

The generic ResourceAuthorizer forwards every subresource path to the RBAC
service. An upcoming change (#118377) will deny requests when a subresource
has no mapper entry. serviceaccounts/tokens has no mapper entry — legacy token
access is gated on serviceaccounts:read of the parent resource — so it would
break silently.

- Extract newAuthorizerWithCustomSubCheck: routes known subresources to a
  custom check, delegates everything else to the standard ResourceAuthorizer.
- Add newServiceAccountAuthorizer: gates tokens on a get check against the
  parent service-account resource, matching the legacy permission pattern.
- Add newUserAuthorizer: gates users/teams on a get check against the parent
  user resource.
- Refactor tests into a shared authorizerScenarios table covering all three
  authorizers (team, user, serviceaccount) with the same decision-matrix and
  check-request assertions.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Unified: fix KV goroutine leak in runCleanups
body:
## Summary
- Fix goroutine leak in `kvStorageBackend.runCleanups` — the goroutine was started with the init context which was never cancelled by `Stop()`, causing it to run indefinitely.
- Add a dedicated cancellable context (`stopCleanups`) that is cancelled when `Stop()` is called.

Addresses review feedback from https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/121007#discussion_r2993404143

## Test plan
- [x] Package compiles successfully
- [ ] Existing tests pass in CI

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: RafaelPaulovic

reviews.body:
lgtm. It would be nice to add a test for detecting leaking goroutines. There may be more of those.

See https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/b84ecb867eb0a4099f56bf557c145605eac6c5cf/pkg/storage/unified/search/bleve_test.go#L39-L46 for an example.

commits.message:
Storage: fix goroutine leak in runCleanups

The runCleanups goroutine was started with the init context which was
never cancelled by Stop(), causing it to leak. Add a dedicated
cancellable context that is cancelled when Stop() is called.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Provisioning: merge git common into common and embed ProvisioningTestHelper in GitTestHelper
body:
## What

Consolidate the two parallel provisioning test-infrastructure packages
(`common/` and `git/common/`) into one unified `common` package.
`GitTestHelper` now embeds `*ProvisioningTestHelper` instead of
duplicating its fields, so git-backed tests gain access to every
provisioning helper (cleanup, REST clients, connections, dashboard
multi-version clients, etc.) for free.

**Net effect:** −1 402 lines removed, +925 added (−477 net). One fewer
package to maintain, smaller public API surface, and git tests can
finally call helpers that were previously exclusive to non-git tests.

## Why

`git/common/testing.go` duplicated most of `common/testing.go`:
identical `Repositories`, `Jobs`, `AdminREST`, `EditorREST`, `ViewerREST`,
`DashboardsV1`, plus its own `FoldersV1` (→ `Folders`). The two types
evolved independently, making it easy to add a helper to one and forget
the other. Merging them removes that duplication and lets `GitTestHelper`
delegate to `ProvisioningTestHelper` for cleanup, job orchestration, and
everything else.

## What changed

### 1. Dead code removal (`common/testing.go`)

Removed 7 symbols with zero callers after the
export-tests-to-SharedGitEnv migration:

| Symbol | Kind |
|--------|------|
| `GitExportHelper` | type + 4 methods |
| `RunGrafanaWithGitServerForExport` | func |
| `gitRepoInfo` | type |
| `RequireRecreated` | func |
| `WithLogs` | func |
| `GetDashboardGeneration` | func |
| `GetDashboardCreationTimestamp` | func |

### 2. Unexport internal helpers

5 helpers only used within `common/testing.go` were lowercased to
shrink the public API:

`MustNestedBool` → `mustNestedBool`, `MustNestedInt64` → `mustNestedInt64`,
`WaitForRepoLastRef` → `waitForRepoLastRef`, `RequireJobSuccess` →
`requireJobSuccess`, `RunGrafanaShared` → `runGrafanaShared`.

### 3. Move `git/common` → `common`

All types and functions from `git/common/testing.go` now live in
`common/testing.go`.

**Key structural change:**

```go
// Before (git/common)
type GitTestHelper struct {
    *apis.K8sTestHelper       // duplicated embed
    gitServer    *gittest.Server
    Repositories *apis.K8sResourceClient  // duplicate
    Jobs         *apis.K8sResourceClient  // duplicate
    AdminREST    *rest.RESTClient         // duplicate
    EditorREST   *rest.RESTClient         // duplicate
    ViewerREST   *rest.RESTClient         // duplicate
    DashboardsV1 *apis.K8sResourceClient  // duplicate
    FoldersV1    *apis.K8sResourceClient  // ≈ Folders
    exportRepoInfos map[string]*exportRepoInfo
}

// After (common)
type GitTestHelper struct {
    *ProvisioningTestHelper                // single embed, all fields promoted
    gitServer       *gittest.Server
    exportRepoInfos map[string]*exportRepoInfo
}
```

`RunGrafanaWithGitServer` now delegates to `RunGrafana` internally;
`runGrafanaWithGitServerShared` delegates to `runGrafanaShared`.

### 4. Update 24 importing files

- `gitcommon "…/git/common"` → `"…/common"` (or removed when already imported)
- `gitcommon.X` → `common.X`
- `helper.FoldersV1` → `helper.Folders`

### 5. Delete `git/common/`

872 lines removed (single file).

### 6. README update

- Documented `SharedGitEnv` pattern alongside `SharedEnv`
- Added comparison table: when to use `SharedEnv` vs `SharedGitEnv`
- Added reference table for the unified `common` package API
- Updated migration checklist

## Test plan

- [x] `go build ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/...` passes
- [x] `go vet ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/...` passes
- [ ] CI integration tests pass (git, foldermetadata, sourcepath_guard, quota, etc.)
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: Awesome! ❤️

commits.message:
refactor(provisioning/tests): remove dead code from common/testing.go

Remove GitExportHelper, RunGrafanaWithGitServerForExport, gitRepoInfo,
RequireRecreated, WithLogs, GetDashboardGeneration, and
GetDashboardCreationTimestamp — all unused after the export tests
migration to SharedGitEnv.

Made-with: Cursor

refactor(provisioning/tests): unexport internal-only helpers in common

Lowercase MustNestedBool, MustNestedInt64, WaitForRepoLastRef,
RequireJobSuccess, and RunGrafanaShared — none are used outside
the common package.

Made-with: Cursor

refactor(provisioning/tests): move git/common into common, embed ProvisioningTestHelper

Move GitTestHelper, SharedGitEnv, and all git-specific methods from
git/common/testing.go into common/testing.go. GitTestHelper now embeds
*ProvisioningTestHelper instead of duplicating fields, gaining access
to all provisioning helpers (cleanup, REST clients, dashboard/folder
clients, etc.).

Made-with: Cursor

refactor(provisioning/tests): replace gitcommon imports with common

Update 24 files to import from common instead of git/common.
Replace all gitcommon.X references with common.X and rename
FoldersV1 to Folders to match ProvisioningTestHelper's field name.

Made-with: Cursor

refactor(provisioning/tests): delete git/common/ directory

All code has been consolidated into common/testing.go.

Made-with: Cursor

docs(provisioning/tests): update README with SharedGitEnv and common package docs

Document the unified common package, SharedGitEnv pattern for git-backed
tests, and when to use SharedEnv vs SharedGitEnv.

Made-with: Cursor

style(provisioning/tests): fix goimports formatting in common/testing.go

Made-with: Cursor

fix(provisioning/tests): update dashboardV1 import to graduated v1 API

The dashboard API was graduated from v1beta1 to v1 in df29a0dd82b.
Our branch had the stale v1beta1 import, causing API version mismatch
errors when creating dashboards via DashboardsV1 client.

title: Provisioning: fix flaky namespace quota token refresh test
body:
## Summary

Fixes a flaky integration test `TestIntegrationProvisioning_HealthAndTokenRefreshWhileOverNamespaceQuota` that consistently fails on SQLite but rarely on PostgreSQL/MySQL.

**Root cause:** Step 4 of the test uses a Get → Modify → `UpdateStatus` pattern to manufacture a near-expiry token on a repo. `UpdateStatus` requires a specific `resourceVersion` and fails with an optimistic-locking conflict when the reconciler updates the object in between. On fast backends (SQLite) the reconciler runs in a tight loop, outpacing even aggressive retry strategies — both a 20-attempt no-delay loop and a 60s `EventuallyWithT` with 10ms polling consistently fail across every single attempt.

With PostgreSQL/MySQL the added I/O latency widens the window enough for the test to occasionally win the race.

**Fix:** Replace the Get → Modify → `UpdateStatus` pattern with a **merge patch** on the status subresource. A `MergePatchType` patch does not carry a `resourceVersion`, so the apiserver applies it to the latest version regardless of concurrent updates — there is no conflict possible.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes on SQLite (the backend where this flake reproduced)
- [ ] The existing Step 5 assertions (`health.checked` advanced, `token.lastUpdated` advanced, quota still exceeded) continue to pass, confirming the near-expiry token state was successfully written before the reconciler picked it up
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: fix flaky namespace quota token refresh test

Replace the Get-Modify-UpdateStatus pattern with a merge patch on the
status subresource in Step 4 of
TestIntegrationProvisioning_HealthAndTokenRefreshWhileOverNamespaceQuota.

UpdateStatus requires a specific resourceVersion and fails with an
optimistic-locking conflict when the reconciler updates the object in
between.  On fast backends (SQLite) the reconciler runs in a tight
loop, outpacing even aggressive retry strategies — both a 20-attempt
no-delay loop and a 60s EventuallyWithT with 10ms polling consistently
fail.

A merge patch does not carry a resourceVersion, so it always applies
cleanly to the latest version regardless of concurrent updates.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: consolidate foldermetadata git tests under shared server
body:
## Summary

- Move `git/foldermetadata_fix/` → `foldermetadata/fix/` using `SharedGitEnv` with `WithProvisioningFolderMetadata`, replacing 4 standalone `RunGrafanaWithGitServer` calls with a single shared Grafana + gittest server
- Move `git/foldermetadata_full/` → `foldermetadata/full/` using `SharedGitEnv` (no flag), replacing 4 standalone `RunGrafanaWithGitServer` calls with a single shared server
- Move `foldermetadata/git/` export tests into `foldermetadata/gitexport/` (flag-enabled) and `foldermetadata/full/` (flag-disabled), both using `SharedGitEnv`
- Add `CreateExportGitRepo`, `GitFileExists`, and `GitReadFile` methods to `GitTestHelper` in `git/common/testing.go` so export (push) tests can reuse the shared git environment instead of requiring standalone `RunGrafanaWithGitServerForExport` servers

This continues the shared-server strategy from #120906 and #121004. All foldermetadata-related git tests now live under `foldermetadata/` and share servers, eliminating per-test Grafana startups and reducing CI wall time.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes for `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/fix/`
- [ ] CI passes for `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/full/`

title: Provisioning: Refactor git integration tests to use SharedGitEnv
body:
## Summary
- Refactors the root `provisioning/git/` integration test package to use the `SharedGitEnv` pattern (one shared Grafana + gittest server per package) instead of spinning up independent servers per test function
- Aligns the root `git/` package with the convention already used by subpackages (`sourcepath_guard`, `foldermetadata_incremental`, `foldermetadata_files`)
- Quota tests are left unchanged since they require per-test server configurations with different `ProvisioningMaxResourcesPerRepository` values

## Changes
- **`helper_test.go`**: Replaced `testsuite.Run(m)` with `NewSharedGitEnv()` + `sharedGitHelper(t)` + `env.RunTestMain(m)`
- **6 test files**: Replaced `gitcommon.RunGrafanaWithGitServer(t)` → `sharedGitHelper(t)` and removed redundant `testutil.SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode(t)` (handled by `GetHelper`)
  - `incremental_test.go` (11 tests)
  - `health_test.go` (1 test)
  - `files_test.go` (6 tests)

  - `folder_file_rejection_test.go` (7 tests)

Provisioning: Refactor git integration tests to use SharedGitEnv

Replace per-test Grafana+git server provisioning with a single shared
environment for the root provisioning/git/ package, matching the pattern
already used by subpackages (sourcepath_guard, foldermetadata_*).

Tests now call sharedGitHelper(t) which returns a clean helper from the
shared SharedGitEnv, reducing integration test overhead by reusing one
Grafana and gittest server across all tests in the package.

Quota tests are left unchanged as they require per-test server configs
with different ProvisioningMaxResourcesPerRepository values.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: fix flaky git quota integration test

Use a single shared Grafana + git server for all subtests instead of
spinning up a new server per subtest. Quota limits are now changed at
runtime via SetQuotaStatus (atomic store in FixedQuotaGetter), matching
the proven pattern from the quota/ package tests.

This removes the t.Skip("flaky") and makes the test reliable by
eliminating 5 redundant server startups and teardowns.

title: fix: add appmanifest to loopback service identity
body:
This PR adds `apps.grafana.app:*` to the service identity token permissions used by Grafana's internal loopback client.

#### Why do we need this feature?

When Flux applies AppManifest resources via server-side apply to the aggregated APIServer (`apps.grafana.app`), the `PATCH` is authorized with the caller's identity. However, if the resource doesn't exist yet, Kubernetes internally converts the operation to a CREATE using the server's own loopback identity. That loopback identity was missing permissions for the `apps.grafana.app` API group, causing the CREATE to be denied with a `403 Forbidden error`.
author: konsalex

commits.message: fix: add appmanifest to loopback service identity

title: Unified Storage: Quotas enforce per resource
body:
**Problem**
Currently we enforce quotas with a single boolean — when true, ALL resources that exceed their quota get blocked. We want to be able to specify which group+resource pairs should have enforcement, so we can roll out quota enforcement incrementally per resource type. Resources not in the enforcement list still log when over quota but don't block writes.

**Fix**
Remove the EnforceQuotas boolean entirely and replace it with an EnforcedResources list. If a resource is in the list, quota violations return an error. If not, violations are logged but allowed through.
author: owensmallwood

reviews.body: LGTM!

commits.message: unified storage quotas enforce per resource

title: Unified Storage: Tenant watcher can force pending delete records to stay
body:
**Problem**
Orphaned tenants can exist where the tenant API thinks a tenant is active, but it's actually deleted in gcom. This applied to tenants deleted before the pending-delete work was added. When the tenant watcher sees these tenants as `active` it clears the pending-delete record, not allowing us to remove tenant data.

**Fix**
Add a `Force` field on the pending delete record. This will allow manually-seeded pending-delete records to be immune to clearing by the watcher, allowing us to clean up orphaned tenants (no pending-delete label in the tenant api).
author: owensmallwood

reviews.body: LGTM

title: IAM: Add UserAuthorizer for teams subresource
body:
## Summary
- Add `newUserAuthorizer()` that delegates to `ResourceAuthorizer` for all subresources except `teams`
- For the `teams` subresource, explicitly check `get` permission on the parent user resource
- Add comprehensive tests for all decision paths (allowed, denied, error, delegation cases)

## Why
Pre-requisite for https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/118377

**Problem:** The generic `ResourceAuthorizer` passes all subresources to the RBAC service. I plan to change the AuthZ Service check so when a subresource lacks a mapper entry (like `users/teams`), the RBAC service denies access. This will break authorization for existing subresources.

**Solution:** Create a dedicated `UserAuthorizer` that intercepts the `teams` subresource and explicitly checks `get` on the parent user (*which is what was currently happening*). This matches the current access control logic where viewing a user's teams requires `users:read` on that user (legacy endpoint: `/api/users/:id/teams`).

## Consistency with legacy access control
| Endpoint | Legacy Permission | New K8s Check |
|----------|------------------|---------------|
| `/api/users/:id/teams` | `users:read` scoped to user | `get` on `users/{name}` |

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/iam/... -run "UserAuthorizer"` to verify new tests pass
- [ ] Verify `TestNewUserAuthorizer_TeamsSubresource_CheckRequest` validates correct CheckRequest is built
- [ ] Verify `TestNewUserAuthorizer_DecisionMatrix` covers all decision paths (allowed, denied, error, delegation, no-opinion)
author: gamab

commits.message:
IAM: Add UserAuthorizer to enforce get permission on teams subresource

**Why:**
In the legacy /api/users/:id/teams endpoint, viewing a user's teams required
`users:read` permission scoped to that specific user (users:id:<id>). This
ensures users can only view teams of users they have permission to access.

The K8s API at /apis/iam.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/{ns}/users/{name}/teams
should maintain the same authorization behavior. Previously, the RBAC service
would ignore the 'teams' subresource and just check get permission on users,
but we now explicitly enforce this through a dedicated UserAuthorizer.

**Changes:**
- Add newUserAuthorizer() that delegates to ResourceAuthorizer for all
  subresources except 'teams'
- For 'teams' subresource, explicitly check get permission on the parent user
- Add comprehensive tests for all decision paths

**Consistency with legacy:**
- Legacy: usersRoute.Get("/:id/teams", authorize(ac.EvalPermission(ac.ActionUsersRead, userIDScope)), ...)
- New: CheckRequest{Verb: utils.VerbGet, Resource: "users", Name: userID}

Made-with: Cursor

title: Storage: improve broadcaster resilience to write bursts
body:
## Summary

The broadcaster disconnects "slow consumers" when their channel buffer is full. Under burst write load, this causes cascading failure: disconnected watchers reconnect with `sendInitialEvents=true`, triggering full LIST replays that overload the database.

**Root cause**: ring buffer cache size (100) == subscriber channel buffer (100). When a new subscriber joins and the cache is full, `readInto` fills the channel completely, leaving zero headroom. The next event triggers immediate disconnect.

**Changes**:
- Increase cache size to 500 and subscriber channel buffer to 1000, so `readInto` never fills the channel completely
- Add per-subscriber overflow slice that absorbs bursts instead of immediately disconnecting. Uses a slice rather than a larger channel because most subscribers keep up — allocating a 50k-deep channel per subscriber would waste memory, while a nil slice costs nothing until actually needed
- Disconnect only when overflow exceeds cap (50,000 items)
- Drain overflow inline during event dispatch + via a 100ms ticker for idle periods, keeping `stream()` as the sole owner of subscriber state (no extra goroutines, no ordering issues)
- Add rate-limited overflow logging with event counter

Relates to https://github.com/grafana/search-and-storage-team/issues/724

## Test plan
- [x] All existing broadcaster tests pass
- [x] New test: overflow spools instead of disconnecting (`TestBroadcasterOverflowSpoolsInsteadOfDisconnecting`)
- [x] New test: disconnect only on overflow cap exceeded (`TestBroadcasterDisconnectsOnOverflowCapExceeded`)
- [x] New test: readInto from full cache doesn't fill channel (`TestBroadcasterReadIntoDoesNotFillChannel`)
- [x] New test: overflow memory released when caught up (`TestBroadcasterOverflowMemoryReleasedWhenCaughtUp`)
- [x] All tests pass with `-race`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: pstibrany

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
Storage: improve broadcaster resilience to write bursts

The broadcaster disconnected "slow consumers" when their channel buffer
was full. Under burst write load, this caused cascading failure:
disconnected watchers reconnect with sendInitialEvents=true, triggering
full LIST replays that overload the database.

Root cause: ring buffer cache size (100) == subscriber channel buffer
(100). When a new subscriber joins and the cache is full, readInto fills
the channel completely, leaving zero headroom for new events.

Changes:
- Increase cache (500) and subscriber buffer (1000) so readInto never
  fills the channel completely
- Add per-subscriber overflow slice that absorbs bursts instead of
  immediately disconnecting. Overflow uses a slice rather than a larger
  channel because most subscribers keep up — allocating a 50k-deep
  channel per subscriber would waste memory, while a nil slice costs
  nothing until actually needed.
- Disconnect only when overflow exceeds cap (50,000 items)
- Drain overflow inline during event dispatch + via a 100ms ticker for
  idle periods, keeping stream() as the sole owner of subscriber state
- Add rate-limited overflow logging with event counter

Relates to https://github.com/grafana/search-and-storage-team/issues/724

title: IAM: Add team authorizer for members subresource

## Summary

Pre-requisite for https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/118377

**Problem:** The generic `ResourceAuthorizer` passes all subresources to the RBAC service. 
I plan to change the AuthZ Service check so when a subresource lacks a mapper entry (like `teams/members`), the RBAC service denies access. This will break authorization for existing subresources.

**Solution:** Create a dedicated `TeamAuthorizer` that intercepts the `members` subresource and explicitly checks `get_permissions` on the parent team. This matches the existing access control logic in the handler while moving it to the proper K8s authorizer layer.

title: Provisioning: metadata fix job cleans .keep files
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Metadata fix job would create _folder.json metadata files but left .keep files in empty folders. With this change, the job will also cleanup .keep files for folders that already had a _folders.json file and for the ones that the actual run creates the metadata file. 

**Why do we need this feature?**

Without it, a repository may get polluted witih uneeded .keep files. For example a repository may end with:
- folderA/
- folderA/_folder.json
- folderA/.keep

The end result should only contain:
- folderA/
- folderA/_folder.json

**Who is this feature for?**

Provisioning feature repos.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1027

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ x ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ x ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ x ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Provisioning: Fix root folder incorrectly showing missing metadata warning
body:
**What is this feature?**

Fixes the permissions drawer for root folders of folder-targeted provisioned repositories. Previously, navigating to the root folder and opening the permissions drawer showed "This folder is missing metadata," preventing users from editing permissions. The root folder now correctly reports its metadata status as ok.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Root folders of folder-targeted repos never have a `_folder.json` file by design -- their identity is inherently stable (UID = repo name). The `useFolderMetadataStatus` hook did not account for this, so it queried for the nonexistent file, got a 404, and incorrectly flagged the metadata as missing. Nested folders were unaffected because they do have `_folder.json` files.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1030
author: Clarity-89

commits.message: Provisioning: Fix root folder incorrectly showing missing metadata warning

body:
**What is this feature?**

Adds `test.slow()` to the i18n language verification e2e test, tripling the default Playwright timeout.

**Why do we need this feature?**

The `verify-i18n` test loops through multiple languages, changing the language preference and verifying the translation each time. This takes longer than the default test timeout allows, causing flaky timeouts in CI.

**Who is this feature for?**

Developers running e2e tests.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes #121078

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: joshhunt

reviews.body: nice 👍

The verify-i18n test loops through many languages, causing timeouts.
Use test.slow() to triple the default timeout.

title: Provisioning: authorize fix metadata job based ref write permissions
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Fix metadata job should not create direct pushes to branches that are not meant to be directly written. This PR adds the job the same auth sequence than the other jobs, getting the same "forbidden" response as other job runs with similar characteristics. This solved the backend part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1028

**Why do we need this feature?**

Without it, fix metadata jobs may run against read-only branch (e.g. main branch on a repository configured with branch workflow), which is unintended and undesired behavior.

**Who is this feature for?**

Provisioning feature users, related to folder permissions.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

title: Provisioning: Refactor OpenAPI version helper and revert NewAPIBuilder signature
body:
## Summary

Addresses PR feedback by:
1. Extracting OpenAPI version replacement logic into a reusable, well-tested helper function
2. Reverting `NewAPIBuilder` from Options struct pattern to individual parameters (with v1beta1 support)
3. Completely eliminating v0alpha1 schema leakage in v1beta1 OpenAPI spec
4. Adding comprehensive integration tests for v1beta1 API

## Changes

### 1. OpenAPI Version Helper (`openapi_version.go`)
**New reusable function**: `ReplaceOpenAPIVersion(defs, group, oldVersion, newVersion)`

- ✅ Recursively transforms all schema references (Properties, Items, AllOf, AnyOf, OneOf, Definitions, etc.)
- ✅ Updates dependency arrays with new version strings
- ✅ **Only includes new version schemas** - removes old version keys to prevent duplicates
- ✅ Preserves non-matching refs (e.g., k8s types)
- ✅ Comprehensive unit tests (11 test cases covering all edge cases)

### 2. NewAPIBuilder Signature Reversion (`register.go`)
**Before (Options pattern)**:
```go
func NewAPIBuilder(opts Options) (*APIBuilder, error)
```

**After (Individual parameters)**:
```go
func NewAPIBuilder(
    gv schema.GroupVersion,           // NEW: for v1beta1 support
    isPreferredVersion bool,          // NEW: for v1beta1 support
    onlyApiServer bool,
    repoFactory repository.Factory,
    // ... 22 more parameters
) (*APIBuilder, error)
```

**Rationale**: Aligns with main branch patterns while preserving v1beta1 multi-version support via `gv` and `isPreferredVersion` parameters.

### 3. Fix v0alpha1 Schema Leakage in v1beta1 OpenAPI
**Problem**: v1beta1 OpenAPI spec contained BOTH v0alpha1 and v1beta1 schemas (60+ duplicates)

**Solution**:
- Updated `ReplaceOpenAPIVersion` to skip old version keys when building result map
- Added filtering in `PostProcessOpenAPI` to remove any remaining v0alpha1 schemas from v1beta1 spec
- Result: **v1beta1 OpenAPI now contains ONLY v1beta1 schemas** (63 types, 0 v0alpha1)

### 4. v1beta1 Integration Tests (`pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/`)
Four comprehensive integration test scenarios:

1. **API Discovery** - Verifies v1beta1 is available in provisioning API group
2. **OpenAPI Schema Validation** - Ensures only v1beta1 schemas (enforces no v0alpha1)
3. **Repository CRUD** - Full create/get/list/delete cycle via v1beta1 endpoints
4. **Connection API** - Verifies connection list endpoint works with v1beta1

### 5. Metrics Test Fix (`pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/metrics_test.go`)
Updated test to expect `NotPanics` on double registration (metrics now use `sync.Once`)

## Testing

### Unit Tests (11 test cases)
```bash
go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning -run TestReplaceOpenAPIVersion -v
```

**Coverage**:
- ✅ Version replacement in keys
- ✅ Removing old keys without creating duplicates
- ✅ Updating schema refs in nested structures
- ✅ Updating dependency arrays
- ✅ Handling AllOf/AnyOf/OneOf constructs
- ✅ Preserving non-matching refs (k8s types)

### Integration Tests (4 scenarios)
```bash
go test ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1 -v
```

**Results**:
- ✅ v1beta1 API group available
- ✅ OpenAPI schema has **only v1beta1 types** (63 schemas)
- ✅ **Zero v0alpha1 schemas** in v1beta1 spec (test fails if any found)
- ✅ Repository CRUD operations work via v1beta1
- ✅ Connection API endpoint responds correctly

### Metrics Test
```bash
go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs -run TestRegisterJobMetrics -v
```
- ✅ Double registration is safe with sync.Once

## Benefits

1. **Reusable**: Helper function can be used for any API group version bump
2. **Well-tested**: 15 total tests (11 unit + 4 integration)
3. **Clean OpenAPI**: v1beta1 spec contains only v1beta1 schemas (no leakage)
4. **Maintainable**: Clear function signature with individual parameters
5. **Future-proof**: Makes future version bumps easier and more reliable

## Before/After

### v1beta1 OpenAPI Spec
**Before**: 63 v1beta1 schemas + 60 v0alpha1 schemas ❌  
**After**: 63 v1beta1 schemas + 0 v0alpha1 schemas ✅

### NewAPIBuilder Signature
**Before**: Options struct pattern (not aligned with main)  
**After**: Individual parameters (aligned with main, plus v1beta1 support)

## Related

- Addresses PR feedback about making version replacement a utility function
- Addresses PR feedback about reverting Options struct pattern
- Fixes v0alpha1/v1beta1 schema duplication in OpenAPI spec
- Fixes metrics test expecting panic with sync.Once

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commits.message:
Provisioning: Extract OpenAPI version replacement into helper function

Extract the inline version replacement logic into a reusable helper function
with proper handling of all schema references and dependencies.

Key improvements:
- Add `group` parameter for better reusability across API groups
- Fix duplicate keys issue by properly removing old keys
- Recursively update all schema refs (Properties, Items, AllOf, etc.)
- Update dependencies list with new version strings
- Add comprehensive unit tests (11 test cases)

This makes it easier to bump API versions when types are structurally
identical, addressing the PR feedback about making this a utility function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Add v1beta1 API integration tests

Add comprehensive integration tests for the v1beta1 provisioning API to verify:
- API group/version availability
- OpenAPI schema correctness (no v0alpha1 duplicates)
- Basic CRUD operations for Repository and Connection resources
- Proper API version handling throughout the request/response cycle

Tests use the REST client to directly test the v1beta1 endpoints and
validate that the OpenAPI version replacement is working correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tests: Fix metrics test expecting panic with sync.Once

The metrics registration now uses sync.Once to prevent double
registration, so it no longer panics. Update the test to verify
this safe behavior instead of expecting a panic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Revert NewAPIBuilder to use individual parameters

Changes:
- Revert NewAPIBuilder signature from Options struct to individual parameters
- Keep gv and isPreferredVersion parameters needed for v1beta1 support
- Update call sites to pass parameters directly instead of Options struct
- Fix ReplaceOpenAPIVersion to only include new version schemas (remove old)
- Add filtering in PostProcessOpenAPI to remove v0alpha1 schemas from v1beta1
- Update integration test to enforce no v0alpha1 schemas in v1beta1

This addresses PR feedback to avoid the Options struct pattern while
maintaining v1beta1 API support. The v1beta1 OpenAPI spec now contains
only v1beta1 schemas, with no v0alpha1 leakage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Add v1beta1 OpenAPI snapshot and fix ref replacement

Changes:
- Add provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1 to OpenAPI snapshot test list
- Fix PostProcessOpenAPI to also update $ref references in API paths
- Add updateOperationRefs helper to replace refs in request/response schemas
- Generate clean v1beta1 OpenAPI snapshot with no v0alpha1 references

Before: v1beta1 snapshot had 110 v0alpha1 $ref references
After: v1beta1 snapshot has 0 v0alpha1 references (177 v1beta1 refs)

The issue was that we were only deleting v0alpha1 schema definitions but
leaving dangling $ref references. Now we recursively update all refs in
both component schemas and API path definitions before deleting the old
schemas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Move OpenAPI ref replacement to helper and add docs

Changes:
1. **New helper function**: `ReplaceOpenAPISpecVersion()` in openapi_version.go
   - Replaces all $ref references in OpenAPI v3 specs
   - Updates refs in both component schemas AND API path operations
   - Removes old version schema definitions after updating refs
   - Comprehensive unit tests (5 test scenarios)

2. **Simplified PostProcessOpenAPI**: Now just calls the helper
   - Reduced cyclomatic complexity
   - Cleaner separation of concerns

3. **Enhanced documentation**: Added detailed comments for NewAPIBuilder
   - Explains `gv` parameter: GroupVersion for multi-version API support
   - Explains `isPreferredVersion`: Controls which version runs controllers
   - Clarifies multi-version registration pattern

4. **Verified snapshot correctness**:
   - v0alpha1 snapshot: 65 v0alpha1 schemas, 0 v1beta1 (correct)
   - v1beta1 snapshot: 67 v1beta1 schemas, 0 v0alpha1 (correct)
   - Version strings in metadata are expected and correct

The helper function is now reusable for any API group that needs to clean
up old version schemas from transformed OpenAPI specs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Fix x-kubernetes-group-version-kind arrays in OpenAPI snapshots

Fixes GVK metadata arrays that incorrectly listed both v0alpha1 and v1beta1
versions in each API version's OpenAPI snapshot.

## Problem
When registering multiple API versions (v0alpha1 and v1beta1) that share
the same Go types, the Kubernetes OpenAPI machinery automatically adds
x-kubernetes-group-version-kind metadata for ALL registered versions to
each schema. This resulted in snapshots showing both versions:

```json
"x-kubernetes-group-version-kind": [
  {"version": "v0alpha1", ...},
  {"version": "v1beta1", ...}
]
```

## Solution
Enhanced ReplaceOpenAPISpecVersion to filter GVK arrays after OpenAPI
definitions are added to the spec. The key fix was handling the correct
Go type: `[]map[string]interface{}` instead of `[]interface{}`.

Filtering logic:
- Keeps only GVK entries matching the target version
- Converts single-element arrays to objects (Kubernetes convention)
- Removes GVK metadata when no matching version found

## Result
- v0alpha1 snapshot: Only v0alpha1 GVK metadata (58 refs, 0 v1beta1)
- v1beta1 snapshot: Only v1beta1 GVK metadata (44 refs, 0 v0alpha1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Reduce cyclomatic complexity of OpenAPI version replacement

Refactored ReplaceOpenAPISpecVersion to reduce cyclomatic complexity
from 41 to under 30 by extracting GVK filtering logic into separate
helper functions:

- filterGVKExtensions: Main dispatcher for different GVK types
- filterGVKArrayTyped: Handles []map[string]interface{}
- filterGVKArrayInterface: Handles []interface{}
- filterGVKObject: Handles map[string]interface{}
- updateGVKExtension: Updates schema extensions based on filter results

Also changed if-else chain to switch statement in register.go for
better readability.

Fixes linting issues:
- gocyclo: cyclomatic complexity now < 30
- staticcheck: QF1003 (use tagged switch)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Add v1beta1 API package and CRUD integration tests

Created the v1beta1 package for provisioning APIs that serves the same
types as v0alpha1 under the v1beta1 API version. This enables multi-version
API support using OpenAPI schema transformation.

## v1beta1 Package Structure

**apps/provisioning/pkg/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/**
- `register.go`: Registers v0alpha1 types under v1beta1 GroupVersion
- `types.go`: Type aliases that re-export v0alpha1 types
- `doc.go`: Package documentation with k8s codegen tags

The v1beta1 package uses type aliases to avoid duplicating type definitions
while providing a separate API version. This pattern allows:
- Same Go types served under multiple API versions
- OpenAPI schema transformation in PostProcessOpenAPI
- Clean version evolution without code duplication

## Integration Tests

**pkg/tests/apis/provisioning_connection_crud_test.go**
- TestIntegrationV1Beta1ConnectionCRUD: Comprehensive CRUD tests for v1beta1 Connections
- Tests cover: Create, Get, List, Update, Delete operations
- Tests all connection types: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket
- Verifies secure values are properly filtered from responses

Test structure follows the established pattern from other API integration tests,
using the K8sTestHelper for test setup and dynamic clients for CRUD operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Reorganize v1beta1 integration tests into separate files

Moved integration tests from a single file to an organized directory structure
under pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/ with tests split by resource type.

## New Structure

**pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/**
- `helpers.go`: Shared test utilities and client factories
  - getConnectionClient(): Returns configured client for Connection tests
  - getRepositoryClient(): Returns configured client for Repository tests
  - Conversion helpers: toUnstructured(), fromUnstructuredToConnection(), etc.

- `connection_test.go`: All Connection CRUD integration tests
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Create_GitHub
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Create_GitLab
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Create_Bitbucket
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Get
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_List
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Update
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Delete

- `repository_test.go`: Placeholder for Repository tests (TODO)
- `spec_test.go`: Placeholder for spec validation tests (TODO)

## Generated Files

The code generation also created:
- zz_generated.deepcopy.go
- zz_generated.openapi.go

These were auto-generated by k8s codegen based on the v1beta1 package structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Remove v1beta1 types package, use v0alpha1 types in tests

Remove the v1beta1 types package (type aliases) and update integration
tests to use v0alpha1 types directly. This approach works because API
versioning is metadata-based (APIVersion field and GVR), not tied to
Go type system.

Changes:
- Deleted apps/provisioning/pkg/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/
  - No longer need type aliases (register.go, types.go, doc.go)
  - Auto-generated files also removed (zz_generated.*.go)

- Updated pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/helpers.go
  - Import v0alpha1 types instead of v1beta1
  - Tests still target v1beta1 API via GVR and APIVersion metadata

- Updated pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/connection_test.go
  - Import v0alpha1 types
  - Add required Title field to ConnectionSpec
  - Use PrivateKey (not Token) for GitHub connections with valid RSA key
  - Use ClientSecret for GitLab/Bitbucket connections
  - Fix assertion: require.Empty instead of require.Nil for RawSecureValue
  - Skip unsupported connection types (GitLab, Bitbucket)

All tests pass - proves v1beta1 API works with v0alpha1 types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Reorganize v1beta1 tests and move helpers to common package

Reorganize tests by moving them to appropriate files and consolidate
helper functions in the common package for better code reuse.

Changes:
- Moved TestIntegrationV1Beta1RepositoryCRUD from basic_test.go to repository_test.go
- Moved spec tests (APIAvailable, OpenAPISchema) from basic_test.go to spec_test.go
- Moved v1beta1 helper functions from pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/helpers.go
  to pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/common/testing.go:
  - GetConnectionClientV1Beta1
  - GetRepositoryClientV1Beta1
  - ToUnstructuredConnection
  - FromUnstructuredToConnection
  - ToUnstructuredRepository
  - FromUnstructuredToRepository
  - TestGithubPrivateKeyBase64 (returns base64-encoded test RSA key)
- Updated connection_test.go to use common package helpers
- Replaced long inline base64 RSA key with common.TestGithubPrivateKeyBase64()
- Deleted pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/helpers.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
WIP: Fix x-kubernetes-group-version-kind to be array

Provisioning: Fix x-kubernetes-group-version-kind to always be array

Fix OpenAPI schema generation to keep x-kubernetes-group-version-kind
as an array with a single element instead of converting it to an object.
Also prevent duplicates when replacing versions.

Changes:
- Modified replaceGVKVersion() to deduplicate GVK entries
- Modified updateGVKExtension() to always keep GVK as array (never convert single element to object)
- Updated filterGVKExtensions() comment to reflect array-only behavior

This ensures the OpenAPI spec has the correct format:
  "x-kubernetes-group-version-kind": [
    {
      "group": "provisioning.grafana.app",
      "kind": "Connection",
      "version": "v1beta1"
    }
  ]

Instead of:
  "x-kubernetes-group-version-kind": {
    "group": "provisioning.grafana.app",
    "kind": "Connection",
    "version": "v1beta1"
  }

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Add x-kubernetes-group-version-kind metadata to v1beta1 OpenAPI schemas

The v1beta1 API was missing x-kubernetes-group-version-kind extensions in the
OpenAPI spec because v1beta1 uses the same types as v0alpha1 (type aliases).
When transforming v0alpha1 OpenAPI definitions to v1beta1, the GVK metadata
wasn't being copied or transformed.

This commit adds:
- GVK extension handling in replaceSchemaVersion to transform GVK versions
- replaceGVKExtension helper to update versions while preserving array format
- extractVersion helper to extract version from full pattern (e.g., ".provisioning.v0alpha1." -> "v0alpha1")
- ensureGVKForVersion to add GVK metadata to v1beta1 top-level resources
- Test coverage for GVK transformation
- Updated OpenAPI snapshots with correct v1beta1 GVK metadata

The GVK extensions are now correctly formatted as arrays with a single element:
[{"group": "provisioning.grafana.app", "kind": "Connection", "version": "v1beta1"}]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Provisioning: make metadata missing/corrupted warnings not block folders check/cleanups
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Missing or corrupted metadata is marking folders as failed update, which may block future checks and cleanup operations in such folders. This PR treats these warnings as non-blocking the folder operation as the folder will still get updated in such cases.

**Why do we need this feature?**

The only reason this bug is not blocking folder deletion is the order in which we are detecting missing metadata (after the cleanup for incremental pulls). That is brittle as future changes (or other checks for updated folder problems) will experience false positive due to missing metadata situations.

**Who is this feature for?**

Provisioning feature.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1024

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ x ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ x  ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ x ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Provisioning: fix flaky namespace quota token refresh test
body:
## Summary

Fixes the flaky `TestIntegrationProvisioning_HealthAndTokenRefreshWhileOverNamespaceQuota` integration test which failed due to persistent optimistic-locking conflicts when the test tried to manipulate repository status.

## Root Cause

Step 4 of the test manufactures a near-expiry token state via a `Get` → modify → `UpdateStatus` cycle on the repository. The controller reconciler runs concurrently and can update the same resource between `Get` and `UpdateStatus`, bumping `resourceVersion` and triggering a conflict:

```
Operation cannot be fulfilled on repositories.provisioning.grafana.app
"ns-quota-token-repo1": the object has been modified; please apply your
changes to the latest version and try again
```

The original code used `EventuallyWithT` with a **200ms poll interval** to retry. This left a wide window between retries for the controller to modify the resource again, and under sustained controller activity (periodic resync, quota re-evaluation) every retry could conflict — exhausting the 60s timeout.

## Fix

Wrap the `Get`/`UpdateStatus` cycle in two layers of retry:

| Layer | Mechanism | Backoff | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Inner** | `retry.RetryOnConflict` | ~10ms exponential (5 attempts) | Handles bursts of conflicts with minimal delay between `Get` and the next attempt |
| **Outer** | `require.EventuallyWithT` | 200ms poll, 60s deadline | Survives sustained controller activity where the inner loop alone exhausts its budget |

This gives up to **~1,500 total attempts** over 60 seconds (300 outer polls × 5 inner retries), compared to ~300 attempts with the original `EventuallyWithT`-only approach — and critically, the inner retries happen with ~10ms gaps instead of 200ms.

## Test plan

- [x] `go build ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/quota/...` compiles
- [ ] CI integration tests pass
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: fix flaky namespace quota token refresh test

Use two layers of retry for the status manipulation step:
- Inner RetryOnConflict (~10ms backoff, 5 retries) handles bursts of
  optimistic-locking conflicts from the controller reconciler.
- Outer EventuallyWithT (60s timeout, 200ms poll) survives sustained
  controller activity (periodic resync, quota re-evaluation) where the
  inner loop alone can exhaust its budget.

Previously the test relied solely on EventuallyWithT with a 200ms poll
interval, which left a wide window for the controller to update the
resource between Get and UpdateStatus on every attempt.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Dashboard: Suppress error alert on API version discovery failure
body: The DashboardAPIVersionResolver calls the k8s discovery endpoint to negotiate API versions. When users lack access to this endpoint, the request fails and getBackendSrv() shows an error toast. Since the resolver already handles failures gracefully by falling back to beta versions, suppress the error alert with showErrorAlert: false.
author: dprokop

The DashboardAPIVersionResolver calls the k8s discovery endpoint to
negotiate API versions. When users lack access to this endpoint, the
request fails and getBackendSrv() shows an error toast. Since the
resolver already handles failures gracefully by falling back to beta
versions, suppress the error alert with showErrorAlert: false.

title: Provisioning: fix flaky ownership protection integration tests
body:
## What

Fix two flaky integration tests that intermittently fail in CI:

- `TestIntegrationProvisioning_FilesOwnershipProtection` — expects 2 dashboards after sync but only finds 1, times out after 60s.
- `TestIntegrationProvisioning_PullJobOwnershipProtection` — `AwaitJobs` fails with "expect at least one job" because historic job records are missing.

## Why

Both tests create two repositories concurrently using goroutines + `sync.WaitGroup`, then assert that both repos' dashboards were provisioned. This pattern is unreliable for two reasons:

1. **Concurrent sync race condition** — When both repos trigger `SyncAndWait` simultaneously, the sync jobs interfere with each other. Observed symptoms include one repo's dashboard never being created, files failing to parse mid-write (`resource validation failed: file does not contain a valid resource`), and `AwaitJobs` unable to find historic job records.

2. **`testing.T` misuse from goroutines** — `require` functions call `t.FailNow()`, which invokes `runtime.Goexit()`. Per [Go's testing contract](https://pkg.go.dev/testing#T.FailNow), this must only be called from the goroutine running the test. When called from a spawned goroutine, it only exits *that* goroutine — the deferred `wg.Done()` still fires, `wg.Wait()` returns, and the test continues with incomplete state.

## How

Replace the concurrent goroutine pattern with sequential `CreateRepo` calls. Both repos still use `SkipResourceAssertions: true` with a combined `EventuallyWithT` check afterward, so there is no change in what the tests validate — only the setup ordering.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes for `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning` and `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/jobs`
- [ ] Both previously flaky tests pass reliably across multiple runs
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: fix flaky ownership protection integration tests

Replace concurrent repository creation (goroutines + sync.WaitGroup)
with sequential creation in both FilesOwnershipProtection and
PullJobOwnershipProtection tests.

The concurrent pattern caused two issues:
- Sync jobs racing with each other, leaving one repo's dashboard
  unprovisioned (or failing to parse files mid-write).
- require/t.FailNow() called from goroutines only exits that goroutine
  (per Go testing contract), so wg.Done() still fires and the test
  continues with incomplete state.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: Add full sync UID takeover recovery test
body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #120962. Adds integration tests covering UID changes across multiple files during provisioning sync — both **one-directional takeover** (A claims B's UID, B gets a new one) and **symmetric swap** (A↔B exchange UIDs).

### The bug (FIXME)

Full sync processes file changes in parallel via `applyResourcesInParallel`. During a UID takeover or swap, a race exists between goroutines: if one goroutine completes its write+delete cycle before the other's write starts, it deletes the UID the other goroutine needs. The subsequent create-after-delete fails against the storage layer, leaving a dashboard missing. The first full sync is therefore **non-deterministic** and may produce an inconsistent state.

A **second full sync** recovers because it compares the git tree against the current Grafana resources and re-creates any missing dashboards.

### Test matrix

| Test | Sync type | Scenario | Deterministic? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IncrementalGitSync_MultiFileUIDTakeover` | Incremental | A takes B's UID, B gets new UID | Yes (alphabetical order) |
| `IncrementalGitSync_MultiFileUIDSwap` | Incremental | A↔B symmetric UID swap | Yes (alphabetical order) |
| `FullSync_MultiFileUIDTakeover_Recovery` | Full (×2) | A takes B's UID, B gets new UID | Recovery sync is deterministic |
| `FullSync_MultiFileUIDSwap_Recovery` | Full (×2) | A↔B symmetric UID swap | Recovery sync is deterministic |

### Other changes

- Migrates the `sourcepath_guard` test package to use `SharedGitEnv` so all four tests share a single Grafana + Git server instance, reducing startup cost.

## Test plan

- [ ] `TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalGitSync_MultiFileUIDTakeover`
- [ ] `TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalGitSync_MultiFileUIDSwap`
- [ ] `TestIntegrationProvisioning_FullSync_MultiFileUIDTakeover_Recovery`
- [ ] `TestIntegrationProvisioning_FullSync_MultiFileUIDSwap_Recovery`
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Add full sync UID takeover recovery test

Add an integration test that verifies a second full sync recovers the
correct dashboard state after a multi-file UID takeover. Full sync
processes files in parallel, so a race between goroutines can cause one
dashboard to be lost on the first sync. The second full sync re-compares
the git tree against Grafana resources and re-creates any missing
dashboards.

Also migrates the sourcepath_guard package to use SharedGitEnv so both
tests share a single Grafana + Git server instance.

Made-with: Cursor

Add symmetric UID swap tests for incremental and full sync

Add two new test cases covering a symmetric UID swap (A→B, B→A):

- IncrementalGitSync_MultiFileUIDSwap: deterministic test verifying the
  sourcePath guard prevents accidental deletion when both files exchange
  their UIDs simultaneously.

- FullSync_MultiFileUIDSwap_Recovery: documents the parallel-processing
  race bug (FIXME) and verifies a second full sync recovers the correct
  dashboard state after a symmetric swap.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: fix folder creation on new branch with folder metadata
body:
Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1025

## What

Fixes a **"ref not found"** error when creating a folder via the provisioning files endpoint using the **branch workflow** with the `provisioningFolderMetadata` feature flag enabled.

## Why

When `CreateFolder` runs with folder metadata enabled, it first calls `ReadFolderMetadata` → `repo.Read()` to check whether the folder already exists. If the target branch doesn't exist yet (the normal case when a user creates a folder on a brand-new feature branch), `Read` fails with `ErrRefNotFound`.

The error guard only accepted `ErrFileNotFound` as the "not found, proceed to create" signal:

```go
// before
if !errors.Is(readErr, repository.ErrFileNotFound) {
    return fmt.Errorf("failed to read folder metadata for %q: %w", folderPath, readErr)
}
```

`ErrRefNotFound` fell through and surfaced as an HTTP 404 `"ref not found"` to the caller.

## How

Accept `ErrRefNotFound` alongside `ErrFileNotFound` so the code proceeds to `WriteFolderMetadata`, which calls `repo.Create` → `ensureBranchExists` and creates the branch automatically:

```go
// after
if !errors.Is(readErr, repository.ErrFileNotFound) && !errors.Is(readErr, repository.ErrRefNotFound) {
    return fmt.Errorf("failed to read folder metadata for %q: %w", folderPath, readErr)
}
```

The non-metadata code path (`folderMetadataEnabled = false`) was **not** affected because it calls `repo.Create` directly, which already handles branch creation via `ensureBranchExists`.

## Test plan

**Unit test** (new, in `dualwriter_test.go`):
- `TestCreateFolder/flag_enabled:_ref_not_found_is_treated_as_file_not_found_(new_branch)` — mocks `Read` returning `ErrRefNotFound` on a branch-workflow repo and asserts `_folder.json` is written to the new branch.

**Integration tests** (new package `foldermetadata_files`):
- Create folder on default branch → `_folder.json` exists
- Create folder on a **new branch** → `_folder.json` exists on the branch but not on the default branch (the bug scenario)
- Create nested folder on a new branch → `_folder.json` for both parent and child segments
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: fix folder creation on new branch with folder metadata

When folderMetadataEnabled is true, CreateFolder reads existing
metadata before writing. If the target branch does not exist yet
(branch workflow), repo.Read returns ErrRefNotFound which was not
handled — only ErrFileNotFound was accepted. This caused a "ref not
found" error instead of proceeding to create the folder.

Treat ErrRefNotFound the same as ErrFileNotFound so that the
subsequent WriteFolderMetadata call creates the branch via
ensureBranchExists and writes the _folder.json normally.

Adds a unit test covering the fix and an integration test package
(foldermetadata_files) exercising folder creation via the files
endpoint on default branches, new branches, and nested paths.

Made-with: Cursor

title: DashboardKind: Add panels from collapsed rows into lookup
body:
**What is this feature?**

Includes panels from collapsed rows to be considered when calling `ReadDashboard`. 

A test case has been added as a devenv dashboard, with the fixture generated showing that it contains the `datasource` array at the root level in the output.

**Why do we need this feature?**

When reading the dashboard with such a lookup:

```go
	lookup := dashboardkind.CreateDatasourceLookup([]*dashboardkind.DatasourceQueryResult{
		// empty values (does not resolve anything)
	})

	dashSummaryInfo, err := dashboardkind.ReadDashboard(bytes.NewReader(payload), lookup)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse dashboard: %w", err)
	}
```

If the dashboard has rows that are collapsed, the dashboard model looks different, and `ReadDashboard` won't be able to find the data sources being used by the dashboard.

With this change, we include the panels from collapsed rows into the lookup.

**Who is this feature for?**

Public Dashboards

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Addresses https://github.com/grafana/support-escalations/issues/20974

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:

title: Annotations: Fix zero-width regions
body: **What is this feature?** Fix for zero width annotation regions. Requires `annotationsClustering` feature flag to be enabled. To fix overlapping point annotations, the annotation cluster panel option must be enabled. Before: After: **Why do we need this feature?** Another portion of the solution to https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/53359. This PR sets a min width on annotation regions, preventing un-clickable zero-width annotation regions. **Who is this feature for?** Annotation users **Special notes for your reviewer:** In conjunction with annotation clustering, this means that annotation _points_ that are overlapping are now interactable within the annotation cluster. This also fixes general zero-width annotation regions (but still requires the `annotationsClustering` flag) by applying the min annotation width to all regions. However, since region support for clustering is not yet available, you can still have multiple regions with the same time stamps that are not clickable (however they can be tabbed through with the keyboard). And regions can still overlap point annotations. [Region clustering](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/119864) would solve both of the above, but it outside the scope of this relatively simple fix. Also clustering should always be enabled for zero-width annotations (or any annotation within half the marker width of another), but we should let the manual panel option see some usage before we start changing existing behavior. Please check that: - [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
