title: IAM: Fix NULL email_verified column causing SQL scan error
body:
## Summary
The `email_verified` column in the database can be NULL, but `queryUsers` scanned it directly into a `bool` — `database/sql` cannot scan NULL into a plain bool, causing a scan error in the reconciler.
- Use `sql.NullBool` as an intermediate scan target and convert to bool afterwards, defaulting NULL to `false`.
- Added tests covering both NULL and `true` cases using `go-sqlmock`.

## Test plan
- [x] `TestQueryUsers_NullEmailVerified` — NULL `email_verified` scans as `false`
- [x] `TestQueryUsers_EmailVerifiedTrue` — `true` `email_verified` scans correctly
- [x] All existing tests in `pkg/registry/apis/iam/legacy/...` pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: mihai-turdean

commits.message:
IAM: Fix NULL email_verified column causing SQL scan error in reconciler

The email_verified column can be NULL in the database, but queryUsers
scanned it directly into a bool, which database/sql cannot handle.
Use sql.NullBool to safely handle NULL values, defaulting to false.

title: Provisioning: Register the existing types under v1beta1
body:
## Summary

Adds v1beta1 API version support for provisioning.grafana.app resources (Repository, Connection, Job, HistoricJob) as type aliases to v0alpha1, enabling multi-version API support without duplicating implementation.

## Background

The Provisioning API is moving from v0alpha1 to v1beta1 as part of API maturity progression. Rather than maintaining duplicate implementations, we use Go type aliases to share the same underlying types across versions while maintaining separate API endpoints.

## Changes

### Type Aliases (v1beta1)
- Created `apps/provisioning/pkg/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/` package with type aliases:
  - `Repository` → `v0alpha1.Repository`
  - `Connection` → `v0alpha1.Connection`
  - `Job` → `v0alpha1.Job`
  - `HistoricJob` → `v0alpha1.HistoricJob`
- Defined `GroupVersion`, resource metadata, and scheme registration

### API Registration
- Updated `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/register.go` to register both versions:
  - v0alpha1 as preferred version
  - v1beta1 with `OnlyApiServer: true` (API access only, no controllers)

### OpenAPI Schema Generation
- Added `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/openapi_version.go` with schema transformation functions:
  - `replaceSchemaVersion()`: Updates API version references in schemas
  - `replaceGVKExtension()`: Updates x-kubernetes-group-version-kind metadata
  - `ensureGVKForVersion()`: Ensures proper GVK metadata for all resource schemas
- Modified `PostProcessOpenAPIDefinitions()` to generate v1beta1-specific schemas

### Testing
- Reorganized test helpers into `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/common` package
- Updated integration tests to verify both v0alpha1 and v1beta1 endpoints
- Added OpenAPI snapshot: `pkg/tests/apis/openapi_snapshots/provisioning.grafana.app-v1beta1.json`

## API Endpoints

Both versions expose the same resources:

**v0alpha1** (preferred):
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/{ns}/repositories`
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/{ns}/connections`
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/{ns}/jobs`
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/{ns}/historicjobs`

**v1beta1** (new):
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1/namespaces/{ns}/repositories`
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1/namespaces/{ns}/connections`
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1/namespaces/{ns}/jobs`
- `/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1/namespaces/{ns}/historicjobs`

## Testing

```bash
# Both versions should work identically:
curl https://localhost:6446/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/default/repositories
curl https://localhost:6446/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1/namespaces/default/repositories

# OpenAPI schema available for both:
curl https://localhost:6446/openapi/v3/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v0alpha1
curl https://localhost:6446/openapi/v3/apis/provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1
```

## Migration Path

- **No breaking changes**: v0alpha1 remains fully functional
- **Gradual adoption**: Clients can migrate to v1beta1 at their own pace
- **Transparent aliasing**: Both versions use identical storage and logic

## Related

- Enterprise PR: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11427

commits.message:
Provisioning: Refactor OpenAPI version helper and revert NewAPIBuilder signature (#121075)

* Provisioning: Extract OpenAPI version replacement into helper function

Extract the inline version replacement logic into a reusable helper function
with proper handling of all schema references and dependencies.

Key improvements:
- Add `group` parameter for better reusability across API groups
- Fix duplicate keys issue by properly removing old keys
- Recursively update all schema refs (Properties, Items, AllOf, etc.)
- Update dependencies list with new version strings
- Add comprehensive unit tests (11 test cases)

This makes it easier to bump API versions when types are structurally
identical, addressing the PR feedback about making this a utility function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Add v1beta1 API integration tests

Add comprehensive integration tests for the v1beta1 provisioning API to verify:
- API group/version availability
- OpenAPI schema correctness (no v0alpha1 duplicates)
- Basic CRUD operations for Repository and Connection resources
- Proper API version handling throughout the request/response cycle

Tests use the REST client to directly test the v1beta1 endpoints and
validate that the OpenAPI version replacement is working correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Tests: Fix metrics test expecting panic with sync.Once

The metrics registration now uses sync.Once to prevent double
registration, so it no longer panics. Update the test to verify
this safe behavior instead of expecting a panic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Revert NewAPIBuilder to use individual parameters

Changes:
- Revert NewAPIBuilder signature from Options struct to individual parameters
- Keep gv and isPreferredVersion parameters needed for v1beta1 support
- Update call sites to pass parameters directly instead of Options struct
- Fix ReplaceOpenAPIVersion to only include new version schemas (remove old)
- Add filtering in PostProcessOpenAPI to remove v0alpha1 schemas from v1beta1
- Update integration test to enforce no v0alpha1 schemas in v1beta1

This addresses PR feedback to avoid the Options struct pattern while
maintaining v1beta1 API support. The v1beta1 OpenAPI spec now contains
only v1beta1 schemas, with no v0alpha1 leakage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Add v1beta1 OpenAPI snapshot and fix ref replacement

Changes:
- Add provisioning.grafana.app/v1beta1 to OpenAPI snapshot test list
- Fix PostProcessOpenAPI to also update $ref references in API paths
- Add updateOperationRefs helper to replace refs in request/response schemas
- Generate clean v1beta1 OpenAPI snapshot with no v0alpha1 references

Before: v1beta1 snapshot had 110 v0alpha1 $ref references
After: v1beta1 snapshot has 0 v0alpha1 references (177 v1beta1 refs)

The issue was that we were only deleting v0alpha1 schema definitions but
leaving dangling $ref references. Now we recursively update all refs in
both component schemas and API path definitions before deleting the old
schemas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Move OpenAPI ref replacement to helper and add docs

Changes:
1. **New helper function**: `ReplaceOpenAPISpecVersion()` in openapi_version.go
   - Replaces all $ref references in OpenAPI v3 specs
   - Updates refs in both component schemas AND API path operations
   - Removes old version schema definitions after updating refs
   - Comprehensive unit tests (5 test scenarios)

2. **Simplified PostProcessOpenAPI**: Now just calls the helper
   - Reduced cyclomatic complexity
   - Cleaner separation of concerns

3. **Enhanced documentation**: Added detailed comments for NewAPIBuilder
   - Explains `gv` parameter: GroupVersion for multi-version API support
   - Explains `isPreferredVersion`: Controls which version runs controllers
   - Clarifies multi-version registration pattern

4. **Verified snapshot correctness**:
   - v0alpha1 snapshot: 65 v0alpha1 schemas, 0 v1beta1 (correct)
   - v1beta1 snapshot: 67 v1beta1 schemas, 0 v0alpha1 (correct)
   - Version strings in metadata are expected and correct

The helper function is now reusable for any API group that needs to clean
up old version schemas from transformed OpenAPI specs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Fix x-kubernetes-group-version-kind arrays in OpenAPI snapshots

Fixes GVK metadata arrays that incorrectly listed both v0alpha1 and v1beta1
versions in each API version's OpenAPI snapshot.

## Problem
When registering multiple API versions (v0alpha1 and v1beta1) that share
the same Go types, the Kubernetes OpenAPI machinery automatically adds
x-kubernetes-group-version-kind metadata for ALL registered versions to
each schema. This resulted in snapshots showing both versions:

```json
"x-kubernetes-group-version-kind": [
  {"version": "v0alpha1", ...},
  {"version": "v1beta1", ...}
]
```

## Solution
Enhanced ReplaceOpenAPISpecVersion to filter GVK arrays after OpenAPI
definitions are added to the spec. The key fix was handling the correct
Go type: `[]map[string]interface{}` instead of `[]interface{}`.

Filtering logic:
- Keeps only GVK entries matching the target version
- Converts single-element arrays to objects (Kubernetes convention)
- Removes GVK metadata when no matching version found

## Result
- v0alpha1 snapshot: Only v0alpha1 GVK metadata (58 refs, 0 v1beta1)
- v1beta1 snapshot: Only v1beta1 GVK metadata (44 refs, 0 v0alpha1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Reduce cyclomatic complexity of OpenAPI version replacement

Refactored ReplaceOpenAPISpecVersion to reduce cyclomatic complexity
from 41 to under 30 by extracting GVK filtering logic into separate
helper functions:

- filterGVKExtensions: Main dispatcher for different GVK types
- filterGVKArrayTyped: Handles []map[string]interface{}
- filterGVKArrayInterface: Handles []interface{}
- filterGVKObject: Handles map[string]interface{}
- updateGVKExtension: Updates schema extensions based on filter results

Also changed if-else chain to switch statement in register.go for
better readability.

Fixes linting issues:
- gocyclo: cyclomatic complexity now < 30
- staticcheck: QF1003 (use tagged switch)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Add v1beta1 API package and CRUD integration tests

Created the v1beta1 package for provisioning APIs that serves the same
types as v0alpha1 under the v1beta1 API version. This enables multi-version
API support using OpenAPI schema transformation.

## v1beta1 Package Structure

**apps/provisioning/pkg/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/**
- `register.go`: Registers v0alpha1 types under v1beta1 GroupVersion
- `types.go`: Type aliases that re-export v0alpha1 types
- `doc.go`: Package documentation with k8s codegen tags

The v1beta1 package uses type aliases to avoid duplicating type definitions
while providing a separate API version. This pattern allows:
- Same Go types served under multiple API versions
- OpenAPI schema transformation in PostProcessOpenAPI
- Clean version evolution without code duplication

## Integration Tests

**pkg/tests/apis/provisioning_connection_crud_test.go**
- TestIntegrationV1Beta1ConnectionCRUD: Comprehensive CRUD tests for v1beta1 Connections
- Tests cover: Create, Get, List, Update, Delete operations
- Tests all connection types: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket
- Verifies secure values are properly filtered from responses

Test structure follows the established pattern from other API integration tests,
using the K8sTestHelper for test setup and dynamic clients for CRUD operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Reorganize v1beta1 integration tests into separate files

Moved integration tests from a single file to an organized directory structure
under pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/ with tests split by resource type.

## New Structure

**pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/**
- `helpers.go`: Shared test utilities and client factories
  - getConnectionClient(): Returns configured client for Connection tests
  - getRepositoryClient(): Returns configured client for Repository tests
  - Conversion helpers: toUnstructured(), fromUnstructuredToConnection(), etc.

- `connection_test.go`: All Connection CRUD integration tests
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Create_GitHub
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Create_GitLab
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Create_Bitbucket
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Get
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_List
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Update
  - TestIntegrationV1Beta1Connection_Delete

- `repository_test.go`: Placeholder for Repository tests (TODO)
- `spec_test.go`: Placeholder for spec validation tests (TODO)

## Generated Files

The code generation also created:
- zz_generated.deepcopy.go
- zz_generated.openapi.go

These were auto-generated by k8s codegen based on the v1beta1 package structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Remove v1beta1 types package, use v0alpha1 types in tests

Remove the v1beta1 types package (type aliases) and update integration
tests to use v0alpha1 types directly. This approach works because API
versioning is metadata-based (APIVersion field and GVR), not tied to
Go type system.

Changes:
- Deleted apps/provisioning/pkg/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/
  - No longer need type aliases (register.go, types.go, doc.go)
  - Auto-generated files also removed (zz_generated.*.go)

- Updated pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/helpers.go
  - Import v0alpha1 types instead of v1beta1
  - Tests still target v1beta1 API via GVR and APIVersion metadata

- Updated pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/connection_test.go
  - Import v0alpha1 types
  - Add required Title field to ConnectionSpec
  - Use PrivateKey (not Token) for GitHub connections with valid RSA key
  - Use ClientSecret for GitLab/Bitbucket connections
  - Fix assertion: require.Empty instead of require.Nil for RawSecureValue
  - Skip unsupported connection types (GitLab, Bitbucket)

All tests pass - proves v1beta1 API works with v0alpha1 types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Provisioning: Reorganize v1beta1 tests and move helpers to common package

Reorganize tests by moving them to appropriate files and consolidate
helper functions in the common package for better code reuse.

Changes:
- Moved TestIntegrationV1Beta1RepositoryCRUD from basic_test.go to repository_test.go
- Moved spec tests (APIAvailable, OpenAPISchema) from basic_test.go to spec_test.go
- Moved v1beta1 helper functions from pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/helpers.go
  to pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/common/testing.go:
  - GetConnectionClientV1Beta1
  - GetRepositoryClientV1Beta1
  - ToUnstructuredConnection
  - FromUnstructuredToConnection
  - ToUnstructuredRepository
  - FromUnstructuredToRepository
  - TestGithubPrivateKeyBase64 (returns base64-encoded test RSA key)
- Updated connection_test.go to use common package helpers
- Replaced long inline base64 RSA key with common.TestGithubPrivateKeyBase64()
- Deleted pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/v1beta1/helpers.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove duplicate test

* WIP: Fix x-kubernetes-group-version-kind to be array

* Provisioning: Fix x-kubernetes-group-version-kind to always be array

Fix OpenAPI schema generation to keep x-kubernetes-group-version-kind
as an array with a single element instead of converting it to an object.
Also prevent duplicates when replacing versions.

Changes:
- Modified replaceGVKVersion() to deduplicate GVK entries
- Modified updateGVKExtension() to always keep GVK as array (never convert single element to object)
- Updated filterGVKExtensions() comment to reflect array-only behavior

This ensures the OpenAPI spec has the correct format:
  "x-kubernetes-group-version-kind": [
    {
      "group": "provisioning.grafana.app",
      "kind": "Connection",
      "version": "v1beta1"
    }
  ]

Instead of:
  "x-kubernetes-group-version-kind": {
    "group": "provisioning.grafana.app",
    "kind": "Connection",
    "version": "v1beta1"
  }

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix duplicates

* Provisioning: Add x-kubernetes-group-version-kind metadata to v1beta1 OpenAPI schemas

The v1beta1 API was missing x-kubernetes-group-version-kind extensions in the
OpenAPI spec because v1beta1 uses the same types as v0alpha1 (type aliases).
When transforming v0alpha1 OpenAPI definitions to v1beta1, the GVK metadata
wasn't being copied or transformed.

This commit adds:
- GVK extension handling in replaceSchemaVersion to transform GVK versions
- replaceGVKExtension helper to update versions while preserving array format
- extractVersion helper to extract version from full pattern (e.g., ".provisioning.v0alpha1." -> "v0alpha1")
- ensureGVKForVersion to add GVK metadata to v1beta1 top-level resources
- Test coverage for GVK transformation
- Updated OpenAPI snapshots with correct v1beta1 GVK metadata

The GVK extensions are now correctly formatted as arrays with a single element:
[{"group": "provisioning.grafana.app", "kind": "Connection", "version": "v1beta1"}]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Use SharedHelper from parent package in v1beta1 tests

Fix: Always initialize shared resources in post-start hook

- Remove early return for non-preferred versions in GetPostStartHooks
- Shared resources (client, jobs, statusPatcher, healthChecker) now initialize for all versions
- Gate only controller/worker startup on isPreferredVersion
- Prevents nil pointer dereferences in v1beta1 handlers that reference these resources

Fix: Move SharedHelper to common package to avoid test-only import

- Added NewDefaultSharedEnv() and SharedHelper() to common package
- Updated both provisioning and v1beta1 packages to use common.SharedHelper
- Fixes build error: test packages cannot be imported across packages

Fix: Use version-specific post-start hook name

- Hook name now includes version: 'grafana-provisioning-v0alpha1', 'grafana-provisioning-v1beta1'
- Prevents registration conflict when both versions are loaded
- Each version maintains its own client/jobs/statusPatcher instances

Fix PR review comments

1. Return preferred (v0alpha1) builder instead of v1beta1
   - v1beta1 is API-only and doesn't run controllers/workers
   - Callers expect the preferred builder that manages lifecycle

2. Register durationHist metric with Prometheus
   - Metric was created but never registered, so it wasn't exposed/scraped
   - Now properly registered like other metrics in the function

Fix metrics test to work with sync.Once singleton pattern

- Added shared testRegistry and testMetrics at package level
- All tests now use the same registry instance
- Fixes test failure where second test couldn't find metrics
  because sync.Once prevented re-registration with new registry

title: QueryEditor: Setup Intercom survey
body:
## Description
<!--- A high level description of what this PR is doing -->
Move to Intercom survey instead of Google form.

## Changes
<!--- Describe your changes in detail -->
Intercom is already integrated into Grafana via Rudderstack (see `RudderstackBackend.ts` — the `identify()` call passes `user_hash` to Intercom's device-mode destination). However, that pipeline only handles user identification, not programmatic survey triggers.

This PR loads the Intercom Messenger script directly (lazy-loaded on first click) and boots it with our workspace's public `app_id`. This gives us access to `Intercom('startSurvey', id)` without adding any npm dependencies or backend changes. The script is only fetched when a user actually clicks a feedback/downgrade button — no impact on page load performance.

## Risks
<!--- Describe what risks you've considered i.e. what features are most likely to break -->
N/A

## Demo
<!--- Attach a demo gif/video or screenshots of the changes if applicable -->
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ba5d2a39-3221-4734-b26a-6aa9630d2162

## Testing Instructions
<!--- Provide step by step instructions and the dashboard if applicable -->
* Pull down this branch locally and interact with the survey. I can share the Intercom dashboard if you want to see how the results are aggregated. 

## Reviewer Checklist
<!--- The reviewer should make sure the following has been done -->
- [ ] Tests are added where applicable
- [ ] Issue is linked to PR
- [ ] Code pulled and tested
- [ ] Code is meaningfully improved if applicable

reviews.body: It's working well! The only thing that I'm curious about improving is unrelated to the PR itself. I'm not sure if this is possible, but it'd be nice if the button on Page 1 said "Next" or something instead of "Submit," and save "Submit" for Page 2. Thanks for tackling this – asking for feedback without opening a new tab feels like a big improvement :)
Flashes white on firefox but that's a known issue

title: Unified: add KV backend migration tests and fix SQLite
body:
**What is this fix?**

Routes all KV operations through the migration's `*sql.Tx` on SQLite to avoid `SQLITE_BUSY` lock contention during bulk import. Uses `Lock`+`SaveRV` through the migration transaction for RV bumping (matching the SQL backend's pattern), since `ExecWithRV` opens its own connection and would deadlock on SQLite.

**Why do we need this fix?**

On SQLite, the migration framework holds a write transaction while KV's separate connections tried to write concurrently, causing `SQLITE_BUSY` errors ("bulk export is already running" and "EOF" failures in migration steps 4 and 5).

**What changed?**

- Added `ContextWithDBTX` / `dbtxFromCtx` context mechanism in `kv/context.go` to route all `conn()` calls through the migration's transaction on SQLite
- Added `conn(ctx)` method to `SqlKV` that checks for an external transaction before falling back to the default DB
- `ProcessBulk` detects SQLite migration context via `inprocgrpc.ClientContext` + `TransactionFromContext`, sets up `rvManagerDB` and `usingMigrationTx` accordingly
- RV bump uses `Lock`+`SaveRV` through migration tx on SQLite (same as SQL backend), `ExecWithRV` otherwise
- Added KV subtest to `TestIntegrationRun_SQLiteRetryReleasesLock` verifying bulk lock release between retry attempts

**Who is this fix for?**

Unified storage developers and operators running migrations with the KV backend on SQLite.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**

Follow-up to #120447

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: RafaelPaulovic

reviews.body: Minor comments.

commits.message:
Unified Storage: KV backend uses batch iteration for BulkProcess

Switch the KV backend's ProcessBulk from single-item iter.Next() to
batchIter.NextBatch(), matching the SQL backend's batching pattern to
reduce gRPC Recv cycles during large migrations. Extract
singleRequestBatchIterator into the resource package so both backends
share the adapter. Add TestIntegrationKVMigrations that runs the full
5-step migration suite against the KV backend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SQLite stale engine cleanup in KV migration tests

Reset the global testSQLStore singleton after the test suite so
subsequent tests don't hit "attempt to write a readonly database"
when t.TempDir() deletes the shared SQLite file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

KV ProcessBulk: SQLite migration tx routing and retry test

Route all KV bulk operations through the migration's *sql.Tx on SQLite
to avoid SQLITE_BUSY from concurrent connections. Buffer requests
in-memory before writing to separate read/write phases. Use Lock+SaveRV
(matching SQL backend) instead of ExecWithRV for RV bumps during
migration. Unexport internal DB Tx interfaces in the kv package.

Add KV subtest to TestIntegrationRun_SQLiteRetryReleasesLock verifying
bulk lock release between retry attempts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify KV ProcessBulk: remove in-memory buffering, clarify context.go

The in-memory buffer and sliceBulkRequestIterator were unnecessary —
routing operations through the migration's *sql.Tx via ContextWithDBTX
is sufficient since in-process gRPC serializes sends and receives.

Guard compatDB assignment behind rvManager != nil check for the
migration tx path. Add file-level doc to context.go explaining the
two independent transaction context mechanisms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix gofmt import ordering in resource_migration_test.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Keep ContextWithTx/TxFromCtx/TxExecer exported, add dbtx context separately

Restore the original exported API for the single-write compat path
(ContextWithTx, TxFromCtx, TxExecer) unchanged from main. The new
bulk-import SQLite transaction routing (ContextWithDBTX, dbtxFromCtx,
dbtx) is added as a separate unexported mechanism with its own context
key and clearer documentation of both paths.

Also use resDB.DriverName() instead of hardcoded "sqlite3" in KV test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename compatDB to rvManagerDB and clarify comment

title: Alerting: Protect /api/v2/status endpoint with dedicated permission
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Adds a dedicated permission `alert.notifications.system-status:read` to protect the `/api/v2/status` endpoint, which by default is granted only to Admin roles.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Disaggregate `alert.notifications:read` and remove it in the future.

This ensures tighter security by restricting access to the endpoint to users with specific permissions.


# Release notice breaking change

The `GET /api/alertmanager/grafana/api/v2/status` endpoint previously required the legacy `alert.notifications:read` permission. It now requires a dedicated `alert.notifications.system-status:read` permission. This new permission is included in the `fixed:alerting.notifications:writer` role, which is granted to Admin users by default.

#### Migration
If you have custom roles that need access to this endpoint, add the `alert.notifications.system-status:read` action to those roles. Admin users are unaffected as they receive this permission automatically through the built-in alerting notifications writer role.
author: yuri-tceretian

commits.message:
Protect /api/v2/status endpoint with dedicated permission

by default granted to Admin

title: Provisioning: share one Grafana server in foldermetadata integration tests
body:
## Summary

The `foldermetadata` test package previously started a separate Grafana server for every test function and many subtests (~55 startups). This PR adopts the shared-server strategy to replace them with a single shared server, dramatically reducing CI time.

### What changed

- **Added `common.SharedEnv` in `helper_test.go`** with `WithProvisioningFolderMetadata` and `TestMain` for the shared server lifecycle.
- **Converted 8 test files to `sharedHelper(t)`**: fixfoldermetadata, sync_folder_metadata, full_sync_move (13 tests), full_sync_invalid (5 subtests), full_permissions_move (6 tests), folder_creation, folder_file_protection, folder_permissions.
- **Moved flag-disabled sync test** (`MissingFolderMetadata_FlagDisabled`) to the core `provisioning` package, where the shared server runs without the folder metadata flag — allowing this test to validate flag-off behavior correctly.
- **Moved `export_folders_test.go` to `foldermetadata/export/`** — this file needs flag-off and git-server Grafana instances that conflict with the shared server DB.
- **Moved `folder_authorization_test.go` to `foldermetadata/authorization/`** — this table-driven test runs with and without the flag, requiring standalone servers.

### Why

- **~55 Grafana server startups → 1**: Each startup takes several seconds; this substantially reduces CI wall time.
- **Standalone tests isolated in subpackages**: Tests needing flag-off or git-server instances can't share a DB with the shared server (controller conflicts), so they get their own package and DB lifecycle via `testsuite.Run(m)`.
- **Consistent with the shared-server strategy** already established in `connection/`, `quota/`, `repository/`, and the core `provisioning` packages.

### Files changed (12 files)

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| `foldermetadata/helper_test.go` (new) | Shared env + `sharedHelper(t)` + `TestMain` |
| 8 test files | `RunGrafana(t, WithProvisioningFolderMetadata)` → `sharedHelper(t)`, removed `SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode` |
| `foldermetadata/export/` (new subpackage) | Moved export tests (flag-off + git server) |
| `foldermetadata/authorization/` (new subpackage) | Moved authorization tests (mixed flag on/off) |
| `provisioning/sync_folder_metadata_flag_disabled_test.go` (new) | Moved flag-disabled sync test to core package |

## Test plan

- [ ] All integration tests in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/` pass
- [ ] All integration tests in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/export/` pass
- [ ] All integration tests in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/authorization/` pass
- [ ] `MissingFolderMetadata_FlagDisabled` passes in the core provisioning package
- [ ] No `gofmt` issues

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

title: [release-12.4.2] Live: Support legacy bare numeric org ID in channel namespace
body:
Backport a376f3a80058d3d64e8465d306f171cdf5098376 from #120384

---

## Summary
- Fixes #119951
- `StripK8sNamespace` now falls back to parsing bare numeric strings (e.g. "1") as legacy org IDs when `authlib.ParseNamespace` returns an unrecognized format
- Subscribe and publish handlers compare by org ID in addition to raw namespace string, so `"1"` matches users with namespace `"default"` (both org 1)
- Added test coverage for legacy format including invalid cases (0, -1)

## Root cause
PR #117275 changed Live channels from bare org IDs (`1/grafana/dashboard/uid/...`) to k8s namespaces (`default/grafana/dashboard/uid/...`), but `StripK8sNamespace` dropped backwards compatibility. `authlib.ParseNamespace("1")` silently returns `OrgID: -1` for unknown formats, triggering a "namespace does not reference a valid org ID" error every ~6 seconds for affected clients.

## Test plan
- [x] Existing `TestStripK8sNamespace` passes
- [x] New test cases for legacy bare numeric org IDs (1, 42) succeed
- [x] Invalid org IDs (0, -1) are still rejected
- [x] Manual: verify a client sending `1/grafana/dashboard/uid/<uid>` can subscribe successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: grafana-delivery-bot

commits.message:
Live: Support legacy bare numeric org ID in channel namespace (#120384)

* Live: Support legacy bare numeric org ID in channel namespace

Fixes #119951. The migration to k8s namespaces in Live channels (#117275)
broke backwards compatibility with clients still sending bare numeric org
IDs (e.g. "1/grafana/dashboard/uid/..."). StripK8sNamespace now falls back
to parsing bare numbers as org IDs, and the subscribe/publish handlers
compare by org ID in addition to raw namespace string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Live: Normalize legacy namespace to canonical in publish handler

Use user's canonical namespace (from GetNamespace()) for pipeline rule
lookups and ProcessInput in the publish handler, instead of the raw
parsed ns.Value which would be "1" for legacy channels. Without this,
pipeline rules keyed by "default" or "org-N" would miss for legacy
clients.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Live: Normalize legacy namespace in StripK8sNamespace, drop org-ID fallback

Move namespace normalization into StripK8sNamespace itself: legacy bare
numeric org IDs are mapped to their canonical k8s form ("1" → "default",
"42" → "org-42") in ns.Value. This lets the subscribe and publish
handlers use a simple string comparison against the user's namespace
without an org-ID fallback, preserving stack namespace isolation
(stacks-X namespaces all share OrgID=1 but must remain distinct).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Live: Simplify comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestion from @RafaelPaulovic

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
(cherry picked from commit a376f3a80058d3d64e8465d306f171cdf5098376)

title: Provisioning: share one Grafana server in core provisioning integration tests
body:
## Summary

The core `provisioning` test package previously started a separate Grafana server for every top-level `TestIntegration*` function (~25 startups). This PR adopts the shared-server strategy (already used by `connection/`) to replace them with a single shared server, dramatically reducing CI time for this package.

### What changed

- **Added `common.SharedEnv` setup in `helper_test.go`** with `SecretsManagerEnableDBMigrations` to cover all test requirements in a single server.
- **Replaced `common.RunGrafana(t)` with `sharedHelper(t)`** in 13 test files (stats, settings, secrets, managed, health, files, librarypanels, folderpermissions, folderownerrefs, fieldselector, client, classic_delete, bom).
- **`ProvisioningMaxRepositories` opts → `SetQuotaStatus`**: Settings authorization tests now set quota limits dynamically via `helper.SetQuotaStatus()` instead of passing them as server startup options.
- **Moved folder-metadata-flag-dependent tests to `foldermetadata/`**: Tests that require `WithProvisioningFolderMetadata` were relocated to the `foldermetadata` package where they belong, each using their own `RunGrafana(t)` call with the flag enabled:
  - `TestIntegrationProvisioning_FolderAuthorizationWithMetadata`
  - `TestIntegrationProvisioning_CreateFolder_FolderMetadataFlag`
  - `TestIntegrationProvisioning_FolderMetadataFileProtection`
  - `TestIntegrationFolderPermissions_ProvisionedFolders_WithFlag`
- **Library panel cleanup**: Added `t.Cleanup` callbacks and a `common.DeleteHelper` to explicitly delete library panels after tests, preventing "folder is not empty" cascading failures.

### Why

- **~25 Grafana server startups → 1**: Each startup takes several seconds, so this substantially reduces CI wall time for the core provisioning package.
- **Correct test separation**: Folder-metadata-flag tests now live in the `foldermetadata` package rather than forcing the shared server to enable the flag globally.
- **Consistent with the shared-server strategy**: Aligns with the pattern already established in `connection/`, `quota/`, and `repository/` packages.

### Files changed (32 files, +1001 / -1057)

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| `helper_test.go` | New shared env setup with `TestMain` and `sharedHelper(t)` |
| 13 core test files | `RunGrafana(t)` → `sharedHelper(t)`, removed `SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode` |
| `folderpermissions_test.go` | Restored 403 Forbidden assertions (flag is off in shared server) |
| `librarypanels_test.go` | Added `t.Cleanup` for library panel deletion |
| `common/testing.go` | Added `DeleteHelper` for HTTP DELETE cleanup |
| `foldermetadata/` (4 new files) | Moved flag-dependent tests with their own `RunGrafana` calls |
| `settings_stats_auth_test.go` | Static `ProvisioningMaxRepositories` → dynamic `SetQuotaStatus` |

## Test plan

- [x] All integration tests in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/` pass
- [x] All integration tests in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/` pass
- [x] `TestIntegrationFolderPermissions_ProvisionedFolders` correctly tests no-flag behavior (403 Forbidden)
- [x] `TestIntegrationFolderPermissions_ProvisionedFolders_WithFlag` correctly tests flag-enabled behavior (200 OK) in `foldermetadata/`
- [x] Settings authorization tests correctly use dynamic `SetQuotaStatus` for MaxRepositories validation
- [x] Library panel tests clean up properly and don't cause cascading failures
- [x] No `gofmt` issues
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Share one Grafana server in core provisioning integration tests

Replace per-test RunGrafana calls with a single shared Grafana server
across all integration tests in the core provisioning package. This
reduces ~25 server startups to 1.

The shared server is configured with WithProvisioningFolderMetadata and
SecretsManagerEnableDBMigrations to cover all test requirements. Tests
that previously passed custom GrafanaOpts for MaxRepositories now use
SetQuotaStatus dynamically.

One exception: TestIntegrationFolderPermissions_ProvisionedFolders
keeps its own RunGrafana(t) because it explicitly tests behavior
WITHOUT the folder metadata flag (permission updates blocked).

Made-with: Cursor

Fix shared server test failures: DB controller conflicts and library panel cleanup

Two issues:
1. TestIntegrationFolderPermissions_ProvisionedFolders used standalone
   RunGrafana(t) alongside the shared server, but both connect to the
   same test DB — the shared server's controller rejected the standalone
   server's path. Removed the standalone test since _WithFlag (now just
   _ProvisionedFolders) already covers the flag-on behavior.

2. Library panel tests created elements that CleanupAllResources couldn't
   delete (non-K8s resources inside folders), causing cascading failures.
   Added t.Cleanup to delete library elements, and added DeleteHelper to
   the common package.

Made-with: Cursor

Move FolderAuthorizationWithMetadata test to foldermetadata package

This test exercises folder metadata behavior (stable UIDs vs hash-based
IDs) and belongs with the other folder metadata tests. Restored the
original per-variant RunGrafana calls so the "without metadata" variant
runs without the feature flag.

Made-with: Cursor

Remove WithProvisioningFolderMetadata from shared server

Moved CreateFolder_FolderMetadataFlag and FolderMetadataFileProtection
tests to the foldermetadata package where they belong. These tests
require the feature flag and now use their own RunGrafana(t) calls.

Restored the original ProvisionedFolders test assertions (403 Forbidden)
now that the shared server no longer has the flag enabled.

Made-with: Cursor

Move ProvisionedFolders_WithFlag test to foldermetadata package

This test requires the provisioningFolderMetadata feature flag and
verifies that permission updates succeed when the flag is enabled.
It belongs in the foldermetadata package alongside the other
flag-dependent tests.

Made-with: Cursor

Fix gofmt: remove trailing blank lines in files_test.go

Made-with: Cursor

Fix testdata path in folder_permissions_test.go

Use ../testdata/ relative path since the foldermetadata package is a
subdirectory of provisioning where testdata lives.

title: Provisioning: Share one Grafana server per package in jobs integration tests
body:
## Summary

Applies the shared Grafana server strategy (from #120952) to the `jobs` integration test package.

- **One shared server** started via `sync.Once` and reused by all `TestIntegration*` functions in the main `jobs` package, with `CleanupAllResources` running between tests for isolation.
- **19 test functions** across 15 files migrated from `common.RunGrafana(t)` to `sharedHelper(t)`.
- **7 tests** with incompatible server configurations moved to dedicated sub-packages (each gets its own database and server):
  - `jobs/disabledfeatures/` — `MigrateDisabledByConfiguration` and `ExportDisabledByConfiguration` (export feature flag disabled)
  - `jobs/conflict/` — `JobConflict` (controllers disabled for testing optimistic locking)
  - `jobs/flagdisabled/` — `FixFolderMetadataJobFlagDisabled` (folder metadata flag disabled)
  - `jobs/foldermetadata/` — `FixFolderMetadataJob`, `FixFolderMetadataJobAuthorization`, and `JobsAuthorization` (require `FlagProvisioningFolderMetadata` enabled). These will be consolidated into the dedicated folder-metadata test package in a follow-up.
- **Server startups reduced from ~30 to 5** (1 shared + 4 isolated sub-packages).

Follows the pattern established in #120952 for the `repository` package.

## Test plan

- [ ] All existing jobs integration tests pass
- [ ] Tests in sub-packages (disabledfeatures, conflict, flagdisabled, foldermetadata) pass independently
- [ ] No cross-test contamination from shared server state
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Share one Grafana server per package in jobs integration tests

Apply the same shared-server strategy from the repository package to
the jobs package. One Grafana server is started via sync.Once and reused
by all TestIntegration* functions, with CleanupAllResources running
between tests for isolation.

Tests that require incompatible server configurations are moved to
dedicated sub-packages so they get their own database and server:

- jobs/disabledfeatures/ — export feature flag disabled
- jobs/conflict/ — controllers disabled (tests optimistic locking)
- jobs/flagdisabled/ — folder metadata flag disabled

This reduces server startups from ~30 to 4 (1 shared + 3 isolated).

Made-with: Cursor

chore: fix gofmt formatting

Made-with: Cursor

fix: remove WithProvisioningFolderMetadata from shared server

The flag caused move/delete jobs to produce warnings about folder
metadata and changed authorization for instance-scoped repos.

Only 3 tests need the flag (FixFolderMetadataJob, its auth test, and
JobsAuthorization). These are moved to jobs/foldermetadata/ with their
own shared server that has the flag enabled.

Made-with: Cursor

refactor: split foldermetadata into separate files matching originals

Made-with: Cursor

fix: move instance-scoped auth tests to isolated sub-package

ExportJobAuthorization and MigrateJobAuthorization tests create
instance-scoped repos and verify editors cannot create export/migrate
jobs. With a shared server, accumulated RBAC state from previous
tests interferes with the authorization checks. Moving them to
jobs/instanceauth/ gives them a fresh server with pristine state.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: Move repository limits tests into quota package
body:
## Summary

The `repository/limits` package ran its own dedicated Grafana server just to test repository creation limits (instance/folder sync constraints and max-repository validation). These tests are a natural fit for the `quota` package, which already tests all other quota/limit enforcement and shares a single Grafana server across ~20 tests.

### What changed

- **Moved `TestIntegrationProvisioning_RepositoryLimits`** from `repository/limits/` into `quota/limits_test.go`. The test uses `sharedHelper(t)` + `helper.SetQuotaStatus(QuotaStatus{MaxRepositories: 10})` instead of baking the limit into server startup options.
- **Deleted the `repository/limits` package** entirely (`helpers_test.go` + `limits_test.go`), removing one Grafana server startup from CI.
- **Removed duplicate provisioning dir cleanup** in `common/testing.go`: `CleanupAllResources` already calls `CleanProvisioningDir`, so the extra call in `GetCleanHelper` and the private `cleanProvisioningPath` helper were redundant.

### Why

- Fewer Grafana server startups = faster CI.
- Quota and limit tests belong in the same package — they exercise the same `QuotaGetter` admission path.
- Reduces code duplication in the common test helpers.

## Test plan
- [ ] `TestIntegrationProvisioning_RepositoryLimits` passes with the shared server (folder/instance sync rejection, sync target change, 10-repo limit validation)
- [ ] All existing quota tests still pass
- [ ] No remaining references to `repository/limits`
author: MissingRoberto

Move the integration tests from repository/limits into the quota
package so they share the same Grafana server and use dynamic quota
configuration via SetQuotaStatus instead of requiring a dedicated
server startup with custom GrafanaOpts.

This eliminates the limits package entirely — one fewer server to
start in CI.

title: Alerting: Fix Alert Activity group-by showing same firing count for all groups
body:
**What is this feature?**

Bug fix for the Alert Activity page: when using **Group by**, all group rows were displaying the same total firing count instead of a per-group count.

**Why do we need this feature?**

A customer reported that grouping by `k8s_cluster_name` showed the same count (e.g. 56) under both `hcp-rosa-nonprod2` and `hcp-rosa-prod1`, rather than the correct split counts per cluster.

The root cause was in `buildRuleCountsMap()`: it used only `ruleUID` as its map key, so when the same alert rule had instances across multiple group values, all counts were collapsed into a single global total per rule. Every group then received that same total.

The fix uses a composite key (`ruleUID:groupVal1:groupVal2:…`) when groupBy is active, and threads the current group path through the `nodeToRows` recursion so each leaf node looks up its own scoped count.

**Who is this feature for?**

Users of the Alert Activity page who use the Group by feature.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes #

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

- The fix is entirely in `dataTransform.ts` — no query or scene changes needed. The PromQL query was already producing correctly split rows per group; it was the count aggregation step that was losing the per-group breakdown.
- One existing test explicitly asserted the buggy behaviour ("should use global rule counts when same rule appears in multiple groups") — it has been updated to expect the correct per-group split counts.

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: gillesdemey

reviews.body: Code LGTM 👍

commits.message:
Alerting: Fix Alert Activity group-by showing same firing count for all groups

When using Group by on the Alert Activity page, all groups displayed the
same total firing count instead of a per-group count. buildRuleCountsMap()
used only ruleUID as its map key, so counts across all group values were
summed into a single global entry per rule.

Fix by using a composite key (ruleUID:groupVal1:groupVal2:...) when
groupBy is active, and threading the current group path through the
nodeToRows recursion so each leaf looks up its own scoped count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Provisioning: Add integration test for deleteOldResource sourcePath guard (incremental sync)
body:
## Summary

Adds an integration test that exercises the `deleteOldResource` **sourcePath guard** in `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/resources.go` via incremental sync.

### The guard

When a provisioned file's UID changes, `deleteOldResource` removes the old resource to prevent orphans. Before deleting, it checks the `grafana.app/sourcePath` annotation: if another file in the same sync has already claimed the old UID (by writing a resource with a different sourcePath), the delete is skipped.

```go
if currentPath := existing.GetAnnotations()[utils.AnnoKeySourcePath]; currentPath != "" && currentPath != sourcePath {
    return fmt.Errorf("skipping delete of old resource %s: now managed by %s, not %s", ...)
}
```

### Test scenario

A **one-directional UID takeover** between two files in a single commit:

| File | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `dashboard_a.json` | `takeover-uid-a` | `takeover-uid-b` ← takes B's UID |
| `dashboard_b.json` | `takeover-uid-b` | `takeover-uid-new` ← brand-new UID |

Incremental sync processes files **serially in alphabetical order**, so the execution is deterministic:

1. **A runs first**: writes `takeover-uid-b` (UPDATE → sourcePath becomes `dashboard_a.json`), then deletes `takeover-uid-a` (sourcePath matches → proceeds).
2. **B runs next**: writes `takeover-uid-new` (CREATE), then tries to delete `takeover-uid-b` — but its sourcePath is now `dashboard_a.json` ≠ `dashboard_b.json` → **guard fires, delete skipped**.

The test asserts both dashboards survive with correct titles and sourcePaths.

### Why incremental only (no full sync test)

Full sync processes files in parallel (`applyResourcesInParallel`), so there is no ordering guarantee between A's write and B's delete. When B's goroutine completes its write+delete before A's write starts, `takeover-uid-b` is deleted before A can claim it. A's fallback create-after-delete fails against the storage layer, making the test non-deterministic. The guard logic is also covered by the unit test `TestDeleteOldResource/skips_delete_when_sourcePath_points_to_a_different_file` in `resources_test.go`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalGitSync_MultiFileUIDTakeover` passes in CI
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Add integration tests for multi-file UID swap

When two files swap their dashboard UIDs in a single commit, the
deleteOldResource sourcePath guard must prevent accidental deletion of a
resource that was just written by the other file. These tests verify
that both dashboards survive a UID swap with correct sourcePath
annotations, covering both full sync and incremental sync paths.

Made-with: Cursor

fix: replace symmetric UID swap with one-directional takeover in integration tests

The symmetric swap caused a race in full sync's parallel processing:
whichever goroutine completed write+delete first destroyed a UID that
the other goroutine needed to recreate, and the create-after-delete
fallback was unreliable against the storage layer. A one-directional
takeover (file A takes B's UID, file B gets a new UID) avoids the
circular dependency while still exercising the deleteOldResource
sourcePath guard.

Made-with: Cursor

move sourcePath guard tests into dedicated package to avoid timeout

The foldermetadata_incremental package has 16 integration tests, each
starting a full Grafana + Git server. Adding our test exceeded the
8-minute binary timeout. Move both tests into a new sourcepath_guard
package so they run in their own binary with a fresh time budget.

Made-with: Cursor

remove full sync test; keep only incremental for sourcePath guard

Full sync processes files in parallel, so there is no way to guarantee
that A's write of uid-b completes before B's deleteOldResource deletes
uid-b. The race consistently causes a create-after-delete that fails
against the storage layer. The incremental sync test is deterministic
(serial, alphabetical) and reliably exercises the guard. A unit test
in resources_test.go also covers the guard logic directly.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Dashboard assistant popover: fix mobile, intrusiveness, and repeated panel issues
body:
## Fix assistant popover mobile UX and interaction issues

  Follow-up to #120051 addressing community feedback on the dashboard assistant popover.

  ### Problems

  1. **Mobile**: Clicking panels opens the popover and auto-focuses the input, triggering the virtual keyboard and taking over the screen
  2. **Overzealous trigger**: Any panel body click (including table column resizes) opens the popover
  3. **Row hover**: Hovering a row shows sparkle icons on ALL panels inside it, not just the hovered panel
  4. **Esc persistence**: Pressing Escape while the kebab menu is open causes the popover to persist
  5. **Repeated panels**: Clicking a sparkle in a repeated row/panel targets the wrong panel (first instance)

  ### Solution

  **Decouple the popover from the element selection system.** The popover is now triggered exclusively via the sparkle button, not through
  panel body clicks.

  - **Mobile**: Popover disabled below `md` breakpoint (768px) via `useMediaQueryMinWidth`. The `AssistantPromptCard` (from
  `@grafana/assistant`) auto-focuses its text input on mount, which triggers the virtual keyboard on mobile and takes over the screen. The card is also hardcoded to 380px wide, which doesn't fit mobile viewports. Since we can't control the auto-focus from the consumer side, disabling on small screens is the simplest fix. A better long-term solution would be adding an `autoFocus` prop to `AssistantPromptCard`.
  - **Sparkle-only trigger**: New `AssistantPopoverContext` lets the hint button open the popover directly, bypassing element selection. Panel body clicks, table resizes, and other interactions no longer interfere.
  - **Row hover fix**: CSS scoped to `section[class*="panel-container"]:hover` — only the individual PanelChrome `<section>` triggers the sparkle, not parent row wrappers.
  - **Repeated panels**: VizPanel reference + DOM element passed directly from the clicked sparkle button (via
  `closest('section[class*="panel-container"]')`), avoiding `sceneGraph.findByKey` ambiguity with cloned panels that share keys.
  - **Accessibility**: Sparkle hint changed from `<span>` to `<button>` with `aria-label`.
  - **Dismiss**: Click-outside handler via `usePopoverDismissOnClickOutside`, Escape via `AssistantPromptCard.onClose`, toggle on re-click.


  ### How to test

  Enable: `dashboardNewLayouts`, `dashboardAssistantPopover`, `extensionSidebar`, `externalServiceAccounts`. Assistant plugin installed, user  signed in.

  1. **Sparkle button only** — click panel body → nothing happens; click sparkle → popover appears
  2. **Table column resize** — drag table column headers → no popover
  3. **Row hover** — hover row header → no sparkle on nested panels; hover individual panel → sparkle appears
  4. **Dismiss** — Escape closes; click outside closes; click same sparkle toggles off
  5. **Repeated panels** — sparkle targets the exact panel clicked, not the first instance
  6. **Mobile** — Chrome DevTools device toolbar, viewport < 768px → no sparkle, no popover
  7. **Edit mode** — normal panel selection works, no popover interference
author: dprokop

commits.message:
Dashboard assistant popover: fix mobile, intrusiveness, and repeated panel issues

- Disable popover on mobile (< 768px) to prevent keyboard takeover
- Decouple popover from element selection system — trigger only from
  sparkle button click, not panel body clicks (fixes table column
  resize interference, kebab menu Esc persistence)
- Fix row hover showing sparkle icons on all nested panels by scoping
  CSS to section[class*="panel-container"]:hover
- Pass VizPanel reference + DOM element directly through context to
  correctly target individual panels in repeated rows/panels
- Change sparkle hint from <span> to <button> for accessibility
- Add click-outside dismiss handler for the popover

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dashboard assistant popover: add multi-panel selection via Shift+click

Plain click on sparkle replaces the selection (single panel).
Shift+click toggles panels in/out, allowing multi-panel context.
Popover anchors to the last selected panel, animated border on all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Faceted filter: fall back to frame names for __name__ facet
body:
Followup to https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/119918

## Fix faceted filter for multi-query panels where all fields share the same name

When multiple queries each return data frames with a generic field name like "Value" but distinct frame names (e.g. "io", "ir", "oe", "ov"), the faceted filter's "By name" section did not appear because `extractFacetedLabels` only considered `field.name` for the `__name__` facet.

This change adds a fallback: when all fields share the same raw name, `extractFacetedLabels` collects unique `frame.name` values instead. `resolveFacetedFilterNames` now matches the `__name__` facet against both `field.name` and `frame.name`.

Also fixes a bug where the filter would incorrectly dim after selecting a checkbox in panels with duplicate display names across queries. The `legendHasPrecedence` comparison now deduplicates expected visible names before comparing with the actual visible set.

## How to test

- Open the "Faceted labels demo" dashboard (provisioned under gdev dashboards)
- Panel 8 ("Multi-query, same field name, frame name disambiguation"): 3 queries, 4 frames each, all with "Value" field, no labels. The "By name" filter should show io, ir, oe, ov.
- Panel 9 ("Multi-query, frame names + labels"): same structure but with region/env labels. Filter should show both "By name" (frame names) and "By labels" (region, env).
- Selecting a filter checkbox should not dim the filter UI.
author: dprokop

reviews.body: LGTM, can verify this fixes the bugs in panels 8 & 9 ### Without fix: No by name facet No facets ### With fix: #### Question: My only question is that it feels a bit unexpected to filter by name (clicking on series name in legend results in 4 series displaying) when clicking directly on the legend, is that because we don't have a way to differentiate a legend click on a series that has the same names as other series in different frames, or is filtering by name on legend click the desired behavior? #### Other Looks like you forgot to re-generate golden checksums, which should get the failing CI job to pass
Tested and LGTM!

commits.message:
Faceted filter: fall back to frame names for __name__ facet

- When all fields share the same raw name (e.g. "Value"),
  extractFacetedLabels now uses frame names for the __name__ facet
- resolveFacetedFilterNames matches against both field.name and
  frame.name for the __name__ facet
- Fix legendHasPrecedence false positive when multiple frames share
  the same display name by deduplicating before comparison
- Add test cases for frame name fallback and matching
- Add demo dashboard panels for multi-query frame name scenarios

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: Share one Grafana server in quota integration tests
body:
## Why

Quota integration tests start **20 separate Grafana servers** — one per subtest. Each startup involves Wire DI, database provisioning, HTTP listener binding, health polling, and shutdown. This is the main bottleneck making these tests slow.

## What

Refactor the `quota` package to share **one Grafana server** across all tests using `sync.Once` + `TestMain` shutdown — the same pattern established in #120906 for connection tests.

**Server startups drop from 20 → 1.**

## How

### Key Insight: `SetQuotaStatus` replaces startup options

Many quota tests previously required different `ProvisioningMaxResourcesPerRepository` or `ProvisioningMaxRepositories` values at Grafana startup. However, `FixedQuotaGetter.SetQuotaStatus()` atomically updates quotas at runtime, making per-test server startup unnecessary. Each test now:

1. Calls `sharedHelper(t)` which cleans all K8s resources, resets quota to unlimited, and clears the provisioning directory
2. Calls `helper.SetQuotaStatus(...)` to configure the quota for that specific test
3. Proceeds with the test logic unchanged

### Per-test Cleanup

`sharedHelper(t)` runs at the start of every test and:

- Calls `CleanupAllResources` to remove all K8s resources in dependency order
- Resets quota to unlimited via `SetQuotaStatus(QuotaStatus{})`
- Restores default GitHub mock client  
- Cleans the shared provisioning directory (removes all files/subdirectories)

### Changes Per Test

Each test/subtest changes from:
```diff
-    testutil.SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode(t)
-    helper := common.RunGrafana(t, func(opts *testinfra.GrafanaOpts) {
-        opts.ProvisioningMaxResourcesPerRepository = 10
-    })
+    helper := sharedHelper(t)
+    helper.SetQuotaStatus(provisioning.QuotaStatus{MaxResourcesPerRepository: 10})
```

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
| ---- | ------ |
| `quota/helpers_test.go` | Rewrite with `common.NewSharedEnv()` + `sharedHelper` + `cleanProvisioningDir` + `TestMain` |
| `quota/quota_test.go` | Replace `RunGrafana` with `sharedHelper` + `SetQuotaStatus` (5 subtests) |
| `quota/sync_quota_test.go` | Replace `RunGrafana` with `sharedHelper` + `SetQuotaStatus` (6 subtests) |
| `quota/namespace_quota_test.go` | Replace `RunGrafana` with `sharedHelper` (2 tests); use `common.TestGithubPrivateKeyPEM` |
| `quota/files_quota_test.go` | Replace `RunGrafana` with `sharedHelper` + `SetQuotaStatus` (4 subtests) |
| `quota/exportjob_quota_test.go` | Replace `RunGrafana` with `sharedHelper` + `SetQuotaStatus` (3 subtests) |

## Test plan
- [ ] `go vet ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/quota/...` passes
- [ ] Integration tests pass: `go test -v -count=1 -run TestIntegration ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/quota/...`
- [ ] No enterprise tests affected (quota package has no enterprise-specific tests)


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Share one Grafana server in quota integration tests

Quota integration tests were starting 20 separate Grafana servers — one per
subtest. Each startup involves Wire DI, DB provisioning, HTTP listener, health
polling, and shutdown, making these tests very slow.

This refactors the quota package to share a single Grafana server across all
tests using the sync.Once + TestMain pattern from the connection package:

- Rewrite helpers_test.go with common.NewSharedEnv() + sharedHelper + TestMain
- Replace common.RunGrafana(t, ...) with sharedHelper(t) in all tests
- Replace Grafana startup options (ProvisioningMaxResourcesPerRepository,
  ProvisioningMaxRepositories) with runtime SetQuotaStatus() calls, since
  FixedQuotaGetter supports atomic updates
- Add per-test provisioning directory cleanup to prevent filesystem leakage
- Reset quota to unlimited (0/0) between tests for proper isolation
- Use common.TestGithubPrivateKeyPEM instead of a local duplicate

Server startups drop from 20 to 1.

Made-with: Cursor

fix: use EventuallyWithT for reconciliation triggers to tolerate conflicts

With the shared server, the controller is more active (processing cleanup,
creation, token refresh concurrently). The 5-retry loop in
TriggerRepositoryReconciliation isn't enough to handle prolonged optimistic
locking conflicts.

Replace all TriggerRepositoryReconciliation calls with a local
triggerReconciliation helper that uses require.EventuallyWithT with 200ms
polling and a 60s timeout, so conflict retries continue until the update
actually succeeds.

Made-with: Cursor

improve reconciliation triggers to tolerate prolonged conflicts

Replace the fragile 5-retry loop in TriggerRepositoryReconciliation and
TriggerConnectionReconciliation with require.EventuallyWithT (60s timeout,
200ms polling). This makes the shared helpers robust against prolonged
optimistic-locking conflicts from concurrent controller reconciliations,
which are common with shared Grafana servers.

Remove the local triggerReconciliation workaround from the quota package
since the common helpers now handle this properly.

Made-with: Cursor

move provisioning directory cleanup into GetCleanHelper

Add CleanProvisioningDir to ProvisioningTestHelper and call it from
GetCleanHelper alongside CleanupAllResources. This ensures leftover
files from previous tests are removed for every shared-server consumer,
not just packages that remember to do it locally.

Remove the now-redundant cleanProvisioningDir from the quota package.

Made-with: Cursor

restore comments accidentally dropped during refactoring

Bring back inline comments that explained quota math and test intent
in files_quota_test.go, quota_test.go, and sync_quota_test.go.

Made-with: Cursor

fix conflict retry in near-expiry token status update

Replace the hardcoded 5-retry loop for the near-expiry token status
manipulation with EventuallyWithT, matching the pattern used in the
common reconciliation triggers. The shared server's concurrent
controller activity can cause more than 5 consecutive conflicts.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: Share one Grafana server per package in repository integration tests
body:
## Why

Repository integration tests start **25 separate Grafana servers** — one per `TestIntegration*` function. Each startup involves Wire DI, database provisioning, HTTP listener binding, health polling, and shutdown. This is the main bottleneck making these tests slow.

This is a follow-up to [#120906](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120906) which introduced the shared Grafana server strategy for the `connection` package.

## What

Apply the shared Grafana server strategy (`sync.Once` + `TestMain`) to the `repository` test package, following the same pattern established for the `connection` package in [#120906](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120906).

**Server startups drop from 25 → 4** (1 shared + 3 tests with custom `GrafanaOptions` that require their own servers).

## How

Following the [migration checklist](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/README.md#migration-checklist-for-other-packages):

1. **`helpers_test.go`** — Rewritten with `common.NewSharedEnv()` + `sharedHelper(t)` + `TestMain`
2. **21 tests** — Replaced `common.RunGrafana(t)` → `sharedHelper(t)` (one-line change)
3. **`testutil.SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode(t)`** — Removed from migrated tests (handled by `GetHelper`)
4. **Enterprise test** — `TestIntegrationRepositoryController_EnterpriseWiring` moved to `enterprise/` sibling package
5. **3 tests with custom options** — Kept on their own servers:
   - `TestIntegrationProvisioning_ViewerSettings_CustomRepositoryTypes` (custom `WithRepositoryTypes`)
   - `TestIntegrationProvisioning_RepositoryLimits` (custom `ProvisioningMaxRepositories`)
   - `TestIntegrationProvisioning_JobPermissions` (custom `FlagProvisioningFolderMetadata`)

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
| --- | --- |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/repository/helpers_test.go` | Rewrite with `common.NewSharedEnv()` |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/repository/repository_test.go` | One-line change ×19, remove enterprise test |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/repository/repository_subresources_auth_test.go` | One-line change |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/repository/pending_delete_test.go` | One-line change |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/enterprise/enterprise_test.go` | Add enterprise repository wiring tests |

## Test plan

- [ ] Repository integration tests pass (`go test -count=1 -run TestIntegration ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/repository/...`)
- [ ] Enterprise integration tests pass (`go test -count=1 -run TestIntegration ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/enterprise/...`)
- [ ] No test depends on global state without prior cleanup
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Share one Grafana server per package in repository integration tests

Apply the shared Grafana server strategy (sync.Once + TestMain) to the
repository test package, following the same pattern established for the
connection package in #120906.

Server startups drop from 25 → 4 (1 shared + 3 with custom options).

- Rewrite helpers_test.go with common.NewSharedEnv() + sharedHelper + TestMain
- Replace common.RunGrafana(t) with sharedHelper(t) in 21 tests
- Remove testutil.SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode (handled by GetHelper)
- Move TestIntegrationRepositoryController_EnterpriseWiring to enterprise/
- Keep 3 tests with custom GrafanaOptions on their own servers

Made-with: Cursor

fix: clean shared provisioning directory between tests

The shared server reuses one provisioning temp directory across all
tests. CleanupAllResources removes K8s objects but files from previous
tests remain on disk, causing tests like ImportAllPanelsFromLocalRepository
to see unexpected dashboards. Clear the provisioning path in sharedHelper
to restore filesystem isolation.

Made-with: Cursor

fix: move provisioning path cleanup into CleanupAllResources

The filesystem cleanup belongs in the common package alongside the K8s
resource cleanup — it's a test isolation concern that every migrated
package needs, not something each sharedHelper should duplicate.

Made-with: Cursor

fix: migrate RepositoryLimits and JobPermissions to shared server

Two standalone servers sharing the same DB as the shared server caused
controller interference — the shared server's controller would claim
jobs from standalone tests and reject paths not in its permitted list.

Add ProvisioningMaxRepositories=10 and FlagProvisioningFolderMetadata
to the shared env so these tests use the same server. Only
ViewerSettings_CustomRepositoryTypes remains standalone (it only reads
settings, no repo creation or job processing).

Server startups: 25 → 2 (1 shared + 1 settings-only).

Made-with: Cursor

fix: move RepositoryLimits to own sub-package for DB isolation

The ProvisioningMaxRepositories=10 setting on the shared server caused
RepositoryValidation subtests to hit the limit (they create 10+ repos
across subtests). It also caused RepositoryLimits itself to see stale
quota counts from the shared DB.

Moving the test to its own sub-package gives it a separate TestMain,
test database, and Grafana server instance — the isolation it needs.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Search: Increase index rebuild jitter range from maxAge/5 to maxAge/2
body:
## Summary

- Increases the per-namespace jitter range for Bleve index rebuild staleness checks from `maxAge/5` to `maxAge/2`
- The previous range (~4.8h for 24h maxAge) wasn't providing enough spread to effectively prevent thundering herd CPU spikes
- New range (~12h for 24h maxAge) distributes rebuilds across ~144 five-minute scan intervals instead of ~57

## Test plan

- [x] `TestJitterForKey` bounds updated and passing
- [x] `TestFindIndexesToRebuildWithJitter` passing
- [x] Existing `TestFindIndexesForRebuild` and `TestRebuildIndexes` passing
- [ ] Monitor CPU usage in staging to verify improved rebuild spread
author: pstibrany

commits.message:
Search: Increase index rebuild jitter range from maxAge/5 to maxAge/2

The previous jitter range (20% of maxAge) wasn't providing enough spread
to effectively prevent thundering herd CPU spikes during periodic index
rebuilds. Increasing to 50% of maxAge gives ~2.5x more scan windows for
rebuilds to distribute across.

For the default 24h maxAge, this increases the jitter window from ~4.8h
to ~12h, spreading rebuilds across ~144 five-minute scan intervals
instead of ~57.

title: E2E: Fix flaky dashboard variable test
body: We have a flaky test in variables e2e. The test fails with this error: The runner can't find the correct label for the variable. The cause is that the label is written twice, or rather, the name and the variable are all written in the same input field: It seems like the flakiness is caused by [this PR](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120094) that fixes autofocus on variable name input. The consequence of this is that the test 1. fills out the name input with the variable name 2. switches to the label field 3. the autofocus hook acts with 200ms delay and returns the autofocus onto the name field 4. playwright fill() method fills out the name input again We could either - change the behaviour to remove the timeout in the hook - adjust the test to act with 250ms timeout before filling out any inputs to make sure the focus won't be unexpectedly switched

title: Provisioning: Validate hidden characters in private key field and surface non-fetch errors
body:
**What is this feature?**

Adds hidden character validation to the private key (PEM) field in the provisioning connection form and fixes silent error swallowing for non-fetch errors during form submission.

**Why do we need this feature?**

When users paste a private key from rich text sources (web pages, PDFs, chat apps, password managers), invisible Unicode characters can be silently included. These characters cause `btoa()` to throw a `DOMException` because they fall outside the Latin1 range.

In the connection wizard, the error was caught by a fallback handler. In the regular connection form (`ConnectionForm.tsx`), the catch block only checked `isFetchError()` — a `DOMException` is not a fetch error, so it fell through silently. The user saw no error message and the connection was not saved.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/995
author: Clarity-89

commits.message:
Provisioning: validate hidden characters in private key field and surface non-fetch errors

- Add validateNoHiddenCharacters utility that catches invisible Unicode
  characters (zero-width spaces, BOM, directional marks, etc.) commonly
  introduced by copy-paste from rich text sources
- Wire validator to the private key Controller in GitHubConnectionFields
- Add submitError state to ConnectionForm to surface non-fetch errors
  (e.g. DOMException from btoa) instead of silently swallowing them
- Add tests for validator (14 cases) and ConnectionForm (2 new cases)

title: TenantWatcher: handle conflict errors in editResourceLabel
body:
**The Bug**
Fixes optimistic locking errors when multiple pods process the same tenant pending-delete event simultaneously:

```
1. Tenant marked pending-delete — all pods receive the event
2. All pods call ListLatestResourceKeys — each gets the same resource keys with the same ResourceVersions
3. Pods iterate through resources and call editResourceLabel for each one
4. editResourceLabel reads the resource data at the listed RV (e.g. RV 100) and writes with PreviousRV: 100
5. One pod's write succeeds, bumping the RV to 101
6. The other pod's write arrives with PreviousRV: 100 — the backwards-compatible update path compares it against the current RV (now 101), finds a mismatch, and returns a conflict error
7. The failing pod aborts the entire tenant labelling operation, even though the resource was already labelled by the other pod
```

**The Fix**
If we encounter an OL error when adding the pending-delete label to the resource, we check if the current resource already has the pending delete label (bc another pod probably added it already).
author: owensmallwood

reviews.body:
> 7. The failing pod aborts the entire tenant labelling operation, even though the resource was already labelled by the other pod

I'm tempted to say this is a good thing, as it would eliminate the current race to update the same records. 😄

I think we should add some jitter in our reaction to tenant deletion events. With N pods, it's very likely that they will all start processing the event ~at the same time, leading to avoidable concurrency for the same resources.

commits.message:
TenantWatcher: handle conflict errors in editResourceLabel

When multiple pods process the same tenant event, they read resources at
the same ResourceVersion. One pod's write succeeds while the other gets
a conflict error, aborting the entire tenant labelling operation.

On conflict, re-read the resource at the latest RV and check whether the
label is already in the desired state. If another pod already applied
the label, treat it as success instead of failing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: TenantWatcher: fix orphaned pending-delete labels after partial failure
body:
Fixes bug where tenant data could become orphaned.

If labelling a tenants resources fails, some of their resources will be labelled and some will not. They will also not have a pending-delete record since labelling didnt finish. If that tenant is restored, then their pending deleted resources will not be unlabelled - effectively orphaning that data.
author: owensmallwood

commits.message:
TenantWatcher: fix orphaned pending-delete labels after partial failure

Write the PendingDeleteRecord before labelling begins (with
LabelingComplete=false) so that clearTenantPendingDelete can clean up
orphaned labels even if labelling fails partway through. Previously the
record was only created after all labels were applied, so a partial
failure followed by a tenant unmark left resources permanently labelled
with no cleanup path.

Also set LabelingComplete=false before unlabelling in
clearTenantPendingDelete so that if unlabelling fails partway and the
tenant is re-marked, reconcileTenantPendingDelete will re-label.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Unified Storage: Tenant watcher fix token renewal
body:
Was running into auth errors. The tenant watcher was listing/watching successfully on startup, but would fail later on. This was bc we got a token on startup, but never renewed it on subsequent informer syncs.

This fixes that to renew the token every time the informer syncs. The tenant api expects the auth token to be in the Authorization header as a bearer token, so had to add a custom wrapper to make that happen.
author: owensmallwood

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message: renew tenant api token on each request. Add custom wrapper to set bearer token
