title: Provisioning: Share one Grafana server per package in connection integration tests
body:
## Why Connection integration tests start **22 separate Grafana servers** — one per `TestIntegration*` function. Each startup involves Wire DI, database provisioning, HTTP listener binding, health polling, and shutdown. This is the main bottleneck making these tests slow. ## What Refactor the `connection` package to share **one Grafana server** across all tests using `sync.Once` + `TestMain` shutdown. Enterprise tests move to a dedicated `enterprise/` sibling package (intended to hold all enterprise provisioning tests) with its own shared server. **Server startups drop from 22 → 1** (one standard; enterprise in separate package). ## Performance Results | | main | branch | delta | |---|---|---|---| | `go test` reported time | 84s | 31s | **-63% (2.7x faster)** | | Wall clock time | 1m39s | 42s | **-58% (2.4x faster)** | | Grafana server startups | 22 | 1 | -21 startups eliminated | | Passing tests | 21/22 | 19/19 | All pass | | Broken tests | 1 (`ConnectionRepositoriesResponseType`) | 0 | Fixed by robust cleanup | > **Note:** `TestIntegrationProvisioning_ConnectionRepositoriesResponseType` fails on `main` due to a resource version conflict when two tests use the same connection name. The new `deleteAndWait` cleanup (which retries deletes on conflict) fixes this pre-existing issue. ## How ### The `t.Cleanup` Problem `testinfra.StartGrafanaEnvWithDB` registers server shutdown on `t.Cleanup`. If multiple top-level tests share a server started with the first test's `t`, the server dies when that first test finishes. ### Solution: Manual Cleanup + `sync.Once` ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph connectionPkg ["connection/ package"] TM1["TestMain"] --> DBSetup["db.SetupTestDB()"] DBSetup --> MRun["m.Run()"] MRun --> T1["TestIntegration_CRUDL"] MRun --> T2["TestIntegration_Mutation"] MRun --> TN["... 19 tests in separate files"] T1 -->|"first call → sync.Once"| Server["Start Grafana once"] T2 -->|"reuses shared helper"| Server TN -->|"reuses shared helper"| Server MRun --> Shutdown["shutdownFunc()"] Shutdown --> DBClean["db.CleanupTestDB()"] end subgraph enterprisePkg ["enterprise/ package"] TM2["TestMain + sync.Once"] --> E1["TestIntegration_EnterpriseMutation"] TM2 --> E2["TestIntegration_EnterpriseValidation"] TM2 --> E3["TestIntegration_EnterpriseWiring"] end ``` ### Layer Diagram ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph "Test Layer (per package)" HT["helpers_test.go
sync.Once + TestMain"] TF["*_test.go files
sharedHelper(t) one-liner"] end subgraph "Common Layer" RGS["common.RunGrafanaShared()
Returns (helper, shutdownFunc)"] CC["CleanupAllResources()
Per-test state isolation"] end subgraph "Infrastructure Layer" KTS["apis.NewK8sTestHelperShared()
Manual cleanup variant"] TI["testinfra.StartGrafanaEnvWithManualCleanup()
Returns cleanup func"] end TF --> HT HT --> RGS HT --> CC RGS --> KTS KTS --> TI ``` ### Implementation 1. **`testinfra.go`** — Extract `StartGrafanaEnvWithManualCleanup` that returns a cleanup func instead of registering on `t.Cleanup`. Existing `StartGrafanaEnvWithDB` delegates to it (backward compatible). 2. **`apis/helper.go`** — Add `NewK8sTestHelperShared` wrapping the manual-cleanup variant. 3. **`common/testing.go`** — Add `RunGrafanaShared` (returns `helper, shutdownFunc`), `SharedEnv` (reusable `sync.Once` + `TestMain` wrapper), and robust `deleteAndWait` cleanup that retries on resource version conflicts. 4. **`connection/helpers_test.go`** — Uses `common.NewSharedEnv()` for `sync.Once` + `TestMain` lifecycle. 5. **All test files** — One-line change per test: ```diff func TestIntegrationProvisioning_ConnectionCRUDL(t *testing.T) { - testutil.SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode(t) - helper := common.RunGrafana(t) + helper := sharedHelper(t) ctx := context.Background() // ... rest unchanged ... } ``` 6. **`enterprise/`** (new package) — All enterprise provisioning tests live here with their own `sharedHelper` that includes `IsEnterprise` guard and enterprise connection types. This package is intended to hold enterprise tests from all provisioning areas, not just connections. ### Test Isolation `sharedHelper(t)` runs at the start of every test and: - Calls `CleanupAllResources` to remove all resources in dependency order (retries on resource version conflicts; failures are fatal to prevent cross-test contamination) - Restores default GitHub mock client ### Parallel Readiness - `sync.Once` is goroutine-safe — `t.Parallel()` can be added later - For parallel: replace per-test cleanup with unique resource names + `t.Cleanup` - Tests that mutate shared mocks stay sequential ## Files Changed | File | Change | |------|--------| | `pkg/tests/testinfra/testinfra.go` | Add `StartGrafanaEnvWithManualCleanup`, refactor existing func | | `pkg/tests/apis/helper.go` | Add `NewK8sTestHelperShared` | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/common/testing.go` | Add `RunGrafanaShared`, `SharedEnv`, `deleteAndWait`, cleanup methods | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/connection/helpers_test.go` | Rewrite with `common.NewSharedEnv()` | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/connection/connection_test.go` | One-line change ×16, remove 3 enterprise tests | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/connection/status_auth_test.go` | One-line change | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/connection/pending_delete_test.go` | One-line change | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/connection/repositories_test.go` | One-line change ×2 | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/connection/github_branch_protection_test.go` | One-line change ×2 | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/enterprise/` | **New** — All enterprise provisioning tests + shared helper | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/README.md` | **New** — Strategy documentation + migration checklist | ## Migration Checklist (for other packages) 1. Add `helpers_test.go` using `common.NewSharedEnv(...)` + `sharedHelper` + `TestMain` 2. Replace `common.RunGrafana(t)` with `sharedHelper(t)` in each test (one-line change) 3. Remove `testutil.SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode(t)` from each test (handled by `GetHelper`) 4. Add resource cleanup to `sharedHelper` for the resource types the package uses 5. Move enterprise tests to the `enterprise/` sibling package 6. Verify no assertions depend on global state without prior cleanup Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

## Pull request overview

This PR refactors provisioning connection integration tests to share a single Grafana server per Go test package (via `sync.Once` + `TestMain` shutdown), significantly reducing test runtime by avoiding repeated server bootstraps. It also moves enterprise-only provisioning tests into a dedicated `enterprise/` sibling package with its own shared server lifecycle.

**Changes:**
- Add manual-cleanup variants for Grafana/K8s test helpers to support suite-scoped server lifetimes.
- Introduce `common.SharedEnv` + `RunGrafanaShared` and per-test cleanup helpers to enable one shared server per package.
- Update connection tests to use `sharedHelper(t)` and move enterprise connection tests into `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/enterprise/`.

commits.message:
Provisioning: Share one Grafana server per package in connection integration tests

Connection integration tests were starting 22 separate Grafana servers — one
per TestIntegration* function. Each startup involves Wire DI, DB provisioning,
HTTP listener, health polling, and shutdown, making these tests very slow.

This refactors the connection package to share a single Grafana server across
all tests using sync.Once + TestMain shutdown:

- Extract StartGrafanaEnvWithManualCleanup in testinfra that returns a cleanup
  func instead of registering on t.Cleanup, so the server can outlive any
  single test.
- Add NewK8sTestHelperShared and RunGrafanaShared that use the manual-cleanup
  variant.
- Rewrite connection/helpers_test.go with sync.Once for lazy init and TestMain
  for shutdown.
- Replace RunGrafana(t) with sharedHelper(t) in all 19 non-enterprise tests
  (one-line change each, preserving top-level TestIntegration* signatures).
- Move 3 enterprise tests to a new connection_enterprise/ package with its own
  shared server and IsEnterprise guard.
- Add CleanupConnections for per-test state isolation.
- Document the strategy in pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/README.md.

Server startups drop from 22 to 2 (one standard, one enterprise).

Made-with: Cursor

fix: address review feedback and enterprise TempDir failure

- deleteAndWait: return nil (not transient firstErr) when all resources gone
- CleanupAllResources: takes *testing.T and t.Fatalf on errors (cleanup
  failures are fatal to prevent cross-test contamination)
- Remove individual Cleanup* methods; consolidate into CleanupAllResources
- testinfra: use fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr) instead of t.Log in shutdown closure
  to avoid panic when called after test completion (shared server case)
- Enterprise wiring test: replace defer with t.Cleanup for connection deletes
- CreateGrafDirShared: uses os.MkdirTemp so grafana home dir outlives the
  initializing test (fixes TempDir RemoveAll cleanup FAIL)
- NewK8sTestHelperShared: cleans up grafDir in shutdown func
- Update README and PR description to match implementation

Made-with: Cursor

fix: address review feedback — init errors, cleanup diagnostics, lint

- SharedEnv.GetHelper: recover from init panics and persist the error so
  subsequent tests get a clear t.Fatalf instead of nil-pointer panics
- deleteAndWait: include firstErr in timeout/cancel messages for better
  debugging of cleanup failures
- NewK8sTestHelperShared: only RemoveAll grafDir when it was created
  internally (don't delete caller-provided directories)
- SharedEnv.RunTestMain: skip DB setup/cleanup in short mode since
  GetHelper will skip all integration tests anyway
- Move nolint:gocyclo directive from CreateGrafDir to createGrafDir
  where the complexity actually lives

Made-with: Cursor

fix: restore accidentally removed comment in helper.go

Made-with: Cursor

fix: restore accidentally removed comment about FlagAppPlatformGrpcClientAuth

Made-with: Cursor

fix: restore accidentally removed GRPC comments in fillK8sOpts

Made-with: Cursor

fix: remove testing.Short() call before flag.Parse in RunTestMain

Made-with: Cursor

fix: address review feedback — init resilience, cleanup errors, README

- SharedEnv.GetHelper: detect both panics and FailNow/Goexit during
  init so subsequent tests get a clear t.Fatal instead of nil crashes
- Enterprise t.Cleanup: report non-NotFound delete errors via t.Errorf
  instead of silently ignoring them
- README: fix markdown table formatting (double leading pipes)
- K8sTestHelper stale *testing.T: acknowledged as pre-existing; full
  refactor deferred to a follow-up PR

Made-with: Cursor

fix: gofmt import order in enterprise_test.go

**What is this feature?**

Incremental permission tests reuse git and grafana servers instead of spin their own.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Reduce integration test execution time as it takes a few seconds to spin-up each instance.

Grafana CI systems, to have shorter integration test runs.

title: Provisioning: Fix orphaned resources when multiple objects share the same source path
body:
## What Harden the provisioning sync engine to correctly handle multiple Grafana resources that share the same `sourcePath` annotation. When duplicates exist, the sync now **keeps the best match** (by content hash) and **deletes all orphan duplicates**, instead of silently dropping them from the diff. This applies to both the full-sync `Changes()` function and the `managedResourceIndex` used during incremental sync. ## Why When a user changes `metadata.name` inside a provisioned file (e.g. renames a dashboard UID), the file path stays the same but a **new** K8s resource is created. The **old** resource — still annotated with the same `sourcePath` — becomes an orphan. PR #120870 addressed this at the *write* layer (`ReplaceResourceFromFile`), but the *diff* layer (`Changes()`) still used a `map[string]*ResourceListItem` — a **single-item-per-path** map. When the managed resource list returned multiple items for the same path, only the **last one** survived in the map (last-wins), and the rest were invisible to the sync engine: ``` Before (bug): Grafana storage Sync lookup map ┌───────────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────┐ │ path: dash.json │──────┐ │ dash.json ──► item-B │ ← last-wins │ name: item-A (orphan) │ │ └──────────────────────┘ ├───────────────────────┤ │ │ path: dash.json │──────┘ item-A is silently dropped │ name: item-B (real) │ from the map — never diffed, └───────────────────────┘ never deleted. ``` This meant orphans accumulated over time, invisible to full sync and never cleaned up. ## How ### Core change: multi-item-per-path lookup The lookup map type is changed from `map[string]*ResourceListItem` to `map[string][]*ResourceListItem`, so **all** resources at a given path are preserved: ``` After (fix): Grafana storage Sync lookup map ┌───────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────────┐ │ path: dash.json │──────┐ │ dash.json ──► [item-A, item-B]│ │ name: item-A (orphan) │ │ └────────────────────────────────┘ ├───────────────────────┤ │ │ path: dash.json │──────┘ Both items visible to the │ name: item-B (real) │ diff engine. └───────────────────────┘ ``` ### Primary selection & orphan deletion algorithm When the source file is matched against the items at its path, the algorithm: 1. **Picks a primary**: prefers the item whose hash matches the source file (preserves the existing K8s UID and avoids unnecessary writes). Falls back to `items[0]`. 2. **Emits `FileActionDeleted`** for every non-primary item (orphan cleanup). 3. **Emits `FileActionUpdated`** for the primary only if its hash differs from the source. ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Source file at path P
hash: H_src"] --> B{"Items at path P
in Grafana?"} B -- "No" --> C["Emit FileActionCreated"] B -- "Yes: items[]" --> D{"Any item.Hash
== H_src?"} D -- "Yes" --> E["primary = matching item"] D -- "No" --> F["primary = items[0]"] E --> G["For each non-primary item"] F --> G G --> H["Emit FileActionDeleted
(orphan cleanup)"] H --> I{"primary.Hash
!= H_src?"} I -- "Yes" --> J["Emit FileActionUpdated"] I -- "No" --> K["No change needed
(hash match)"] ``` ### Leftover cleanup Items remaining in the lookup after all source files have been processed are resources in Grafana with **no matching source file at all**. The nested loop now iterates all items per path (not just one): ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Remaining lookup entries
(no source file match)"] --> B["For each path"] B --> C["For each item at path"] C --> D{"Folder still
referenced?"} D -- "Yes" --> E["Skip (keep)"] D -- "No" --> F["Emit FileActionDeleted"] ``` ### Same pattern in `managedResourceIndex` The `byPath` map in `managedResourceIndex` (used by incremental sync for folder metadata lookups) received the same `map[string][]*ResourceListItem` treatment. `ExistingAt()` returns the first item and `DirectChildrenOf()` deduplicates paths. ### Files changed | File | What changed | |------|-------------| | `changes.go` | Multi-item lookup, primary selection, orphan deletion loop, updated `emitDirectChildrenChanges()` | | `managed_resource_index.go` | Multi-item `byPath` map, updated `ExistingAt()` and `DirectChildrenOf()` | | `changes_test.go` | +5 unit tests: hash match, no match, source deleted, 3+ duplicates, isolation | | `managed_resource_index_test.go` | +3 unit tests: `ExistingAt` with duplicates, `DirectChildrenOf` dedup, nil target | | `metadata_name_change_test.go` (full) | Updated assertions to expect cleanup; +3 integration tests (chained, multi-file, idempotent re-sync) | | `metadata_name_change_test.go` (incremental) | Updated assertions to expect cleanup (`count=1`) | ## Test plan - [x] Unit tests pass: `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...` - [ ] Integration tests pass (full sync): `go test -run TestIntegrationProvisioning_FullSync -tags integration ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_full/...` - [ ] Integration tests pass (incremental sync): `go test -run TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalGitSync -tags integration ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_incremental/...` - [ ] No regressions in existing provisioning sync tests
author: MissingRoberto

## Pull request overview

This PR hardens Grafana’s provisioning sync diff/index logic to correctly handle multiple managed resources that share the same `sourcePath`, ensuring the “best match” is retained and orphaned duplicates are surfaced for deletion (both in full sync and incremental sync flows).

**Changes:**
- Update full-sync diffing (`Changes()`) to track multiple managed items per path and emit orphan deletions instead of losing duplicates via last-write-wins maps.
- Update incremental sync folder-metadata indexing and orphan-folder cleanup to avoid dropping duplicates that share a path.
- Expand unit + integration test coverage for duplicate-path scenarios and metadata.name/UID change cleanup behavior.

### Reviewed changes

| pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes.go | Switch lookup to multi-item-per-path; select primary; emit orphan deletions; adjust remaining cleanup + direct-children emission. |
| pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes_test.go | Add unit tests covering duplicate-path selection and orphan deletion behavior. |
| pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/managed_resource_index.go | Make `byPath` multi-item-per-path; adapt `ExistingAt`/`DirectChildrenOf` semantics. |
| pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/managed_resource_index_test.go | Add tests for duplicate paths, deduped children, and nil target handling. |
| pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff.go | Rewrite metadata change handling to evaluate/replace multiple existing items at the same folder path. |
| pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff_test.go | Add tests for multi-orphan replacement behavior across create/update/delete cases. |

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go:388** * With `foldersToDelete` now allowing multiple deletions for the same `Path`, the guard `progress.HasDirPathFailedDeletion(entry.Path)` can cause later duplicates at the same path to be skipped after the first deletion failure. This happens because folder-deletion errors are recorded in `failedDeletions` under the folder path, and `HasDirPathFailedDeletion` is a prefix check. Consider grouping deletions by path and attempting all UIDs for that path, or adjusting the failure gating so a failed folder deletion at `entry.Path` doesn't block deleting other UIDs at the same `entry.Path` (while still preventing deletion when child-resource deletions failed). ``` for _, entry := range foldersToDelete { if progress.HasDirPathFailedCreation(entry.Path) || progress.HasDirPathFailedDeletion(entry.Path) || progress.HasChildPathFailedCreation(entry.Path) || progress.HasChildPathFailedUpdate(entry.Path) { progress.Record(ctx, jobs.NewFolderResult(entry.Path). WithAction(repository.FileActionIgnored). WithName(entry.UID). WithWarning(fmt.Errorf("folder %s was not deleted because a related operation failed", entry.UID)). Build()) ```

commits.message:
Provisioning: Fix orphaned resources when multiple objects share the same source path

When metadata.name changes in a provisioned file, the old resource was
left behind as an orphan because the sync lookup map only tracked one
resource per path (last-wins). This converts the lookup to store all
resources per path, picks the best match by hash, and emits deletions
for any orphan duplicates.

Includes unit tests for duplicate-path scenarios in Changes() and
managedResourceIndex, plus integration tests for chained name changes,
multi-file name changes, and orphan cleanup on subsequent syncs.

Made-with: Cursor

Merge origin/main and resolve conflicts in integration tests

Adopted the more precise apierrors.IsNotFound assertions from main while
keeping our additional ChainedNameChanges, MultipleFilesNameChange, and
OrphanCleanupOnSubsequentSync integration tests.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Return all items from ExistingAt and fix incremental orphan deletion

ExistingAt previously returned only the first item at a given path,
hiding duplicate orphans from its callers. This caused the incremental
sync folder-metadata diff builder to only schedule one old UID for
deletion when multiple orphaned folders shared the same path.

Changes:
- ExistingAt now returns []*ResourceListItem (the full slice)
- replacementsForMetadataChange iterates all items and returns a
  replacedFolder for each one whose UID differs from the new metadata
- rewriteDeletedMetadataChange checks directory existence once, then
  evaluates each item independently
- folderDeletion replaces map[string]string in deleteFolders to avoid
  silently dropping duplicate UIDs at the same path

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add integration tests for chained folder UID changes

Two new integration test cases for the incremental sync folder UID
change path:

- "chained UID changes never accumulate orphans": changes the
  _folder.json UID three times (v1->v2->v3) with incremental syncs
  between each, asserting exactly 1 folder and no orphans at every step.

- "full sync after incremental UID changes cleans up any remaining
  orphans": performs an incremental UID change then runs a full sync,
  verifying idempotency and that no stale folders reappear.

These cover the ExistingAt multi-item return and the folderDeletion
slice changes introduced in the previous commit.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Rewrite orphan cleanup test to inject real duplicate resources

The previous test only did sequential name changes with syncs in
between, so ReplaceResourceFromFile already handled each change and the
Changes() duplicate-path detection never actually fired.

The new test creates the real dashboard via sync, then injects a second
dashboard directly through the K8s API with the same manager annotations
and sourcePath — producing two resources at the same path. A subsequent
full sync then exercises the multi-item lookup in Changes() and verifies
it deletes the orphan while keeping the real dashboard.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix orphan cleanup test to bypass admission control

Create the orphan dashboard without provisioning annotations first,
then update it to add the sourcePath and manager annotations. This
avoids the "file already exists" rejection from admission control
while still producing the two-resources-at-same-path state needed
to exercise the Changes() multi-item cleanup logic.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Inject orphan via gRPC resource client to bypass admission

The K8s API layer routes updates to repo-managed resources through the
provisioning FILES API, which requires a commit message and rejects
duplicate sourcePaths. Bypass this entirely by writing the orphan
directly into unified storage via the gRPC ResourceClient with a
provisioning identity context. This correctly simulates the corrupt
state (two resources at the same path) that the Changes() multi-item
cleanup is designed to fix.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add unit tests for multi-item folder metadata diff handling

Cover the new multi-orphan code paths in
folder_metadata_incremental_diff.go:

- metadata create/update with multiple orphans at same path replaces
  all non-matching UIDs
- metadata update with mixed matching/orphan UIDs only replaces orphans
- metadata deletion with multiple orphans when directory still exists
- metadata deletion with multiple orphans when directory is gone
- metadata deletion with mix of fallback-matching and orphaned UIDs

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Skip folder orphan cleanup in Changes() and fix _folder.json best-match selection

Address review feedback from Copilot and Codex:

1. Skip duplicate-path orphan deletion for folder resources in Changes()
   because directory tree hashes don't match the _folder.json blob hash
   stored on managed folders, making the primary selection unreliable.
   Folder orphan cleanup is already handled downstream by
   augmentChangesForFolderMetadata (full sync) and the incremental diff
   builder.

2. When processing _folder.json, select the best-matching parent folder
   by comparing metadata hashes instead of always using parentItems[0].
   This ensures the correct folder UID is targeted for updates when
   multiple managed folders share the same path.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix gofmt indentation in changes.go

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Use index-based loop for lookup and best-match in emitDirectChildrenChanges

Address additional Copilot review comments:

1. Use index-based iteration (for i := range target.Items) when building
   the lookup map in Changes(), avoiding &item on the range variable.
   While Go 1.22+ makes per-iteration copies safe, index-based access
   is clearer and mutates the original slice element directly.

2. In emitDirectChildrenChanges, select the best-matching item by hash
   instead of always using items[0] when multiple managed resources
   share the same sourcePath. This is consistent with the primary
   selection logic elsewhere in the diff computation.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix folder orphan cleanup, child reparenting, and dedup folder deletions

Address three new review comments:

1. (Copilot) When processing _folder.json, emit deletions for all
   non-primary parent folder items. Since file.Hash (_folder.json blob
   hash) is directly comparable to managed folder hashes, this is the
   correct place for folder orphan cleanup — complementing the earlier
   skip of folder duplicates in the directory-entry processing block.

2. (Codex P1) Exclude DELETE actions when building pathsWithChanges in
   augmentChangesForFolderMetadata. Orphan-cleanup deletions at a child
   path were suppressing the reparent UPDATE that the surviving primary
   needs when a parent folder UID changes. Since emitDirectChildrenChanges
   iterates source tree entries (deleted-from-source files won't appear),
   excluding DELETEs is safe and ensures the primary still gets updated.

3. (Codex P2) Deduplicate foldersToDelete before calling deleteFolders
   in incremental sync. The same (path, UID) pair can be produced by
   both findOrphanedFolders and replaced-folder metadata cleanup,
   causing a spurious NotFound error on the second RemoveFolder call.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Exclude orphan deletions from rename detection and filter deleted items in child reparenting

Address two new review comments:

1. (Codex P1) Orphan-cleanup deletions are now marked with
   OrphanCleanup=true on ResourceFileChange. DetectRenames skips these
   entries so that an orphan deletion with the same content hash as a
   new file is not consumed as a rename candidate, which would prevent
   the orphan from being cleaned up.

2. (Copilot) emitDirectChildrenChanges now builds a set of items
   already scheduled for deletion and excludes them when selecting the
   best-match item for reparent updates. This prevents emitting an
   UPDATE for a resource that is about to be deleted (e.g., an orphan
   folder whose tree hash happened to match).

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Use pointer identity for deletedItems map in emitDirectChildrenChanges

The deletedItems map was keyed by ResourceListItem.Name, but Name is
only unique within a (Group, Resource) pair — different resource kinds
can legally share the same name. Switch to *ResourceListItem pointer
keys since all items reference the same target.Items backing array,
providing exact identity without needing a composite key.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Suppress orphan-cleanup folder deletes when metadata is invalid

When _folder.json is invalid and multiple managed folders exist at the
same path, the hash-based primary selection is unreliable since the
metadata can't be parsed to confirm intent. Previously,
processInvalidFolderMetadataChanges only suppressed invalid folder
updates but let orphan-cleanup deletes through, potentially deleting
a valid folder UID based on fallback ordering.

Now collect folder paths with invalid metadata during validation and
suppress OrphanCleanup deletes at those paths in a second pass.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Restore explanatory comment in rewriteDeletedMetadataChange

Made-with: Cursor

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into rjimenez/sync-multi-object-orphan-cleanup

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: Track old folder on renamed folder metadata
body:
## What

Handle `FileActionRenamed` for `_folder.json` files in the incremental metadata diff builder so that renamed folder metadata correctly cleans up the old folder resource and creates/updates the folder at the destination path.

## Why

When `provisioningFolderMetadata` is enabled, `splitMetadataChanges` correctly identifies renamed `_folder.json` files, but the `rewriteMetadataChange` dispatcher only handled `Created`, `Updated`, and `Deleted` actions. The `FileActionRenamed` case fell through to the `default` no-op, silently dropping the change.

This matters in **file-only rename diffs** — when git emits individual file-level renames (e.g. `git mv old/_folder.json new/_folder.json`) rather than a directory rename. In this scenario `change.PreviousPath` was never recorded, so the old folder resource was left **orphaned** in Grafana with no way to clean it up short of a full sync.

Ref: #120835 (comment)

## How

### Core fix: `folder_metadata_incremental_diff.go`

Add `FileActionRenamed` case in the `rewriteMetadataChange` switch, delegating to a new `rewriteRenamedMetadataChange` method that decomposes the rename into two logical parts:

1. **Create/update at the new path** (via existing `rewriteCreatedOrUpdatedMetadataChange`) — ensures the destination folder is created/updated with the correct identity from `_folder.json`, and direct children are replayed.
2. **Old-path cleanup** — determines whether the old directory still exists at `currentRef` and handles accordingly:
   - If the directory is gone: tracks the old folder UID for deletion (via `replacedFolder`).
   - If the directory still exists: emits `Updated` entries for the old folder path and its direct children so they are re-parented under a new path-derived folder identity.
   - If a directory rename already covers the old path in the original diff: skips cleanup entirely to avoid duplicate handling.

### Defensive guards

Three additional safety measures prevent incorrect behavior in edge cases:

- **Non-metadata `PreviousPath` guard** (`folder_metadata_diff_split.go` + `folder_metadata_incremental_diff.go`): If git reports a rename *to* `_folder.json` from a non-metadata file (e.g. `config.json → _folder.json`), the old path is not a metadata folder. The `PreviousPath` is only registered in `metadataFolderPaths` when it is actually a `_folder.json` file, and `rewriteRenamedMetadataChange` skips old-path cleanup entirely for non-metadata sources.

- **Identity-preserving rename check** (`resolveNewMetadataUID`): When the `_folder.json` contains the same UID at both old and new paths, the folder is being **moved**, not replaced. The old UID is not scheduled for deletion — doing so would destroy the folder that was just moved to the destination path.

- **Nested metadata rename protection** (`splitMetadataChanges`): When both `old/_folder.json` and `old/child/_folder.json` are renamed in the same diff, the parent's synthetic delete expansion could emit a spurious `Updated old/child/` because `metadataFolderPaths` only tracked new paths. Registering the old folder path for renamed metadata changes ensures the child's old path is recognized and skipped.

### What this does NOT change

When a directory rename event IS present in the diff (the common case with `git mv dir/`), the existing code path in `applyIncrementalChanges` handles the folder rename via `RenameFolderPath` and tracks the old folder in `affectedFolders`. That path is unaffected.

## Test plan

### Unit tests (`folder_metadata_incremental_diff_test.go` — 8 new tests)

| Test | Verifies |
|------|----------|
| renamed metadata with no existing resources | emits folder update for new path only |
| renamed metadata, old directory gone | tracks old folder UID for orphan cleanup |
| renamed metadata, old directory exists | re-parents children and tracks replacement |
| renamed metadata, directory rename in diff | skips redundant old-path cleanup |
| nested metadata renames | no spurious `Updated old/child/` entries |
| rename from non-metadata to `_folder.json` | only emits create, no old-path cleanup |
| identity-preserving rename (same UID) | does NOT schedule old UID for deletion |
| nested metadata changes (created) | both expanded deterministically (pre-existing, still passes) |

### Integration tests (`incremental_folder_metadata_test.go` — 2 new tests)

| Test | Scenario |
|------|----------|
| folder rename with dashboard | `git mv` of `_folder.json` + dashboard verifies UID preservation, dashboard re-parenting, old folder cleanup |
| empty folder rename | `git mv` of `_folder.json` only verifies old folder removal and new folder creation |
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes incremental provisioning behavior when `_folder.json` is renamed (file-only rename diffs) by ensuring the old folder path is tracked so the orphaned Grafana folder resource can be cleaned up. **Changes:** - Handle `FileActionRenamed` for `_folder.json` in `rewriteMetadataChange`, decomposing it into update-at-new-path plus cleanup tracking for the old path. - Add unit tests covering renamed metadata scenarios (no existing resources, orphan cleanup, re-parenting, and directory-rename-present skip logic). - Add integration tests validating end-to-end orphan cleanup and UID preservation on metadata renames. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff.go | Adds rename handling for `_folder.json` to avoid silently dropping rename events and to track old folders for cleanup. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff_test.go | Adds unit test coverage for renamed metadata rewrite behavior and cleanup tracking. | | pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_incremental/incremental_folder_metadata_test.go | Adds integration coverage for renamed folder metadata orphan cleanup scenarios. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. commits.message:
Provisioning: Track old folder on renamed _folder.json updates

When provisioningFolderMetadata is enabled, renamed _folder.json files
were routed to the metadata change handler but the FileActionRenamed
case fell through to the default no-op, silently dropping the change.
In file-only rename diffs (no separate directory rename event), this
meant the old folder was never added to affectedFolders for orphan
cleanup.

Add rewriteRenamedMetadataChange that decomposes a _folder.json rename
into a create/update at the new path and a synthetic delete at the old
path, so the old folder is properly tracked for cleanup. When a
directory rename already covers the old path in the original diff, the
delete side is skipped to avoid duplicate handling.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add integration tests for renamed _folder.json orphan cleanup

Add TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalSync_RenamedFolderMetadataOrphanCleanup
with two sub-tests that exercise incremental sync when _folder.json files
are moved between directories via file-level git renames:

- metadata-only folder rename with a dashboard verifies the old folder
  is cleaned up and the new folder inherits the stable UID
- metadata-only empty folder rename verifies the same for folders
  containing only _folder.json

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Address PR review feedback for renamed _folder.json handling

- Use PreviousRef instead of Ref in synthetic delete to match codebase
  convention for FileActionDeleted changes
- Replace TriggerJobAndWaitForComplete with SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental
  to assert job success and avoid false positives
- Remove misleading comment about "empty folder" in test with dashboard

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Prevent orphan child folders on nested metadata renames

When both parent and child _folder.json files are renamed in the same
diff, the parent's synthetic delete expansion would emit a spurious
Updated entry for old/child/ because metadataFolderPaths only tracked
new paths. Register PreviousPath-derived folder paths for renamed
metadata changes so the child's old path is recognized and skipped.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Tolerate non-empty folder error in rename integration test

RenameResourceFile currently fails to delete non-empty folders during
incremental sync (known limitation shared with other rename tests).
Use TriggerJobAndWaitForComplete instead of SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental
for the test case that includes a dashboard alongside the _folder.json rename.
The empty-folder test case keeps asserting success since no children exist.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Harden renamed metadata handling for edge cases

Address two defensive concerns raised in review:

1. Guard against non-metadata PreviousPath: if git reports a rename
   from a non-_folder.json file to _folder.json (e.g. config.json ->
   _folder.json), skip old-path cleanup since no metadata folder
   existed there. Also only register PreviousPath in
   metadataFolderPaths when it is actually a _folder.json file.

2. Identity-preserving renames: when the _folder.json UID is the same
   at both old and new paths, the folder is being moved, not replaced.
   Skip scheduling the old UID for deletion to avoid destroying the
   folder that was just moved to the destination path. Inline the
   old-path handling in rewriteRenamedMetadataChange so replacement
   tracking can be conditionally skipped while still emitting directory
   and child updates when the old directory persists.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add unit tests for folder_metadata_diff_split rename behavior

Update the existing renamed metadata split test to assert that the old
folder path (from PreviousPath) is registered in metadataFolderPaths.

Add a new test verifying that renames from non-metadata files to
_folder.json do not register the old path in metadataFolderPaths.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix misleading doc comment on resolveNewMetadataUID

The comment said "Returns empty string if the metadata cannot be read"
but the function actually returns a non-nil error on read failure.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Improve inline comments in rewriteRenamedMetadataChange

Add comments to each logical block explaining its purpose — destination
vs source side handling, early-exit conditions, identity-preserving
move guard, and child re-parenting — so the function reads top-to-bottom
without needing to cross-reference the doc comment.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Assert incremental sync success in rename integration test

RenameResourceFile can now handle non-empty folders, so replace
TriggerJobAndWaitForComplete with SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental
and remove the outdated FIXME comment.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add integration tests for metadata move to existing folders

Two new sub-tests in RenamedFolderMetadataOrphanCleanup:

- metadata moved to folder with dashboard but no metadata: moves
  _folder.json from src/ to dst/ where dst/ already has a dashboard
  but no metadata. Verifies dst/ adopts the metadata UID and src/
  falls back to a hash-derived identity.

- metadata moved to folder with dashboard and pre-existing metadata:
  deletes src/_folder.json and overwrites dst/_folder.json with the
  source identity. Verifies dst/ adopts the new UID, the old dst UID
  is deleted, and src/ reverts to a hash-derived folder.

Made-with: Cursor

chore: fix gofmt alignment in integration test map literals

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Tolerate warnings in metadata-move integration tests

When only _folder.json moves but dashboards stay behind, the old
folder identity cannot be deleted because it still has children.
Use TriggerJobAndWaitForComplete instead of asserting success for
these two cases — the final resource state is still verified.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix test failures and address Copilot review feedback

1. Use SyncAndWaitWithWarning for the initial full sync in the
   "metadata moved to folder with dashboard but no metadata" test,
   since dst/ has no _folder.json and full sync now reports
   missing-metadata warnings (added in main).

2. Fix diff_split_test: use different directories for Path and
   PreviousPath so HasMetadataFolderAt actually tests the guard
   against registering non-metadata old paths (Copilot feedback).

3. Remove redundant HadChangeOriginallyAt check in
   rewriteRenamedMetadataChange — the function already returns
   early when that condition is true (Copilot feedback).

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Prevent deletion of folder UIDs claimed by another metadata change

When a _folder.json is deleted from one path and the same UID is written
to a different path's _folder.json in the same diff, the old UID was
incorrectly scheduled for deletion. This destroyed the folder at the
destination path.

Track UIDs actively written by metadata create/update changes and filter
them out of the replacement list before cleanup. A UID that moves between
paths is not orphaned — it should not be deleted.

Example: deleting src/_folder.json (UID=X) while updating dst/_folder.json
to contain UID=X no longer schedules X for deletion.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Assert warning state in metadata-move integration tests

Replace TriggerJobAndWaitForComplete with SyncAndWaitIncrementalWithWarning
so both metadata-move tests assert a clean warning (missing-metadata at
the source folder) instead of silently tolerating errors.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Track active UIDs for brand-new folder creates

Read metadata before checking existing folder in
replacementForMetadataChange so that the UID is always resolved and
tracked via TrackActiveUID—even when no managed folder exists yet at
the path. This closes a gap where a delete+create of the same UID in
one diff could incorrectly delete a UID being actively written to a
new folder.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Remove redundant resolveNewMetadataUID read in rename handler

rewriteRenamedMetadataChange re-read the destination _folder.json via
resolveNewMetadataUID to check for identity-preserving moves, but
rewriteCreatedOrUpdatedMetadataChange already reads the same file and
tracks the UID via TrackActiveUID. The active-UID filtering in
ReplacedFolders() now handles this case, making the second read
redundant.

Remove resolveNewMetadataUID entirely and rely on ReplacedFolders()
to exclude identity-preserving moves. This eliminates duplicate repo
reads and simplifies both the code and the test mock expectations.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: Track failed renames by source path for cleanup safety
body:
## What

Fix a safety gap in incremental sync where a failed cross-folder rename does not prevent the old folder from being deleted, potentially removing a folder that still contains children.

Addresses review comment: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120848#discussion_r2969575778

## Why

When a `FileActionRenamed` operation fails, the resource stays at its **source** location. However, `Record` in `progress.go` only appends `result.Path()` (the **destination** path) to `failedUpdates`. The safety check `HasChildPathFailedUpdate` in `deleteFolders` evaluates against the **old folder** path, so for cross-folder renames the failure is invisible and the old folder can be deleted.

### Example: cross-folder rename failure causes data loss

Consider a folder UID change that triggers re-parenting of `oldfolder/dash.json` to `newfolder/dash.json`:

```
Rename: oldfolder/dash.json -> newfolder/dash.json   (FAILS)
```

**Before this fix:**

| Step | What happens |
|------|-------------|
| Rename fails | `dash.json` stays at `oldfolder/dash.json` |
| `Record` stores | `failedUpdates = ["newfolder/dash.json"]` |
| `deleteFolders` checks | `HasChildPathFailedUpdate("oldfolder/")` |
| `InDir("newfolder/dash.json", "oldfolder/")` | returns **false** |
| Result | `oldfolder/` is **deleted** despite still containing `dash.json` |

**After this fix:**

| Step | What happens |
|------|-------------|
| Rename fails | `dash.json` stays at `oldfolder/dash.json` |
| `Record` stores | `failedUpdates = ["newfolder/dash.json", "oldfolder/dash.json"]` |
| `deleteFolders` checks | `HasChildPathFailedUpdate("oldfolder/")` |
| `InDir("oldfolder/dash.json", "oldfolder/")` | returns **true** |
| Result | `oldfolder/` is **kept** safely |

## How

1. **`job_resource_result.go`** -- Add `previousPath` field, `WithPreviousPath()` builder method, and `PreviousPath()` accessor to `JobResourceResult`.

2. **`progress.go`** -- In `Record()`, for both error and warning branches, when a `FileActionRenamed` has a non-empty `PreviousPath()`, also append it to `failedUpdates`. This ensures both source and destination folders are protected.

3. **`incremental.go`** -- Add `resultBuilder.WithPreviousPath(change.PreviousPath)` at the top of the `FileActionRenamed` case so the source path is always carried in the result.

4. **Tests** -- Update rename failure tests to use cross-folder scenarios and assert both source and destination folders are protected by `HasChildPathFailedUpdate`.

## Test plan

- [x] Updated `TestJobProgressRecorderHasChildPathFailedUpdate` to use cross-folder rename scenarios and assert both source and destination folders are protected
- [x] Updated `TestIncrementalSync/file_rename` mock expectation to verify `PreviousPath` is set
- [x] Updated hierarchical test rename matcher to assert `PreviousPath()` is propagated
- [x] All existing tests pass (`go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/...`)
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Fixes incremental provisioning cleanup safety when cross-folder renames fail by ensuring failures are tracked against both the destination and the source path, so `deleteFolders` doesn’t incorrectly delete folders that still contain resources. **Changes:** - Add `previousPath` to `jobs.JobResourceResult` plus builder/accessor support. - Propagate `change.PreviousPath` into rename results in `applyIncrementalChanges`. - Update progress recorder + tests to assert failed rename protection for both source and destination folders. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go | Sets `WithPreviousPath(change.PreviousPath)` for rename results so progress tracking can consider source paths. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/job_resource_result.go | Introduces `previousPath` field + builder/accessor to carry rename source path through recording. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/progress.go | Records failed rename `PreviousPath()` into `failedUpdates` so `HasChildPathFailedUpdate` catches source-folder failures. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/progress_test.go | Extends `HasChildPathFailedUpdate` tests to cover cross-folder renames and ensure both folders are protected. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental_test.go | Updates mock expectations to require `PreviousPath` propagation for rename recording. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental_hierarchical_test.go | Updates rename error matcher to assert `PreviousPath()` is present on recorded results. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for s

commits.message:
Provisioning: Track failed renames by source path for cleanup safety

Record currently appends result.Path() (destination) for failed
FileActionRenamed operations, but deleteFolders safety checks
(HasChildPathFailedUpdate) evaluate against the old folder path.
For cross-folder renames the destination is outside the old folder
while the resource stays at the source on failure, so the failure
is missed and the old folder can be deleted.

Fix by adding a previousPath field to JobResourceResult and recording
both source and destination paths in failedUpdates for failed renames.

Closes grafana/grafana#120848 (comment r2969575778)

Made-with: Cursor

Run gofmt -s on test files

title: Provisioning: Fix false orphan deletion of renamed folders during incremental sync
body:
## Summary

Fixes a bug where renaming a directory in git (e.g. `old-team` → `new-team`) during incremental sync would incorrectly attempt to delete the folder, producing `"Folder cannot be deleted: folder is not empty"` errors. The rename itself succeeded via fallback, but the job reported errors.

### Root cause

When a directory is renamed in git, incremental sync processes each file rename individually via `RenameResourceFile`. For dashboard files, this correctly updates the resource in place but also returned the old parent folder UID as a signal that the folder may now be empty. This UID was added to `affectedFolders[oldPath]`, and since the old directory path no longer exists in git, `findOrphanedFolders` marked the folder for deletion — even though it was simply moved in place (same UID at the new path) and still had children.

```
git mv old-team new-team
```
```
Diff:
  old-team/_folder.json → new-team/_folder.json   (renamed, same UID)
  old-team/dashboard.json → new-team/dashboard.json (renamed, same UID)

Flow:
  1. dashboard rename → RenameResourceFile → updates in place
     → returns oldFolderName = "rr-folder-uid"
     → affectedFolders["old-team"] = "rr-folder-uid"
  2. findOrphanedFolders: "old-team" gone from git → mark for deletion
  3. deleteFolders: delete "rr-folder-uid" → ERROR: folder is not empty
     (the folder was moved to "new-team", not emptied)
```

### Fix

In `RenameResourceFile`, after writing the resource at its new path, compare the old and new parent folder IDs. When they match (the folder was moved in place along with its contents), return an empty `oldFolderName` so the caller doesn't mark it for orphan deletion.

```go
// resources.go — after writeResourceFromParsed succeeds
if newParsed.Meta.GetFolder() == oldFolderName {
    oldFolderName = ""
}
```

This correctly handles:
- **Directory renames with metadata** (same stable UID) — signal suppressed
- **File moves between different folders** (different UIDs) — old folder still flagged for cleanup
- **Non-metadata directory renames** (hash-based UIDs change) — old folder still flagged for cleanup

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `resources/resources.go` | Compare old/new parent folder IDs after write; suppress signal when equal |
| `resources/resources_remove_test.go` | Add 2 unit tests covering same-folder suppression and cross-folder cleanup |
| `foldermetadata_incremental/...test.go` | Replace 5 FIXME workarounds with `SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental` assertions |

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1009
Related: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120856 (uncovered the issue), https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120835 (unblocked this fix)

## Test plan
- [x] All `RenameResourceFile` unit tests pass (7 tests, including 2 new)
- [x] All incremental sync unit tests pass
- [x] All 6 `TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalSync_GracefulFolderRename` sub-tests pass (5 previously failing now pass)
- [ ] CI green
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes incremental provisioning sync falsely attempting to delete folders after a git directory rename by suppressing orphan-cleanup signaling when the resource’s parent folder UID is unchanged. **Changes:** - Update `RenameResourceFile` to clear the “old folder” cleanup signal when the new resource is written under the same folder UID as before. - Adjust folder-rename integration tests to assert incremental sync job success (instead of only waiting for completion). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_incremental/incremental_folder_metadata_test.go | Switches 5 rename sub-tests to require successful incremental sync completion. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/resources.go | Suppresses orphan folder deletion signals when parent folder UID didn’t change across a rename. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
Provisioning: Fix false orphan deletion of renamed folders during incremental sync

When a directory is renamed in git (e.g. old-team → new-team), incremental
sync processes each file rename individually via RenameResourceFile. For
dashboards, this correctly updates the resource in place but also returned
the old parent folder UID. That UID was added to `affectedFolders` for
orphan detection. Since the old directory path no longer exists in git,
findOrphanedFolders incorrectly marked the folder for deletion — even
though it was simply moved (same UID at the new path) and still had
children.

The fix compares old and new parent folder IDs after writing the resource.
When they match (the folder was moved in place along with its contents),
the old folder name signal is suppressed so the caller does not mark it
for orphan deletion.

Also updates the 5 affected integration tests to assert success instead
of working around the error with bare wait-for-completion calls.

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1009

title: deps(go): bump modernc.org/sqlite from 1.44.3 to 1.47.0
body:
Bumps [modernc.org/sqlite](https://gitlab.com/cznic/sqlite) from 1.44.3 to 1.47.0.
Changelog

Sourced from modernc.org/sqlite's changelog.

Changelog

  • 2026-03-17 v1.47.0: Add CGO-free version of the vector extensions from https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec. See vec_test.go for example usage. From the GitHub project page:

    • Important: sqlite-vec is a pre-v1, so expect breaking changes!
    • Store and query float, int8, and binary vectors in vec0 virtual tables
    • Written in pure C, no dependencies, runs anywhere SQLite runs (Linux/MacOS/Windows, in the browser with WASM, Raspberry Pis, etc.)
    • Store non-vector data in metadata, auxiliary, or partition key columns
    • Thanks Zhenghao Zhang!

  • 2026-03-16 v1.46.2: Upgrade to SQLite 3.51.3.

  • 2026-02-17 v1.46.1:

    • Ensure connection state is reset if Tx.Commit fails. Previously, errors like SQLITE_BUSY during COMMIT could leave the underlying connection inside a transaction, causing errors when the connection was reused by the database/sql pool. The driver now detects this state and forces a rollback internally.
    • Fixes [GitHub issue #2](modernc-org/sqlite#2), thanks Edoardo Spadolini!

  • 2026-02-17 v1.46.0:

    • Enable ColumnTypeScanType to report time.Time instead of string for TEXT columns declared as DATE, DATETIME, TIME, or TIMESTAMP via a new _texttotime URI parameter.
    • See [GitHub pull request #1](modernc-org/sqlite#1), thanks devhaozi!

  • 2026-02-09 v1.45.0:

    • Introduce vtab subpackage (modernc.org/sqlite/vtab) exposing Module, Table, Cursor, and IndexInfo API for Go virtual tables.
    • Wire vtab registration into the driver: vtab.RegisterModule installs modules globally and each new connection calls sqlite3_create_module_v2.
    • Implement vtab trampolines for xCreate/xConnect/xBestIndex/xDisconnect/xDestroy/xOpen/xClose/xFilter/xNext/xEof/xColumn/xRowid.
    • Map SQLite’s sqlite3_index_info into vtab.IndexInfo, including constraints, ORDER BY terms, and constraint usage (ArgIndex → xFilter argv[]).
    • Add an in‑repo dummy vtab module and test (module_test.go) that validates registration, basic scanning, and constraint visibility.
    • See [GitLab merge request #90](https://gitlab.com/cznic/sqlite/-/merge_requests/90), thanks Adrian Witas!

  • 2026-01-19 v1.44.3: Resolves [GitLab issue #243](https://gitlab.com/cznic/sqlite/-/issues/243).

  • 2026-01-18 v1.44.2: Upgrade to SQLite 3.51.2.

  • 2026-01-13 v1.44.0: Upgrade to SQLite 3.51.1.

  • 2025-02-26 v1.36.0: Upgrade to SQLite 3.49.0.

  • 2024-11-16 v1.34.0: Implement ResetSession and IsValid methods in connection

  • 2024-07-22 v1.31.0: Support windows/386.

  • 2024-06-04 v1.30.0: Upgrade to SQLite 3.46.0, release notes at https://sqlite.org/releaselog/3_46_0.html.

  • 2024-02-13 v1.29.0: Upgrade to SQLite 3.45.1, release notes at https://sqlite.org/releaselog/3_45_1.html.

  • 2023-12-14: v1.28.0: Add (*Driver).RegisterConnectionHook,

title: Provisioning: react on invalid folder metadata in incremental pull
body:
## What

This PR makes incremental sync tolerate invalid `_folder.json` files instead of failing folder reconciliation.

This work is partially based on the full-sync behavior introduced in https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120882, adapted to the incremental diff/replay flow.

Incremental sync now reacts to malformed or incomplete folder metadata during folder-metadata create/update flows. In particular:
- existing folders with invalid `_folder.json` keep their current UID and continue to reconcile child resources
- new folders with invalid `_folder.json` fall back to the path-derived unstable UID and still reconcile their children
- invalid folder metadata is surfaced to the user as a warning in the sync job
- moving resources into folders with invalid metadata is supported
- moving a folder with invalid metadata falls back to delete+recreate semantics, so the folder transitions from its previous stable metadata-backed UID to a new unstable path-derived UID at the destination

This PR fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1016.

## Why

Before this change, incremental sync was too brittle around invalid folder metadata:
- malformed or incomplete `_folder.json` could abort folder metadata handling
- child resources under folders with invalid metadata could fail to reconcile
- incremental rename flows involving invalid folder metadata were not handled consistently
- warnings shown to users did not reflect the actual action being taken during sync

This change makes incremental sync degrade safely:
- folder identity falls back to the current or path-derived UID when metadata is invalid
- resource reconciliation continues whenever possible
- sync jobs surface clear warnings instead of failing outright

## Diff summary

### Incremental metadata diff handling
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff.go`
  - tolerates invalid `_folder.json` during create/update rewrites
  - keeps replaying folder/resource diffs without scheduling replacement when metadata is invalid
  - treats metadata-file renames as owned by the directory rename flow instead of replaying them separately

- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_diff_split.go`
  - recognizes `_folder.json` renames as handled metadata actions so the builder can suppress the raw metadata-file rename entry

### Incremental sync behavior
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go`
  - collects invalid folder metadata warnings from the original git diff
  - records action-aware warnings in progress results (`created`, `updated`, or `renamed` where applicable)
  - preserves the rewritten incremental diff flow for folder metadata handling

### Folder resolution during invalid metadata
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/folders.go`
  - extends incremental folder rename handling so invalid metadata can still fall back safely:
    - moving resources into invalid-metadata folders works
    - moving folders with invalid metadata uses delete+recreate semantics

### Test coverage
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff_test.go`
  - covers invalid metadata during incremental diff rewriting
  - covers suppression of metadata-file rename entries

- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental_test.go`
  - covers action-aware invalid metadata warnings in incremental sync
  - covers renamed invalid metadata fallback behavior

- `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_incremental/incremental_invalid_folder_metadata_test.go`
  - adds end-to-end incremental coverage for:
    - invalid metadata on existing folders
    - invalid metadata on new folders
    - invalid metadata updates on stable folders
    - moving resources into existing/new folders with invalid metadata
    - moving folders with invalid metadata using delete+recreate semantics

## Scope

This PR is about **incremental sync** behavior when `_folder.json` is invalid.

It does not change full sync behavior.

## Testing

- `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...`
- `go test -tags=enterprise ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_incremental/... -run 'TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalSync_InvalidFolderMetadata/moving_a_resource_into_an_existing_folder_with_invalid_metadata_still_works$'`
- `go test -tags=enterprise ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_incremental/... -run 'TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalSync_InvalidFolderMetadata/moving_a_resource_into_a_new_folder_with_invalid_metadata_falls_back_to_unstable_uid$'`
- `go test -tags=enterprise ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/foldermetadata_incremental/... -run 'TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalSync_InvalidFolderMetadata/moving_a_folder_with_invalid_metadata_falls_back_to_delete_and_recreate$'`

title: Provisioning: Full Sync - react on invalid folder metadata
body: ## What This PR makes full sync tolerate invalid `_folder.json` files instead of failing the entire reconciliation. Full sync now treats malformed or incomplete folder metadata as a warning and keeps reconciling the rest of the repository. In particular: - existing folders with invalid `_folder.json` keep their current unstable UID - new folders with invalid `_folder.json` fall back to the path-derived unstable UID - child resources can still reconcile when their parent folder metadata is invalid - moves into folders with invalid metadata are supported - when a folder with invalid metadata is moved, full sync falls back to delete+recreate semantics, so the folder transitions from its previous stable metadata-backed UID to a new unstable path-derived UID at the destination - invalid folder metadata warnings now preserve the effective sync action when one exists, so the job output distinguishes between invalid metadata handled via `created` and `updated` flows instead of always reporting `ignored` This PR fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1015. ## Why Before this change, invalid folder metadata could stop full sync entirely or block unrelated resource reconciliation. That made `_folder.json` errors too disruptive: - a malformed file could prevent child dashboards from updating - a new folder with invalid metadata could fail to reconcile at all - move handling was brittle because metadata-backed folder moves assumed valid metadata This change makes the system degrade more safely: - metadata problems are surfaced to the user as warnings - folder identity falls back to the current or path-derived UID when needed - unrelated resources and child resources continue to reconcile whenever possible ## Diff summary ### Invalid metadata classification - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/folder_metadata.go` - adds/uses `ErrInvalidFolderMetadata` and `InvalidFolderMetadata` - treats malformed JSON and missing `metadata.name` as invalid folder metadata ### Folder path resolution fallback - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/folders.go` - `EnsureFolderPathExist(...)` now resolves folders through a fallback path: - valid metadata -> use metadata-defined folder identity - invalid metadata on existing folder -> reuse the folder already known at that path - invalid metadata on new folder -> use the path-derived unstable UID - this lets child resources reconcile even when parent metadata is invalid - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/tree.go` - adds path-based lookup support to the in-memory folder tree - keeps the path index in sync when folder entries are updated or removed ### Full sync compare/apply behavior - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes.go` - invalid folder metadata is detected during compare and surfaced as warnings - existing-folder metadata updates with invalid `_folder.json` are suppressed so the current UID is preserved - created folders with invalid metadata keep their create change so apply can fall back safely - metadata-backed folder moves only collapse into in-place updates when `_folder.json` is valid - invalid-metadata folder moves are left as delete+create, which gives safe recreate semantics - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/full.go` - records invalid folder metadata as warning results visible in the job - preserves the effective action (`created` / `updated`) when one exists instead of always reporting `ignored` ### Test coverage - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/folder_metadata_test.go` - covers malformed JSON, missing `metadata.name`, and action-aware invalid metadata warnings - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/tree_test.go` - covers path-based folder lookup and path index updates - `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes_test.go` - covers compare-time invalid metadata warnings - covers created-folder invalid metadata preserving folder creation - covers invalid folder move behavior staying as delete+create - covers action-aware invalid metadata warnings for created vs updated folder paths - `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/full_sync_invalid_folder_metadata_test.go` - adds end-to-end coverage for: - invalid metadata on existing folders - invalid metadata on new folders - changed child resources under invalid metadata - moving resources into existing/new folders with invalid metadata - moving folders with invalid metadata using delete+recreate semantics - warning output reflecting the effective action taken by full sync ## Scope This PR is about **full sync** behavior when `_folder.json` is invalid. It does not change incremental sync behavior. ## Testing - `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/...` - `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...` - `go test -tags=enterprise ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/...`

title: extractErrorMessage: make extractErrorMessage optional with explicit fallbacks
body:
**What is this feature?**

- update `extractErrorMessage` to use overloads and return `undefined` when no valid message is present unless a fallback is provided.
- Add unit tests 
This is blocked by enterprise PR to fix tests: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11406

**Why do we need this feature?**

The previous helper contract always returned a string, even for invalid shapes, which masked missing/invalid error payloads and surfaced low-quality messages in the UI. Moving to string | undefined makes error handling explicit and safer at call sites.

**Who is this feature for?**

Primarily for devs need to extract message from error response obj

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

N/A, follow up from https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120560#discussion_r2969250775

title: Provisioning: thread static ref through folder metadata parsing
body:
## Summary

- Threads `currentRef` through every repository read path during sync (both full and incremental) so all reads are pinned to the same commit, preventing race conditions when new pushes arrive mid-sync
- Adds `ref` parameter to `EnsureFolderPathExist`, `CreateFolderWithUID`, and threads it through the full sync apply chain (`applyChanges` -> `applyChange` -> `WriteResourceFromFile` / `EnsureFolderPathExist`)
- Fixes `parser.Parse` to use `info.Ref` instead of `""` when calling `ReadFolderMetadata`, and updates `cleanupOrphanedFolders` to use `currentRef`

## Problem

During sync, the code resolves `currentRef` via `LatestRef()` at the start, but several downstream operations read folder metadata with `ref=""`, which resolves to the **current branch HEAD**. If a new push arrives between `LatestRef()` and the actual reads, the sync mixes data from two different commits.

### Affected call sites (before this PR):

| Location | Code | Context |
|----------|------|---------|
| `folders.go` | `ParseFolderWithMetadata(ctx, fm.repo, dir, "", ...)` | `EnsureFolderPathExist` |
| `parser.go` | `ReadFolderMetadata(ctx, r.reader, dirPath, "")` | Parser parent folder ID |
| `full.go` | `WriteResourceFromFile(writeCtx, change.Path, "")` | Full sync applying changes |
| `incremental.go` | `EnsureFolderPathExist(ctx, safeSegment)` | Incremental folder creation |
| `incremental.go` | `readerRepo.Read(ctx, path, "")` | `cleanupOrphanedFolders` |

## Test plan

- [x] All existing provisioning tests pass (`go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/... -count=1`)
- [ ] Manual verification with concurrent pushes during sync
- [ ] Verify incremental sync correctly pins reads to `currentRef`
- [ ] Verify full sync correctly pins reads to `currentRef`

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1001

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message: feat: integration test for permission persistance in folders

Provisioning: Update resource in place on rename during full sync (#120868)

* Provisioning: Update resource in place on rename during full sync

During full sync, Changes() produces separate delete+create pairs when
a file is moved/renamed. applyChanges runs deletions first, destroying
the K8s object UID, creationTimestamp, and generation before recreating
the resource at the new path.

Add DetectRenames post-processing step that identifies delete+create
pairs sharing the same resource identity (Name + Group + Resource) and
collapses them into a single FileActionUpdated change. This lets
WriteResourceFromFile -> ParsedResource.Run() perform a GET -> UPDATE
in place, preserving the K8s object identity.

The function:
- Indexes non-folder file deletions by resource identity from Existing
- Parses each created file via ParseFileResource to extract name + GVK
- Resolves GVK -> GVR via ResourceClients.ForKind for exact matching
- Gracefully skips files on read/parse/resolution errors
- Skips folder paths (handled by augmentChangesForFolderMetadata)

Mirrors the incremental sync fix from PR #120835.

Made-with: Cursor

* Refactor: move helpers to common packages and add rename detection logging

- Move ResourceIdentity to resources/id.go for reuse
- Move test helpers (SnapshotDashboardsBySourcePath, RequireDashboardsUpdatedInPlace) to common/testing.go
- Add Warn-level logging to DetectRenames error paths for observability

Made-with: Cursor

* Provisioning: Use hash-based rename detection in full sync

Replace file-reading identity-based rename detection with lightweight
hash matching. DetectRenames now compares Existing.Hash (stored
sourceChecksum) against the FileTreeEntry hash propagated on created
changes, avoiding redundant repo.Read calls during full sync.

Made-with: Cursor

* Fix: add missing syncAndWaitIncremental helper for git integration tests

The method was referenced by tests added in 672dba91313 but never
defined, causing a compilation error.

Made-with: Cursor

* Address PR review comments

- Fix docstring: FileActionUpdated -> FileActionRenamed
- Fix test name to match FileActionRenamed semantics
- Skip ambiguous hash matches (multiple deletions with same hash)
- Add FullSyncPhaseFileRenames: run renames before deletions so
  parent folders are not deleted while children still reference them
- Add test for duplicate hash edge case

Made-with: Cursor

* Reorder full sync phases: file deletions before folder creations

File deletions have no dependency on destination folders, so they
can run first to free up folder contents earlier. The new order is:
file deletions → folder creations → file renames → folder deletions
→ file creations → old folder cleanup.

Made-with: Cursor

* Rename test to DashboardMoveInPlace and document ambiguous hash handling

Rename TestIntegrationProvisioning_FullSync_DashboardMovePreservesUID to
DashboardMoveInPlace for clarity. Add inline comments in DetectRenames
explaining the ambiguous hash tracking logic.

title: Provisioning: Delete old resource when metadata.name changes at same path
body:
## What

When a resource's `metadata.name` (UID) changes inside a file at the same path in a Git-managed repository, the provisioning system now detects the identity change and deletes the old resource, preventing orphans. This applies to both full sync and incremental sync.

## Why

Previously, if a user changed `metadata.name` in a dashboard JSON file (e.g. from `dashboard-001` to `dashboard-002`) without changing the file path, sync would create a new resource with the new name but leave the old one behind. The old resource became an orphan — still present in Grafana but no longer tracked by any file in the repository. Over time this leads to stale resources that are invisible to the provisioning system.

This was reported in #120848 and tracked via integration tests in #120664.

## How

### New methods on `ResourcesManager`

- **`ReplaceResourceFromFile(ctx, path, ref, oldName, oldGVR)`** — Used by **full sync**. Writes the new resource via `WriteResourceFromFile`, then compares the resulting name against `oldName` (from `change.Existing`). If they differ, calls `deleteOldResource` to remove the previous resource.

- **`ReplaceResourceFromFileByRef(ctx, path, ref, previousRef)`** — Used by **incremental sync**. Reads and parses the old file at `previousRef` to discover the old name/GVR, writes the new resource, and if names differ, delegates to `deleteOldResource`.

- **`deleteOldResource(ctx, oldName, oldGVR, newName)`** — Shared helper that sets the provisioning identity on the context, fetches the existing resource, verifies ownership via `CheckResourceOwnership`, and deletes it using the dynamic client directly. Gracefully handles not-found (resource already gone).

### Sync layer changes

- **`full.go`**: `applyChange` calls `ReplaceResourceFromFile` for `FileActionUpdated` changes that have `Existing` set, passing the known old name and GVR.

- **`incremental.go`**: The `FileActionUpdated` case checks for `change.PreviousRef` — if present, calls `ReplaceResourceFromFileByRef`; otherwise falls back to `WriteResourceFromFile` (for synthetic changes from folder metadata that don't have a previous ref).

### Git layer fix

- **`git/repository.go`**: `CompareFiles` now sets `PreviousRef: base` on `FileStatusModified` changes. This was the missing piece that prevented incremental sync from detecting name changes — without it, `PreviousRef` was always empty for modified files.

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `resources/repository.go` | Added `ReplaceResourceFromFile` and `ReplaceResourceFromFileByRef` to the interface |
| `resources/resources.go` | Implemented both Replace methods and the `deleteOldResource` helper |
| `sync/full.go` | Calls `ReplaceResourceFromFile` for updated files with existing resources |
| `sync/incremental.go` | Calls `ReplaceResourceFromFileByRef` when `PreviousRef` is available |
| `git/repository.go` | Populates `PreviousRef` for modified files in `CompareFiles` |
| Integration tests | Assert old resource is deleted (count=1) instead of orphaned (count=2) |

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `ReplaceResourceFromFile` (6 cases: name unchanged, empty oldName, name changed, write failure, delete failure, ForResource error)
- [x] Unit tests for `ReplaceResourceFromFileByRef` (7 cases: name unchanged, name changed, read/parse errors, write failure, delete failure, empty old name)
- [x] Unit tests for `deleteOldResource` (4 cases: success, ForResource error, custom namespace, ownership conflict)
- [x] Unit tests for `CompareFiles` modified file `PreviousRef` (4 cases)
- [x] Unit tests for incremental sync Replace path (3 cases: with PreviousRef, without PreviousRef, error)
- [x] Integration tests assert old resource deleted for both full and incremental sync
- [ ] `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/...`
- [ ] `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...`
- [ ] `go test ./apps/provisioning/pkg/repository/git/...`
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the provisioning sync pipeline to detect when a resource’s identity (`metadata.name`/UID) changes while the file path stays the same, and to delete the old resource to avoid leaving orphaned objects in Grafana/K8s.

**Changes:**
- Add `ReplaceResourceFromFile` (full sync) and `ReplaceResourceFromFileByRef` (incremental sync) to write the new resource and delete the old one when the name changes.
- Populate `PreviousRef` for modified files in git `CompareFiles`, enabling incremental sync to read the previous file content and discover the old identity.
- Update sync flows and tests (unit + integration) to exercise and assert the orphan-prevention behavior.

### Reviewed changes

When a file's metadata.name (UID) changes but the file path stays the
