title: Provisioning: Update resource in place on rename during full sync
body:
## Why

During full sync, `Changes()` compares the repo source tree against the current Grafana state. When a file is moved (e.g., `dashboard.json` to `folder/dashboard.json`), it produces two separate changes:

- `FileActionDeleted` for the old path (in Grafana but not in source)
- `FileActionCreated` for the new path (in source but not in Grafana)

`applyChanges` then runs **deletions first**, destroying the K8s object, and **creates a new one** — losing UID, `creationTimestamp`, and `generation`. This is the same problem PR #120835 solved for incremental sync, where git provides `FileActionRenamed` events.

## What

Add a `DetectRenames` post-processing step after `Changes()` that identifies delete+create pairs sharing the same **content hash** and collapses them into a single `FileActionRenamed` change. Since both sides of the comparison already carry content hashes (the stored `sourceChecksum` on deleted resources and the `FileTreeEntry.Hash` from the repo tree), no extra file reads are needed.

This lets `WriteResourceFromFile` -> `ParsedResource.Run()` perform a GET -> UPDATE in place, preserving the K8s object identity.

## How

### `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes.go`

- `ResourceFileChange` gains a `Hash` field, populated from `FileTreeEntry.Hash` for `FileActionCreated` entries in `Changes()`.
- New `DetectRenames` function that:
  - Indexes non-folder file deletions by `Existing.Hash` (the stored `sourceChecksum`)
  - Skips ambiguous hashes (multiple deletions with the same hash)
  - For each `FileActionCreated` file, looks up its `Hash` in the deletion index
  - On match: converts Created -> Renamed with the deletion's `Existing`, removes the deletion
  - Skips folder paths (already handled by `augmentChangesForFolderMetadata`)

### `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/full.go`

- Wires `DetectRenames` into `FullSync()` after `Compare()` and before `applyChanges()`, with its own tracing span.
- Adds a new `FullSyncPhaseFileRenames` phase that runs **before** file deletions and folder deletions, ensuring renamed resources are updated in place before their old parent folders can be deleted.

### `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/common/testing.go`

- `SnapshotDashboardsBySourcePath`: batch-captures `ObjectSnapshot`s for dashboards by source path.
- `RequireDashboardsUpdatedInPlace`: asserts UID/timestamp/generation preservation across multiple paths.

### Tests

**Unit tests** (`changes_test.go`):
- Matching hash collapses delete+create into rename
- Different hash kept as separate delete+create
- No deletions / no creations fast paths
- Empty hash on deletion/creation is skipped
- Multiple rename pairs in one batch
- Mixed renames with genuine deletes/creates
- Folder deletions not matched
- Duplicate hashes across deletions are not matched

**Integration tests** (`full_sync_move_test.go`):
- Dashboard move preserves UID
- Folder move with metadata preserves dashboard UIDs

## Trade-off

This detects only **simple renames** (content unchanged). If a file is moved AND its content is modified in the same sync, hashes won't match and it falls back to the existing delete+create behavior. This is acceptable because content-changing moves are rare, and the delete+create path is correct — just not UID-preserving.
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Update resource in place on rename during full sync

During full sync, Changes() produces separate delete+create pairs when
a file is moved/renamed. applyChanges runs deletions first, destroying
the K8s object UID, creationTimestamp, and generation before recreating
the resource at the new path.

Add DetectRenames post-processing step that identifies delete+create
pairs sharing the same resource identity (Name + Group + Resource) and
collapses them into a single FileActionUpdated change. This lets
WriteResourceFromFile -> ParsedResource.Run() perform a GET -> UPDATE
in place, preserving the K8s object identity.

The function:
- Indexes non-folder file deletions by resource identity from Existing
- Parses each created file via ParseFileResource to extract name + GVK
- Resolves GVK -> GVR via ResourceClients.ForKind for exact matching
- Gracefully skips files on read/parse/resolution errors
- Skips folder paths (handled by augmentChangesForFolderMetadata)

Mirrors the incremental sync fix from PR #120835.

Made-with: Cursor

Refactor: move helpers to common packages and add rename detection logging

- Move ResourceIdentity to resources/id.go for reuse
- Move test helpers (SnapshotDashboardsBySourcePath, RequireDashboardsUpdatedInPlace) to common/testing.go
- Add Warn-level logging to DetectRenames error paths for observability

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Use hash-based rename detection in full sync

Replace file-reading identity-based rename detection with lightweight
hash matching. DetectRenames now compares Existing.Hash (stored
sourceChecksum) against the FileTreeEntry hash propagated on created
changes, avoiding redundant repo.Read calls during full sync.

Made-with: Cursor

Fix: add missing syncAndWaitIncremental helper for git integration tests

The method was referenced by tests added in 672dba91313 but never
defined, causing a compilation error.

Made-with: Cursor

Address PR review comments

- Fix docstring: FileActionUpdated -> FileActionRenamed
- Fix test name to match FileActionRenamed semantics
- Skip ambiguous hash matches (multiple deletions with same hash)
- Add FullSyncPhaseFileRenames: run renames before deletions so
  parent folders are not deleted while children still reference them
- Add test for duplicate hash edge case

Made-with: Cursor

Reorder full sync phases: file deletions before folder creations

File deletions have no dependency on destination folders, so they
can run first to free up folder contents earlier. The new order is:
file deletions → folder creations → file renames → folder deletions
→ file creations → old folder cleanup.

Made-with: Cursor

Rename test to DashboardMoveInPlace and document ambiguous hash handling

Rename TestIntegrationProvisioning_FullSync_DashboardMovePreservesUID to
DashboardMoveInPlace for clarity. Add inline comments in DetectRenames
explaining the ambiguous hash tracking logic.

title: Provisioning: Fix data race in jobProgressRecorder.TooManyErrors()
body:
## Summary

- `TooManyErrors()` reads `errorCount` and `maxErrors` without holding the `RWMutex`, while `Record()` increments `errorCount` under a write lock from concurrent goroutines (e.g. `applyResourcesInParallel` in full sync). This triggers data races under `go test -race`.
- Adds `r.mu.RLock()`/`r.mu.RUnlock()` to `TooManyErrors()`, consistent with how `Complete()` already reads the same fields.
- Adds a concurrent test (`TestJobProgressRecorderTooManyErrorsConcurrency`) that exercises the race with 20 goroutines calling `Record()` + `TooManyErrors()` simultaneously.

## Test plan

- [x] `go test -race -run TestJobProgressRecorderTooManyErrorsConcurrency ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/` passes
- [x] Full package test suite passes with `-race`


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

TooManyErrors() reads errorCount and maxErrors without holding the
RWMutex, while Record() increments errorCount under a write lock from
concurrent goroutines (e.g. applyResourcesInParallel). This triggers

title: Provisioning: Handle folder metadata deletion in incremental sync
body:
## Summary

- Extends incremental sync to handle `_folder.json` deletion: when a folder's metadata file is removed, the folder transitions from its stable UID (`metadata.name`) back to a hash-derived UID, with all direct children re-parented and the old folder deleted.
- Adds a safety guard in the apply phase so deleted `_folder.json` files are properly skipped instead of being treated as deletable Grafana resources.
- Builds on top of #120848 which adds support for `_folder.json` UID **changes**; this PR handles the **removal** case.

## Changes

**`pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go`**
- `planFolderMetadataChanges` now scans for `FileActionDeleted` on `_folder.json` files. When the parent directory still exists in Git, it computes the hash-based UID via `ParseFolder`, records the old UID for deferred cleanup, and emits synthetic `FileActionUpdated` entries for the folder and its direct children.
- `applyIncrementalChanges` extends the `IsFolderMetadataFile` guard to cover all action types, preventing deleted metadata files from reaching `RemoveResourceFromFile`.

**`pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental_test.go`**
- Unit tests for `planFolderMetadataChanges` deletion scenarios: UID transition, directory also gone, folder not in Grafana, same UID, child deduplication, and `List` error propagation.
- Full-flow tests: re-parenting + old folder deletion, apply-phase skip for deleted metadata.

**`pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/incremental_folder_metadata_test.go`**
- Integration tests: simple metadata deletion with re-parenting, nested child folder, and combined deletion + dashboard update in the same commit.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests pass (`go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...`)
- [x] Integration tests pass (`go test ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/...`)
- [x] No regressions in broader module (`go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/...`)
- [ ] CI passes

commits.message:
Provisioning: Handle _folder.json deletion in incremental sync

When a _folder.json is deleted during incremental sync, the folder now
transitions from a stable UID (metadata.name) back to a hash-derived
UID. The sync detects the deletion, creates the folder with the new
hash-based UID, re-parents all direct children, and deletes the old
folder. This mirrors the UID-change handling added in the parent PR
but is triggered by metadata file removal.

Key changes:
- planFolderMetadataChanges: also scans for FileActionDeleted on
  _folder.json files. When the directory still exists in git, computes
  the hash-based UID via ParseFolder and emits synthetic child updates
  for re-parenting. When the directory is gone, skips the transition
  (orphan cleanup handles it).
- applyIncrementalChanges: extends the IsFolderMetadataFile guard to
  handle all actions (not just Created/Updated), so deleted metadata
  files are properly skipped instead of reaching RemoveResourceFromFile.
- Unit tests cover planning (UID transition, directory gone, folder not
  in Grafana, same UID, dedup, List error) and full flow (re-parenting
  + old folder deletion, apply-phase skip).
- Integration tests cover simple deletion, nested child folder, and
  combined deletion + dashboard update.

Made-with: Cursor

Add integration test for re-parenting all direct children on metadata deletion

Adds a test case that verifies multiple dashboards (3) and a sub-folder
are all correctly re-parented from the old stable UID to the new
hash-derived UID when _folder.json is deleted. The test explicitly
asserts the grafana.app/folder annotation on both the child folder
and every dashboard before and after the deletion.

Fix gofmt alignment in integration test map literals

title: Unistore: avoid KV GC deleting history for live or recreated resources
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Tightens KV **garbageCollectGroupResource** so we do not remove history resource records for a (namespace, group, resource, name) when that object still exists in the store. That matches how the SQL backend already selects GC candidates.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Feature parity between SQL backend and KV backend

**Who is this feature for?**

@grafana/grafana-search-and-storage 

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

-->

Part of https://github.com/grafana/search-and-storage-team/issues/682

title: Provisioning: re-enable flaky incremental metadata name change test
body:
## Summary

Fixes the flaky `TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalGitSync_MetadataNameChange` test that was disabled in #120851, and hardens all incremental sync integration tests.

### Root cause

Two issues caused the flaky timeout:

1. **`lastRef` race**: The incremental sync could silently fall back to a full sync if `status.sync.lastRef` wasn't yet visible from the previous sync. A full sync with a UID change deletes the original dashboard, causing assertions (expecting both old and new) to fail and retry until timeout.

2. **Silent job failures**: `syncAndWaitIncremental` only waited for jobs to disappear from the active queue — it never checked if the job actually **succeeded**. A failed job would let subsequent `require.EventuallyWithT` assertions retry for their full 60s timeout, silently consuming the 8-minute test budget.

### Changes

- **Re-enable the test** — remove the `t.Skip` added in #120851.
- **New `common.SyncHelper` interface** — both `ProvisioningTestHelper` and `gitTestHelper` satisfy it, enabling shared sync helpers across test packages.
- **`common.WaitForRepoLastRef`** — standalone function that polls until `status.sync.lastRef` is non-empty.
- **`common.RequireJobSuccess`** — standalone assertion that a completed job has state `success` and no errors.
- **`common.SyncAndWaitWithSuccess`** — triggers a full sync, asserts success, waits for `lastRef`. Used as the initial sync before all incremental tests.
- **`common.SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental`** — triggers an incremental sync and asserts success.
- **Updated all incremental sync tests** to use the new helpers, providing:
  - Fast failure on job errors (no more 60s retry loops on a failed sync)
  - Guaranteed `lastRef` before incremental syncs
  - Consistent assertion patterns across all test files

Tests that expect non-success states (quota warnings, missing folder metadata) continue to use `triggerJobAndWaitForComplete` directly.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes for `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/...`
- [ ] `TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalGitSync_MetadataNameChange` no longer flakes
- [ ] Tests expecting warnings (e.g. missing folder metadata) still pass

commits.message:
fix: re-enable flaky incremental metadata name change test

The test was disabled in #120851 because it timed out in CI. Two changes
make it more robust:

1. Wait for status.sync.lastRef to be set after the initial full sync
   before triggering the incremental sync. Without this the syncer may
   fall back to a full sync if the status patch from the previous job
   has not yet been observed.

2. Switch from syncAndWaitIncremental to triggerJobAndWaitForComplete so
   the test verifies the job succeeded. This provides faster failure and
   better diagnostics when the sync produces errors instead of silently
   waiting for assertions to time out.

Made-with: Cursor

add common.RequireJobSuccess helper and use it in test

Extract the job-state-and-errors assertion into a reusable
RequireJobSuccess function in the common test helpers, matching
the existing AwaitJobSuccess pattern but as a standalone function
that works with any completed job object.

Made-with: Cursor

add syncAndWaitWithSuccess helper for incremental sync tests

Replaces syncAndWait with syncAndWaitWithSuccess in all tests that
precede an incremental sync. The new helper triggers a full sync,
asserts job success via common.RequireJobSuccess, and waits for
lastRef to be populated — ensuring subsequent incremental syncs
actually run incrementally rather than falling back to a full sync.

Made-with: Cursor

move WaitForRepoLastRef and SyncAndWaitWithSuccess to common package

WaitForRepoLastRef is now a standalone function in common, reusable
by any test helper that has a repositories client.

SyncAndWaitWithSuccess is a method on ProvisioningTestHelper that
triggers a full sync, asserts success, and waits for lastRef.

The gitTestHelper wrapper delegates to these common implementations.

Made-with: Cursor

add SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental and use in all success-expecting tests

New helper triggers an incremental sync, waits for completion, and
asserts job success. Replaces the old syncAndWaitIncremental which
only waited without checking the outcome, masking silent failures.

Applied to all 27 incremental sync call sites that expect success.
Tests expecting warnings or specific states still use
triggerJobAndWaitForComplete directly.

Made-with: Cursor

make SyncAndWaitWithSuccess and SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental standalone common functions

Introduces a SyncHelper interface so both ProvisioningTestHelper and
gitTestHelper can be passed to the common sync functions directly.
Removes the thin wrapper methods from gitTestHelper — all git test
files now call common.SyncAndWaitWithSuccess(t, helper, repoName)
and common.SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental(t, helper, repoName).

Made-with: Cursor

fix: revert to syncAndWait for test that expects warning state

The "noop incremental sync still detects missing metadata" test seeds
a repo with a folder missing _folder.json, so the full sync finishes
with a warning — not success. Using SyncAndWaitWithSuccess here would
incorrectly fail the assertion.

Made-with: Cursor

use common.SyncAndWaitSuccessfulIncremental in metadata name change test

Made-with: Cursor

remove unused common import from helpers_test.go

Made-with: Cursor

address review feedback: align timeout and fix warning-expecting syncs

- Bump common.WaitTimeoutDefault from 30s to 60s to match the git
  integration test budget and avoid CI flakes on slower runners.

- Revert three sync calls in incremental_folder_metadata_test.go back
  to non-success-asserting helpers: folders without _folder.json finish
  with JobStateWarning when WithProvisioningFolderMetadata is enabled,
  so RequireJobSuccess would cause false failures.

Made-with: Cursor

add warning sync helpers and fix all folder metadata/rename test failures

New common helpers:
- RequireJobWarning: asserts job completed with "warning" state, no errors
- SyncAndWaitWithWarning: full sync + assert warning + wait for lastRef
- SyncAndWaitIncrementalWithWarning: incremental sync + assert warning

Test fixes:
- GracefulFolderRename subtests (root_to_root, nested_to_nested, etc.)
  revert to triggerJobAndWaitForComplete — the rename implementation
  produces "Folder cannot be deleted: folder is not empty" errors that
  are expected (FIXME already tracked). RequireJobSuccess was surfacing
  these previously-silent errors.

- Missing-metadata tests now use the warning helpers instead of raw
  triggerJobAndWaitForComplete, giving proper state assertions.

title: Zanzana: Fix reconciler failure on deleted/archived namespaces
body:
## Summary

- Add `ErrStackNotFound` error to `legacysql` package so IAM storage backends can detect deleted/archived stacks
- IAM resource permission and legacy identity store backends now return K8s `NotFound` (instead of 500 `InternalError`) when the stack database doesn't exist
- Add `DeleteStore` to the Zanzana `ServerInternal` interface so the reconciler can clean up orphaned OpenFGA stores
- Reconciler detects `NotFound` errors during `fetchAndTranslateTuples` and deletes the orphaned store instead of failing

**Why**: The MT reconciler lists ALL OpenFGA stores. Deleted/archived stacks still have stores but no database. The `database_provider.go` returns `ErrStackNotFound` for these, but IAM storage backends wrapped all `dbProvider` errors as generic 500 InternalErrors, losing the distinction from transient failures.

## Test plan

- [x] `go test ./pkg/storage/legacysql/...` — passes
- [x] `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/iam/...` — passes
- [x] `go test ./pkg/services/authz/zanzana/server/...` — passes
- [x] `make build-go` — builds successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: mihai-turdean

commits.message:
Zanzana: Fix reconciler failure on deleted/archived namespaces

Move ErrStackNotFound to OSS legacysql package so IAM storage backends
can detect it and return K8s NotFound (instead of 500 InternalError).
The reconciler now detects NotFound errors and deletes orphaned OpenFGA
stores for namespaces that no longer exist.

- Add ErrStackNotFound sentinel to pkg/storage/legacysql
- IAM resourcepermission backends: return NotFound for ErrStackNotFound
- IAM legacy store: add getDB() helper wrapping sql() with ErrStackNotFound detection
- Add DeleteStore to ServerInternal interface and implement on Server
- Reconciler: detect deleted namespaces and clean up orphaned stores

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Zanzana: Simplify ErrStackNotFound handling

- Use v0alpha1 GroupResource constant instead of hardcoded "iam.grafana.app"
- Simplify GroupResource construction in storage_backend.go to use .GroupResource() directly
- Remove redundant isNamespaceDeletedOrArchived wrapper, inline apierrors.IsNotFound

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Zanzana: Use defer for mutex unlock in DeleteStore

Extract removeStore helper so the mutex is unlocked via defer,
preventing a deadlock if a panic occurs between Lock and Unlock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Zanzana: Use IAM group constant in getDB NotFound error

Use schema.GroupResource{Group: iamv0.GROUP} instead of
iamv0.UserResourceInfo.GroupResource() so the error is not
tied to a specific resource type. getDB is shared across
users, teams, service accounts, and other IAM resources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Zanzana: Rename ErrStackNotFound to ErrNamespaceNotFound

Replace cloud-specific "stack" terminology with "namespace" in the
OSS codebase.

Deleted the following exports from `@grafana/ui`, which have been deprecated since January 2026.

title: Provisioning: Handle folder UID changes in incremental sync
body:
## What

Adds support for handling `metadata.name` (UID) changes in `_folder.json` during incremental sync. When a folder's UID changes, the sync now:

1. Detects the UID change by comparing the new `_folder.json` against existing Grafana folders
2. Creates the folder with the new UID
3. Re-parents all direct children (dashboards and sub-folders) to the new folder
4. Deletes the old folder after all children are safely moved

## Why

Previously, changing `metadata.name` in `_folder.json` during incremental sync would create a new folder but leave children orphaned under the old UID, which would never be cleaned up. This follows the plan-then-apply pattern already established in full sync (PR #120717).

## How

### `planFolderMetadataChanges` (new function)
- Scans the diff for `_folder.json` `FileActionUpdated` entries
- Lists existing Grafana resources to find the current folder UID
- Reads the new `_folder.json` to get the new UID via `ParseFolderWithMetadata`
- If UIDs differ, emits synthetic `FileActionUpdated` for direct children (files and sub-folders) and tracks the old UID for cleanup

### `applyIncrementalChanges` (updated)
- Accepts `replacedFolderUIDs` map from the planning phase
- Calls `RemoveFolderFromTree(oldUID)` before `EnsureFolderPathExist` for affected `_folder.json` changes
- Handles directory `FileActionUpdated` (synthetic entries) by calling `EnsureFolderPathExist` to re-parent child folders
- Calls `deleteReplacedFolders` after the main loop to remove old folder K8s objects

### Safety
- Old folder is **not** deleted if the new folder creation fails (`HasDirPathFailedCreation` check)
- Only `FileActionUpdated` metadata files trigger UID change detection (not creates or deletes)
- Children already in the diff are not duplicated

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `planFolderMetadataChanges` (7 cases: no updates, no existing folder, same UID, UID change with children, dedup, child folder trailing slash, List error)
- [x] Unit tests for full UID change flow (3 cases: re-parent + delete, child folder re-parenting, creation failure safety)
- [x] Integration tests (3 cases: simple UID change, nested child folder, UID change + dashboard update in same commit)
- [x] All existing incremental sync tests pass
- [ ] CI passes


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Adds incremental-sync support for `_folder.json` UID (`metadata.name`) changes by planning a safe “replace folder UID” operation, re-parenting direct children, and cleaning up old folder objects after apply—matching the plan-then-apply approach used elsewhere. **Changes:** - Add `planFolderMetadataChanges` to detect UID changes in updated `_folder.json` and synthesize updates for direct children so they get re-parented. - Refactor incremental cleanup into `findOrphanedFolders` + `deleteFolders`, and include replaced-folder deletions in the same cleanup phase. - Add/extend unit and integration tests covering UID-change flows, safety conditions, and cleanup behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go | Implements planning for folder UID changes, synthetic child updates, and unified folder cleanup/deletion. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental_test.go | Adds unit tests for planning, UID-change application flow, and folder deletion ordering/safety. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental_hierarchical_test.go | Updates hierarchical error-handling expectations to align with new cleanup logic. | | pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/incremental_folder_metadata_test.go | Adds integration coverage for UID-change scenarios (simple, nested, and combined update). | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. commits.message:
Provisioning: Handle folder UID changes in incremental sync

When a _folder.json's metadata.name changes, incremental sync now
detects the UID change, creates the folder with the new UID, re-parents
all direct children (dashboards and sub-folders), and deletes the old
folder. This mirrors the plan-then-apply pattern used by full sync.

Key changes:
- planFolderMetadataChanges: scans the diff for _folder.json updates,
  compares the new UID against the existing Grafana folder, and emits
  synthetic FileActionUpdated entries for direct children that need
  re-parenting.
- applyIncrementalChanges: calls RemoveFolderFromTree(oldUID) before
  EnsureFolderPathExist for affected folders, handles directory
  FileActionUpdated for child folder re-parenting, and deletes old
  folder objects after the main loop.
- Integration and unit tests cover simple UID change, nested folders,
  combined UID + dashboard changes, and creation failure safety.

Made-with: Cursor

Refactor folder deletion into shared find + delete pattern

Extract findOrphanedFolders from cleanupOrphanedFolders and introduce a
generic deleteFolders function (sorted deepest-first) shared by both
orphaned and replaced folder cleanup. Move RemoveFolderFromTree into
the planning phase and remove replacedFolderUIDs from
applyIncrementalChanges to separate apply from cleanup concerns.

Made-with: Cursor

Move safety checks into deleteFolders for both orphaned and replaced folders

Consolidate HasDirPathFailedCreation and HasDirPathFailedDeletion checks
into deleteFolders so it is self-contained regardless of whether folders
come from orphan detection or UID replacement. findOrphanedFolders no
longer filters on progress state, keeping it a pure git-existence check.

Made-with: Cursor

Address review feedback: strengthen safety checks and improve observability

- Include PreviousPath in dedupe map so renamed children inside a
  UID-changed folder are not emitted as stale synthetic updates
- Add HasChildPathFailedCreation to block old-folder deletion when a
  descendant reparenting operation fails (not just the folder itself)
- Use NewFolderResult for synthetic directory re-parenting entries so
  group/kind appear correctly in job summaries
- Record a warning via progress.Record when ParseFolderWithMetadata
  fails in planFolderMetadataChanges instead of silently continuing
- Assert old UID folder is deleted (NotFound) in integration tests

Made-with: Cursor

Add unit test for HasChildPathFailedCreation in progress recorder

Made-with: Cursor

Block old-folder deletion when child resource updates fail

Track failed FileActionUpdated operations in the progress recorder so
deleteFolders skips cleanup when a synthetic child re-parenting update
fails, closing the gap where only creation/deletion failures were
guarded.

Made-with: Cursor

Track warning-level update failures in failedUpdates

Update operations classified as warnings (e.g. validation errors,
ownership conflicts) were not tracked, allowing old-folder deletion
even when child re-parenting did not fully succeed. Mirror the
existing failedDeletions pattern by also appending to failedUpdates
in the warning branch of Record.

Made-with: Cursor

Clean up planFolderMetadataChanges: remove progress.Record, use struct{}

- Replace progress.Record in the planning phase with a log warning to
  avoid skewing progress counts before SetTotal is called
- Remove the now-unused progress parameter from planFolderMetadataChanges
- Change oldUIDSet from map[string]string to map[string]struct{} since
  only membership is checked

Made-with: Cursor

Address review feedback: dedupe, error recording, and early safety check

- Stop tracking warning-level updates as failedUpdates — benign warnings
  from detectMissingFolderMetadata were incorrectly blocking old-folder
  cleanup during UID changes
- Mark parent directory as covered in pathsInDiff when a _folder.json is
  in the diff, preventing duplicate synthetic updates for child folders
- Record non-NotFound errors from readerRepo.Read in findOrphanedFolders
  on the span instead of silently skipping
- Filter replaced folders early: skip adding to foldersToDelete and
  record a warning when the replacement folder creation failed

Made-with: Cursor

Harden UID-change safety: error propagation, action-aware dedupe, warning tracking

- Return error from planFolderMetadataChanges when ParseFolderWithMetadata
  fails, preventing partial UID change without child re-parenting
- Track diff actions (not just presence) in pathsInDiff so directory
  Created/Deleted entries don't suppress synthetic re-parenting updates
- Re-add warning-level update tracking in failedUpdates so validation
  warnings from WriteResourceFromFile block old-folder cleanup
- Move detectMissingFolderMetadata after deleteFolders so its benign
  warnings don't interfere with HasChildPathFailedUpdate safety checks

Made-with: Cursor

Simplify dedupe guard and clarify skipped-folder result builder

- Skip synthetic updates for any path already in the diff regardless of
  action type, preventing conflicting operations when Created/Deleted
  directory entries coexist with synthetic Updated entries
- Replace WithAction(Deleted)+WithError+AsSkipped with explicit
  WithAction(Ignored)+WithWarning to make the builder chain
  self-documenting

Made-with: Cursor

Track failed renames in failedUpdates to block old-folder cleanup

FileActionRenamed was not tracked alongside FileActionUpdated, so a
failed child rename during a folder UID change would not prevent
deleteFolders from removing the old folder. Include Renamed in both
the error and warning branches of Record.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Gauge: Drop v1 gauge
body:
### Summary
- deletes v1 Gauge stuff
- moves v2 Gauge stuff up to the root of the repo

### Background

As we have GA'd the feature flag for `newGauge`, it would be good to delete the Gauge component from `@grafana/ui`. (We've researched - this component was never used by the community, and does not appear on the [Grafana plugin stats dashboard](https://ops.grafana-ops.net/d/dmb2o0xnz/imported-property-details?orgId=1&var-propertyName=Gauge&var-packageName=@grafana%2Fui&from=now-6h&to=now&timezone=utc), which indicates it's unused by signed community plugins at least.

This'll need to happen in multiple steps:
- drop v1 gauge code, which is the last usage of the `Gauge` 
- separate PR to make the breaking change to delete `Gauge` from `@grafana/ui`.
- (we'll also delete the `newGauge` flag before G13 since it does nothing after this.)

title: Provisioning: Update resource in place on rename when identity unchanged
body:
## Why

During incremental sync, `RenameResourceFile` always deleted and recreated a resource — even when the resource identity (`metadata.name`, API group, kind) was unchanged. This caused unnecessary delete+create cycles for simple file renames and moves (e.g., `git mv dashboard.json folder/dashboard.json`), destroying the Kubernetes object UID, `creationTimestamp`, and `generation` in the process. This made it impossible to distinguish a rename from an actual resource replacement.

Fixes grafana/git-ui-sync-project#998

## What

**Core behavior change in `RenameResourceFile`**: compare old and new resource identity (name + GVK group + kind) before acting:
- **Same identity** → skip the delete, fetch the existing object via a delete `DryRun` (to get ownership validation and the old folder annotation for free), then perform an in-place update through `writeResourceFromParsed`.
- **Different identity** → fall back to the previous delete + create behavior to avoid orphans.

**New `ParsedResource` methods** to centralize repeated patterns:
- `SameIdentity(other)` — compares `metadata.name`, GVK group, and GVK kind (version intentionally excluded).
- `ExistingFolder()` — extracts `grafana.app/folder` from the `Existing` object, replacing 3 duplicate `MetaAccessor` + `GetFolder` call sites.

**Incremental sync filter**: extend the `IsFolderMetadataFile` guard to also cover `FileActionRenamed`, so `_folder.json` renames are routed through `applyFolderMetadataUpdate` / `EnsureFolderPathExist` instead of hitting `RenameResourceFile`.

**Refactored `WriteResourceFromFile`**: extracted `writeResourceFromParsed` helper so `RenameResourceFile` can reuse an already-parsed `ParsedResource` without a redundant repo read + parse on every rename.

## How

### `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/resources.go`
- `RenameResourceFile` now parses both old and new files, compares identity via `SameIdentity`, and branches:
  - Same identity: `oldParsed.Action = Delete` → `oldParsed.DryRun(ctx)` (populates `Existing` with ownership check) → `writeResourceFromParsed(newParsed)`.
  - Different identity: `oldParsed.Action = Delete` → `oldParsed.Run(ctx)` (actual delete) → `writeResourceFromParsed(newParsed)`.
- `RemoveResourceFromFile` simplified to use `parsed.ExistingFolder()`.
- `writeResourceFromParsed` extracted from `WriteResourceFromFile` to accept an already-parsed resource.

### `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/parser.go`
- Added `SameIdentity`, `ExistingFolder` methods on `ParsedResource`.

### `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go`
- Extended the `_folder.json` filter at the top of the change loop to include `FileActionRenamed`, preventing folder metadata files from reaching `RenameResourceFile`.

### Tests

**Unit tests** (`resources_remove_test.go`, `parser_test.go`):
- `TestRenameResourceFile`: same name (no delete, update in place), different name (delete then create), parse errors, read errors, resource not found in Grafana.
- `TestSameIdentity`: name/group/kind match/mismatch, version-only diff, symmetry.
- `TestExistingFolder`: nil Existing, missing annotation, populated annotation, empty annotations map.
- `TestDryRunDeletePopulatesExisting`: found, NotFound, non-NotFound error, nil client, integration with `ExistingFolder`.

**Integration tests** (`incremental_test.go`, `incremental_folder_metadata_test.go`, `full_sync_move_test.go`):
- Added `SnapshotObject` + `RequireUpdatedInPlace` assertions to all 7 rename/move tests in `incremental_test.go` to verify K8s UID preservation.
- Changed 5 `RequireRecreated` → `RequireUpdatedInPlace` in `incremental_folder_metadata_test.go`, removing their `FIXME` comments.
- Added dashboard UID preservation checks to `full_sync_move_test.go`.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests cover all `RenameResourceFile` branches and new `ParsedResource` methods
- [x] All provisioning unit tests pass (`go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/... -short`)
- [ ] Incremental sync integration tests pass (verify K8s UID preservation on rename/move)
- [ ] Full sync integration tests pass (verify dashboard UIDs preserved after folder moves)

commits.message:
Provisioning: Update resource in place on rename when identity unchanged

RenameResourceFile previously always deleted and recreated the resource,
even when the resource identity (metadata.name) stayed the same. This
caused an unnecessary delete+create cycle during incremental sync for
file moves/renames.

Now the function compares the old and new resource names before acting:
- Same name: skips the delete and calls WriteResourceFromFile, which
  performs an in-place update via ParsedResource.Run().
- Different name: falls back to the previous delete+create behaviour.

The old folder annotation is resolved upfront so orphaned-folder cleanup
still works correctly in both paths.

Fixes grafana/git-ui-sync-project#998

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Replace ad-hoc test assertions with shared helpers

- Add RequireRepoFile/RequireNoRepoFile helpers to common/testing.go
- Replace manual assertions in movejob_test.go with RequireDashboards
  to verify dashboard UID preservation after moves
- Replace manual assertions in deletejob_test.go with RequireDashboardCount
  and repo file helpers
- Add RequireDashboards to full_sync_move_test.go to verify dashboard UIDs
  are preserved (not recreated) after folder moves with metadata

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Use Run's Existing field for old folder instead of extra GET

Replace the standalone existingFolder helper with the same pattern used
by RemoveResourceFromFile: read the folder annotation from
oldParsed.Existing which is populated by Run() during the delete path,
avoiding a redundant Client.Get call.

Made-with: Cursor

Revert movejob and deletejob test changes

These FIXME cleanups are out of scope for this PR.

Made-with: Cursor

Revert common/testing.go helper additions

RequireRepoFile/RequireNoRepoFile helpers are out of scope for this PR.

Made-with: Cursor

Inline folderFromExisting and assert in-place updates in incremental tests

Remove the folderFromExisting helper, inlining the logic directly in
RenameResourceFile. Fix 5 FIXME blocks in incremental_folder_metadata_test.go
by switching RequireRecreated to RequireUpdatedInPlace now that
RenameResourceFile updates in place. Add SnapshotObject +
RequireUpdatedInPlace assertions to all 7 rename/move tests in
incremental_test.go to verify K8s UID preservation.

Made-with: Cursor

Fix _folder.json sourcePath to use directory path instead of file path

The parser was setting the sourcePath annotation to the full file path
(e.g. new-parent/_folder.json) for folder metadata files, but the folder
system (EnsureFolderPathExist) uses the directory path (e.g. new-parent).
This mismatch caused sourcePath assertions to fail in incremental sync
tests for folder renames with metadata.

For _folder.json files, strip the filename and use safepath.Dir to store
the directory path, matching the convention used everywhere else.

Made-with: Cursor

Filter _folder.json renames in incremental sync instead of parser

Revert the parser.go sourcePath change — _folder.json files should
never reach RenameResourceFile. The real fix is in the incremental sync
handler: extend the IsFolderMetadataFile filter to also cover
FileActionRenamed, routing it through applyFolderMetadataUpdate
(EnsureFolderPathExist) like Created/Updated already are. The directory
rename event already handles the folder via RenameFolderPath.

Made-with: Cursor

Treat GVK change as identity change and use provisioning identity in RenameResourceFile

Extend the identity comparison to also check GVK group and kind, not
just metadata.name. A rename that changes the resource kind should
delete the old-kind resource to avoid orphans.

Use identity.WithProvisioningIdentity for the Client.Get call in the
same-name path, matching how Run/DryRun obtain existing resources.

Made-with: Cursor

Reuse parsed resource in RenameResourceFile and handle Get errors

Extract writeResourceFromParsed from WriteResourceFromFile so
RenameResourceFile can pass newParsed directly, eliminating the
duplicate repo read + parse on every rename.

In the same-name path, propagate non-NotFound errors from Client.Get
instead of silently dropping them, so orphan-folder cleanup is not
skipped when the GET fails for auth or connectivity reasons.

Update unit tests to reflect the single-parse flow.

Made-with: Cursor

Add ParsedResource.FetchExisting to encapsulate existing resource lookup

Move the provisioning-identity-aware GET logic into a FetchExisting
method on ParsedResource, matching the pattern used by DryRun and Run.
FetchExisting is a no-op when Existing is already populated, treats
NotFound as non-error (Existing stays nil), and propagates real errors.

RenameResourceFile now calls oldParsed.FetchExisting(ctx) instead of
manually setting up the identity context and calling Client.Get,
removing that concern from resources.go entirely.

Made-with: Cursor

Add unit tests for ParsedResource.FetchExisting

Cover all branches: no-op when Existing already set, no-op when Client
is nil, successful fetch populates Existing, NotFound leaves Existing
nil without error, and non-NotFound errors are propagated.

Made-with: Cursor

Add ParsedResource.SameIdentity for resource identity comparison

Encapsulate the name + group + kind comparison into SameIdentity so
RenameResourceFile reads as: if !oldParsed.SameIdentity(newParsed).
Version is intentionally excluded since a version change does not
constitute a different resource identity.

Made-with: Cursor

Replace FetchExisting with ResourceActionRead in Run

Introduce ResourceActionRead as a first-class action handled by
ParsedResource.Run, replacing the standalone FetchExisting method.
This makes Read consistent with Delete — both go through Run,
getting provisioning identity and tracing for free.

Also adds ExistingFolder() to ParsedResource to eliminate repeated
MetaAccessor+GetFolder patterns across RenameResourceFile and
RemoveResourceFromFile.

Moves ParsedResource method tests (SameIdentity, ExistingFolder,
RunRead) from resources_remove_test.go to parser_test.go where
the methods are defined, and adds new edge-case coverage.

Made-with: Cursor

Fix goimports formatting in parser.go and resources_remove_test.go

Made-with: Cursor

Use delete DryRun instead of ResourceActionRead to fetch existing resource

Replace the custom ResourceActionRead action with a delete DryRun to
populate Existing in the same-identity rename path. DryRun with Delete
already fetches the existing resource, checks ownership, and handles
NotFound gracefully — no need for a separate action type.

This removes ResourceActionRead from the enum and the Read case from
Run(), keeping the action surface minimal.

Made-with: Cursor

Fix misleading rename test to actually exercise the NotFound path

The "folder name empty when resource does not exist" test was stubbing
the new file parse to error, causing RenameResourceFile to return
before reaching the DryRun/identity check. The Get mock was dead code.

Now both parses succeed (same name → same-identity path), Get returns
NotFound (resource absent in Grafana), and the write fails (no client).
This properly validates that folderName is empty when Existing is nil
and that Delete is never called.

Made-with: Cursor

Revert generated openapi file to match main

The zz_generated.openapi.go file was inadvertently modified when
ResourceActionRead was temporarily added and then removed. Restore
the file to its main branch state.

Made-with: Cursor

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into rjimenez/rename-resource-update-in-place

Made-with: Cursor

# Conflicts:
#	pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go
#	pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/full_sync_move_test.go

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into rjimenez/rename-resource-update-in-place

Made-with: Cursor

# Conflicts:
#	pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go

Fix test for renamed _folder.json now being handled by metadata split

The renamed _folder.json is now split out of the regular diff by
isHandledFolderMetadataChange rather than left as-is. Update the test
to mock List() and expect an empty diff since the rename is a no-op
in rewriteMetadataChange.

Made-with: Cursor

Add nil guard to SameIdentity and clarify DryRun usage comment

Handle nil receiver and nil argument in ParsedResource.SameIdentity to
prevent panics. Add a comment explaining why delete dry-run is used to
populate the existing object for identity comparison.

Made-with: Cursor

title: StatsPicker: Convert to Combobox
body:
### Summary

- updated StatsPicker to use Combobox and MultiCombobox instead of Select
- adds unit tests for StatsPicker
- **breaking** deleted `inputId` prop in favor of a matching `id` prop for Combobox.
- **breaking** deletes the `className` prop from `StatsPicker` in favor of passing `Combobox` and `MultiCombobox`'s props through, allowing customization that way.

According to our [plugin stat dashboards](https://ops.grafana-ops.net/d/dmb2o0xnz/imported-property-details?orgId=1&var-propertyName=StatsPicker&var-packageName=@grafana%2Fui&from=now-6h&to=now&timezone=utc), StatsPicker is not used by any plugins, so we should be safe to make the breaking prop changes. _Grafana 13 is probably a good time to do it since that's the case._

### Background and demo

While testing Presets, we noticed the Calculation Select in Value options had an annoying flickering when we tested with high-refresh live data.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/702ee785-f596-4bfa-9b66-76038377c6f9

As an attempt at fixing it, I thought I'd just try converting the StatsPicker to a Combobox/MultiCombobox. It fixes the issue and is also a nice upgrade for anything using StatsPicker.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fabc7f74-d4da-42b9-a071-2de02011754c

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a2c64260-3983-485c-b005-a93500097675

I opted to go a step further and set up the element to pass props through to Combobox and MultiCombobox, which makes props like `data-testid` or setting custom widths much simpler.
author: fastfrwrd

reviews.body: Can verify this fixes the bug locally and is generally a **much** nicer UX when multiple select is used, and now clicking on the label actually displays the select options instead of just focusing the input. ### legend > values is missing a placeholder: #### main: #### fastfrwrd/stats-picker-combobox: ### Keyboard a11y: These are differences, not necessarily bugs. #### main: Pressing "spacebar" opens the options. Pressing spacebar selects the option. #### fastfrwrd/stats-picker-combobox: * Initially I thought there was mo way to display the options besides hitting spacebar and then deleting the space, but you can hit the "down arrow" key and then pressing "enter" * * When in a multi-select, the initially focused element after this spacebar hack is the last element in the list — this probably isn't a bug, but it will likely frustrate users that have gotten used to the existing behavior of using spacebar on the old select component. * Pressing spacebar does not select the option, it inserts a space Weird bug double focus bug (both main/this branch): On this branch, if you add a text search and then delete it, you get multiple options with the focus styles, which is pretty confusing, but probably a bug in the base combobox component and not something to be addressed on this branch. It also doesn't reproduce every time, which makes it extra weirld. #### This branch #### main

only found some nits in the code, feel free to disregard/fix at your discretion. 
Only request is to fix the placeholder before merge

title: Provisioning: handle folder metadata creation in incremental pull
body:
## What

This PR teaches incremental git sync to reconcile folder metadata creation.

When `_folder.json` is added to an existing folder, incremental sync now treats that change as a folder transition rather than a plain file write. The diff is rewritten into folder-aware work so the sync can:
- replay the folder path as a folder change
- replay direct children that need to follow the folder transition
- avoid duplicating work when the same commit already contains real child changes or renames
- delete the superseded path-based folder after the new metadata-backed folder is in place

The result is that a folder can move from its path-derived UID to the stable UID declared in `_folder.json` during incremental sync, with existing children following in the same run.

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1003

## Why

Before this change, adding `_folder.json` during incremental sync did not converge cleanly.

Incremental sync processed the metadata file as a normal file change, but the real effect is a folder identity change. That could leave:
- the old path-based folder behind
- dashboards or child folders still attached to the old UID
- interference with other file changes in the same commit

This PR makes incremental sync reason about folder metadata creation at the folder level, so the system converges immediately instead of relying on a later full sync.

## Diff overview

### Folder-metadata diff building
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff.go`
  - introduces a dedicated builder for incremental folder-metadata creation
  - rewrites handled `_folder.json` creations into synthetic folder and direct-child changes
  - reserves real diff paths, including rename sources, so synthetic replay does not duplicate or conflict with real changes
  - tracks folders that must be removed after the metadata-backed folder has been created

### Incremental sync orchestration
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go`
  - runs the folder-metadata diff builder before progress totals are computed
  - removes replaced folders from the in-memory tree before replaying synthetic changes
  - applies the rewritten diff through the normal incremental flow
  - deletes replaced folders deepest-first after apply

### Unit and integration coverage
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/folder_metadata_incremental_diff_test.go`
  - covers the diff-builder behavior directly, including nested metadata creation and rename interactions

- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental_test.go`
  - updates incremental-sync unit coverage to match the new builder-driven flow

- `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/incremental_folder_metadata_test.go`
  - expands end-to-end incremental coverage for `_folder.json` creation, including stable UID adoption, child re-parenting, and rename cases

### Shared test helpers
- `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/common/testing.go`
  - extracts reusable folder/dashboard assertions used by incremental and full-sync provisioning tests

- `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/full_sync_move_test.go`
- `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/permissions_move_test.go`
- `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/sync_folder_metadata_test.go`
  - updated to use the shared helpers above; no production behavior change in these files

## Scope

This PR covers incremental handling for `_folder.json` creation. It does not change metadata deletion or rename behavior.

## Testing

- `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...`
- `go test -tags=enterprise ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/...`

title: Provisioning: Keep folder up-to-date with metadata in incremental pull
body:
## What

Route `_folder.json` changes through `EnsureFolderPathExist` during incremental sync instead of `WriteResourceFromFile`. Both created and updated `_folder.json` files are intercepted. Deleted `_folder.json` files continue through the existing `RemoveResourceFromFile` path.

## Why

When `_folder.json` is modified (e.g. `spec.title` changes), incremental sync routed it through `WriteResourceFromFile`. This was the wrong path because `WriteResourceFromFile` calls `EnsureFolderPathExist` for the **grandparent** directory — the parent of the folder — not the folder itself. The folder's own `EnsureFolderExists` update logic was never invoked, so title changes were silently ignored.

Full sync already handles this correctly by converting `_folder.json` hash changes into parent-directory updates and routing them through `RemoveFolderFromTree` + `EnsureFolderPathExist`.

## How

In `applyIncrementalChanges`, **before** the quota acquisition gate, check if the changed file is a `_folder.json` with `folderMetadataEnabled` and the action is `FileActionCreated` or `FileActionUpdated`:

- Compute the folder directory via `safepath.Dir(change.Path)` and call `EnsureFolderPathExist` for it
- This triggers `ParseFolderWithMetadata` → detects the metadata hash change → `EnsureFolderExists` updates title, checksum, and annotations
- Record the result and `continue`, bypassing quota acquisition (metadata files don't create dashboard/alert resources) and `WriteResourceFromFile`
- Deleted `_folder.json` files are not intercepted — they fall through to `RemoveResourceFromFile` as before

The folder metadata handling is extracted into `applyFolderMetadataUpdate` to keep the main loop readable. This follows the same interception pattern used for directory renames in #120717.

## Test plan

- [x] All existing unit tests pass
- [x] 4 new unit tests: updated `_folder.json` routes to `EnsureFolderPathExist`, created routes to `EnsureFolderPathExist`, disabled flag falls through to `WriteResourceFromFile`, error recording
- [x] 3 integration tests pass end-to-end: simple title update, nested folder title update, title + dashboard update in same commit


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Add integration tests for incremental folder title updates

Add tests that verify incremental sync propagates title changes from
_folder.json to Grafana folders. These tests will pass once the
companion folder title update logic lands.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Route _folder.json changes through folder manager in incremental sync

When _folder.json is created or updated during incremental sync, route
the change through EnsureFolderPathExist instead of WriteResourceFromFile.
This ensures folder title updates, metadata hash changes, and annotations
are properly reconciled — matching how full sync already handles
_folder.json changes.

The fix intercepts IsFolderMetadataFile paths when folderMetadataEnabled
is true, computes the folder directory via safepath.Dir, and delegates
to the folder manager. When the flag is disabled, _folder.json files
continue through the existing WriteResourceFromFile path.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Ignore created _folder.json in incremental sync, only handle updates

Created _folder.json files are already handled when WriteResourceFromFile
processes the accompanying resources (EnsureFolderPathExist runs for the
parent directory). Only updated _folder.json files need explicit routing
through EnsureFolderPathExist to propagate title changes.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add TODO for created _folder.json reconciliation

Made-with: Cursor

Merge origin/main and resolve conflict in incremental_folder_metadata_test.go

Keep both the title update tests (this branch) and the graceful folder
rename tests (from PR #120717) since they are independent test functions.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix gofmt formatting in integration test file

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Extract applyFolderMetadataUpdate to reduce cyclomatic complexity

The _folder.json interception pushed applyIncrementalChanges to 31
cyclomatic complexity (limit: 30). Extract the folder metadata handling
into a dedicated function that returns (name, error).

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Route created _folder.json through EnsureFolderPathExist

Don't skip created _folder.json — route it through EnsureFolderPathExist
like updated files. Without this, a _folder.json committed alone (before
any dashboards) would never create the folder during incremental sync.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Handle _folder.json before quota check in incremental sync

Move the _folder.json interception above the quota acquisition gate.
Folder metadata files don't create dashboard/alert resources, so they
should not consume quota tokens. Previously, a created _folder.json
would acquire a token via TryAcquire but never release it (since it
breaks out without calling WriteResourceFromFile), causing false
"quota exceeded" errors for subsequent resource creations in the
same sync.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Scope _folder.json interception to created/updated actions

Without the action guard, deleted _folder.json files would also match
IsFolderMetadataFile and get routed to EnsureFolderPathExist instead of
RemoveResourceFromFile. Restrict the interception to FileActionCreated
and FileActionUpdated so deletions continue through the existing path.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add nolint:gocyclo to applyIncrementalChanges

The function was already at the complexity limit before the _folder.json
interception. Extracting further would hurt readability since the
remaining branches share loop state (affectedFolders, resultBuilder,
quotaTracker). Suppress the lint instead.

title: Alerting: Support all label matcher operators on /api/v1/rules/history
body:
**What is this feature?**

Extends the `/api/v1/rules/history` endpoint and its frontend consumers to support all four PromQL label matcher operators: `=`, `!=`, `=~`, `!~`.

Previously only exact equality (\`=\`) was supported for label filtering — non-equality matchers were silently dropped on the frontend before being sent to the backend.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Users querying alert state history often need to filter by regex patterns (e.g. \`severity=~"crit.*"\`) or exclude certain label values (e.g. \`env!="dev"\`). Without this, the only workaround was client-side filtering after fetching a potentially large result set, which is inefficient and doesn't reduce the data transferred from the backend.

**Who is this feature for?**

Grafana users and operators who use the central alert history view or query the history API directly, particularly those managing large numbers of alert instances and needing precise label-based filtering.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes #

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

- The annotation backend still ignores label filters (pre-existing behaviour, unrelated to this change).
- The \`labels_<key>=<value>\` URL param format is preserved for backward compatibility with existing API clients.
- Client-side filtering in \`useRuleHistoryRecords\` / \`groupDataFramesByTimeAndFilterByLabels\` is kept in place as defence-in-depth but is now redundant for label matching.

> **Why frontend and backend changes are in the same PR** (\`no-check-mt-service-compatibility\`): The frontend change switches from the \`labels_<key>=<value>\` param format to the new \`matchers\` param, which only exists after the backend change lands. These changes are backward compatible in both directions — the backend still accepts the old \`labels_\` params, and the frontend gracefully omits \`matchers\` when the filter is empty — so they can be deployed independently without breakage. However, keeping them together simplifies review and ensures the feature works end-to-end from a single PR.

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Unified Storage: defer search index build from module init to starting()
body:
## Summary

- On search-api pod restart, `buildIndexes()` runs during dskit module init, before the ring service has started. The ring is in `New` state, so `OwnsIndex` returns an error for every tenant. The fail-open fallback then builds indexes for ALL tenants instead of only the owned shard, causing a CPU spike on every pod restart.
- Move the `Init()` call (which triggers `buildIndexes`) from the server constructors to the `starting()` lifecycle callback, where the ring is guaranteed to be `Running`.
- Switch to `ACTIVE` only after initial indexing completes, so other ring members don't route queries to this instance before its indexes are ready. The instance stays in `JOINING` during indexing — `JOINING` is included in `searchOwnerRead` op, so `OwnsIndex` checks still work correctly.

**Changes:**
- Add `NewUninitializedSearchServer` / `NewUninitializedResourceServer` in resource and sql packages that create servers without calling `Init`
- Refactor existing constructors to delegate to the uninitialized variants + immediate `Init`
- Add `Init(ctx context.Context) error` to the `SearchServer` interface
- In `service.starting()`: wait for `JOINING` → build indexes → switch to `ACTIVE`
- Remove unused `sql.NewSearchServer`

**Safety:**
- `server.Init()` has `sync.Once` guard — safe if called multiple times
- gRPC handlers registered during module init don't need indexes — only need the server object
- dskit health checks report `NOT_SERVING` until `starting()` completes, so no traffic arrives before indexes are built
- Non-sharded path works correctly: `OwnsIndex` returns `true` when `searchRing == nil`
- All existing callers of `NewSearchServer`/`NewResourceServer` are unchanged — they still Init immediately

Fixes: grafana/search-and-storage-team#713

## Test plan

- [x] Existing tests pass: `go test ./pkg/storage/unified/resource/...` and `go test ./pkg/storage/unified/sql/...`
- [x] With sharding enabled, the warn log `"failed to check index ownership, building index"` with `error="ring is not Running: New"` should no longer appear on startup
- [x] Non-sharded path: verify Init still runs in `starting()` when `EnableSharding == false`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: pstibrany

reviews.body: LGTM, thank you.

commits.message:
Unified Storage: defer search index build from module init to starting()

On search-api pod restart, buildIndexes() was running during dskit module
init, before the ring service had started. The ring was in New state, so
OwnsIndex returned an error for every tenant. The fail-open fallback then
built indexes for ALL tenants, defeating sharding and causing a ~2 min CPU
spike on every pod restart.

Move the Init() call (which triggers buildIndexes) from the server
constructors to the starting() lifecycle callback, where the ring is
guaranteed to be Running. This ensures ownership checks work correctly
and only owned tenant indexes are built.

Changes:
- Add NewUninitializedSearchServer/NewUninitializedResourceServer that
  create servers without calling Init
- Refactor existing constructors to delegate to the uninitialized variants
- Add Init to the SearchServer interface
- Call Init in service.starting() after the ring reaches ACTIVE
- Remove unused sql.NewSearchServer

Fixes: grafana/search-and-storage-team#713

Switch to ACTIVE only after initial indexing completes

Move the ACTIVE state transition to after Init() so that other ring
members don't route queries to this instance until its indexes are
built. The instance stays in JOINING during indexing, which is included
in searchOwnerRead op so OwnsIndex checks still work correctly.

title: feat(prometheus): add Query with Assistant button to Prometheus query
body:
**What is this feature?**
Adds a "Query with Assistant" button to the Prometheus query editor, matching the existing behavior in the Tempo query editor. The button appears in the editor header when the queryWithAssistant feature toggle is enabled and the user is in Explore, Dashboard, or Panel Editor contexts. The generic header-level button for Prometheus is removed since the editor now has its own.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Users working with Prometheus metrics often need help constructing or understanding PromQL queries. By surfacing the Grafana Assistant directly inside the Prometheus query editor, users can get AI-assisted query help without leaving their current context — the same experience already available to Tempo users.

**Who is this feature for?**
Grafana users (Cloud and OSS) who use Prometheus as a data source and want AI-assisted help writing or understanding PromQL queries in Explore, dashboards, or the panel editor.

title: Provisioning: Add MockGrafanaLiveSrv for watch stream testing and fix useCallback stability
body:
**What is this feature?**

- Add a controllable `MockGrafanaLiveSrv` test utility that lets provisioning tests emit watch events and errors via RxJS Subjects, enabling full integration testing of the watch data flow (RTK Query → `onCacheEntryAdded` → LiveChannel → UI). 
- Wrap `startSynchronization`, `retryJob`, and `createSyncJob` in `useCallback` to fix a render stability issue.

**Why do we need this feature?**

The existing test mock used `NEVER` (an Observable that never emits), making it impossible to test how UI components react to real-time watch updates — job status transitions, repository reconciliation lifecycle, and error/warning scenarios. The missing `useCallback` wrappers caused an infinite re-render loop when `JobContent`'s `useEffect` fired on every render due to unstable function references.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/984

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

- `MockGrafanaLiveSrv` implements `GrafanaLiveSrv` with RxJS Subjects keyed by channel address, with `emitWatchEvent()` and `emitWatchError()` helpers
- Added missing `listJobsHandler` to MSW handlers — without it, `onCacheEntryAdded` watch subscriptions never activated
author: Clarity-89

reviews.body: LGTM! Also tested locally 🎉

commits.message:
Provisioning: Add MockGrafanaLiveSrv for watch stream testing and fix useCallback stability

Add a controllable mock for GrafanaLiveSrv that lets tests emit watch events
and errors via RxJS Subjects, enabling integration tests for the full
watch data flow (RTK Query → onCacheEntryAdded → LiveChannel → UI).

- Add MockGrafanaLiveSrv with emitWatchEvent/emitWatchError helpers
- Add missing listJobsHandler to MSW handlers (watch subscriptions need it)
- Wrap startSynchronization, retryJob, createSyncJob in useCallback to fix
  infinite re-render loop (git-ui-sync-project#984)
- Add reconciliation lifecycle tests (loading → unhealthy → healthy)
- Add synchronization step tests (success, warning, error, stream error, retry)
- Remove obsolete "should progress through first 3 steps" test (subsumed)

Provisioning: Fix type errors in test factories and MockGrafanaLiveSrv

Use type assertions in test factory functions to handle the mismatch
between generated API types (all-optional fields) and strict Resource types.
Loosen emitWatchEvent signature to accept generated types directly.

Provisioning: Address PR review feedback — shared factories, precise channel matching, ByRole queries

- Extract shared createJob/createRepository factories into mocks/factories.ts
- Fix emitWatchEvent/emitWatchError channel matching to use /v0alpha1/{resource} for precision
- Replace getByPlaceholderText URL queries with getByRole('textbox') per testing guidelines
- Add Jest worker isolation comment on module-level MockGrafanaLiveSrv singleton

title: Stars: Use StarToolbarButton in legacy DashNav and remove dead star code
body:
**What is this feature?**

Replaces the star button in the legacy `DashNav` with the shared `StarToolbarButton` component (already used by the scene path), and removes dead star-related code from `DashboardScene` and `DashboardSrv`.

**Why do we need this feature?**

When `kubernetesDashboards` is enabled, the K8s DTO endpoint does not return `isStarred` in `DashboardAccess`, so `meta.isStarred` is always `undefined`. This caused the legacy `DashNav` star button to always show "Mark as favorite" even for already-starred dashboards. `StarToolbarButton` queries star state independently via `useStarredItems()`, so it works correctly regardless of how the dashboard was loaded.

fixing wrong star state when kubernetesDashboards is enabled. Remove

DashboardSrv.

Stars: Remove orphaned translation keys and add clarifying comment

title: Short URL: add unified storage migration phase timing logs
body:
Short URL unified storage migration currently logs overall progress, but not how long the legacy read, conversion, and bulk write phases take. This change adds explicit timing log lines for each phase so we can see where time is spent while the migration holds the legacy `short_url` table lock. The migrator now reads one extra row per batch so it can detect the final batch cleanly and emit each phase-complete log exactly once, and the test covers both single-batch and multi-batch runs.

## Testing
- `go test ./pkg/registry/apps/shorturl/migrator`

title: CI: Remove profiled mode from SQLite integration tests
body:
## Summary
- Remove the `profiled` shard from both OSS SQLite (4+1 → 4 shards) and Enterprise SQLite (16+1 → 16 shards) integration test matrices
- Remove the `SKIP_PACKAGES` / `grep -Ev` logic that excluded `pkg/tests/apis/folder` and `pkg/tests/apis/dashboard` from regular shards
- Delete the "Run profiled tests" and "Output test profiles and traces" steps from both jobs

These packages now run in normal shards like every other integration test package. The profiled mode added complexity (skip lists, separate step with CPU/memory/trace profiling, artifact upload) but the profiling data was only uploaded on test failures, making it a rarely-used debugging aid.

## Test plan
- [x] CI passes — the previously-profiled packages (`pkg/tests/apis/folder`, `pkg/tests/apis/dashboard`) should appear in normal shard output
- [x] Verify no packages are lost: `pkgs-with-tests-named.sh -b TestIntegration` still includes both packages, and they are no longer filtered out
author: pstibrany

commits.message:
CI: Remove profiled mode from SQLite integration tests

The profiled shard ran pkg/tests/apis/folder and pkg/tests/apis/dashboard
with CPU/memory profiling while excluding them from regular shards. This
complexity is no longer needed — these packages now run in normal shards
like every other package.

title: Chore: Prepare @grafana/sql for npm publishing
body:
**What is this feature?**

For our externalization efforts this is needed as a published package. This PR introduces necessary changes to make this package published.

Makes the @grafana/sql package publishable to npm. It was previously internal-only. This package provides shared UI components used by sql-based observability datasource plugins (mysql, postgres, mssql, influxdb (partially)).

**Verification**
- Build 
```
yarn nx run @grafana/sql:build
```
- Pack
```
mkdir -p npm-artifacts
cd packages/grafana-sql && yarn pack --out '../../npm-artifacts/%s-%v.tgz'
```
- Validate
```
./scripts/validate-npm-packages.sh
```
- Dry-run
```
npm publish ./npm-artifacts/grafana-sql-*.tgz --dry-run --tag pre
```

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.

title: Provisioning: Add releaseResources and deleteResources job actions for orphan cleanup
body:
## Summary

- Adds two new job actions (`releaseResources` and `deleteResources`) for cleaning up orphaned resources when a repository is deleted but its finalizers fail
- `releaseResources` removes ownership annotations, leaving resources in Grafana as unmanaged; `deleteResources` deletes all resources managed by the missing/terminating repository
- Uses inverted validation: these actions are only allowed when the target repository does NOT exist or is stuck in `Terminating` state
- Extracts shared cleanup logic (patch generation, resource sorting) from finalizers into a reusable utility to avoid duplication
- Includes codegen updates for Go types, OpenAPI snapshot, and frontend TypeScript API clients

## Implementation details

**New job actions** (`apps/provisioning/pkg/apis/provisioning/v0alpha1/jobs.go`):
- `JobActionReleaseResources` — removes `grafana.app/managedBy` and `grafana.app/managerId` annotations
- `JobActionDeleteResources` — deletes all managed resources (children before parents)

**Jobs connector** (`pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs.go`):
- `handleOrphanCleanupJob` with inverted repo validation — rejects if repo exists and is healthy, allows if missing or terminating
- Requires admin privileges for cleanup actions

**Cleanup worker** (`pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/cleanup/worker.go`):
- New `Worker` implementing the jobs `Worker` interface
- Lists resources via `ResourceLister`, creates dynamic clients via `ClientFactory`
- Handles both release (JSON patch) and delete operations with per-resource error tracking

**Job driver** (`pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/driver.go`):
- `isRepoOptionalAction` helper allows cleanup jobs to proceed with `nil` repository
- Bypasses the "repo marked for deletion — skip" early return for cleanup actions

**Shared utilities** (`pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/cleanup.go`):
- `GetReleasePatch`, `EscapePatchString`, `SortResourceListForDeletion` extracted from finalizers

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `isOrphanCleanupAction` helper (`jobs_test.go`)
- [x] Unit tests for `isRepoOptionalAction` helper (`driver_test.go`)
- [x] Unit tests for cleanup worker — release and delete paths (`cleanup/worker_test.go`)
- [x] Existing finalizer tests updated to use shared utilities (`finalizers_test.go`)
- [ ] Manual testing: trigger `releaseResources` job for a non-existent repository
- [ ] Manual testing: trigger `deleteResources` job for a terminating repository
- [ ] Manual testing: verify healthy repository rejects cleanup job with 409 Conflict


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

## Pull request overview

This PR adds two new provisioning job actions—`releaseResources` and `deleteResources`—to clean up orphaned Grafana resources when a repository has been deleted (or is stuck terminating) and finalizers cannot complete. It also extracts shared cleanup utilities from finalizers for reuse, and updates generated OpenAPI/client artifacts.

**Changes:**
- Add orphan-cleanup job actions with “inverted” repo validation (allowed only when repo is missing or terminating) and admin-only creation.
- Introduce new cleanup workers for releasing ownership annotations or deleting managed resources (with shared sorting/patch helpers).
- Update tests, OpenAPI snapshots, and generated TypeScript API clients to reflect the new actions.
