commits.message:
Provisioning: Add releaseResources and deleteResources job actions for orphan cleanup

When a repository is deleted but its finalizers fail, managed resources
(dashboards, folders, etc.) become orphaned — still annotated as managed
but with no owning repository. This adds two new job actions to handle
manual cleanup of these orphaned resources:

- `releaseResources`: removes ownership annotations, leaving resources
  in Grafana as unmanaged
- `deleteResources`: deletes all resources that were managed by the
  missing/terminating repository

These actions use inverted validation: they are only allowed when the
target repository does NOT exist or is stuck in Terminating state.
The job driver supports repo-optional processing so workers receive
a nil repository when it no longer exists.

Shared cleanup logic (patch generation, resource sorting) is extracted
from finalizers into a reusable utility to avoid duplication.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Move and expand cleanup utility tests

Move TestSortResourceListForDeletion from controller/finalizers_test.go
to resources/cleanup_test.go where the implementation now lives. Add
tests for EscapePatchString and GetReleasePatch, plus additional edge
cases for the sort function (empty list, single item, non-folders only).

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Split cleanup worker into releaseresources and deleteresources packages

Replace the single jobs/cleanup package with two focused packages:
- jobs/releaseresources: handles JobActionReleaseResources (patch annotations)
- jobs/deleteresources: handles JobActionDeleteResources (delete resources)

Each worker is single-responsibility with its own tests. Wired as
two separate workers in register.go.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add integration tests for releaseResources and deleteResources jobs

Add integration tests verifying authorization and inverted validation:
- Admin can create both job types for a nonexistent repository (202)
- Editor and viewer are rejected with 403 Forbidden
- Both job types are rejected with 409 Conflict when the repository
  exists and is healthy

Also fix the job admission validator to recognize the two new actions
(releaseResources, deleteResources) and add corresponding unit tests.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Register releaseresources and deleteresources workers in job operator

The workers were wired in register.go (in-process API server) but
missing from the standalone job operator in jobs_operator.go. This
adds both workers to setupWorkers so the operator mode also supports
orphan cleanup jobs.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Consolidate IsOrphanCleanupAction into jobs package

Replace the two duplicate unexported helpers (isOrphanCleanupAction in
jobs.go and isRepoOptionalAction in driver.go) with a single exported
jobs.IsOrphanCleanupAction function in queue.go. Move the test to
queue_test.go alongside the function.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Harden orphan cleanup workers with repo validation, parallelism, and phases

- Add ValidateRepoForCleanup to reject cleanup if the repository was
  recreated between job creation and execution (defense in depth at
  driver + worker level).
- Parallelize non-folder resource operations using concurrency.ForEachJob
  (folders remain sequential to respect hierarchy), matching the
  finalizer pattern.
- Structure workers into named phases with progress messages and tracing
  spans (list → folders → resources or vice versa) for frontend
  visibility and observability.
- Extract shared SplitItems into resources/cleanup.go and reuse from
  finalizers.
- Add comprehensive tests for all new logic.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix NewRepositoryController call after upstream signature change

Add missing folderMetadataEnabled parameter after merge with origin/main.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix lint errors in jobsConnector

- Add missing exhaustive switch cases for ReleaseResources and
  DeleteResources in authorizeJob.
- Reduce cyclomatic complexity of Connect by extracting handleGetJob,
  handleCreateJob, and validateWriteAccess methods.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix TS7053 in RecentJobs for new action types

The action enum now includes releaseResources and deleteResources which
have no corresponding options field on JobSpec. Cast to Record for safe
indexing since the undefined case is already handled.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Avoid type assertion in RecentJobs action options lookup

Use an explicit Record mapping from action to its options object instead
of casting JobSpec. Actions without options (releaseResources,
deleteResources) naturally resolve to undefined.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Address PR review feedback for orphan cleanup jobs

- Use sort.SliceStable in SortResourceListForDeletion to preserve
  original ordering of equal-priority items.
- Resolve namespace from request context in handleGetJob so clients
  can poll job results even when the repository has been deleted.
- Populate GVK and Path in releaseItem/deleteItem progress results
  so job summaries and metrics correctly attribute each operation
  to its resource type.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add integration test for GET orphan cleanup jobs

Verify that the GET /repositories/{name}/jobs endpoint works for
releaseResources and deleteResources jobs even when the repository
does not exist, covering both list and single-job-by-UID retrieval.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Make actionPriority switch exhaustive without default

Replace the default case with an explicit FileActionIgnored case
to satisfy the exhaustive linter in golangci-lint 2.9.0.

title: Provisioning: Preserve folder identity on metadata-backed folder moves during full sync
body:
## Summary

- When a metadata-backed folder (with `_folder.json`) is moved to a different directory without changing its UID, the sync now performs an in-place update instead of deleting and recreating the folder. This preserves the K8s resource generation and avoids unnecessary churn.
- Fix `EnsureFolderExists` to also update the `sourcePath` annotation when the folder's repository path changes, not just when the checksum changes.

## Details

Previously, moving a metadata-backed folder directory (e.g. `teamA/` to `parent/teamA/`) while keeping the same UID in `_folder.json` caused the compare phase to emit a DELETE for the old path and a CREATE for the new path. Since folder deletions run before folder creations, this destroyed and recreated the folder, resetting its K8s generation to 1.

The new `augmentChangesForFolderMoves` function in the compare phase detects DELETE+CREATE pairs that share the same metadata UID and merges them into a single UPDATE. The existing `applyChange` flow for updated folders already handles removing the stale tree entry and calling `EnsureFolderPathExist`, which updates the folder's parent, title, sourcePath, and checksum in-place.

A secondary bug was also fixed: `EnsureFolderExists` previously only compared `source.Checksum` when deciding whether to update source properties. It now also checks `source.Path`, ensuring the `grafana.app/sourcePath` annotation reflects the folder's current repository location after a move.

## Test plan

- [ ] New integration test `TestIntegrationProvisioning_FullSync_FolderMovePreservesGeneration` verifies:
  - Metadata-backed folder UID is preserved after rename
  - K8s generation does not reset to 1 (proves in-place update)
  - `sourcePath` annotation is updated to the new directory
  - Non-metadata folder renames still work correctly (hash-based UID changes as expected)
  - Dashboards are re-parented to the correct folders
- [ ] Existing unit tests for `EnsureFolderExists` and `EnsureFolderPathExist` updated to reflect new source path behavior
- [ ] All existing sync and resources package unit tests pass

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/991
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR improves provisioning sync behavior for metadata-backed folder moves by preserving folder identity (stable UID) and avoiding delete+recreate churn, while also ensuring folder `sourcePath` annotations stay accurate after moves. **Changes:** - Add compare-phase logic to merge DELETE+CREATE folder pairs (same `_folder.json` UID) into a single UPDATE for in-place reconciliation. - Update `EnsureFolderExists` to refresh `grafana.app/sourcePath` when a folder’s repo path changes (not only when checksum changes). - Add/adjust unit and integration tests covering folder move behavior, generation preservation, and `sourcePath` updates. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes.go | Adds `augmentChangesForFolderMoves` and wires it into `Compare` when folder metadata is enabled. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes_test.go | Adds unit tests for move-detection/merge behavior and sorting expectations. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/folders.go | Updates folder reconciliation to update `sourcePath` when the folder’s repo path changes. | | pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/folders_test.go | Updates tests to reflect new `sourcePath` reconciliation behavior. | | pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/full_sync_move_test.go | Adds integration test intended to validate in-place folder move behavior and preserved identity. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

commits.message:
Provisioning: Preserve folder identity on metadata-backed folder moves

When a metadata-backed folder is moved to a different directory without
changing its UID in _folder.json, the compare phase now detects that the
DELETE (old path) and CREATE (new path) share the same UID and merges
them into a single UPDATE. This preserves the K8s resource generation
instead of resetting it to 1 via a delete+create cycle.

Also fixes EnsureFolderExists to update the sourcePath annotation when
the folder's repository path changes, not just when the checksum changes.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Alerting: Use notification_settings.policy in import wizard
body:
**What is this feature?**

When the `alertingPolicyRoutingSettings` feature flag is enabled, the import wizard (Step 2 — alert rules) now uses the new `notification_settings.policy` mechanism instead of the legacy `__grafana_managed_route__` extra label to assign imported rules to a notification policy.

This aligns the import wizard with the changes introduced in #120610 for the rule editor.

### Changes

- **`convertToGMAApi.ts`**: Added `notificationSettings` parameter to the `convertToGMA` mutation, sent via the `X-Grafana-Alerting-Notification-Settings` HTTP header.
- **`useImport.ts`**: Extracted `buildRoutingParams` pure function that returns either `notificationSettings` (new) or `extraLabels` (legacy) based on the feature flag. Enforced mutual exclusivity at the type level via `MigrateRulesParams`.
- **`ImportToGMA.tsx`**: Updated `handleConfirmImport` to use `buildRoutingParams` and branch the `importRules` call accordingly.
- **`useImport.test.ts`**: Added unit tests covering all branches (FF ON/OFF × routing tree selected/undefined/empty).

### Behavior

| Feature flag | Routing tree selected | Mechanism used |
|---|---|---|
| `alertingPolicyRoutingSettings` ON | Yes | `X-Grafana-Alerting-Notification-Settings: {"policy":"<name>"}` |
| `alertingPolicyRoutingSettings` ON | No | No routing params sent |
| OFF (or missing) | Yes | `X-Grafana-Alerting-Extra-Labels: __grafana_managed_route__=<name>` |
| OFF (or missing) | No | No routing params sent |

No backend changes needed , the convert API already supports the `X-Grafana-Alerting-Notification-Settings` header.


**Why do we need this feature?**

We should use notification settings if it's available

**Who is this feature for?**

Alerting users.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/alerting-squad/issues/1478

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: soniaAguilarPeiron

reviews.body: LGTM!

When alertingPolicyRoutingSettings is ON, the import wizard now sends

__grafana_managed_route__ extra label. Extracts buildRoutingParams as a

title: SigV4: fix AWS auth settings not getting forwarded to data proxy HTTP client
body: **What is this feature?** After https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/107522 we no longer pass in aws auth settings as the middleware would read the auth settings from ctx. But for frontend-only datasources using the data proxy (like `alertmanager`), GrafanaConfig is never injected into the request context. This causes them to no longer respect the aws auth settings The fix here is to pass the grafana cfg as a fallback when the context's auth settings are not found **Why do we need this feature?** Fixes regression **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**: Fixes #120766 **Special notes for your reviewer:** Previously 1. Set ini `allowed_auth_providers = ec2_iam_role,keys,default` 2. Set up prometheus data source with sigv4 + AWS IAM auth. Confirmed it works 3. Set up alertmanager data source with same setup. Confirmed it fails health check Now 1. Set ini `allowed_auth_providers = ec2_iam_role,keys,default` 2. Set up prometheus data source with sigv4 + AWS IAM auth. Confirmed it works 3. Set up alertmanager data source with same setup. Confirmed it works Please check that: - [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Provisioning: Full Sync - react when metadata is deleted
body:
## What

This PR teaches full sync to reconcile folder metadata deletion.

When a folder previously had a `_folder.json` and that file is deleted while the folder directory still exists, full sync now treats that as a real folder identity change:
- the folder reverts from the metadata-defined stable UID to the directory-derived/hash-based UID
- the old folder is cleaned up after children are re-parented
- direct child resources are emitted into the apply flow so they follow the parent change in the same sync
- the existing missing-metadata warning behavior is preserved

This brings `_folder.json` deletion in line with the other folder metadata reconciliation flows already handled in full sync.

## Why

Before this change, deleting `_folder.json` did not converge correctly during full sync.

Grafana could keep the old metadata-backed folder around, leave children attached to the wrong parent, or create duplicates on later syncs. The source of truth had changed, but full sync did not have an explicit compare/apply path for that transition.

This PR closes that gap and makes metadata deletion settle cleanly in one sync.

## Files changed

- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes.go`
  - extends folder-metadata augmentation beyond UID changes
  - detects folders that lost `_folder.json` while still existing in source
  - marks those folders with `FolderRenamed` so the normal replacement flow handles cleanup and child re-parenting
  - emits direct child updates for affected folders so hierarchy changes are applied in the same run

- `apps/provisioning/pkg/safepath/dir.go`
  - adds `EnsureTrailingSlash`
  - this gives compare-time code a single canonical way to represent folder paths with trailing `/`
  - needed because source tree directories and managed folder paths are not always shaped identically, and the metadata augmentation logic relies on exact path-key matching

- `apps/provisioning/pkg/safepath/dir_test.go`
  - adds coverage for `EnsureTrailingSlash`

- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/changes_test.go`
  - adds unit coverage for metadata deletion detection
  - covers direct-child emission, dedupe, no-op cases, and path normalization regressions

- `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/sync_folder_metadata_test.go`
  - adds enterprise integration coverage for full-sync behavior after `_folder.json` deletion
  - covers nested folders, warning behavior, UID replacement, re-parenting, and the mixed case where a parent loses metadata while a child UID changes in the same sync

## Testing

- `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...`
- `go test -tags=enterprise ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/foldermetadata/...`

title: Logs: Fix missing log entry in TXT export when fields are selected
body:
**What is this feature?**

This is a bug fix for the log data export functionality. It ensures that the log line body is correctly included in the Plain Text export when users select specific fields in the Data Inspector.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Currently, when exporting logs as plain text with field selection active, the actual log content (row.entry) is lost. This happens because the text exporter only looks for data within row.labels, while the log line body is a virtual field. This fix maps the log entry to the LOG_LINE_BODY_FIELD_NAME during the export process.

**Who is this feature for?**

Users who need to export specific log fields along with the log message from the Data Inspector for external analysis or auditing.

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: State Timeline: fix axis text color not updating on theme change
body:
Fixes #120721.

When switching between light and dark themes, the state timeline panel's axis text colors stayed stale until a page reload. Other panels like timeseries updated correctly.

The `TimelineChart` component was missing `'theme'` in its `propsToDiff` array, so `GraphNG` never detected the theme change and didn't rebuild the plot config. The `TimeSeries` component already includes `'theme'` for this reason.
author: moktamd

commits.message:
State Timeline: update axis colors on theme change

TimelineChart was missing 'theme' in propsToDiff, so GraphNG never
rebuilt the plot config when the Grafana theme changed. The TimeSeries
component already includes 'theme' in its propsToDiff for this reason.

Fixes #120721

title: Alerting: Promote notifications.alerting.grafana.app resources to v1beta1
body:
## Context

Promotes all `notifications.alerting.grafana.app` API resources from `v0alpha1` to `v1beta1`: Receiver, RoutingTree, TemplateGroup, TimeInterval, InhibitionRule.

`v0alpha1` is retained for backwards compatibility.

## Changes

### CUE kinds & code generation (`apps/alerting/notifications/`)
- Added `kinds/v1beta1/` spec files for all 5 resources
- Updated kind `.cue` files and `manifest.cue` to add v1beta1 variants with `preferredVersion: v1beta1`
- Ran `make generate` to produce the full `v1beta1` Go package
- Added manual `ext.go` / `fakes/` files for the v1beta1 package (copy-paste from `v0alpha1` with only package name changes)
- Updated `pkg/app/app.go` to register v1beta1 kinds and routes
- Updated CRD YAML definitions and manifest YAML to set v1beta1 as the storage version

### Registry (`pkg/registry/apps/alerting/notifications/`)
- Updated all `type.go`, `conversions.go`, `legacy_storage.go`, `subresource.go`, and `handler.go` files to import and use v1beta1 types
- Fixed `GetLegacyStorage` to match on `gvr.Resource` only (ignoring version), so both v0alpha1 and v1beta1 requests are routed to the same storage backend

### Tests (`pkg/tests/apis/alerting/notifications/`, `openapi_test.go`)
- Updated all test files and test-data JSON fixtures to use v1beta1 types and `apiVersion`
- Added `notifications.alerting.grafana.app/v1beta1` to the OpenAPI snapshot list

### OpenAPI snapshots (`pkg/tests/apis/openapi_snapshots/notifications.alerting.grafana.app-v1beta1.json`)
- Ran `UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=true go test -v ./pkg/tests/apis/ -run TestIntegrationOpenAPIs`

### Frontend clients (`packages/grafana-api-clients/src/scripts/generate-rtk-apis.ts`, `packages/grafana-api-clients/src/clients/rtkq/notifications.alerting/v1beta1/`)
- Updated API generation script to generate `v1beta1` clients
- Ran `yarn generate-apis`

### Scheme conversion layer (pkg/registry/apps/alerting/notifications/register.go)

Added an AddToScheme override on AppInstaller to register explicit `v0alpha1` <-> `v1beta1` conversion functions for all List types.

Why: With two served versions, the Kubernetes API server must convert objects between versions on the fly - e.g. a LIST request against v0alpha1 requires converting stored `v1beta1` objects back to `v0alpha1`. The grafana-app-sdk registers conversions for individual resource types (e.g. `Receiver` <-> `Receiver`) but does not register the corresponding List types (`ReceiverList` <-> `ReceiverList`), causing a runtime panic on any list request to the non-storage version.

The conversions use direct pointer reinterpretation (`unsafe.Pointer`) rather than JSON marshalling (the same approach conversion-gen uses) because `v0alpha1` and `v1beta1` were generated from identical CUE schemas and have the same memory layout.
author: titolins

reviews.body: LGTM for the api-client 👍
LGTM. ~Let's keep the integration tests for alpha version and just clone them for v1beta. Later, when we delete alpha, we will delete tests~

commits.message: alerting: promote notifications api's to v1beta1

alerting: fix GetLegacyStorage panic for v0alpha1 notifications resources

GetLegacyStorage was matching on the full GVR (group + version + resource),
so requests for v0alpha1 resources would panic since ResourceInfo now points
to v1beta1. Since v0alpha1 is still a served (non-storage) version, the SDK
calls GetLegacyStorage for both versions.

Fix by switching to match on gvr.Resource only (ignoring version). This is
correct because legacy storage is backed by the domain service layer, not
tied to a specific API version _ the SDK handles v0alpha1_v1beta1 conversion
at the API layer before storage is invoked.

title: Chore: Add asset filtering capability to `yarn build:stats`
body: i've wanted to do this ever since we started i18n work, when passive/lazy-loaded translation files now dominate the webpack-bundle-analyzer treemap, making it difficult to assess the impact of dependency and bundle optimizations, and adds a lot of noise. additionally we have some lazy-loaded, but gigantic Monaco and Kusto bundles that further obscure the grafana core makeup. i have wanted to filter these for along time so i can better focus on finding high-value optimizations, such as [Chore: Evict `react-awesome-query-builder` from initial chunks](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120652). the approach in this PR may seem unusual, but the traditional approaches (`excludeAssets`, `statsOptions.exclude`) do not work as i want, or work at all. my goal is to exclude everything up the tree (to the top-level bundle) when based on the content of child nodes. for example, i want to filter out the whole bundle file when it contains monaco, or contains a `grafana.json` file in public/locales, etc. i first tried a cleaner `generateStatsFile: true` approach that dumps the stats json to disk, but this generates a 1GB unparsable JSON due to quickly v8 mem limits for loading such a huge string into memory for parsing/filtering. instead, the strategy here is to create a static html report, with all js and data embedded. this is ~4MB, then read that into memory, throw away the unwanted top-level bundles using a recursive descendant filter and write back to disk. then we boot up `http-server` with the `-o` option to open a browser to the local filtered stats html file. right now i'm filtering out the i18n translation files, monaco, and kusto. works well! ``` yarn run build:smolstats ``` before: --- after: --- --- with highlighted optimizations from above-linked PR (`awesome|plugin-ui|sqled`): before: --- after: ---
author: leeoniya

reviews.body: into it!

commits.message: Chore: Add asset filtering capability to `yarn build:stats`
extract plugin to file, add yarn run build:statsFilt, add minDominance param

title: Dashboard: Smart fill-to-limit pagination and merge-sort for mixed v1/v2 history
body:
## Summary

Alternative approach to #120710. Replaces the drain-all-pages strategy with a smarter two-layer pagination design:

- **v1/v2 clients**: Fill-to-limit loop — follows k8s continue tokens only until `limit` items are collected, requesting `limit - items.length` per page
- **UnifiedDashboardAPI**: Three fast paths (pure v1, pure v2, mixed merge) with a composite base64 continue token encoding both k8s pagination cursors
- **Mixed v1/v2 merge**: Sorted by generation descending, preserving correct chronological order across pages
- **Consistent composite tokens**: All return paths (including pure-v1 and pure-v2 early returns) wrap continue tokens in the composite format, preventing cursor misrouting on subsequent pages

### Why this approach?

The drain-all-pages approach in #120710 has three issues for mixed v1/v2 dashboards:

1. **Unbounded fetching**: For a 3MB dashboard with 50 versions, drains all ~100 pages (50 from v1 + 50 from v2) on first load
2. **No sort on merge**: v1 and v2 valid items are concatenated, not interleaved by generation
3. **Ignores `limit`**: Returns all items at once regardless of requested page size

This approach fetches exactly what the UI needs per page and handles pagination correctly.

### Scenario: 83 versions with mixed v1/v2 storage

<details>
<summary>Detailed comparison of both approaches (click to expand)</summary>

**History layout** (chronological, oldest to newest):

| Generations | Stored as |
|------------|-----------|
| 1-10 | v1 |
| 11-12 | v2 |
| 13-42 | v1 |
| 43-82 | v2 |
| 83 | v1 |

UI requests `limit=10`, 3MB dashboard (1 item per 2MB k8s page).

#### Drain-all-pages (#120710)

**Page 1 request: `{ limit: 10 }`**

1. v1 drains ALL pages → 83 sequential k8s fetches
2. v2 drains ALL pages → 83 more fetches
3. Returns **83 items, unsorted** (v1 block then v2 block)
4. `limit` ignored, "load more" hidden

```
Version 83  (v1)    ← newest
Version 42  (v1)    ← jumps to 42
...
Version 1   (v1)
Version 82  (v2)    ← jumps back to 82
...
Version 11  (v2)
```

~166 network requests, all items at once, wrong order.

#### Fill-to-limit + merge-sort (this PR)

```
Page 1: [83, 82, 81, 80, 79, 78, 77, 76, 75, 74]   mixed   ~20 fetches
Page 2: [73, 72, 71, 70, 69, 68, 67, 66, 65, 64]   pure v2 ~20 fetches
Page 3: [63, 62, 61, 60, 59, 58, 57, 56, 55, 54]   pure v2 ~20 fetches
Page 4: [53, 52, 51, 50, 49, 48, 47, 46, 45, 44]   pure v2 ~20 fetches
Page 5: [43, 42, 41, 40, 39, 38, 37, 36, 35, 34]   mixed   ~20 fetches
Page 6: [33, 32, 31, 30, 29, 28, 27, 26, 25, 24]   pure v1 ~10 fetches
Page 7: [23, 22, 21, 20, 19, 18, 17, 16, 15, 14]   pure v1 ~10 fetches
Page 8: [13, 12, 11, 10,  9,  8,  7,  6,  5,  4]   mixed   ~20 fetches
Page 9: [ 3,  2,  1]                                pure v1 ~10 fetches
```

Every page returns exactly `limit` items in correct generation order.
</details>

### Key invariant

Both v1 and v2 history APIs iterate the same generation sequence. Every generation is valid in exactly one stream. So `v1Valid.length + v2Valid.length = limit` — the merge never loses or duplicates items.

### Composite continue token design

The unified API maintains two independent k8s pagination cursors (one for v1, one for v2). These are encoded as a single base64 JSON token `{v1: "...", v2: "..."}` so the caller (UI) only sees one opaque continue token. All return paths — including pure-v1 and pure-v2 early returns — produce composite tokens to prevent cursor misrouting on subsequent pages.

Fixes #120212

## Test plan

- [x] All existing v1/v2/Unified API tests pass
- [x] New tests: composite token encode/decode round-trip, generation ordering, exhausted streams, pure v2 fast path
- [x] Tests verify both v1 and v2 clients receive correct decoded tokens in mixed scenarios
- [x] Tests verify composite tokens in response metadata for all return paths (pure-v1, pure-v2, mixed)
- [x] Manual: verified mixed v1/v2 dashboard shows correct paginated history without duplicates (`dashboardNewLayouts` disabled)
- [ ] Manual: verify large dashboard (3MB+) shows full version history with pagination

Add auto-pagination to K8sDashboardAPI.listDashboardHistory and
K8sDashboardV2API.listDashboardHistory so they follow all
metadata.continue tokens instead of returning only the first page.

Fix Bug 2A in UnifiedDashboardAPI where pure v2 dashboards showed
empty history because 0 === 0 trivially passed the "all v1 items
valid" check. Apply the same fix to listDeletedDashboards.

Remove spec document from PR

The spec is a planning artifact and should not be committed to the repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dashboard: Smart fill-to-limit pagination and merge-sort for mixed v1/v2 history

Replace the drain-all-pages approach with a fill-to-limit strategy that
stops fetching as soon as `limit` items are collected. For mixed v1/v2
dashboards, use a composite continue token to track both pagination
streams independently, and merge-sort valid items by generation desc.

- v1/v2 clients: fill-to-limit loop requesting `limit - items.length`
  per page instead of draining all pages unconditionally
- UnifiedDashboardAPI: three fast paths (pure v1, pure v2, mixed merge)
  with composite base64 continue token encoding both k8s tokens
- listDeletedDashboards: same composite-token and sort logic
- Tests: composite token encode/decode, generation ordering, exhausted
  streams, pure v2 fast path

Fixes #120212

fix: decode composite continue token in listDeletedDashboards and improve test coverage

- Make sortByGenerationDesc generic to preserve type info and non-mutating
- Decode composite continue tokens on input in listDeletedDashboards
  (previously only encoded on output, breaking pagination on subsequent pages)
- Remove meaningless generation sort for deleted dashboards (different
  dashboards don't share a generation sequence)
- Fix tests that weren't exercising claimed code paths (v2 client was
  never called due to early returns on all-valid v1 responses)
- Add composite continue token assertion for mixed deleted dashboards response

fix: wrap all return paths in composite continue tokens to prevent duplicate pages

Early returns (pure-v1 and pure-v2 paths) were returning raw k8s continue
tokens. On the next "load more" call, decodeCompositeToken misinterpreted
the raw v2 token as a v1 token (fallback path), causing v2 to restart from
the beginning and producing duplicate items in the version history UI.

All return paths now encode continue tokens via encodeCompositeToken:
- v1 early return: preserves the incoming v2 cursor position
- v2 early return: advances both v1 and v2 cursors
- merged path: unchanged (already encoded)

title: Scopes: fix flaky selectScope E2E helper by waiting for viewport
body:
## Summary

- `selectScope()` in `e2e-playwright/utils/scope-helpers.ts` called `scrollIntoViewIfNeeded()` followed immediately by `click({ force: true })`, with no guarantee the scroll had settled before the click was dispatched
- On loaded CI runners, the element's bounding box was still outside the Playwright viewport at click-time, causing `locator.click: Element is outside of the viewport` — failing `scope-redirect.spec.ts:277` on both initial run and retry
- Fix adds `await expect(element).toBeInViewport()` between scroll and click; this is a retrying assertion that polls until the element's rect intersects the viewport, acting as a stable synchronization point

## Test plan

**Local stress test verification** using `stress-ng --cpu 7` (87.5% CPU load) + default Playwright workers to simulate a loaded CI runner:

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Pre-fix, 10 repeats under load | **4/10 failed** — `locator.click: Element is outside of the viewport` |
| With fix, 10 repeats under load | **1/10 failed** (original error eliminated, see note) |

The one remaining failure in the fixed run was a **different issue**: trace analysis confirmed `toBeInViewport` passed, the checkbox click succeeded, but the overall test hit the 30s timeout due to cumulative action slowdown under extreme CPU contention. That failure mode is not present in normal CI conditions.

- [x] Failure reproduced locally without fix (`stress-ng --cpu 7`, 4/10 failures)
- [x] Fix eliminates the original viewport error under the same load (0/10)
- [x] Trace analysis confirms fix works at the exact failing step — residual timeout is a separate, unrelated flake from extreme artificial load
- [x] Fix is targeted at `selectScope()` helper only — no production code changes
- [x] All other scope-redirect tests unaffected

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: ericrshields

title: Suggested Dashboards: `dashvalidator` Remove flaky timing-based performance tests
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**
The tests `TestPerformanceBaseline_ExtractQueries` and `TestPerformanceBaseline_GroupQueries` asserted that in-memory operations finish within 5ms, causing flaky [CI](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/actions/runs/23300664561/job/67760567809?pr=120606#step:8:51) failures (e.g. 5.5ms) despite the code being fast (~94µs).

Wall-clock assertions in Go tests are unreliable due to GC, scheduling, and CPU variability, especially in CI. The Go team recommends using benchmarks (testing.B) with `benchstat` instead.

These tests were redundant. Existing benchmarks already cover the same code paths. Performance regressions can be detected with:

`go test -bench=. -count=10 ./apps/dashvalidator/pkg/validator/... ` and comparing via benchstat.

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

title: Provisioning: Handle tree renames gracefully in incremental sync
body:
## Summary

When Git detects a directory rename (`git mv old-dir new-dir`), `CompareFiles` previously decomposed the tree entry into `FileActionDeleted` + `FileActionCreated`. This caused the corresponding Kubernetes folder object to be deleted and recreated — resetting its `generation`, losing its identity, and potentially disrupting references.

This PR treats directory renames as first-class `FileActionRenamed` events. For **metadata-backed folders** (`_folder.json` with a stable UID), the existing K8s object is updated in place — preserving its UID, generation, and creation timestamp. Dashboards inside renamed folders are also updated in place. Non-metadata folders continue to work via the delete+create path since their UIDs are path-derived.

### Changes

**Git layer:**
- **`repository.go`**: Emit `FileActionRenamed` for tree entries (`protocol.ObjectTypeTree`) with trailing-slash paths via `safepath.EnsureTrailingSlash`, instead of decomposing into delete+create.

**Folder comparison API:**
- **`id.go`**: Add `Folder.Equal(other Folder, opts ...FolderCompareOption)` to centralise staleness detection (title, path, metadata hash, parent). `IgnoreParent()` option allows skipping parent comparison when `ParentID` is not yet resolved.

**Folder management:**
- **`folders.go`**: Refactor `EnsureFolderExists` and `EnsureFolderPathExist` to use `Folder.Equal` for staleness checks. Add `RenameFolderPath` that handles the full rename flow: ensures the folder exists at the new path and returns the old folder ID only when UIDs differ (non-metadata case).

**Sync logic:**
- **`incremental.go`**: Detect directory renames via `safepath.IsDir` in the `FileActionRenamed` handler and dispatch to `RenameFolderPath` instead of `RenameResourceFile`.

**Test helpers (common):**
- `ObjectSnapshot`, `SnapshotObject`, `RequireUpdatedInPlace` moved to `common` for reuse across integration tests.
- `ExpectedDashboard` extended with an optional `Folder` field for parent annotation checks.

**Unit tests:**
- Tree entry rename tests updated to expect `FileActionRenamed` with trailing-slash paths.
- `TestFolder_Equal` covers all comparison fields and `IgnoreParent()`.
- Folder test helpers updated to include `sourcePath` annotations.

**Integration tests** (`TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalSync_GracefulFolderRename`):
- **Root → root** rename: folder and dashboard updated in place, folder has no parent.
- **Nested → nested** rename (same parent): child folder stays parented, dashboard parented under child.
- **Root → nested** rename: folder gains correct parent, dashboard parented under moved folder.
- **Nested → root** rename: folder loses parent, dashboard parented under moved folder.
- **Non-metadata** rename: still works via delete+create.
- All cases verify: `creationTimestamp` preserved, `generation` does not decrease, `sourcePath` updated, `grafana.app/folder` correct for both folders and dashboards.

## Test plan

- [x] `repository_test.go` — tree rename tests pass with new expectations
- [x] `folders_test.go` — `EnsureFolderExists` / `EnsureFolderPathExist` tests pass
- [x] `id_test.go` — `Folder.Equal` and `IgnoreParent()` unit tests pass
- [x] `sync/` — incremental sync unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration: `GracefulFolderRename` — root-to-root, nested-to-nested, root-to-nested, nested-to-root
- [ ] Integration: `GracefulFolderRename` — non-metadata folder rename (delete+create)
- [ ] Integration: existing `RenameFolder` / `RenameNestedFolder` tests still pass
- [ ] Integration: existing folder metadata title tests still pass
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Handle tree renames gracefully in incremental sync

Instead of decomposing directory renames into delete+create events,
emit a single FileActionRenamed with trailing-slash paths so downstream
code can identify directories via safepath.IsDir. For metadata-backed
folders (_folder.json with stable UID), the K8s folder object is updated
in place — preserving its identity and generation — rather than being
deleted and recreated.

Key changes:
- repository.go: emit FileActionRenamed for tree entries (mode 0o40000)
- id.go: add FolderState + Folder.NeedsUpdate to centralise staleness checks
- folders.go: refactor EnsureFolderExists to use NeedsUpdate (adds source
  path comparison), add RenameFolderPath for in-place folder updates
- incremental.go: detect directory renames via safepath.IsDir and dispatch
  to RenameFolderPath instead of RenameResourceFile
- Integration tests verify UID preservation and generation continuity

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Improve readability of tree rename handling

Add safepath.EnsureTrailingSlash and isTreeEntry helper to replace
inline magic constants and string concatenation. Rename error
variables to descriptive boolean flags for clearer control flow.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Replace NeedsUpdate with FolderState.Equal

Introduce FolderState as a value type capturing the comparable folder
properties (title, path, metadata hash, parent). Folder.State()
extracts one from a Folder; FolderState.Equal provides a clean
equality check. This avoids constructing half-populated Folder
structs for comparison and keeps field names consistent.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Improve folder comparison API and expand rename integration tests

Replace NeedsUpdate with FolderState.HasState using FolderCompareOption
for cleaner staleness checks. Add IgnoreParent() option for the
pre-walk early return where parent hasn't been resolved yet.

Expand integration tests to cover nested-to-nested, root-to-nested,
and nested-to-root folder renames. All rename scenarios now verify
both folders and dashboards are updated in place by checking
generation and creationTimestamp preservation.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Simplify folder comparison to Folder.Equal and move snapshot helpers to common

- Replace FolderState/HasState with direct Folder.Equal(other Folder, opts...)
- Remove FolderState struct and Folder.State() method
- Move ObjectSnapshot, SnapshotObject, RequireUpdatedInPlace to common test package

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Normalize folder paths and strengthen in-place update detection

ParseFolder now always produces paths with a trailing slash via
EnsureTrailingSlash, making Folder.Path consistent regardless of whether
the input came from safepath.Dir or safepath.Walk. Folder.Equal compares
paths after stripping trailing slashes so existing K8s objects stored
without them don't trigger unnecessary updates.

ObjectSnapshot and RequireUpdatedInPlace now compare metadata.uid, which
is the definitive K8s object identity and immune to the sub-second
delete+recreate timing issue that creationTimestamp alone cannot catch.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Revert ParseFolder trailing-slash normalization, keep Equal fix

The EnsureTrailingSlash change in ParseFolder caused all sourcePath
annotations to gain trailing slashes, breaking integration tests across
multiple packages. The path normalization in Folder.Equal alone is
sufficient to fix the fast-path mismatch in EnsureFolderPathExist
without changing stored annotation values.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Hoist rename boundary check above trailing-slash guard

Move the newInsidePath/oldInsidePath booleans right after the RelativeTo
calls so the intent is clear: a git rename can straddle the repository
boundary, and these flags resolve that once. The trailing-slash guard
now uses the booleans instead of re-checking the raw errors.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add unit tests for RenameFolderPath and fix trailing-slash test leftovers

Add TestRenameFolderPath covering stable UID in-place updates, different
UID cleanup, hash-based ID fallback, and error propagation paths.

Fix six pre-existing trailing-slash assertion mismatches left over from
the reverted ParseFolder normalization change.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Handle cross-boundary directory changes in incremental sync

Route directory-level FileActionCreated to EnsureFolderPathExist and
skip directory-level FileActionDeleted (per-file deletes handle cleanup)
to prevent a runtime panic when WriteResourceFromFile or
RemoveResourceFromFile receives a trailing-slash directory path.

Also update RequireUpdatedInPlace to accept two ObjectSnapshot values
instead of mixing snapshot + raw Unstructured.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add integration test for renaming folder with mixed children

Test renaming a parent folder that contains both a dashboard and a child
folder (which itself contains a dashboard), verifying all four K8s
objects are updated in place with correct sourcePath and parent annotations.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix gofmt alignment in integration test map literals

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix integration test assertions for dashboard renames

Remove RequireUpdatedInPlace assertions for dashboards inside renamed
folders — RenameResourceFile is a delete+create by design, so dashboard
UIDs change. Also fix sourcePath expectations to not include trailing
slashes, matching actual K8s annotation values.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Skip quota for directory entries and fix SA4011 lint

Move directory-path handling (IsDir check) above the quota acquisition
so cross-boundary directory creates don't consume resource quota. This
also eliminates the ineffective `break` inside the switch that triggered
staticcheck SA4011 — directory paths now continue early.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Simplify directory entry handling in incremental sync

Directory entries from cross-boundary renames don't need explicit
EnsureFolderPathExist calls — WriteResourceFromFile already ensures
parent folders exist for each file it processes. Just skip directory
paths entirely to avoid routing them to file-processing logic, consuming
quota, and the SA4011 ineffective break.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Allow renamed directories through to RenameFolderPath

The directory-skip guard must not block FileActionRenamed entries —
only created/deleted directory paths should be skipped.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Assert dashboards are recreated during folder renames (FIXME)

Add RequireRecreated helper and use it to document that dashboards
inside renamed folders are currently deleted+recreated (new UIDs)
because RenameResourceFile does a remove+write. This is a known bug —
dashboards should be updated in place instead.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Assert dashboards are recreated during folder renames (FIXME)

Add RequireRecreated helper and use it to document that dashboards
inside renamed folders are currently deleted+recreated (new UIDs)
because RenameResourceFile does a remove+write. This is a known bug —
dashboards should be updated in place instead.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix flaky dashboard assertions in folder move tests

Full sync recreates dashboards inside moved folders (delete+create),
so generation and creationTimestamp assertions are invalid. Remove
them from FolderMovePreservesGeneration and add FIXME. Also update
RequireRecreated to be the inverse of RequireUpdatedInPlace.

Made-with: Cursor

Merge branch 'provisioning/graceful-tree-renames' of github.com:grafana/grafana into provisioning/graceful-tree-renames

Made-with: Cursor

# Conflicts:
#	pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/common/testing.go

Provisioning: Fix RequireRecreated flaky generation assertion

A recreated K8s object always starts at generation=1. When the original
also had generation=1 (never updated), require.Less(1, 1) fails. Replace
with require.Equal(1, after.Generation) which correctly asserts the
generation reset. Also drop the creationTimestamp check since sub-second
delete+create can produce identical timestamps.

title: Unified Storage: allow admins to release repo-managed resources
body:
Part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/993

## What

Allow Grafana server admins and org admins to release (detach) repo-managed dashboards and folders by removing manager annotations.

## Why

When an admin tried to release a repo-managed dashboard via PATCH, the API returned a 403:

```
PATCH /apis/dashboard.grafana.app/v2beta1/namespaces/default/dashboards/dffgcb82k699tsd
```
```json
{
    "metadata": {
        "annotations": {
            "grafana.app/managedBy": null,
            "grafana.app/managerId": null,
            "grafana.app/managerAllowsEdits": null,
            "grafana.app/sourcePath": null,
            "grafana.app/sourceChecksum": null,
            "grafana.app/sourceTimestamp": null
        }
    }
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "kind": "Status",
  "apiVersion": "v1",
  "status": "Failure",
  "message": "Can not remove resource manager from resource",
  "reason": "Forbidden",
  "code": 403
}
```

This happened because `enforceManagerProperties` only allowed the provisioning service identity (`access-policy:provisioning`) to modify repo-managed resources. Admin users were treated the same as regular editors.

There is no reconciliation loop that automatically releases orphaned resources. When a resource has manager annotations pointing to a repository that never existed, or that was removed without proper cleanup, admins need the ability to manually detach the resource. Without this change, the only recourse is direct database manipulation or deleting and re-creating the resource.

## How

In `checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec` (`pkg/storage/unified/apistore/managed.go`), specifically in the release branch (where manager annotations are being removed: `!hasNew && hasOld`), added an admin bypass that:

1. Checks `errors.Is(err, errResourceIsManagedInRepository)` -- only applies to repo-managed resources, not other manager kinds
2. Type-asserts `AuthInfo` to `identity.Requester` to check:
   - `GetIsGrafanaAdmin()` -- Grafana server admin
   - `HasRole(identity.RoleAdmin)` -- org admin

The bypass is **only** in the release path. Spec edits and deletes on managed resources still route through the provisioning API via `handleManagedResourceRouting` as before, preserving the `updatedBy: access-policy:provisioning` behavior.

## Test plan

### Unit tests (`pkg/storage/unified/apistore/managed_test.go`)

- [x] Server admin can release repo-managed dashboard (update path, removes annotations)
- [x] Server admin can release repo-managed folder (update path, unstructured)
- [x] Org admin can release repo-managed dashboard (update path)
- [x] Non-admin user cannot release repo-managed resource (negative case)

### Integration tests (`pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/managed_test.go`)

- [ ] Editor cannot release repo-managed dashboard via update
- [ ] Admin can release repo-managed dashboard via update
- [ ] Editor cannot release via merge patch (setting annotations to null)
- [ ] Editor cannot release via JSON patch (remove operations)
- [ ] Admin can release via merge patch
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Enables Grafana server admins and org admins to “release” (detach) repo-managed dashboards/folders in Unified Storage by allowing manager-annotation removal on update, while keeping normal managed-resource edit routing through the provisioning identity. **Changes:** - Added an admin/org-admin bypass in the managed-resource update validation for the “release manager” path (repo-managed only). - Added unit tests covering admin release behavior for dashboards and folders (including unstructured objects). - Added integration tests covering release attempts via Update and Patch (merge + JSON patch negative cases for editors, merge patch positive case for admins). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `pkg/storage/unified/apistore/managed.go` | Allows admin/org-admin callers to remove repo manager annotations during update (release path). | | `pkg/storage/unified/apistore/managed_test.go` | Adds unit coverage for admin/org-admin release of repo-managed resources. | | `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/managed_test.go` | Adds integration coverage for release via Update and Patch APIs (including editor forbidden cases). | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).
LGTM

commits.message:
Managed resources: allow admins to release repo-managed resources

Previously, only the provisioning service identity could remove manager
annotations from repo-managed dashboards and folders. This meant Grafana
admins and org admins received a 403 Forbidden when trying to release
(detach) a resource from its repository via the API.

This adds an admin bypass in enforceManagerProperties for ManagerKindRepo
so that both server admins (IsGrafanaAdmin) and org admins (RoleAdmin)
can release managed resources. Unit and integration tests are included.

Made-with: Cursor

Add PATCH-based integration tests for admin release

Adds TestIntegrationProvisioning_AdminCanReleaseManagedResourceViaPatch
covering both merge patch (setting annotations to null) and JSON patch
(remove operations) for releasing repo-managed dashboards. Includes
negative cases verifying editors are still blocked for both patch types.

Made-with: Cursor

Scope admin bypass to release-only, not all managed operations

The previous approach added the admin check in enforceManagerProperties,
which is shared across create, update, and delete paths. This caused
spec updates by admins to bypass the provisioning routing (writes went
directly to storage instead of through the provisioning API), breaking
the updatedBy annotation (user UID instead of access-policy:provisioning).

Move the admin check to only the release branch (!hasNew && hasOld) in
checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec. Spec edits and deletes still route
through provisioning as before; only releasing (removing annotations) is
now allowed for admins directly.

Made-with: Cursor

Scope admin bypass to repo-managed resources only

Check errors.Is(err, errResourceIsManagedInRepository) before applying
the admin bypass, so it only takes effect for repo-managed resources.
If a future manager kind returns a different error from
enforceManagerProperties, admins will not silently bypass it.

Made-with: Cursor

Add comment explaining why admins can release managed resources

Made-with: Cursor

Fix test assertions flagged by Copilot review

- Use require.ErrorContains instead of require.Error so error messages
  are actually verified (also fixes two pre-existing wrong messages)
- Use require.NotContains on the annotations map instead of
  require.Empty on the value, to distinguish key absence from empty
  string

Made-with: Cursor

Fix integration tests: release folder before dashboard (top-down)

The folder-manager consistency check (from main) rejects unmanaged
resources inside managed folders. Update the admin-release integration
tests to release the parent folder first, then the dashboard.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Folder: Fix pagination stopping early due to post-search k6 filter
body:
## Summary

- Fix folder pagination stopping early when the k6 folder (`k6-app`) is present in search results
- The k6 folder is hidden from non-service-account users. Previously, it was filtered **after** the search LIMIT was applied, returning one fewer result than the page size. The frontend interprets `count < PAGE_SIZE` as the last page, so pagination stopped prematurely
- The fix adds a `selection.NotIn` filter on `SEARCH_FIELD_NAME` to exclude k6 at the query level, so the search engine applies the exclusion **before** the LIMIT

### Dual search backend handling

The search has two backend implementations:
- **Unified/bleve (mode 4+)**: Handles `NotIn` correctly — k6 is excluded at query level, full page is returned, pagination works correctly
- **Legacy SQL (modes 0-3)**: Does not support `NotIn` on the name field. The legacy client now explicitly ignores `NotIn` on this field (instead of silently misinterpreting it as `In`). A post-filter fallback in `GetChildren` removes k6 from results. The 49-result edge case remains on legacy mode but is acceptable as legacy search is being removed

This aligns with how the legacy SQL folder store already handles k6 exclusion — filtering in the query (`WHERE uid != ?`) before `LIMIT`:
- [`sqlstore.go:369-372`](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/sqlstore.go#L369-L372) — `GetChildren`
- [`sqlstore.go:526-528`](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/sqlstore.go#L526-L528) — `GetFolders`

## Test plan

- [x] `go test ./pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/ -run TestGetChildren` — k6 NotIn filter and service account tests pass
- [x] `go test ./pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/...` — all folder unit tests pass
- [x] `go test ./pkg/tests/apis/folder -run TestIntegrationFoldersGetAPIEndpointK8S` — integration test passes (all modes 0-4)
- [ ] Manual: create >50 root folders locally, verify infinite scroll loads all pages in Dashboards > View by Folders
author: pstibrany

reviews.body: LGTM, thank you!

commits.message:
Folder: Fix pagination stopping early due to post-search k6 filter

Move k6 folder exclusion from post-search filtering into the search
request using selection.NotIn on SEARCH_FIELD_NAME. Previously, the
search LIMIT was applied before the k6 filter, causing one fewer result
than requested. The frontend interprets fewer results than the page size
as the last page, stopping pagination prematurely.

Folder: Use limit+1 approach instead of NotIn for k6 filter

Replace the NotIn search filter with a limit+1 strategy for excluding
the k6 folder from GetChildren results. The search has two backend
implementations (legacy SQL and unified/bleve), and the legacy one
does not support NotIn on the name field — it ignores the operator
and treats NotIn as In, breaking search results entirely.

The new approach requests one extra result from search, post-filters
k6-app from the response, and trims back to the original limit. This
works correctly with both search backends.

Folder: Revert to NotIn approach, skip legacy search integration tests

Revert to the NotIn query filter for k6 exclusion (instead of limit+1)
since limit+1 breaks offset-based pagination by duplicating items
across pages.

The legacy search backend does not support NotIn on the name field,
but legacy search is being removed. Skip the integration test for
legacy dual-writer modes (0-3) until legacy search is fully removed.
Mode 4 (unified-only) passes correctly.

Folder: Handle NotIn gracefully in legacy search, add post-filter fallback

The legacy search backend does not support NotIn on SEARCH_FIELD_NAME.
Previously it silently treated NotIn as In, breaking folder listings
entirely on dual-writer modes 0-3.

Fix: legacy search now ignores NotIn on the name field (skips the
filter), and GetChildren keeps a post-filter fallback to remove k6
from results when the search backend doesn't handle the exclusion.

On unified/bleve (mode 4+), NotIn works at query level giving correct
pagination. On legacy (modes 0-3), the post-filter catches k6 with
the minor edge case of returning 49 results when k6 is on the page.
This is acceptable as legacy search is being removed.
Add TODO to remove post-filter once legacy search is removed

title: Alerting: Fix flaky test in policies list
body:
**Problem**
A 500ms useDebounce in NotificationPoliciesFilter fires after mount with unchanged values (empty `[]` matchers, undefined contact point), calling resetExpandState() and clobbering user-initiated expand/collapse state. This creates a timing-dependent race with React Testing Library's asyncWrapper macrotask boundary.

**Fix**
Added refs to track the previous values of both filter callbacks and skip resetExpandState() when the value hasn't meaningfully changed.
author: rodrigopk

reviews.body: LGTM!

This creates a timing-dependent race with React Testing Library's asyncWrapper macrotask boundary.

Both SQL and KVSQL currently keep their own `customPrunerHistoryLimits` maps even though they contain the same entries. - update SQL and KVSQL to use the shared lookup
- add a table test for the shared lookup

This version keeps the original behavior where metric queries substracted 1 step from the end of the interval.
All the unit tests created for the original query splitting are now running with and without this behavior, with some minor adjustments to the expected generated timestamps.

### How to test

Enable `lokiAlignedQuerySplitting`
Run logs and metric queries, single day, multi-day.
Multi-day queries should create splits aligned to midnight.

title: Search: Add per-namespace jitter to Bleve index rebuild staleness check
body:
## Summary

- Add deterministic per-namespace jitter to `findIndexesToRebuild` to spread index rebuilds across scan intervals, preventing thundering herd CPU spikes when all Bleve indexes become stale simultaneously
- Jitter uses FNV-1a hash of the namespace key, bounded to `[0, maxAge/5)` — for a 24h maxAge this creates ~57 distinct 5-minute scan windows
- Jitter is only applied during periodic scans (`applyJitter: true`), not during explicit RPC rebuild requests (`applyJitter: false`)

## Test plan

- [x] `TestJitterForKey` — determinism, zero maxAge, bounded output, different keys produce different values
- [x] `TestFindIndexesToRebuildWithJitter` — with jitter only a subset are queued vs. all without jitter
- [x] Existing `TestFindIndexesForRebuild` and `TestRebuildIndexes` pass unchanged
- [ ] Verify in staging that index rebuilds are spread across scan intervals (monitor CPU usage)
author: pstibrany

reviews.body: LGTM, hoping there are less spikes afterwards

commits.message:
Search: Add per-namespace jitter to Bleve index rebuild staleness check

Add deterministic per-namespace jitter to findIndexesToRebuild so
different namespaces become stale at different scan intervals, spreading
rebuilds over time and avoiding thundering herd CPU spikes when all
indexes trip the staleness threshold simultaneously.

The jitter uses FNV-1a hashing of the namespace key, bounded to 20% of
maxAge. It is only applied during the periodic scan, not during explicit
RPC rebuild requests.

title: IAM: Fix user StoreWrapper panic in multi-tenant deployments
body:
## Summary
- Adds `setting.Service` as an alternative config source in the user `StoreWrapper` for multi-tenant (MT) deployments where `ConfigProvider` is nil
- Introduces `getHiddenUsers` helper that routes between `ConfigProvider` (single-tenant) and `setting.Service` (multi-tenant), returning an error if neither is configured
- Adds `setting.Service` as a dependency to `NewAPIService` and wires it through the `IdentityAccessManagementAPIBuilder`

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests added for all `StoreWrapper` methods using `setting.Service` (`AfterGet`, `FilterList`, `BeforeCreate`, `BeforeUpdate`, `BeforeDelete`)
- [x] Tests cover: hidden user filtering, comma-separated values, self-visibility, error propagation, nil-providers error
- [x] Verified existing code compiles (`go build ./pkg/registry/apis/iam/...`)
- [ ] Verify MT callers of `NewAPIService` pass the `setting.Service` parameter
author: mgyongyosi

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR fixes a nil-pointer panic in IAM’s user `StoreWrapper` for multi-tenant deployments by adding `setting.Service` as an alternate source of the `users.hidden_users` configuration when `ConfigProvider` is not available. **Changes:** - Extend `user.StoreWrapper` to fetch hidden users from either `configprovider.ConfigProvider` (single-tenant) or `setting.Service` (multi-tenant), and return a clear error if neither is configured. - Wire `setting.Service` through `iam.NewAPIService` → `IdentityAccessManagementAPIBuilder` → `UpdateUsersAPIGroup`. - Add unit tests covering the `setting.Service` path for `AfterGet`, `FilterList`, and write hooks, plus error propagation and nil-provider behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `pkg/registry/apis/iam/user/store_wrapper.go` | Adds `setting.Service` support and a helper to retrieve/parse `hidden_users` safely without panicking when `cfgProvider` is nil. | | `pkg/registry/apis/iam/user/store_wrapper_test.go` | New tests validating hidden-user behavior and error handling when using `setting.Service` (and when both providers are nil). | | `pkg/registry/apis/iam/register.go` | Extends `NewAPIService` to accept `setting.Service` and passes it into the user store wrapper wiring. | | `pkg/registry/apis/iam/models.go` | Adds `settingService` to the IAM API builder for downstream wiring. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

You can also share your feedback

commits.message:
IAM: Support setting.Service as alternative config source in user StoreWrapper

The user StoreWrapper previously required a ConfigProvider to resolve
hidden users, which is nil in multi-tenant (NewAPIService) deployments
causing a panic. This adds setting.Service as an alternative source
that queries the remote settings API for the hidden_users config.

The getHiddenUsers helper routes between ConfigProvider (single-tenant)
and setting.Service (multi-tenant), returning an error if neither is
configured.

title: OpenFeature: Fix namespace fallback for unauthenticated requests
body:
Flag evaluations via `openfeature.TransactionContext(ctx)` rely on the namespace being set by `ContextHandler.setRequestContext`, which derives it from the authenticated identity. 
When auth hasn't run yet or doesn't produce a namespaced identity, it fell back to `default` namespace causing the features service to reject requests.

Observed on `reportRenderBinding` and `react19`, both evaluated in paths where a namespaced identity isn't guaranteed.

The fix is to sall back to "stacks-" + cfg.StackID when id is nil and StackID is set.
author: undef1nd

commits.message: OpenFeature: Fix namespace fallback for unauthenticated requests

title: Provisioning: add integration tests for metadata.name change during sync
body:
## Summary
- Adds two integration tests that verify the current behavior when a resource's `metadata.name` (uid) changes inside a file at the same path during full sync and incremental sync.
- Both tests document that sync matches by file path (not `metadata.name`), so changing the identity inside a file is effectively a create-without-delete, leaving the old resource orphaned.

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `go test ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/... -run TestIntegrationProvisioning_FullSync_MetadataNameChange -count=1`
- [ ] Run `go test ./pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/... -run TestIntegrationProvisioning_IncrementalGitSync_MetadataNameChange -count=1`

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: add integration tests for metadata.name change during sync

Add two integration tests that verify the current behavior when a
resource's metadata.name (uid) changes inside a file at the same path:

- Full sync: creates a new resource with the new name; old resource
  remains orphaned (2 dashboards total).
- Incremental sync: same outcome — new resource created, old one stays.

This documents that sync matches by file path, not metadata.name, so
changing the identity inside a file is effectively a create-without-delete.

Made-with: Cursor

title: fix: ensure kv-GC process each key only once
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Ensures the unified storage KV garbage collector processes each logical resource (group + resource + namespace + name) at most once per GC run. Previously, the same resource could be considered multiple times when multiple deleted versions appeared in a batch, leading to redundant work and possible double-counting.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Avoid double counting resources when running the KV garbage collector

**Who is this feature for?**

@grafana/grafana-search-and-storage 

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Part of https://github.com/grafana/search-and-storage-team/issues/682

title: Provisioning: Full Sync - react when folder name changes in metadata
body:
## What is this feature?

When a user changes the `metadata.name` (UID) in `_folder.json` and triggers a full sync, Grafana now detects the UID change, creates the folder with the new UID, re-parents all direct children (child folders and dashboards), and deletes the old folder.

Part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/936

## Why do we need this?

Changing a folder's UID in `_folder.json` is a valid user action (e.g. to adopt a more meaningful identifier). Without this change, the sync would create a new folder but leave children orphaned under the old UID, and the old folder would linger.

## How it works

### Detection (`jobs/sync/changes.go`)

A new `augmentChangesForUIDChanges` step runs after `Changes()` inside `Compare()`. For each folder update (detected via P2's hash comparison), it reads `_folder.json` and compares the new `metadata.name` against the existing Grafana folder name. When the UID actually changed:

- Sets `OldFolderUID` on the folder change (used later for cleanup)
- Emits `FileActionUpdated` for **direct children only** — grandchildren are unaffected since they point to their own (unchanged) parent

### Re-parenting

Child folders and dashboards are re-parented through the existing apply flow, no special re-parenting logic needed:

- **Child folders**: `EnsureFolderExists` now checks `folder.ParentID` against the stored `grafana.app/folder` annotation. When they differ, it updates the parent — this is the new parent annotation check added to the UPDATE path.
- **Dashboards**: `WriteResourceFromFile` → `EnsureFolderPathExist` resolves the new parent UID naturally.

A new `ParentID` field on the `Folder` struct tracks the parent UID throughout path walks, tree operations, and folder creation.

### Old folder cleanup (`jobs/sync/full.go`)

Old folders are deleted in a **new phase 5** that runs after all folder and file processing is complete. This ensures all children have been re-parented before the old folder is removed. Old folders are deleted deepest-first (children before parents) to avoid "folder not empty" errors. If a deletion fails, the error is recorded but the sync continues.

### Phase ordering

1. File deletions (parallel)
2. Folder deletions (serial, deepest first)
3. Folder creations (serial, deepest first) — creates new UIDs, re-parents child folders
4. File creations (parallel) — re-parents dashboards
5. **NEW**: Old folder cleanup (serial, deepest first) — deletes old UIDs

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `resources/id.go` | Added `ParentID string` to `Folder` |
| `resources/folders.go` | Set `ParentID` in walk + `CreateFolderWithUID`; parent annotation check in `EnsureFolderExists` UPDATE path |
| `resources/tree.go` | Set `ParentID` in `Add`, `AddUnstructured`, `NewFolderTreeFromResourceList` |
| `jobs/sync/changes.go` | Added `OldFolderUID` to `ResourceFileChange`; added `augmentChangesForUIDChanges`; called from `Compare()` |
| `jobs/sync/full.go` | Added deferred old-folder cleanup phase; removed previous UID change handling and unused imports |
| Integration + unit test files | See test plan below |

## Test plan

- [x] `TestEnsureFolderExists_ParentUpdate` — parent differs/matches/empty (3 subtests)
- [x] `TestFolderTree_Add_SetsParentID` — verifies Add populates ParentID
- [x] `TestNewFolderTreeFromResourceList/populates_ParentID` — verifies tree from resource list
- [x] `TestAugmentChangesForUIDChanges` — emits children, skips title-only, excludes grandchildren, no duplicates, no-op (5 subtests)
- [x] `TestApplyChanges_DefersOldFolderDeletion` — verifies phase ordering
- [x] `TestApplyChanges_OldFolderDeletion_DeepestFirst` — deeper paths deleted first
- [x] `TestApplyChanges_OldFolderDeletion_ErrorContinues` — errors recorded but sync continues
- [x] Integration: `UID change replaces folder and re-parents dashboard` (existing, still passes)
- [x] Integration: `UID change deletes old folder and re-parents child folder`
- [x] Integration: `nested UID changes — both parent and child UIDs change`
author: ferruvich

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR improves provisioning full-sync behavior when a folder’s `_folder.json` changes `metadata.name` (folder UID), ensuring Grafana replaces the folder UID cleanly, re-parents direct children, and deletes the old folder after the sync completes.

**Changes:**
- Detect folder UID changes during `Compare()` and emit additional “updated” changes for direct children to trigger normal apply-based re-parenting.
- Track parent UID (`ParentID`) through folder parsing/tree operations and update stale `grafana.app/folder` annotations during folder updates.
- Add a deferred “old folder UID cleanup” step after folder+file application to delete replaced folders deepest-first, plus accompanying unit/integration tests.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/id.go | Adds `ParentID` to `Folder` to carry parent UID through operations. |

title: Add tenant deleter for periodic cleanup of expired tenant data
body:
Implements a background service that periodically deletes resource data for tenants marked for deletion after their expiration time has passed. This complements the existing tenant watcher by actually removing data from the unified storage backend once the grace period has elapsed.

Key changes:
- Add `TenantDeleter` service that runs on a configurable interval to identify and remove expired pending-delete tenant data
- Add configuration flags: `enable_tenant_deleter`, `tenant_deleter_dry_run`, and `tenant_deleter_interval`
- Implement dry-run mode to preview deletions without modifying data
- Add comprehensive test suite covering expiration logic, multi-tenant scenarios, and dry-run behavior
- Only present on the kv storage backend
- This will only run on dev in `dry-run` mode for now. Added a TODO about double checking the deletion with gcom before doing the delete.

title: Provisioning: Release orphaned resources
body:
**What is this feature?**
When user have an orphaned resources, they currently don't have a way to properly convert to regular dashboards or delete them entirely. This PR adds detection of orphaned resources, visible warnings across the UI, and admin actions (release or delete all affected resources) powered by backend jobs.

**To follow up:** Improve after success redirect user need to manual refresh page to see latest change 


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/298ffbe2-0d7f-4495-98b8-3903098d5cf2




This needs to merge after https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120784

**Why do we need this feature?**

Git sync users who encounter orphaned provisioned dashboards/folders and need to understand why they can't edit them.

**Who is this feature for?**

Git sync users

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/972

## Pull request overview

Adds frontend support for “orphaned” provisioned resources (dashboards/folders whose managing provisioning repository was deleted) and exposes admin-only actions to release or delete all resources tied to the missing repo, aligning with the new backend job actions introduced in grafana/grafana#120784.

**Changes:**
- Introduces an `Orphaned` repository-view status and propagates it through provisioning hooks and drawers to block Git-based save/move/delete flows.
- Adds UI surfaces for orphaned state: warning banners/badges, confirmation modal, and an actions hook to start `releaseResources` / `deleteResources` jobs.
- Adds i18n strings and updates/extends unit tests for the new behavior.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

**What is this feature?**
Follow up to https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/119623

This change 

- Adds the legacy team service as a dependency for `TeamK8sService` (Used to resolve int64 ID -> k8s UID during the migration period)
- implements the `GetTeamByID()` method from `TeamK8sService` (if caller provides legacy ID, calls legacy service to resolve ID -> UID)
- implements the `UpdateTeam()` method from `TeamK8sService` (calls legacy service directly to resolve ID -> UID, instead of new GetTeamByID() to avoid double-fetching)


**Why do we need this feature?**
For migrating the teams to use the new app platform APIs

**Who is this feature for?**
all users
