title: A11y: Add aria-live to notification list for screen reader announcements
body:
## Summary
- Adds a visually-hidden `aria-live="polite"` live region inside `AppNotificationList` so screen readers announce dynamically added notifications
- Uses `aria-label` (not text content) to avoid duplicating notification text in the DOM, which would break Playwright's strict-mode `getByText` queries
- The existing `role="status"` on individual `Alert` components doesn't trigger announcements when freshly mounted — only when content changes within an already-present live region

Fixes #118640

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify notifications are announced by a screen reader (VoiceOver/NVDA) when they appear
- [ ] Existing unit tests pass with updated assertions
- [ ] E2E tests no longer fail with strict mode violations on `getByText('Dashboard saved')`
author: tdbishop

commits.message:
A11y: Add aria-live to notification list for screen reader announcements

The Alert component has role="status" on individual notifications (added
in #61242), but this doesn't work for dynamically inserted elements.
role="status" (which implies aria-live="polite") only triggers screen
reader announcements when content changes within an already-present live
region — not when the element itself is mounted fresh into the DOM.

Fix by adding aria-live="polite" to the AppNotificationList wrapper div,
which is always present in the DOM. This ensures all notifications,
including the "Press ESC to exit kiosk mode" message, are announced.

Fixes #118640

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A11y: Use visually-hidden live region for notification announcements

The initial approach of adding aria-live="polite" directly on the wrapper
didn't work in Chrome + VoiceOver because dynamically inserting complex
child DOM nodes into a live region isn't reliably announced.

Instead, add a dedicated visually-hidden div with aria-live="polite" and
aria-atomic="true" whose text content is updated with notification titles.
Text content changes within an already-present live region are reliably
announced across screen reader/browser combinations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A11y: Use aria-label for live region to avoid Playwright strict mode violations

The sr-only div with text content caused `getByText('Dashboard saved')` to
resolve to 2 elements in E2E tests. Switch to aria-label so the notification
text only appears once in the DOM for text queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A11y: Remove role=status from live region to fix strict mode violations

The role="status" on the visually-hidden live region caused
getByRole('status') to resolve to multiple elements in both Playwright
E2E tests and Jest unit tests across the codebase (alerting, notification
policies, etc). aria-live="polite" alone is sufficient for screen reader
announcements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A11y: Add role=log to live region to fix aria-prohibited-attr violation

aria-label is prohibited on a generic div without an explicit role (axe
rule aria-prohibited-attr). Add role="log" which permits aria-label and
is semantically appropriate for a notification stream. Unlike role="status",
role="log" doesn't conflict with existing getByRole('status') queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: [release-12.4.2] A11y: Fix missing focus indicator on dashboard location items
body:
Backport 58923a21300491a7c70d8a8fe0a1879c2ef8bdfa from #120536

---

## Summary

- Removes `overflow: hidden` from `locationContainer` in `SearchResultsTable`, which was silently clipping the focus ring (box-shadow) from child `<a>` elements
- The parent `cell` already provides the overflow boundary; each `locationItem` handles its own text truncation via its own `overflow: hidden`
- No custom focus styles needed — the global `a:focus-visible` rule (`getFocusStyles`) now works naturally, identical to the name column

Fixes #118643 (WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible, Sev3)

## Why `overflow: hidden` was safe to remove

Checked git history back to the introduction of `locationContainer` in #70814. It was added alongside a flex layout refactor with no accompanying bug reference or comment — almost certainly precautionary rather than fixing a real overflow issue. The parent `cell` already clips this container.

## Test plan

- [ ] Tab to a location item in the dashboards browse table — focus ring should match the name column style
- [ ] Verify location text still truncates correctly for long folder paths
- [ ] Test both light and dark themes
author: grafana-delivery-bot

commits.message:
A11y: Fix missing focus indicator on dashboard location items (#120536)

A11y: Add focus-visible indicator to dashboard location items (#118643)

Location elements in the dashboards browse table lacked visible focus
styles, making keyboard navigation inaccessible (WCAG 2.4.7 violation).
Adds :focus-visible ring using theme tokens consistent with other
focusable elements in the codebase.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
(cherry picked from commit 58923a21300491a7c70d8a8fe0a1879c2ef8bdfa)

title: A11y(#118639): fix "Skip to main content" link only working on first activation
body:
Fixes #118639

## Summary

- Added `tabIndex={-1}` to `<main id="pageContent">` — makes it programmatically focusable. Without this, calling `.focus()` on the element is a no-op since `<main>` is not natively interactive.
- Added `onClick` handler on the skip link that calls `e.preventDefault()` and `document.getElementById('pageContent')?.focus()` directly — prevents the URL hash from accumulating `#pageContent`. On a SPA, once `#pageContent` is in the URL, subsequent clicks on `href="#pageContent"` are treated as no-ops by the browser (no navigation occurs).

## Test plan

- [ ] Open Grafana, tab to "Skip to main content" link, press Enter — focus moves to main content
- [ ] Shift+Tab back to the top, tab to the skip link again, press Enter — focus moves to main content a second time (previously broken)
- [ ] Verify no focus outline appears on `<main>` (`:focus-visible` is not triggered by programmatic focus)
- [ ] All `AppChrome` tests pass: `yarn jest --no-watch AppChrome.test`
author: tdbishop

commits.message:
A11y(#118639): fix skip to main content link only working once

Adds tabIndex={-1} to the <main id="pageContent"> target so it is
programmatically focusable, and handles the skip link click via
preventDefault + explicit focus() call so the URL hash does not
accumulate. Without this, repeated activations were a no-op because
the browser saw no navigation was needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: ShortURL: Validate target URL to always be relative
body:
## Summary
- **Fixes open redirect vulnerability** in short URLs (grafana/grafana-enterprise#10848) where attackers could create short URLs redirecting to external domains
- Adds comprehensive `ValidateRelativePath()` that rejects protocol-relative URLs (`//evil.com`), backslash paths (`\evil.com`), scheme-based URLs (`http://`, `javascript:`), path traversal (`../`), and absolute paths
- Applies validation at two layers for defense-in-depth: at creation time (reject bad paths) and before redirecting (safety net for pre-existing malicious entries in the DB)

## Changes
- `pkg/services/shorturls/models.go` — New exported `ValidateRelativePath()` function
- `pkg/services/shorturls/shorturlimpl/shorturl.go` — Use centralized validation in `CreateShortURL`
- `apps/shorturl/pkg/app/app.go` — Updated K8s admission validator + safety-net in goto handler
- `pkg/api/short_url.go` — Safety-net validation in both legacy and K8s redirect handlers
- New test files with 35+ test cases covering all attack vectors

## Test plan
- [x] `go test ./pkg/services/shorturls/...` — all pass
- [x] `go test ./apps/shorturl/pkg/app/...` — all pass
- [x] `go build ./pkg/api/...` — compiles cleanly
- [ ] Manual test: attempt to create short URL with `//evil.com` path → should be rejected
- [ ] Manual test: attempt to create short URL with `http://evil.com` path → should be rejected
- [ ] Manual test: valid relative paths still work as expected

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/issues/10848
author: evictorero

reviews.body: sick!

commits.message:
ShortURL: Validate target URL to always be relative

Add comprehensive validation to prevent open redirect attacks via short URLs.
Previously, only path.IsAbs and ../ traversal were checked, allowing attackers
to create short URLs redirecting to external domains (e.g., //evil.com,
http://evil.com, \evil.com, javascript:alert(1)).

Validation is applied at two layers for defense-in-depth:
1. At creation time (reject malicious paths)
2. Before redirecting (safety net for pre-existing entries)

Fixes grafana/grafana-enterprise#10848

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

ShortURL: Address review feedback on validation

- Add backslash path traversal (..\) check alongside forward-slash (../)
- Make AppURL host comparison case-insensitive and fail explicitly on
  empty/misconfigured AppURL
- Add cross-reference comments between duplicated validation logic
- Add tests for empty string and backslash traversal edge cases

ShortURL: Move validateRelativePath to its own validate.go file

Pairs the validation logic with the existing validate_test.go
and keeps app.go focused on app setup and routing.

title: Alerting: Use notification settings instead of labels for assigning a managed route to an alert rule
body:
**What is this feature?**

PR #117628 added a policy field to `AlertRuleNotificationSettings`, and this PR adds frontend changes to use notification settings instead of labels for assigning a managed route to an alert rule. All changes are gated behind the `alertingPolicyRoutingSettings` FF.

**How to test**
1. Prerequisites 
  - Ensure the `alertingMultiplePolicies` and `alertingPolicyRoutingSettings` feature flags are enabled
  - Ensure at least one named notification policy tree exists (e.g. "TestPolicy") — visible at Alerting → Notification policies.

2. Test new rule creation with custom policy
  - Go to Alerting → Alert rules → New alert rule
  - Fill in a name, query, and folder
  - In the Notifications section, confirm the "Default policy" badge is visible (collapsed state)
  - Click Change, then select "TestPolicy" from the dropdown
  - Click Save rule
  - Check DevTools → Network → the POST /api/ruler/... request body
```
"grafana_alert": {
  "notification_settings": { "receiver": "", "policy": "TestPolicy" }
},
"labels": {}  // no __grafana_managed_route__ key
```

3. Test new rule creation with default policy
  - Same as above but leave the "Default policy" badge as is
  - Click Save rule
  - Check DevTools → Network → the POST /api/ruler/... request body
```
"grafana_alert": {
  "notification_settings": undefined // absent entirely
},
"labels": {}
```


**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/alerting-squad/issues/1459

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Provisioning: include _folder.json in incremental sync safety check
body:
Counterpart: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11384

Part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/996

## Summary

When the `provisioningFolderMetadata` feature flag is enabled, folder metadata is stored in `_folder.json` files alongside resources in git. These files, like `.keep` files, are not Grafana resources — they only carry the folder's stable UID and title. This means incremental sync cannot resolve the folder UID from a `_folder.json` deletion alone, leaving orphaned folders behind.

This PR extends `CanUseIncrementalSync` to treat `_folder.json` the same as `.keep`: if either metadata file is the **only** deletion inside a directory (no sibling resource files deleted), a full sync is forced so the folder cleanup is handled correctly.

### Changes

- **`CanUseIncrementalSync`** — Generalized from `.keep`-only to all folder-metadata files via a new `isFolderMetadataFile` helper. Accepts a `folderMetadataEnabled` flag that gates whether `_folder.json` is considered a metadata file.
- **Controller** — `RepositoryController` now receives `folderMetadataEnabled` and forwards it to `shouldUseIncrementalSync`.
- **GitHub webhook** — `githubWebhookRepository` receives and passes the flag when calling `CanUseIncrementalSync` from `parsePushEvent`.
- **Call sites** — `register.go`, `extras/register.go`, `repo_operator.go`, and `config.go` resolve the flag from `resources.IsFolderMetadataEnabled(cfg)` and thread it through.

## Test plan

- [x] 8 new unit test cases in `TestCanUseIncrementalSync` covering `_folder.json` with flag on/off, alone/alongside resources, nested paths, root path, and mixed directories
- [x] Existing `.keep` test cases still pass (flag defaults to `false` in pre-existing tests)
- [x] `TestShouldUseIncrementalSync` updated to pass the flag
- [x] `TestExtra` and `TestIsWriteAllowed` updated and passing
- [x] All affected packages compile cleanly
- [ ] CI integration tests
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: Changes LGTM

Bump nanogit to v0.12.0 for rename support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: include _folder.json in incremental sync safety check

CanUseIncrementalSync now also treats _folder.json as a folder-metadata
file (like .keep) when the provisioningFolderMetadata feature flag is
enabled. If either file is the only deletion in a directory, a full sync
is triggered because incremental sync cannot resolve the folder UID from
metadata-only files.

Provisioning: move CanUseIncrementalSync to sync package and inject as function

Move CanUseIncrementalSync from the repository package to

Introduce NewCanUseIncrementalSyncFn that closes over the feature flag,
returning a func([]string) bool injected into the controller and webhook

title: Provisioning: Handle file renames gracefully in CompareFiles
body:
## Summary

When users reorganize dashboards in a Git repository using `git mv` (renaming files, moving files between folders, or renaming entire folders), incremental sync now detects these as renames instead of treating them as separate delete + create operations.

**Scope:** This PR handles **file-level renames only**. Tree (directory) rename entries emitted by `CompareCommits` (mode `0o40000`) are decomposed into delete + create pairs for backwards compatibility — the downstream `RenameResourceFile` only handles resource files and would fail on bare directory paths. Individual file renames within renamed directories are already reported as separate entries and cover the actual resource movements.

### What changed

- **Enable rename detection** in `CompareFiles` by passing `nanogit.WithRenameDetection()` to `CompareCommits`, connecting the nanogit v0.12.0 rename support ([nanogit#243](https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/pull/243), [nanogit#245](https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/pull/245)) to the existing downstream `RenameResourceFile` handler.

- **Map `FileStatusRenamed` to the correct action** based on path boundaries relative to the repository's configured path:
  | Old path | New path | Resulting action |
  |----------|----------|-----------------|
  | Inside configured path | Inside configured path | `FileActionRenamed` (with `PreviousPath`) |
  | Outside | Inside | `FileActionCreated` (moved into managed directory) |
  | Inside | Outside | `FileActionDeleted` (moved out of managed directory) |
  | Outside | Outside | Skipped |

- **Decompose tree entry renames into delete + create** — for backwards compatibility, directory rename entries (mode `0o40000`) are converted to a `FileActionDeleted` for the old path and a `FileActionCreated` for the new path. This preserves folder bookkeeping through the existing incremental sync path without requiring changes to `RenameResourceFile`, which only handles resource files.

- **Fix orphaned folder cleanup** — the rename case in `applyIncrementalChanges` was tracking the *new* folder path for orphan cleanup instead of the *old* one. Source folders that became empty after a rename were never checked and never cleaned up.

- **Move test helpers to common package** — `RequireDashboards`, `RequireRepoFolders`, `RequireDashboardCount`, and `RequireDashboardTitle` are now shared in the common test package for reuse across test suites.

### How it flows

```
git mv old.json new.json
    → nanogit CompareCommits (with rename detection)
    → FileStatusRenamed with OldPath + Path
    → CompareFiles maps to FileActionRenamed (files) or delete+create (directories)
    → applyIncrementalChanges calls RenameResourceFile for files
    → dashboard is deleted at old path and recreated at new path (UID preserved)
    → old folder cleaned up if now empty in git
```

Ref: https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/990

## Test plan

### Unit tests (`repository_test.go`)
- [x] Rename within configured path → `FileActionRenamed` with both paths
- [x] Rename from outside to inside → `FileActionCreated`
- [x] Rename from inside to outside → `FileActionDeleted`
- [x] Both paths outside configured path → no change emitted
- [x] Tree entry rename (both inside) → decomposed into `FileActionDeleted` + `FileActionCreated`
- [x] Tree entry rename from outside to inside → only `FileActionCreated`
- [x] Tree entry rename from inside to outside → only `FileActionDeleted`
- [x] Tree entry rename both outside → skipped
- [x] Rename with no configured path → `FileActionRenamed`

### Integration tests (`incremental_test.go`)
- [x] Simple file rename at root (`git mv a.json b.json`)
- [x] Move dashboard from root into a folder
- [x] Move dashboard between folders
- [x] Move dashboard from folder back to root
- [x] Rename entire folder (2 dashboards inside)
- [x] Move dashboard between nested sibling folders
- [x] Rename a nested folder while sibling folder is untouched

All tests verify dashboard count, title, `grafana.app/sourcePath` annotation, and managed folder state (before and after the operation).
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR improves incremental Git provisioning by detecting `git mv` operations as renames (instead of delete+create), and mapping rename events correctly relative to the repository’s configured subpath so dashboards/folders survive repository reorganizations.

**Changes:**
- Enable nanogit rename detection in `CompareFiles` and map `FileStatusRenamed` into `Renamed`/`Created`/`Deleted` actions depending on whether old/new paths fall inside the configured repo path.
- Fix incremental folder-cleanup tracking for renames by keying affected folders off the *previous* path.
- Add/extend unit + integration tests and centralize dashboard/folder assertions into shared test helpers.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `apps/provisioning/pkg/repository/git/repository.go` | Enables rename detection and implements correct rename-to-action mapping with path-boundary handling. |
| `apps/provisioning/pkg/repository/git/repository_test.go` | Adds unit coverage for rename scenarios across configured path boundaries (and no-path mode). |
| `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/incremental.go` | Tracks affected folders using `PreviousPath` for rename operations to support correct orphan cleanup. |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/common/testing.go` | Introduces reusable assertion helpers for dashboards and repo-managed folders. |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/incremental_test.go` | Adds integration coverage for file renames and moves across folder structures (including folder renames). |
| `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/git/metadata_name_change_test.go` | Switches to shared assertion helpers for consistency and reduced duplication. |
</details>

commits.message:
Provisioning: Handle renames gracefully in CompareFiles

Enable nanogit's rename detection in CompareFiles so that file
renames (git mv) produce FileActionRenamed instead of separate
delete+add pairs. This connects the nanogit v0.12.0 rename
support to the already-implemented downstream rename handling
in incremental sync (RenameResourceFile).

Changes:
- Pass WithRenameDetection() to CompareCommits
- Handle FileStatusRenamed with path boundary logic:
  both inside = rename, only new inside = create,
  only old inside = delete, both outside = skip
- Skip tree/directory entries (mode 0o40000)
- Fix CompareCommitsArgsForCall for nanogit v0.12.0 signature
- Add unit tests for all rename path-boundary scenarios
- Add integration test for git mv + incremental sync

Ref: https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/990
Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add integration tests for folder moves and renames in incremental sync

Cover move/rename scenarios: root-to-folder, folder-to-folder,
folder-to-root, whole-folder rename, nested dashboard move, and
nested folder rename.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix orphaned folder cleanup for renames and move test helpers to common

The rename case in applyIncrementalChanges was tracking the new folder
path for orphaned folder cleanup instead of the old one. This meant
source folders that became empty after a rename were never checked
and never cleaned up.

Also moves RequireDashboards, RequireRepoFolders, RequireDashboardCount,
and RequireDashboardTitle helpers to the common test package so they can
be reused across test suites.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Add folder assertions after initial sync in incremental tests

Assert the expected managed folders after the initial syncAndWait in
all rename/move integration tests, ensuring the baseline folder state
is verified before the incremental operation under test.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Update metadata_name_change_test to use common test helpers

Migrate requireDashboardCount and requireDashboardTitle calls to the
shared common package after the helpers were moved from the git package.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Fix initial folder assertions to include .keep-created folders

The .keep files also trigger folder creation during full sync, so
folder-b and parent/child-b exist as managed folders from the start.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Skip tree entry renames in CompareFiles

When CompareCommits emits a rename for a directory (mode 0o40000),
skip it rather than emitting FileActionRenamed. Tree entries sent
downstream to RenameResourceFile cause parse/validation errors
because they are not resource files. Individual file renames within
renamed directories are already reported as separate entries.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Decompose tree entry renames into delete + create

Instead of skipping directory rename entries (mode 0o40000), decompose
them into a FileActionDeleted for the old path and a FileActionCreated
for the new path. This preserves folder bookkeeping through the normal
incremental sync path while avoiding RenameResourceFile parse errors.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Alerting: extend admin_config API with ExternalAlertmanagerUID and partial-update semantics
body:
## Context

Extends `POST/GET /api/v1/ngalert/admin_config` to store and expose the UID of a Mimir/Cortex Alertmanager datasource. This is the data-layer foundation for the background remote-AM config sync feature; no sync logic is included here.

Also fixes a footgun in the existing API: omitting `alertmanagersChoice` from a POST body previously silently overwrote the stored value with the zero value. Fields are now pointers with `omitempty` so absent fields mean "no change".

Related to https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/119910

## Changes

**Model / migration**
- `AdminConfiguration.SendAlertsTo` promoted to `*AlertmanagersChoice`
- `AdminConfiguration.ExternalAlertmanagerUID *string` added
- New nullable `remote_alertmanager_uid` column in `ngalert_configuration`
- `sender/router.go` updated to dereference `SendAlertsTo` (defaults to zero value when nil)

**Store**
- `UpdateAdminConfiguration` replaced `AllCols().Update()` with a `buildUpdateCols` helper that only includes columns for non-nil pointer fields, preventing silent overwrites of unset fields

**API types**
- `PostableNGalertConfig.AlertmanagersChoice` _ `*AlertmanagersChoice`
- `ExternalAlertmanagerUID *string` added to both Postable and Gettable types

**API handler**
- POST returns 400 if all pointer fields are nil (nothing to update)
- GET conditionally sets fields based on nil checks

**Tests**
- `TestExternalAlertmanagerChoice` updated for pointer fields
author: titolins

reviews.body:
Thanks for addressing my previous comments!

I don't agree with suddenly allowing partial updates via POST requests to this endpoint tbh. It would be better to either not allow partial updates or change the method to PATCH.

Also, from the PR's description:
> When `FlagAlertingRemoteAMConfigSync` is enabled, POST validates that the supplied UID refers to an `alertmanager`-type datasource with `implementation=mimir|cortex`; empty string explicitly clears the setting

We're dropping the UID when `POST`ing 😬 

Don't forget to update the title and description with `remote -> external`!
All (important) comments addressed. LGTM! 🚀

commits.message:
Alerting: extend admin_config API with RemoteAlertmanagerUID and partial-update semantics

Add RemoteAlertmanagerUID to AdminConfiguration (model, DB column, API
types) and change the admin_config POST handler to partial-
update semantics _ only non-nil pointer fields are persisted, so callers
can change one setting without overwriting others. AlertmanagersChoice
is promoted to a pointer for the same reason.
don't accept setting remote alertmanager uid for now

title: E2E: Fix table-nested test timeout and hover state
body:
## What is this feature?

Fixes a flaky Playwright e2e test that fails in CI with `Target page, context or browser has been closed`. The test performs many sequential UI interactions on a nested table, which renders slower than flat tables, causing it to exceed the default 30-second timeout in CI.

## Why do we need this feature?

The test was timing out and being killed mid-assertion. This fix provides adequate time for all interactions to complete and ensures the cell actions are properly visible before attempting to click the inspect button.

## Special notes for your reviewer:

- `test.slow()` triples the timeout from 30s to 90s for this test
- Added explicit hover before clicking "Inspect value" to ensure cell actions CSS is active

clients do not parse the error message out of the response. This is especially
problematic because we use 403 for quota errors, but show up as permission errors

title: Provisioning: use folder metadata checksum to update Folder title in Full sync
body:
**What is this feature?**

Detect and reconcile `_folder.json` title changes during full sync.

When a user edits the title in `_folder.json` and triggers a full sync, Grafana now detects the change via hash comparison and updates the folder's title to match. This works even when `_folder.json` is the only file that changed in the repository.

Two coordinated fixes:

1. **Change detection** (`changes.go`): when `_folder.json` exists in the source tree and its hash differs from the stored `sourceChecksum` on the Grafana folder, a `FileActionUpdated` change is emitted for the parent directory. This ensures the folder enters the apply path;
2. **Tree early-return relaxation** (`folders.go`): `EnsureFolderPathExist` now compares metadata hashes via `tree.Get()` instead of just checking `tree.In()`. When the stored hash matches the current `_folder.json` hash, the folder is skipped (no change). When they differ, the code falls through to `EnsureFolderExists` which reconciles the title and updates the stored checksum. Ancestor folders with matching hashes are still skipped — only the changed folder is re-evaluated.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Before this change, editing `_folder.json` (e.g. changing a folder's display title) was silently ignored during full sync. Two independent gaps caused this:

- `Changes()` skipped `_folder.json` entirely — it was added to the "keep" trie but never compared, so no change was emitted. If `_folder.json` was the only edit, the sync exited with "no changes to sync".
- `EnsureFolderPathExist` checked `tree.In()` and returned early for any folder already in the tree. Since the tree is pre-populated from Grafana's state at sync start, existing folders were always skipped — `EnsureFolderExists` (which does the actual title reconciliation) was never called.

**Who is this feature for?**

Users of GitSync

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/936

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [x] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [x] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Provisioning: Bump nanogit to v0.12.0 for rename support
body:
## Summary

Updates nanogit from v0.7.0 to v0.12.0 to add support for file rename detection in git operations.

## Motivation

This update is a prerequisite for implementing less disruptive pulling for file renames in the provisioning system. Currently, when files are renamed in git, the system treats them as a delete + add operation. With rename detection support, we can handle renames more gracefully in a follow-up PR, preserving file history and reducing unnecessary churn in the provisioning workflow.

The rename detection feature was implemented in [grafana/nanogit#242](https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/issues/242).

## Changes from v0.7.0 to v0.12.0

**Features:**
- ✨ Add directory/tree rename detection support ([#245](https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/issues/245)) - v0.12.0
- ✨ Add optional rename detection to `CompareCommits` ([#243](https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/issues/243)) - v0.11.0
- Add CLI check command to verify server protocol compatibility ([#232](https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/issues/232)) - v0.10.0

**Bug Fixes:**
- Update testcontainers-go dependency to v0.41.0 - v0.12.0
- CI improvements for CHANGELOG generation - v0.10.0

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/compare/v0.7.0...v0.12.0

## Changes in this PR

- Bumped `github.com/grafana/nanogit` from v0.7.0 to v0.12.0 in both main go.mod and apps/provisioning/go.mod
- Updated go.sum files accordingly
- Ran `make update-workspace` to sync all workspace modules

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify existing provisioning tests pass
- [ ] Verify git operations continue to work as expected
- [ ] Rename detection functionality will be implemented and tested in follow-up PR

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MissingRoberto

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix tests for nanogit v0.12.0 API changes

The CompareCommits method now accepts a variadic options parameter for
rename detection. Updated test to handle the 4th return value from

Add FileStatusRenamed case for exhaustive linter

Added explicit handling for the new FileStatusRenamed status introduced

is implemented in follow-up PR.

title: Provisioning: skips jobs for repositories that are pending deletion
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Job controller skip jobs if the associated repository is pending deletion.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Repositories that are pending deletion should not cause side effect as they belong to soft-deleted stacks. After a period of time the stack will be hard-delete. Jobs can cause side effects (e.g. a pull job could create grafana resources for such a delete stack) which should be avoided.

**Who is this feature for?**

Provisioning feature

title: Unified Storage: Refactor manager update check to compare only kind and identity
body:
Follow-up to #120521. Addresses [review feedback](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120521/changes/BASE..0f026d4e72d93e597ee3b5250c3eefad332cbbcd#r2952669641).

## Problem

The manager change check in `checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec` compared the full `ManagerProperties` struct, which meant updating operational flags like `AllowsEdits` or `Suspended` on an existing manager would be rejected as a "manager change". It also had a confusing control flow: an early-exit based on `AnnoKeyManagerKind`, followed by an intermediate `enforceManagerProperties` call, then separate `ok`-based branches that were hard to reason about.

## Changes

Refactors `checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec` into a linear flow with clear early returns for each transition:

1. **Not managed** (`!hasNew && !hasOld`) -- return nil
2. **Removing** (`!hasNew && hasOld`) -- authorize against the old manager, return
3. **Owner changed** (`hasOld && kind/identity differ`) -- reject with Forbidden
4. **Adding or updating flags** -- authorize against the new manager

Key improvements:
- Compares only `Kind` and `Identity`, not the full struct. Updating `AllowsEdits` or `Suspended` no longer requires a remove-then-add workflow.
- Uses `GetManagerProperties()` as the single source of truth for whether a resource is managed, instead of the previous mismatch where the early-exit checked `AnnoKeyManagerKind` but `GetManagerProperties` keys off `AnnoKeyManagerIdentity`.
- `enforceManagerProperties` is called once at the end for the common path (add / same-owner update), instead of being scattered across three branches.
- `.Kind` and `.Identity` are only accessed when `hasOld` is true, avoiding reliance on zero-value struct semantics.

## Test plan

Existing unit tests in `managed_test.go` and integration tests in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/managed_test.go` (from #120521) cover all transitions and pass without changes.
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: LGTM, thank you.

commits.message:
Apistore: refactor manager change check with switch, compare only kind/identity

Refactors checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec into a clear switch on the
four (hasOld, hasNew) transitions:

- (!hasNew, !hasOld): not managed, nothing to check
- (hasNew, !hasOld): adding a manager, validate caller permissions
- (!hasNew, hasOld): removing a manager, validate caller owns the old one
- (hasNew, hasOld): block kind/identity changes, allow flag updates

Addresses review feedback from #120521: compares only Kind and Identity
instead of the full ManagerProperties struct, so changing operational
flags like AllowsEdits or Suspended does not require remove-then-add.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Alerting: Remove DELETE and restrict GET/history legacy Alertmanager config API endpoints
body:
## Summary

Removes and restricts legacy Alertmanager config API endpoints that were deprecated in Grafana v12 and rely on globally-scoped RBAC actions incompatible with the resource-level permissions model.

**DELETE `/api/alertmanager/grafana/config/api/v1/alerts`** — removed entirely. No usage detected since deprecation in v12.

**GET `/api/alertmanager/grafana/config/api/v1/alerts`** — restricted to admin-only. Adds a `Warning: 299` response header to surface any remaining automated consumers. Full removal planned for v14.

**GET `/api/alertmanager/grafana/config/history`** and **POST `/api/alertmanager/grafana/config/history/{id}/_activate`** — also restricted to admin-only, under the same new permission scope.

Two new admin-only actions gate the restricted endpoints:
- `alert.notifications.config-history:read` — GET config + GET history
- `alert.notifications.config-history:write` — POST history activate

Adds v13 breaking changes docs entry.

## Release notice breaking change

`DELETE /api/alertmanager/grafana/config/api/v1/alerts` has been removed. `GET /api/alertmanager/grafana/config/api/v1/alerts`, `GET /api/alertmanager/grafana/config/history`, and `POST /api/alertmanager/grafana/config/history/{id}/_activate` now require admin permissions. All were deprecated in v12.

Migration: use the `notifications.alerting.grafana.app/v0alpha1` Kubernetes APIs for receivers, routing trees, templates, time intervals, and inhibition rules.
author: titolins

reviews.body: FE code changes LGTM!

replacing the broad alert.notifications:read/write actions.

title: FS: Support multiple parallel Tilt instances via PORT and WORKSPACE_NAME
body:
**What is this feature?**

This PR enables running multiple frontend-service instances simultaneously without port or resource name conflicts, which is useful for parallel (agentic) development workflows.

The services in the `make frontend-service` tiltfile now use a contiguous block of ports, starting from port 3000. Setting the `PORT=7000 make frontend-service` will change the range of ports used, running Grafana itself on port 7000.

Additionally, setting the env var `WORKSPACE_NAME` will be used as a suffix for the docker compose project name. 

These two changes make it possible to run multiple parallel instances of the development stack locally, perfect for multiple-agent development workflows with tools like Conductor. e.g. in one folder run `WORKSPACE_NAME=capybara PORT=7000 make frontend-service` and have Grafana running on port 7000, and in another run `WORKSPACE_NAME=zebra PORT=8000 make frontend-service` and have Grafana on port 8000.
author: joshhunt

reviews.body: LGTM! 🚀

commits.message:
FS: Support multiple parallel Tilt instances via PORT and WORKSPACE_NAME

Configure frontend-service Tilt setup to support running multiple instances in parallel across Conductor workspaces. Uses PORT environment variable (typically provided by Conductor) to derive all container ports dynamically (+0 to +9 range, with Tilt UI on +9). WORKSPACE_NAME environment variable (defaults to 'dev') scopes Docker project names and cookie names to prevent conflicts.

- Remove hardcoded ports from docker-compose.yaml, set them dynamically in Tiltfile
- Derive Tilt UI port from PORT+9 in run.sh
- Use workspace-scoped Docker project names (grafana-fs-{WORKSPACE_NAME})
- Scope login cookies and other resources by workspace name
- Log Grafana URL on startup

title: Chore: Bump Ubuntu base image from 22.04 to 24.04
body:
## Summary

- Bumps the Ubuntu base image from 22.04 (Jammy) to 24.04 LTS (Noble Numbat) across all Docker build files
- Ubuntu 24.04 has been available since April 2024 and is supported until 2029
- This brings glibc from 2.35 to 2.39, along with newer system packages and security patches

## Changes

All 6 occurrences of `ubuntu:22.04` updated to `ubuntu:24.04`:

| File | Context |
|------|---------|
| `Dockerfile` | Main `ubuntu-base` stage |
| `Makefile` | `build-docker-full-ubuntu` target |
| `packaging/docker/build.sh` | Packaging script |
| `pkg/build/daggerbuild/artifacts/package_targz.go` | Dagger e2e test container |
| `pkg/build/daggerbuild/fpm/verify.go` | Dagger .deb verification container |
| `scripts/verify-repo-update/Dockerfile.deb` | .deb repo update verification |

## Motivation

- Ubuntu 22.04 ships glibc 2.35. Some community plugins now require glibc >= 2.38, which is only available on Ubuntu 24.04+
- Alpine (the default base) is regularly bumped by Dependabot (currently 3.23.3), but Dependabot doesn't auto-bump across Ubuntu LTS tracks
- The last Ubuntu bump (18.04 → 22.04) was in November 2022 — over 3 years ago

## Test plan

- [x] CI passes (the Docker build, .deb verification, and tarball e2e tests all exercise the Ubuntu image)
- [ ] Verify `grafana/grafana:*-ubuntu` images start correctly
- [ ] Verify no regressions in `apt-get install` steps (ca-certificates, curl, tzdata, musl)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

title: Provisioning: set checksum on provisioned folders
body:
**What is this feature?**

Store the `_folder.json` file hash on the Grafana folder object as `sourceChecksum` (`grafana.app/sourceChecksum` annotation).

When a folder is created or updated during sync, and it has a `_folder.json` metadata file, the SHA-1 hash of that file's content is persisted alongside the folder's other source properties. This is a data-only change — no new sync behavior is triggered by the hash yet.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Today, when a user edits `_folder.json` (e.g. changes the folder title) and no other files in that directory change, the full sync silently ignores the edit. There is no mechanism to detect that `_folder.json` changed because its hash is never stored.

This PR lays the foundation for detecting those changes. By persisting the hash now, follow-up PRs can cheaply compare the stored hash against the source tree's hash during sync — without re-reading the file — and trigger reconciliation only when something actually changed. Without this stored hash, there is nothing to compare against.

**Who is this feature for?**

Users of GitSync

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/936

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [x] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [x] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Unified Storage: Enforce folder manager consistency for repository managed folders
body:
Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/988

## Summary

- Moves the folder-manager consistency check from the dashboard admission layer to the unified storage apistore layer, making it apply to **all resources with folder support** (dashboards, nested folders, etc.)
- Only enforces for **repository-managed folders** (`ManagerKindRepo`). Folders managed by terraform, kubectl, or other manager kinds are not checked.
- On **create**: always validates that the resource's manager matches the containing folder's manager.
- On **update**: validates when the folder annotation changes (resource is being moved) **or** when manager properties change/are removed while the resource stays in the same folder. This prevents resources from becoming unmanaged or managed-by-another while remaining under a repo-managed folder.
- Returns `403 Forbidden` when a resource's manager does not match its containing folder's repo manager.
- Refactors the provisioning finalizer to release resources **top-down** (folders shallowest-first, then resources) so the consistency invariant is maintained during repository deletion.

## Why K8s dynamic client instead of `store.Read()`

The parent-folder lookup in `getParentFolder()` uses a K8s dynamic client (REST call to the API server) rather than the internal `s.store.Read()` (gRPC `ResourceClient`). This is an intentional design choice:

- **`s.store` is group-scoped in legacy/dual-write modes.** In these modes, the `ResourceClient` is backed by `DashboardSQLAccess`, which validates every read key against the dashboard group via `isDashboardKey()`. A cross-group read to `folder.grafana.app` is rejected with _"expecting dashboard group (folder.grafana.app != dashboard.grafana.app)"_.
- **In unified mode, `s.store` could read any group** — the universal gRPC client carries explicit `Group`/`Resource` fields in `ResourceKey`. However, using it would require mode detection and two code paths.
- **The K8s dynamic client provides a single, uniform code path** that works whenever the API server is available. The request is routed through standard K8s API layer to the folder group's own handler, regardless of the caller's storage mode.
- **When `configProvider` is nil** (legacy/dual-write modes where the REST config is not available), the `getDynClient` field is left nil, `getParentFolder` returns early, and the check is skipped. A warning is logged once at startup.

## Performance impact

This change introduces an **additional K8s dynamic client call** for every resource that has `EnableFolderSupport` and a non-empty folder annotation:

- **On create**: one extra read to fetch the parent folder's metadata.
- **On update**: one extra read when the folder annotation changes or when manager properties change/are removed.
- **Resources without folder support**: no impact — the check is skipped entirely via `EnableFolderSupport`.
- **Resources with no folder annotation**: no impact — the check short-circuits before any read.

The dynamic client is lazily initialized and cached (via `sync.Once`), accepting the caller's context so that the REST config resolution respects request deadlines. Lazy initialization is necessary because the REST config is not available during API group installation.

Currently, resources with folder support include **dashboards** and **folders** (nested folders).

## Validation matrix (for repo-managed folders)

| Folder state | Resource state | Trigger | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Unmanaged | Unmanaged | create / update | OK |
| Unmanaged | Managed | create / update | OK |
| Repo-managed | Same repo manager | create / update | OK |
| Repo-managed | Unmanaged | create / update | **403 Forbidden** |
| Repo-managed | Different manager | create / update | **403 Forbidden** |

The check triggers on update when:
1. The folder annotation changes (resource is being moved), **or**
2. Manager properties change or are removed while the resource stays in the same folder (e.g. a user attempts to strip manager annotations from a repo-managed resource).

## Changes

### Unified Storage (apistore)

- `pkg/storage/unified/apistore/managed.go` — Added `ensureSameRepoManager` pure validation function, scoped to `ManagerKindRepo`.
- `pkg/storage/unified/apistore/prepare.go` — Added `ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder` (gates on `EnableFolderSupport`, fetches parent folder via K8s dynamic client, delegates to `ensureSameRepoManager`). Wired into create and update paths. On update, triggers when folder changes **or** when manager properties change/are removed in the same folder.
- `pkg/storage/unified/apistore/store.go` — Added `getDynClient func(ctx context.Context) (dynamic.Interface, error)` field, lazily initialized with `sync.Once` using the caller's context. Logs a warning at startup if `configProvider` is nil.
- `pkg/storage/unified/apistore/prepare_test.go` — Unit tests for `ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder`, `getParentFolder`, and manager-change-in-same-folder scenarios.
- `pkg/storage/unified/apistore/managed_test.go` — Updated tests for repo-only enforcement.

### Dashboard admission (removed)

- `pkg/registry/apis/dashboard/register.go` — Removed redundant `validateFolderManagedBySameManager` and its call sites from dashboard admission hooks.
- `pkg/registry/apis/dashboard/register_test.go` — Removed corresponding tests.

### Provisioning finalizer (release order)

- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/controller/finalizers.go` — Split `processExistingItems` into two dedicated functions:
  - `deleteExistingItems` — deletes non-folder resources concurrently first, then folders sequentially deepest-first (bottom-up, so folders are empty before removal).
  - `releaseExistingItems` — releases folders sequentially shallowest-first (top-down), then non-folder resources concurrently. This ensures parent folders are unmanaged before their children are released, satisfying the repo-manager consistency invariant during repository deletion.
  - Extracted reusable `processFolderItems` (sequential) and `processResourceItems` (concurrent) helpers.
  - Added `sortResourceListForRelease` — sorts all items top-down by depth; at equal depth, folders before resources.
- `pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/controller/finalizers_test.go` — Added ordering tests (`TestDeleteExistingItems_ResourcesBeforeFolders`, `TestReleaseExistingItems_FoldersBeforeResources`), concurrency test (`TestReleaseExistingItems_ResourcesConcurrent`), and sort tests (`TestSortResourceListForRelease`).

## Related

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120521

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: `TestEnsureSameRepoManager` — table-driven cases covering repo-managed, terraform-managed, kubectl-managed folders and various resource states
- [x] Unit tests: `TestPrepareObjectForStorage` — existing tests plus new cases for `getDynClient` nil/error, manager-change-in-same-folder, manager-addition, and manager-removal scenarios
- [x] Unit tests: `TestSortResourceListForRelease` — verifies top-down depth ordering with folders before resources at equal depth
- [x] Unit tests: `TestDeleteExistingItems_ResourcesBeforeFolders` — verifies resources are deleted before folders (bottom-up)
- [x] Unit tests: `TestReleaseExistingItems_FoldersBeforeResources` — verifies folders are released before resources (top-down)
- [x] Unit tests: `TestReleaseExistingItems_ResourcesConcurrent` — verifies non-folder resources are released concurrently
- [x] Integration tests: `TestIntegrationFolderManagerConsistency` — scenarios covering dashboards and nested folders in managed/unmanaged folders, including move operations (unmanaged-to-managed, managed-to-unmanaged)
- [x] Integration tests: `TestIntegrationProvisioning_BlockManagerChange` — verifies manager removal from repo-managed dashboard is blocked


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

## Pull request overview

**Changes:**
- Added unified-storage layer validation to ensure resources in **repo-managed folders** have matching repo manager annotations (create + relevant update paths).
- Introduced lazy initialization of a Kubernetes dynamic client for reading parent folder metadata during validation.
- Refactored provisioning finalizer processing to release folders before resources (and delete resources before folders), with additional unit/integration tests.

commits.message:
Unified Storage: Enforce folder-manager consistency on create and update

Move folder-manager consistency validation from the dashboard admission
layer to the unified storage apistore layer, making it apply to all
resources with folder support. When a folder is managed, any resource
created or moved into it must be managed by the same manager (same Kind
and Identity). Creating managed resources in unmanaged folders is
allowed.

Made-with: Cursor

Add integration tests for folder-manager consistency

Integration tests in pkg/tests/apis/provisioning that verify:
- Unmanaged dashboard in a managed folder is rejected (403)
- Dashboard managed by a different manager in a managed folder is rejected (403)
- Managed dashboard in an unmanaged folder is allowed
- Unmanaged dashboard in an unmanaged folder is allowed

Made-with: Cursor

Add nested folder cases to folder-manager consistency integration tests

Cover the same validation matrix for sub-folders nested inside managed
and unmanaged parent folders: reject unmanaged or differently-managed
sub-folders inside a managed parent, allow any sub-folder inside an
unmanaged parent.

Made-with: Cursor

Fix nil store panic in checkFolderManager

Skip the folder-manager consistency check when s.store is nil, which
happens in unit tests that construct a Storage without a backing store.

Made-with: Cursor

Use K8s dynamic client for cross-group folder lookup

The resource store (s.store) is scoped to a single resource group, so
reading folder resources from a dashboard store fails with "expecting
dashboard group". Switch to using the configProvider's REST config with
a dynamic K8s client to read folders via the API server, which works
across all dual-write modes. Falls back to skipping the check when
configProvider is nil (unit tests).

Made-with: Cursor

Unified Storage: only check folder-manager consistency on folder change

During repository deletion, the release process removes manager annotations
from resources. The unconditional folder-manager check on updates was blocking
this by rejecting the now-unmanaged resource against its still-managed folder.
Restrict the check to only fire when the folder annotation actually changed
(i.e., a resource is being moved to a different folder).

Made-with: Cursor

fix integration test: remove unnecessary file copy from managed_test.go

The test only needs a managed folder (the repo root), not a dashboard
inside it. The Copies map was creating a dashboard and subfolder that
didn't match the expected counts (0 dashboards, 1 folder).

Made-with: Cursor

Merge origin/main and resolve conflicts

- prepare.go: keep main's placement of checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec
  (outside hasChanged block), retain folder-manager check inside it
- managed_test.go: keep both TestIntegrationFolderManagerConsistency and
  TestIntegrationProvisioning_BlockManagerChange from main

Made-with: Cursor

Scope folder-manager consistency check to repo-managed folders only

Only enforce folder-manager consistency when the folder's manager kind
is ManagerKindRepo. Folders managed by terraform, kubectl, or other
manager kinds are not checked.

Made-with: Cursor

Use store.Read() for folder lookup instead of K8s API

Replace the dynamic client approach (which required configProvider and
made a full API server round-trip) with a direct s.store.Read() call
using a ResourceKey for folder.grafana.app/folders. This reads from
unified storage directly, consistent with how other store operations
work.

Made-with: Cursor

Move folder-manager check to folder-change detection site

Place checkFolderManager right where the folder change is detected
(replacing the TODO comment), instead of re-checking the condition
inside the hasChanged block. This eliminates a redundant
GetFolder() != previous.GetFolder() comparison.

Made-with: Cursor

Simplify folder-manager check API

- getFolderManagerProperties → getFolder(): returns the full
  GrafanaMetaAccessor instead of extracted properties, making it
  reusable for future folder checks.
- checkFolderManagerConsistency → checkFolderManager(): takes two
  accessors (folder, resource) and handles nil folder internally.
- Restore the TODO comment at the folder-change detection site.

Made-with: Cursor

Rename getFolder → getParentFolder, flatten if-else-if style

Made-with: Cursor

Extract ensureManagedByParentFolder as self-contained method

Wrap folder fetch + manager validation into a single Storage method
that gates on EnableFolderSupport. Rename the pure validation function
to ensureSameManager for clarity.

Made-with: Cursor

Rename to ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder / ensureSameRepoManager

Make it clear the check only applies to repository-managed folders.

Made-with: Cursor

Consolidate no-parent-folder guard into ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder

All preconditions (folder support, folder annotation, store available)
are checked in one place. getParentFolder and ensureSameRepoManager
no longer need nil guards — they always receive valid inputs.

Made-with: Cursor

Use folders.FolderResourceInfo for folder group/resource constants

Made-with: Cursor

Assert specific error messages in integration tests

Verify the 403 Forbidden responses contain the expected error messages
from ensureSameRepoManager, including the folder/resource manager
identities.

Made-with: Cursor

Fix goimports ordering in prepare.go

Made-with: Cursor

Use K8s dynamic client for cross-group folder lookup

The store.Read() approach fails in dual-writer modes 0 and 1 because
the ResourceClient is scoped to the current resource group and rejects
reads for folder.grafana.app. Switch back to using configProvider with
a dynamic client which works across all dual-writer modes.

Made-with: Cursor

Make parent folder read non-fatal for repo-manager consistency check

In some dual-writer modes (0, 1), the folder API is not reachable
from the storage layer due to group-scoped legacy backends. Instead
of blocking the operation, log a warning and skip the check. Folder
existence is already validated by admission hooks.

Made-with: Cursor

Add unit tests for ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder

Tests cover the three short-circuit conditions (folder support
disabled, empty folder, nil configProvider), the non-fatal error
path when the folder read fails, and end-to-end through both
prepareObjectForStorage and prepareObjectForUpdate.

Made-with: Cursor

Hard-fail when parent folder read fails

Return the error from getParentFolder instead of swallowing it.
Update unit tests to expect errors when configProvider fails.

Made-with: Cursor

Warn on nil configProvider and cache the dynamic client

- Log a warning when configProvider is nil and the folder-manager
  check would otherwise apply, so the bypass is never silent.
- Cache the K8s dynamic client via sync.Once instead of creating
  a new one on every getParentFolder call.

Made-with: Cursor

Use assert.NoError inside EventuallyWithT to avoid FailNow panic

require.NoError with assert.CollectT short-circuits the polling loop
due to FailNow semantics. Switch to assert.NoError with early return
to keep EventuallyWithT retry behavior reliable.

Made-with: Cursor

Add update-path tests for moving dashboards between folders

- reject moving unmanaged dashboard to managed folder (forbidden)
- allow moving dashboard to unmanaged folder (folder change via update)

Made-with: Cursor

Enforce folder-manager consistency on manager annotation changes

The parent-folder repo-manager check only ran when the folder reference
changed. A resource's manager annotations could change (e.g. removal by
provisioning identity) while staying in the same repo-managed folder,
bypassing the invariant. Now ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder also fires
when manager properties change without a folder move.

Also refactors the dynamic client: replaces three struct fields
(dynClient, dynClientOnce, dynClientErr) with a single sync.OnceValues
closure, moves the nil-configProvider warning to NewStorage init, and
adds unit + integration tests for the new code path.

Made-with: Cursor

Allow manager removal on update to unblock repo deletion

The manager-change-in-same-folder check was also firing on manager
removal, which blocked the provisioning system from releasing resources
during repository deletion. Now only manager addition or identity change
triggers ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder; removal is intentionally
allowed.

Made-with: Cursor

Block unmanaged resources in managed folders; fix release order

Reverts the manager-removal exemption — all manager changes (including
removal) are checked against the parent folder's manager. Unmanaged
resources must not exist in repo-managed folders.

To support repository deletion, the release finalizer now processes
folders before resources (top-down, shallowest first) so parent folders
are unmanaged before their children are released. The removal finalizer
keeps the existing bottom-up order (resources first, deepest folders
first) since folders must be empty before deletion.

Made-with: Cursor

Refactor processExistingItems to accept a sort strategy function

Replace the processOrder enum with a direct sort function parameter,
letting callers pass sortResourceListForDeletion or the new
sortResourceListForRelease. The processing loop now walks items in
sorted order, batching consecutive non-folder items for concurrent
execution and processing folders sequentially.

Made-with: Cursor

Add unit tests for sortResourceListForRelease

Made-with: Cursor

Split processExistingItems into deleteExistingItems and releaseExistingItems

deleteExistingItems removes non-folder resources concurrently first,
then folders sequentially deepest-first so they are empty before removal.

releaseExistingItems walks items top-down by depth, releasing folders
sequentially and batching consecutive non-folder resources concurrently,
so parent folders are unmanaged before their children.

Made-with: Cursor

Simplify finalizer processing with dedicated delete and release functions

Replace the generic processExistingItems with deleteExistingItems and
releaseExistingItems, each building their callback internally. Extract
reusable processFolderItems (sequential) and processResourceItems
(concurrent) helpers. Add ordering and concurrency tests for both paths.

Made-with: Cursor

Pass caller context to getDynClient and consolidate nil checks

Change getDynClient signature to func(ctx context.Context) so the
caller's deadline/cancellation flows through to GetRestConfig instead
of using an unbounded context.Background().

Consolidate the three separate dynamic-client nil checks into a single
guard in getParentFolder, which returns (nil, nil) when unavailable.
ensureRepoManagedByParentFolder treats a nil folder as skip.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Alerting: Update provisioning APIs to accept resource-specific permissions
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**
This PR updates provisioning API endpoints to accept resource specific permissions. To perform modify methods, user has to have a permission `alert.provisioning.provenance:write` in addition to write permissions for a particular resource.

For example, a user with permissions `alert.notifications.receivers:create` and  `alert.provisioning.provenance:write` are able to create new receivers via provisioning API.  
To update an integration in a receiver with UID `A` user should have `alert.notifications.receivers:write` in the scope of the receiver UID + `alert.provisioning.provenance:write` (+ in some cases `alert.notifications.receivers.protected:write` in the same scope)
The same semantics is applicable to other resources.

**Why do we need this feature?**
- Match the semantics of provisioning rules API
- Will allow us to deprecate blanket scopeless actions such as  `alert.notifications:write|read`  `alert.provisioning:read|write` etc.

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: yuri-tceretian

title: Live: Add RBAC for pushing to live
body:
**What is this feature?**

This PR adds a `live:push` permission and grants it to Editors & Admins by default. Viewers can no longer push unless they are given the `live:push` permission.

Note: for those using the "Live" (alpha) panel, this will be a breaking change, and viewers will no longer be able to use the "Publish" button
i.e:
```
[panels]                                                                                                                                                                
enable_alpha = true 
```
And having a panel "Live", set-up with "Influx" as the Publish. 

This panel is meant for debugging live, so hopefully this is mainly used by admins / editors. If not, you can add the `live:push` permission to viewers.

title: A11y: Fix missing focus indicator on dashboard location items
body:
## Summary

- Removes `overflow: hidden` from `locationContainer` in `SearchResultsTable`, which was silently clipping the focus ring (box-shadow) from child `<a>` elements
- The parent `cell` already provides the overflow boundary; each `locationItem` handles its own text truncation via its own `overflow: hidden`
- No custom focus styles needed — the global `a:focus-visible` rule (`getFocusStyles`) now works naturally, identical to the name column

Fixes #118643 (WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible, Sev3)

## Why `overflow: hidden` was safe to remove

Checked git history back to the introduction of `locationContainer` in #70814. It was added alongside a flex layout refactor with no accompanying bug reference or comment — almost certainly precautionary rather than fixing a real overflow issue. The parent `cell` already clips this container.

## Test plan

- [ ] Tab to a location item in the dashboards browse table — focus ring should match the name column style
- [ ] Verify location text still truncates correctly for long folder paths
- [ ] Test both light and dark themes
author: tdbishop

commits.message:
A11y: Add focus-visible indicator to dashboard location items (#118643)

Location elements in the dashboards browse table lacked visible focus
styles, making keyboard navigation inaccessible (WCAG 2.4.7 violation).
Adds :focus-visible ring using theme tokens consistent with other
focusable elements in the codebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Secrets: Use updated field for making inactive secure values eligible for garbage collection
body:
The GC must not delete an in-flight version whose `created` timestamp was inherited from an older version.

The added test simulates the race where GC runs between `metadata.Create` (inactive) and `SetVersionToActive` during `createNewVersion`.

Without the fix, which uses `updated` instead of `created` in the lease query, the inherited `created` timestamp makes the new version immediately GC-eligible.
author: macabu

reviews.body: nice!

commits.message: Secrets: Use updated field for making inactive secure values eligible for garbage collection

title: Remote Alertmanager: Remove silence UID from metrics
body:
This PR removes silence UIDs from the `operation` label, reducing the cardinality of the `grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_*` metrics.

Before the change:
```
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="DELETE /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/55f6f4ab-4a5e-44a9-8d72-9aa68a5ab829",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 
...
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="DELETE /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/7661ed30-d12f-4de7-b071-f7cb8d6597b9",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 1
...
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="DELETE /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/a8829149-3475-4ca3-9460-e91f85829d59",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 1
...
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="GET /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/55f6f4ab-4a5e-44a9-8d72-9aa68a5ab829",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 1
...
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="GET /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/7661ed30-d12f-4de7-b071-f7cb8d6597b9",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 1
...
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="GET /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/a8829149-3475-4ca3-9460-e91f85829d59",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 1
...
```

After the change:
```
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="DELETE /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/{uid}",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 2
...
grafana_alerting_remote_alertmanager_latency_seconds_bucket{operation="GET /alertmanager/api/v2/silence/{uid}",status_code="200",le="0.005"} 2
...
```

**What is this feature?**

Provisioning should not reconcile connection if they're labelled as pending delete.

Pending delete label implies a soft-deletion happened for the resource, so it should be treated as effectively deleted for all side-effects and system implications.

title: Unified Storage: Block changing manager properties
body:
## Motivation

Previously, an update could silently change a resource's manager from one value to another (e.g., `repo:abc` to `terraform:xyz`). This opened the door to accidental collisions between two competing managers — for example, a Terraform apply could unintentionally take over a resource that was already managed by a repository, or vice versa, leading to unpredictable reconciliation conflicts and data loss.

## What changed

`checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec` now rejects any update that changes manager properties when both the old and new objects have a manager set. Only two transitions are allowed:

- **Add**: unmanaged -> managed (no manager to a manager)
- **Remove**: managed -> unmanaged (a manager to no manager)

Direct changes (managed -> differently managed) return a `403 Forbidden` with the message:
> Cannot change resource manager; remove the existing manager first, then add the new one

This enforces an explicit two-step workflow (remove, then add) that makes manager ownership transfers intentional and auditable, preventing silent takeovers by competing managers.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests in `managed_test.go`: 3 new cases covering identity change, kind change, and both — blocked even for the provisioning service account
- [ ] Integration test in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/managed_test.go`: provisions a dashboard via repo, attempts to change manager to kubectl/terraform, asserts Forbidden
- [ ] Integration test in `pkg/tests/apis/dashboard/integration/api_validation_test.go`: marks dashboard as kubectl-managed, rejects identity/kind changes, validates remove-then-add workflow
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Apistore: block changing manager properties, require remove-then-add workflow

Prevent updates that change resource manager properties from one value
to another (e.g., switching from repo:abc to terraform:xyz). Only adding
a manager (from none) and removing a manager (to none) are allowed.
This enforces a two-step workflow: remove the existing manager first,
then add the new one.

title: Alerting: Add protected fields authorization check to provisioning API
body:
## Summary

This PR adds authorization checks for protected fields (e.g., webhook URLs) when updating contact points through the provisioning API. Previously, users could modify protected fields through the provisioning API without the required `receivers:update.protected` permission, bypassing security controls that were in place for the receiver API.

## Changes

- Add `checkProtectedFields` method to `ContactPointService` that validates user has permission to modify protected fields before updates
- Export `MakeProtectedFieldsAuthzError` to share error formatting logic between services
- Add `EmbeddedContactPointToIntegration` conversion utility in `compat.go`
- Extract shared `CheckProtectedFieldsAuthorization` function to eliminate code duplication between `ReceiverService` and `ContactPointService`
- Add `ProtectedFieldsAuthz` interface for protected field authorization
- Add required permissions (`ActionAlertingReceiversRead`, `ActionAlertingReceiversUpdate`, `ActionAlertingReceiversUpdateProtected`) to file provisioner user
- Add comprehensive tests for protected field authorization

## Files Modified

- `pkg/services/ngalert/notifier/protected_fields.go` (NEW)
- `pkg/services/ngalert/notifier/errors.go`
- `pkg/services/ngalert/notifier/receiver_svc.go`
- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/compat.go`
- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/contactpoints.go`
- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/contactpoints_test.go`
- `pkg/services/ngalert/api/api_provisioning.go`
- `pkg/services/ngalert/api/api_provisioning_test.go`
- `pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go`
- `pkg/services/provisioning/alerting/contact_point_provisioner.go`
- `pkg/services/provisioning/alerting/rules_provisioner.go`
- `pkg/services/provisioning/provisioning.go`

## Testing

All existing tests pass, and new integration tests have been added covering:
- User without protected permission cannot modify protected fields
- User with protected permission can modify protected fields
- User without protected permission can modify non-protected fields
- Update with nil user returns error
- User without protected permission cannot rename and modify protected fields simultaneously
author: yuri-tceretian

title: Dashboard: Optimize DashboardDocumentBuilder by avoiding full JSON unmarshal
body:
## Summary
- Replace `unstructured.UnmarshalJSON` (which recursively parses the entire dashboard JSON including the huge `spec`) with a metadata-only partial unmarshal that skips `spec` entirely
- Only `metadata`, `apiVersion`, and `kind` are needed from the unstructured parse — the actual dashboard content is parsed separately via `ReadDashboardWithLogContext`
- Add benchmark test for `BuildDocument`

Relates to ticket: https://github.com/grafana/search-and-storage-team/issues/702

## Benchmark results (small 3KB test dashboard)

| Metric | Before (full unmarshal) | After (metadata-only) | Improvement |
|---|---|---|---|
| **ns/op** | ~33,000 | ~24,400 | **~26% faster** |
| **B/op** | 30,188 | 18,978 | **~37% less memory** |
| **allocs/op** | 586 | 379 | **~35% fewer allocs** |

Savings scale with dashboard size — production dashboards with large specs (100KB+) will see much larger improvements.

## Test plan
- [x] Existing `TestDashboardDocumentBuilder` snapshot test passes with unchanged output
- [x] All builder tests pass (`go test ./pkg/storage/unified/search/builders/...`)
- [x] New `BenchmarkDashboardBuildDocument` confirms improvement

title: Dashboard: Add runtime API version negotiation for dashboard.grafana.app
body:
Runtime API version negotiation for `dashboard.grafana.app`, enabling the frontend to discover and use stable API versions (v1/v2) when the backend graduates them from beta.

- **`DashboardAPIVersionResolver`** singleton calls `GET /apis/dashboard.grafana.app/` once, caches resolved versions, falls back to beta on failure and retries on next call
- **`getDashboardAPI()` is now async** — awaits version resolution before returning a client. All call sites updated.
- **v1.ts/v2.ts** read version from resolver instead of hardcoded `v1beta1`/`v2beta1`
- **`isV2StoredVersion`/`isV0V1StoredVersion`** updated to recognize stable versions
- **Debug logging** via `localStorage.setItem('grafana.debug.dashboardAPI', 'true')`

Design doc: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ft-dyqwdE_0mi7DvT1E2RFXNa8ZEYpiJbZkjfRSU5Oo/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.i450k8t7bja3

  ## Test
  - [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly
  - [x] ESLint + Prettier pass
  - [x] Unit tests pass (30 tests across resolver + dashboard_api)
  - [x] Regression test: discovery failure → recovery → clients rebuilt with correct versions
  - [x] Manual: enable debug logging, verify discovery request fires once and versions are logged
  - [x] Manual: load/save dashboards, verify API calls use correct version paths
  - [x] E2E: existing dashboard tests pass (no behavioral change when backend serves only beta versions)
author: dprokop

reviews.body:
✅  With the current backend, it returns beta versions.
✅  It retries in the next call in case the new backend arrives with MT Frontend active
✅  In case the discovery fails, with 500 still working with beta versions

So everything is very well safeguarded. LGTM!

Side note:
Have we considered not making the getDashboardAPI async and use dashboardAPIVersionResolver.resolve() in the Grafana app initialization? Even if the api is not resolved it won't break since it will use the beta one, but we avoid having a double network request and an small preformance degradation for the first `getDashboardAPI`

Looks great, I tested these two scenarios - because they were not checked off in the PR description - and they work as expected: 
- Manual: enable debug logging, verify discovery request fires once and versions are logged
- Manual: load/save dashboards, verify API calls use correct version paths

is now async, awaiting resolution before returning a client. Falls back
