title: Folders: Block setting ownerReferences on repository-managed folders
body:
Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/987

Context: https://raintank-corp.slack.com/archives/C091NJNABJA/p1770984078226609

## What and why

The Team Folders feature uses `ownerReferences` on folder metadata to associate a folder with a team. However, provisioning mechanisms (Git Sync, Terraform, etc.) don't yet support team folder ownership.

Since `ownerReferences` live in ObjectMeta, anyone can set them on a repo-managed folder via the K8s API, even though the UI already blocks it. Those references then get silently wiped on the next resync that touches the folder (e.g. a rename), leading to confusing behavior.

For Git Sync we only have problems with folders. For that reason, I have limited the scope of the PR to folders admission (instead of `checkManagerPropertiesOnUpdateSpec`) and Repository managed resources.

## How

New `validateOwnerReferencesOnManagedFolder` function in `pkg/registry/apis/folders/validate.go`, wired into the folder `Validate` admission hook in `register.go`:

- **Create**: Rejects if the folder is repo-managed and has `ownerReferences`.
- **Update**: Rejects if either the old or new folder is repo-managed and `ownerReferences` differ (compared via `apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual`).
- Returns `403 Forbidden` via `apierrors.NewForbidden`.
- Checks both old and new objects to cover cases where manager annotations are being added or removed alongside ownerRef changes.

The legacy folder API (`/api/folders`) is unaffected since it doesn't handle `ownerReferences` at all.

## Test plan

**Integration tests** in `pkg/tests/apis/provisioning/folderownerrefs_test.go`:

- `TestIntegrationFolderOwnerRefs_ProvisionedFolders` — Setting ownerReferences on a provisioned folder is blocked via both JSONPatch and full Update (403 Forbidden)
- `TestIntegrationFolderOwnerRefs_UnprovisionedFolders` — Setting ownerReferences succeeds on released folders (after repo deletion with `release-orphan-resources`) and on freshly created unmanaged folders
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: LGTM, thank you!

commits.message:
Folders: Block setting ownerReferences on provisioned folders

Provisioning mechanisms (Git Sync, Terraform, etc.) don't yet support
team folder ownership. Setting ownerReferences on managed folders via the
API bypasses the storage-layer manager check (which only fires on spec
changes) and results in ownerReferences being wiped on resync.

Add admission validation to reject ownerReference changes on folders
managed by provisioning, and integration tests to verify the behavior.

Made-with: Cursor

Return proper Forbidden status error from ownerRef validation

The admission Validate hook must return an apierrors.StatusError
(not a plain fmt.Errorf) so the K8s apiserver returns a 403 Forbidden
response instead of a 500 Internal Server Error.

Made-with: Cursor

Move ownerRef blocking to storage layer, scope to repository-managed resources

Instead of folder-specific admission validation, block ownerReferences
changes on repository-managed resources in the unified storage layer
(prepareObjectForUpdate). This returns a direct 403 Forbidden that
bypasses provisioning rerouting.

Adds unit tests for the new check and ownerRef-triggered generation
bumping. Updates integration tests to match the new error message.

Made-with: Cursor

Check both obj and previous for repo-manager annotations on ownerRef changes

The ownerRef block now checks both the new and previous object for
ManagerKindRepo annotations. This covers cases where manager annotations
are being added or removed alongside ownerReferences changes.

Made-with: Cursor

Extract isRepoManaged into a standalone function

Made-with: Cursor

Split ownerRef handling: storage layer bumps generation, folder admission blocks repo-managed

The storage layer (prepareObjectForUpdate) detects ownerReferences
changes and bumps generation, but does not block them — that would
reroute through provisioning instead of rejecting.

The folder admission Validate hook blocks ownerReferences changes on
repo-managed folders with a direct 403 Forbidden, scoped to folders
only since that is where the Team Folders problem exists.

Made-with: Cursor

Remove ownerRef change detection from storage layer

OwnerReferences are metadata like labels and annotations — they should
not bump generation. The folder admission hook handles blocking for
repo-managed folders, so the storage layer change is unnecessary and
could cause unintended rerouting through provisioning for other
resource types.

Made-with: Cursor

Revert whitespace changes in prepare.go and prepare_test.go

Made-with: Cursor

title: Provisioning: Fix editors not being able to open dashboard preview
body:
## Summary

- Dashboard preview URLs under `/admin/provisioning/*/dashboard/preview/*` now only require `reqSignedIn` instead of `reqOrgAdmin`
- Non-admin users (editors, viewers) can now access preview links posted in PR comments by the git-sync backend
- All other `/admin/provisioning/*` paths still require OrgAdmin — no change in behavior for the admin UI

## Context

Related to #119987. The current backend route `/admin/provisioning/*` uses `reqOrgAdmin`, which blocks non-admin users from accessing dashboard preview links before the SPA even loads. The frontend redirect added in that PR is ineffective because the backend middleware rejects the request first.

This PR takes a backend-only approach that is **backward-compatible with all frontend versions**, avoiding issues during the ~8-week frontend rollout window. No URL changes are made — the existing frontend route at `/admin/provisioning/:slug/dashboard/preview/*` continues to work.

### How it works

A new `ProvisioningAuth()` middleware replaces `reqOrgAdmin` on the `/admin/provisioning/*` catch-all route. It checks the URL path:
- **Preview paths** (`/{slug}/dashboard/preview/{rest}`): only requires the user to be signed in
- **All other paths**: requires OrgAdmin (same as before)

The Grafana web framework stops the handler chain once a response is written (`pkg/web/macaron.go`), so when the middleware allows a preview request through without writing a forbidden response, `hs.Index` serves the SPA normally.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for path detection (`isProvisioningPreviewPath`) — 13 cases
- [x] Integration tests for the middleware (`TestProvisioningAuth`) — 8 cases covering viewer/editor/admin on preview paths, anonymous rejection, and admin-only on non-preview paths
- [x] Full `pkg/middleware/` test suite passes
- [ ] Manual: sign in as an editor, navigate to a preview link — should load the dashboard preview
- [ ] Manual: sign in as an editor, navigate to `/admin/provisioning` — should be blocked (403/redirect)

Fixes grafana/git-ui-sync-project#963

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: relax auth for dashboard preview paths

Dashboard preview URLs under /admin/provisioning/*/dashboard/preview/*
now only require reqSignedIn instead of reqOrgAdmin. This allows
non-admin users to access preview links posted in pull-request comments
by the git-sync backend.

The rest of /admin/provisioning/* still requires OrgAdmin.

No URL changes are needed, so this fix is backward-compatible with all
frontend versions and avoids issues during the 8-week frontend rollout
window.

Fixes grafana/git-ui-sync-project#963

title: Alerting: Pre-select Step 2 routing tree from Step 1 policy tree name
body:
**What is this feature?**

When the user completes Step 1 of the import wizard and enters a policy tree name, that name is now automatically pre-selected as the routing tree in Step 2.

**Why do we need this feature?**

In the import wizard, the policy tree name from Step 1 and the routing tree selection in Step 2 are closely related — users almost always want to associate their imported alert rules with the same policy tree they just configured. Pre-selecting this value reduces friction and avoids a redundant manual selection.

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.

title: unified-storage: don't run regression tests in parallel and bump timeout
body:
These tests started flaking around last Friday, March 13 2026. I believe the reason for this is that all of the parallel tests serialize when they call `newTestInfraDB`, and we started doing more work after #120162 was merged to generate the configuration.

This PR removes the `t.Parallel()` calls and doubles the timeout (from 1s to 2s).

title: CommandPalette: Fix flaky empty state test
body:
**What is this feature?**
FIxes flakey test. Types a nonsense search query (`zzznomatch`) in the command palette tests so the empty state is triggered by genuinely having no matches, rather than relying on timing.

**Why do we need this feature?**
Flakey flakey 🥐 

The tests were flaky because `useRegisterStaticActions()` registers nav actions with kbar, and `useMatches()` returns them when the search is empty. kbar's `useThrottledValue` introduces timing variability — sometimes actions register before the throttled value updates (test fails), sometimes after (test passes).

**Who is this feature for?**
UI devs
author: tomratcliffe

commits.message: CommandPalette: Fix flaky empty state test

title: Import dashboard: v1 collapsed row panels
author: kristinademeshchik

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the v1 dashboard import input-application logic to correctly resolve templated datasource UIDs not only on top-level panels, but also for panels nested inside collapsed row panels, aligning import behavior with how exports already treat collapsed rows.

**Changes:**
- Refactors panel datasource/target replacement into `resolveInputDatasource` and `resolveInputTargets`.
- Extends `processPanel` to handle collapsed row panels by updating nested panels’ `datasource` and `targets`.
- Adds unit tests covering datasource replacement within collapsed row panels (including the row panel’s own datasource).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| public/app/features/manage-dashboards/import/utils/inputs.ts | Adds helpers + updates `processPanel` to replace templated datasource UIDs inside collapsed row panels. |
| public/app/features/manage-dashboards/import/utils/inputs.test.ts | Adds regression tests ensuring nested panels in collapsed rows have datasources/targets rewritten. |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**public/app/features/manage-dashboards/import/utils/inputs.ts:617**
* `resolveInputTargets` calls `hasUid(target.datasource)` without ensuring `target.datasource` is an object; if a target datasource is a string, `hasUid` will throw at runtime. Add an object check (or harden `hasUid`) before using the `in` operator so imports of older dashboards don't crash.
```
  return targets?.map((target) => {
    if (target.datasource && hasUid(target.datasource) && target.datasource.uid.startsWith('$')) {
      const userInput = checkUserInputMatch(target.datasource.uid, inputs.dataSources, form.dataSources);
      if (userInput) {
        return {
          ...target,
          datasource: {
            ...target.datasource,
            uid: userInput.uid,
          },
```
</detai
LGTM!

commits.message:
Import dashboard: fix v1 collapsed row panels

title: unified-storage: cluster scope resource support in sqlkv
body:
for https://github.com/grafana/hyperion-planning/issues/508

  - Cluster-scoped resources now use empty namespace natively in the KV storage layer, matching how etcd handles them
  - Removes the `__cluster__` sentinel namespace and the `WithExperimentalClusterScope` option — no more mapping `""` ↔ `"__cluster__"` at the storage boundary
  - KV keys are now 4 segments for cluster-scoped (`group/resource/name/rv~action~folder`) vs 5 for namespaced (`group/resource/namespace/name/rv~action~folder`)
  - Updates SQL resource_history_update_rv template to conditionally include `namespace` in `key_path`
  - Adds integration tests for cluster-scoped CRUD across both badger and sqlkv backends, plus cross-backend compatibility tests (can write and list from both sqlkv and sqlbackend at the same time)
author: gassiss

reviews.body:
### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `baa6a0d4eb`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `a0fb72d7e8`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

Minor comments on the current code itself, but leaving a couple of questions:

* Does the distinction between 4 vs 5 part keys serve a correctness purpose, or is it just so we avoid a double slash in the key path? If it's the latter, I think we should at least consider accepting the double slash. It would simplify and eliminate all (or at least most?) of the special logic and `if Namespace == ""` code we're doing at the moment. I can imagine it being a source of errors in the future too if we forget to add the check. It's also arguably easier to identify cluster scoped resources when doing ad-hoc analysis by checking if the key path has '//' in it vs counting the number of '/' characters. But maybe this serves a more structural purpose that I'm missing.

* Do we want to allow listing from history for cluster scoped resources?

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/86465d300d2fb7df90a5e204435454c9f107477d/pkg/storage/unified/resource/storage_backend.go#L1051-L1053

* We should be pruning the history for cluster-scoped resources too, right? There's a validation for namespace currently. On that note, we should probably log a warning when adding to the pruner fails 🤔 

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/86465d300d2fb7df90a5e204435454c9f107477d/pkg/storage/unified/resource/pruner.go#L20-L22

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/86465d300d2fb7df90a5e204435454c9f107477d/pkg/storage/unified/resource/storage_backend.go#L798-L803

* Related: I think we'd want to search the cluster scoped resources at least at some point, especially when start relying on search for `List`. Just noting that there's also a validation for namespace at the moment:

https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/86465d300d2fb7df90a5e204435454c9f107477d/pkg/storage/unified/resource/search.go#L43-L45

Thanks for the updates!

I think we can keep the current logic and avoid the double slash just to be on the safe side as far as blob storage is concerned. It's probably possible to refactor the code so that we don't have to repeat the empty namespace check in so many places, but it can be done in follow up work.

commits.message: unified-storage: cluster scope resources in sqlkv
Merge branch 'main' of github.com:grafana/grafana into kvstore-cluster-scoped-resources2

title: [release-12.3.6] Public Dashboards: Prevent unintended CRUD operations from different orgs
body:
Backport f62299e5de8ed0a002d6ef23b8b7a839a1efa810 from #120339

---

**What is this feature?**

A subset of public dashboard REST endpoints were only validating the role of the user making the request while ignoring which org the request was made from. This allowed users in one org to improperly access public dashboards in another, without actually having access to the source dashboard. 

Note that this is difficult to perform accidentally, as it requires Editor+ permissions and knowledge of the target dashboard uid and public dashboard uid ahead of time. 

**Why do we need this feature?**

Remove unintended cross-org behavior, orgs are meant to be completely isolated from one another. 

**Who is this feature for?**

On-prem Grafana installations with multiple orgs. 

**Other**

Example with improper public dashboard deletion, using basic auth as an admin in OrgB to attempt to delete a public dashboard in OrgA:
```
$ curl -X DELETE http://orgb:orgb@localhost:3000/api/dashboards/uid/orrskxl/public-dashboards/bffx4thvyzh8gf
{"statusCode":403,"messageId":"publicdashboards.wrongOrg","message":"Public dashboard does not belong to this organization"}%         
```      
        
Same thing but as OrgA admin:                                                                                            
```
$ curl -X DELETE http://orga:orga@localhost:3000/api/dashboards/uid/orrskxl/public-dashboards/bffx4thvyzh8gf`
// succeeds
```
author: grafana-delivery-bot

commits.message:
Public Dashboards: Prevent unintended CRUD operations from different orgs (#120339)

* check org associated with the request and ensure it matches the orgID of the dashboard

* check org associated with the request and ensure it matches the orgID of the dashboard

* address other uses for orgId

* fix tests

* Update pkg/services/publicdashboards/service/service.go

* fix test

---------

Co-authored-by: Matheus Macabu <macabu.matheus@gmail.com>
(cherry picked from commit f62299e5de8ed0a002d6ef23b8b7a839a1efa810)

title: Alerting: Add tracking events for multi notification policies feature
body:
**What is this feature?**

This PR adds tracking for the following events in the notification policies pages:
- Notification policy creation
- Notification policy creation error
- Notification policy export
- Notification policy deletion
- Default notification policy reset
- Notification policy tree selection in alert rule creation
- Filter notification policy list by matchers, contact point and policy
- Expand / collapse notification policy list

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e1950e53-f4f8-431b-a302-8e9253debf0f


**Why do we need this feature?**

To collect data points regarding the notificaton policies section of the alerting app

**Who is this feature for?**

Alerting users

title: DashboardScene: Decouple it from mutation-api to eliminate circular deps
body:
## Summary

Decouple `DashboardScene` from `mutation-api/` imports to eliminate all mutation-api circular dependencies at the root.

## Problem

`DashboardScene` statically imports `DashboardMutationClient`, which eagerly pulls the entire command registry and all scene component dependencies. Since scene components transitively import `DashboardScene`, every mutation command that touches scene internals creates a circular dependency.

```
DashboardScene → DashboardMutationClient → registry → commands → scene components → DashboardScene
```

## Solution

Replace the direct import with a lightweight bridge module (`DashboardMutationClientSetter`) that decouples the two sides:

- **`dashboardMutationApi.ts`** calls `provideMutationClientFactory(...)` at app init to register how to create a mutation client for a scene.
- **`DashboardScene`** calls `createMutationClient(this)` on activation and `destroyMutationClient()` on cleanup — without importing anything from `mutation-api/`.

The bridge module has zero dependencies on mutation-api or scene components, so no cycles are created.

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scene/DashboardMutationClientSetter.ts` | New bridge module (23 lines) |
| `scene/DashboardScene.tsx` | Replace mutation-api imports with bridge import |
| `dashboardMutationApi.ts` | Register mutation client factory via bridge |

## Result

- Zero mutation-api circular dependencies
- Net reduction of ~5 total circular dependencies
- Future mutation commands can freely import any scene component

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `yarn lint:circular` shows no increase
- [ ] Verify dashboards load correctly (mutation API registered on activation)
- [ ] Verify Grafana Assistant panel mutations work end-to-end
author: ivanortegaalba

reviews.body: Looks good! Ship it.

commits.message:
refactor(dashboard-scene): decouple DashboardScene from mutation-api to eliminate circular dependencies

Move DashboardMutationClient lifecycle out of DashboardScene into
dashboardMutationApi.ts via a lightweight setter module. This removes
the DashboardScene -> DashboardMutationClient import edge that caused
all mutation-api commands to create circular dependencies with scene
components.

- Create DashboardMutationClientSetter: a zero-dependency module that
  lets dashboardMutationApi register a factory for creating the mutation
  client, which DashboardScene invokes on activation/deactivation
- Move mutation client creation into dashboardMutationApi.ts which
  is already loaded at app initialization
- Remove DashboardMutationClient and setDashboardMutationClient imports
  from DashboardScene

Result: zero mutation-api cycles. Future mutation commands can freely
import any scene component without creating cycles.
fix: resolve merge conflicts, fix lint errors and update test for mutation client setter
fix: warn when createMutationClient is called before factory is provided

title: perf(unified-storage): batch BulkProcess migration writes
body:
Short URL migration to unified storage currently streams and writes one bulk item at a time, which makes large migrations much slower than they need to be. This change keeps the `BulkProcess` contract intact while batching stream ingestion and SQL `resource_history` inserts, so migrations do fewer `Recv` and `Exec` cycles without changing the external API. It also adds focused tests and a shorturl-shaped benchmark so we can compare `batch_size=1` against the new batching behavior.

## What changed
- add an internal batch-aware `BulkProcess` reader in `pkg/storage/unified/resource` that flushes on item count, payload bytes, or idle time
- keep the existing single-item iterator behavior for non-SQL callers, while letting SQL consume request batches directly
- batch `resource_history` inserts in `pkg/storage/unified/sql` with a dedicated multi-row SQL template
- keep SQLite safe by capping SQL insert chunks to 8 rows, while MySQL/Postgres use 1000-row chunks
- add unit coverage for batch iteration behavior, rollback propagation, and batched SQL insert handling
- add a shorturl benchmark that exercises 26k shorturl-shaped bulk requests across multiple batch sizes

## Benchmark
Command:

```bash
env GOCACHE=/tmp/codex-gocache-bench2 go test ./pkg/storage/unified/sql -run '^$' -bench 'BenchmarkShortURLBulkProcessBatching' -benchtime=1x -count=3
```

Environment:
- `darwin/arm64`
- Apple M4 Pro
- default SQLite test setup

Median results from 3 runs:

| Case | Median ns/op | Median B/op | Median allocs/op |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `batch_size_1` | 2.478s | 508.2 MB | 10.02M |
| `batch_size_4` | 2.296s | 368.7 MB | 7.17M |
| `batch_size_8` | 2.292s | 368.5 MB | 7.17M |
| `batch_size_16` | 2.344s | 368.4 MB | 7.17M |
| `batch_size_32` | 2.358s | 368.6 MB | 7.17M |
| `batch_size_128` | 2.268s | 368.4 MB | 7.17M |
| `batched_default` | 1.531s | 260.2 MB | 4.99M |

Compared to `batch_size_1`, the default batching path was about 38% faster, with about 49% lower allocated bytes and about 50% fewer allocations in this setup.

## Testing
- `env GOCACHE=/tmp/codex-gocache-resource go test ./pkg/storage/unified/resource -run 'TestBatchRunner'`
- `env GOCACHE=/tmp/codex-gocache-bench2 go test ./pkg/storage/unified/sql -run 'TestBackendInsertHistoryBatch|TestBulkHistoryInsertRowLimit|TestUnifiedStorageQueries'`
- `env GOCACHE=/tmp/codex-gocache-bench2 go test ./pkg/storage/unified/sql -run '^$' -bench 'BenchmarkShortURLBulkProcessBatching' -benchtime=1x -count=3`

title: ShortURL: Do not memoize error responses when creating a shortURL
body:
**What is this feature?**
Fix short URL creation caching failed responses and add loading state to share button

  Summary

  - Fix createShortLink caching rejected promises via memoizeOne — after a failure (e.g. 504 timeout), subsequent calls with the same path now retry the
  request instead of returning the cached rejection
  - Handle errors in ShareLinkTab.buildUrl() so isBuildUrlLoading is reset to false on failure, preventing the component from getting stuck in a loading state
  - Add loading indicator (spinner icon) to the Share toolbar button, matching the existing RefreshPicker pattern

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/120439

title: Chore: Prepare @grafana/o11y-ds-frontend for npm publishing
body:
**What is this feature?**

For our externalization efforts this is needed as a published package. This PR introduces necessary changes to make this package published.

Makes the @grafana/o11y-ds-frontend package publishable to npm. It was previously internal-only. This package provides shared UI components used by observability datasource plugins (Tempo, Jaeger, Zipkin).


**Verification**
- Build 
```
yarn nx run @grafana/o11y-ds-frontend:build
```
- Pack
```
mkdir -p npm-artifacts
cd packages/grafana-o11y-ds-frontend && yarn pack --out '../../npm-artifacts/%s-%v.tgz'
```
- Validate
```
./scripts/validate-npm-packages.sh
```
- Dry-run
```
npm publish ./npm-artifacts/grafana-o11y-ds-frontend-*.tgz --dry-run --tag next
```

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.

displaying the contact point. Using multiple integrations per contact point
is somewhat of an edge case, so:

- Show the integration number, but only if it's not the first
- Add a tooltip for the integration name and number

title: AuthN: groundwork for MT authn service
body:
Note this is a rebuild of https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/119738 which got too hairy with conflicts.

Register authn-server as a Grafana module target with a dependency on InstrumentationServer. The proto definition lives in authlib (see https://github.com/grafana/authlib/pull/254) so this PR also bumps the authlib version to latest. 

This PR also adds a basic authnserver implementation which can be extended with enterprise-only auth clients. For example, our session client will live in OSS, but a pass-through JWT client will exist in enterprise-grafana.

Part of https://github.com/grafana/identity-access-team/issues/1825 and https://github.com/grafana/identity-access-team/issues/1951
author: Rydez

commits.message: update iam app with new authlib version

title: RadioButton: Correctly set accessible labels
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

- modifies `Field`/`InlineField` to handle the presence of a `RadioButtonGroup` as a child correctly
- for `Field`, we use a combination of `<fieldset>` and `<legend>` to correctly set the label for the radio button group
- for `InlineField`, we instead use a `<span>` and `aria-labelledby`
  - this is because browsers render `<fieldset>` and `<legend>` specially - importantly it doesn't seem possible(?) to render the legend inline - it's always block.
- adds unit tests to verify the labels are present

**Why do we need this feature?**

- so screenreaders correctly announce radio button group labels

**Who is this feature for?**

- everyone

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

For https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/118763

title: [release-12.3.6] RBAC: deduplicate concurrent permission assembly with singleflight
body:
Backport 69ac7a05220a19b737e36ac7db6520adccc4548d from #120331

---

**What is this feature?**

Wraps the permission sub-cache assembly in `getCachedUserPermissions` with a `singleflight.Group` so that concurrent requests for the same user share one in-flight assembly instead of each independently re-assembling.

**Why do we need this feature?**

When the alerting UI creates an alert rule it fires ~800 parallel datasource buildinfo proxy requests (one per Prometheus datasource). Each request goes through the RBAC middleware which calls `GetUserPermissions`. Without deduplication, all 800 requests independently re-assemble permissions from sub-caches on every cache expiry — a thundering herd that amplifies DB load for users belonging to many teams.

This became a regression in ~12.1.x when PR #105607 removed the outer assembled-permissions cache (which was necessary to avoid OOM). The singleflight approach avoids the OOM risk entirely: nothing is held in memory after the in-flight call completes, while still collapsing N concurrent callers into one assembly.

Related: https://github.com/grafana/support-escalations/issues/19881

**Who is this feature for?**

Grafana instances where users belong to many teams and experience high concurrency on permission-checked endpoints (e.g. datasource proxy, alert rule creation).

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

- `ReloadCache` bypasses singleflight to preserve explicit refresh semantics
- The type assertion on the singleflight result uses the safe two-value form with an explicit error
- Benchmark `BenchmarkGetUserPermissions_ManyTeams_*` in `service_bench_test.go` covers the thundering herd scenario at realistic scale (100–200 teams, 100–800 concurrent goroutines)

Please check that:
- [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: linoman

commits.message:
RBAC: deduplicate concurrent permission assembly with singleflight (#120331)

* RBAC: deduplicate concurrent permission assembly with singleflight

Without this, N concurrent requests for the same user each independently
re-assemble permissions from sub-caches. On cache expiry this causes a
thundering herd: all N requests hit the sub-cache assembly simultaneously,
potentially triggering redundant DB queries.

This is particularly visible when the alerting UI fires ~800 parallel
datasource proxy requests (one buildinfo call per Prometheus datasource)
for a user who belongs to many teams — each request goes through the RBAC
middleware which calls GetUserPermissions.

Fix: wrap the sub-cache assembly in a singleflight.Group keyed by the
user cache key. Concurrent callers for the same user share one in-flight
assembly and get the same result. ReloadCache bypasses the deduplication
to preserve explicit refresh semantics.

* review: drop instance-specific scale from comment, suppress false positive

* revert: remove trufflehog:ignore from fields.ts

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
(cherry picked from commit 69ac7a05220a19b737e36ac7db6520adccc4548d)
remove stale sql and serverLock fields from Service struct

title: InlineSecureValues: Allow passing optional custom description to value
body:
This lets clients optionally pass a custom `description` to their inline secure values, in order to provide a more accurate description of what it is for.

If nothing is passed, we produce a default description.
author: macabu

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Enables callers to attach an optional custom `description` when creating inline secure values, falling back to a default description when omitted/blank, and propagates the field through OpenAPI/client generation and gRPC plumbing.

**Changes:**
- Add `description?: string` / `Description *string` to `InlineSecureValue` and OpenAPI schemas/snapshots.
- Extend `InlineSecureValueSupport.CreateInline` to accept an optional description and thread it through unified storage and the gRPC client.
- Update deepcopy generation to correctly copy the new pointer field, plus adjust tests accordingly.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 19 out of 19 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| pkg/apimachinery/apis/common/v0alpha1/secure_values.go | Adds `Description *string` to `InlineSecureValue` and exposes it in OpenAPI definition |
| pkg/apimachinery/apis/common/v0alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go | Deep-copies `InlineSecureValue.Description` pointer |
| pkg/apis/datasource/v0alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go | Fixes deepcopy of map values to include deep-copied `InlineSecureValue` |
| apps/provisioning/pkg/apis/provisioning/v0alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go | Ensures nested secure fields are deep-copied (not shallow-copied) |
| pkg/storage/unified/apistore/secure.go | Passes inline secure value `Description` into `CreateInline` |
| pkg/storage/unified/apistore/secure_test.go | Updates mocks for new `CreateInline` signature |
| pkg/registry/apis/secret/contracts/inline.go | Extends `CreateInline` contract to include optional description |
| pkg/registry/apis/secret/inline/inline_secure_value.go | Implements default-vs-custom description selection (trim/blank handling) |
| pkg/registry/apis/secret/inline/inline_secure_value_test.go | Adds integration coverage asserting persisted secure value description |
| pkg/registry/ap

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `c4c00ac914`

[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. title: Logs Panel: Added support to pass custom grammar definition for plugin developers
author: matyax

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR exposes a `grammar` option on the core Logs panel so plugin developers can provide a custom Prism grammar for syntax highlighting, switching styling to Prism token colors when a custom grammar is used.

**Changes:**
- Added a `grammar` option to the Logs panel config/schema and plumbed it through `LogsPanel -> LogList`.
- Introduced an `isCustomGrammar` flag in `LogListContext` and used it to toggle between `log-syntax-highlight` and `prism-syntax-highlight` CSS classes in log line rendering.
- Added/updated tests and mocks to cover custom-grammar highlighting behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| public/app/plugins/panel/logs/types.ts | Adds `isGrammar` type guard for validating/casting the new `grammar` option. |
| public/app/plugins/panel/logs/panelcfg.cue | Extends composable Logs panel options with `grammar`. |
| public/app/plugins/panel/logs/panelcfg.gen.ts | Regenerated TS options interface to include `grammar?: unknown`. |
| packages/grafana-schema/src/raw/composable/logs/panelcfg/x/types.gen.ts | Regenerated schema TS options interface to include `grammar?: unknown`. |
| public/app/plugins/panel/logs/LogsPanel.tsx | Passes validated `grammar` into `<LogList />`. |
| public/app/features/logs/components/panel/LogList.tsx | Flags custom grammar usage in context via `isCustomGrammar`. |
| public/app/features/logs/components/panel/LogListContext.tsx | Adds `isCustomGrammar` to context props/value. |
| public/app/features/logs/components/panel/LogLine.tsx | Switches wrapper class to `prism-syntax-highlight` when custom grammar is active. |
| public/app/features/logs/components/panel/LogListContext.test.tsx | Adds coverage for the new context field. |
| public/app/features/logs/components/panel/LogLine.test.tsx | Adds custom-grammar h
Added 👍 . I'm not sure how plugin developers use this though, is it an option on the LogsPanel?

title: [release-12.4.2] Dashboards a11y: Do not open time zonemenu on focus
body:
Backport 7b372e4c17bdd785dd0f177f31f2b38aa4b32626 from #120101

---

When we're selecting time zone in the timerange picker, the menu opens on focus. This focuses away from the input label and onto the menu and causes screen readers to not announce the actual label:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d5d770bd-1e63-46f6-816b-cf033db4a6fc


The bug was reported in an [ a11y ticket](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/117837#issuecomment-3909257189)
I think the best solution here is to disable opening on focus, and opening the menu explicit. An additional reason is that the combobox component that will replace this select, doesn't have that functionality. (reasoning [here](https://raintank-corp.slack.com/archives/C025WTVRBSN/p1744038629297399?thread_ts=1744038333.835929&cid=C025WTVRBSN))

After:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19f77a97-ee20-49ed-8dd0-4cb9470c571f



### Testing:
enable voice over in settings, then press CMD + Fn + F5 to launch voice over utility, then navigate to the time zone picker with the keyboard

title: Provisioning: support folder metadata for folder move operations in Full Sync
body:
**What is this feature?**

When a folder containing a `_folder.json` metadata file (with a stable UID) is moved to a different path in a git repository, full sync now correctly recreates the folder at the new location while preserving its stable UID and custom title.

The fix adds a `Remove` method to the in-memory `FolderTree` and calls it after each successful folder deletion during full sync. This ensures the tree stays in sync with actual Grafana state throughout the sync process.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Full sync handles folder moves as DELETE (old path) + CREATE (new path). This works fine for folders without `_folder.json`. However, when a folder has _folder.json with a stable UID, there's a tree staleness bug: after the folder is deleted from Grafana in the deletion phase, its UID remains in the in-memory `FolderTree`. When the creation phase runs, `EnsureFolderPathExist` reads the `_folder.json`, gets the same UID, checks `tree.In(uid)` - which returns true - and short-circuits without recreating the folder. The folder ends up deleted from Grafana with no replacement.

The fix is minimal: remove the folder's UID from the in-memory tree when it's deleted, so the existing DELETE + CREATE flow works correctly with stable UIDs. Additionally, `FolderTree.Add` is made idempotent (re-adding an existing folder updates metadata without double-counting).

**Who is this feature for?**

Users of GitSync.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/936

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [x] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [x] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Live: Support legacy bare numeric org ID in channel namespace
body:
## Summary
- Fixes #119951
- `StripK8sNamespace` now falls back to parsing bare numeric strings (e.g. "1") as legacy org IDs when `authlib.ParseNamespace` returns an unrecognized format
- Subscribe and publish handlers compare by org ID in addition to raw namespace string, so `"1"` matches users with namespace `"default"` (both org 1)
- Added test coverage for legacy format including invalid cases (0, -1)

## Root cause
PR #117275 changed Live channels from bare org IDs (`1/grafana/dashboard/uid/...`) to k8s namespaces (`default/grafana/dashboard/uid/...`), but `StripK8sNamespace` dropped backwards compatibility. `authlib.ParseNamespace("1")` silently returns `OrgID: -1` for unknown formats, triggering a "namespace does not reference a valid org ID" error every ~6 seconds for affected clients.

## Test plan
- [x] Existing `TestStripK8sNamespace` passes
- [x] New test cases for legacy bare numeric org IDs (1, 42) succeed
- [x] Invalid org IDs (0, -1) are still rejected
- [x] Manual: verify a client sending `1/grafana/dashboard/uid/<uid>` can subscribe successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commits.message:
Live: Support legacy bare numeric org ID in channel namespace

Fixes #119951. The migration to k8s namespaces in Live channels (#117275)
broke backwards compatibility with clients still sending bare numeric org
IDs (e.g. "1/grafana/dashboard/uid/..."). StripK8sNamespace now falls back
to parsing bare numbers as org IDs, and the subscribe/publish handlers
compare by org ID in addition to raw namespace string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Live: Normalize legacy namespace to canonical in publish handler

Use user's canonical namespace (from GetNamespace()) for pipeline rule
lookups and ProcessInput in the publish handler, instead of the raw
parsed ns.Value which would be "1" for legacy channels. Without this,
pipeline rules keyed by "default" or "org-N" would miss for legacy
clients.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Live: Normalize legacy namespace in StripK8sNamespace, drop org-ID fallback

Move namespace normalization into StripK8sNamespace itself: legacy bare
numeric org IDs are mapped to their canonical k8s form ("1" → "default",
"42" → "org-42") in ns.Value. This lets the subscribe and publish
handlers use a simple string comparison against the user's namespace
without an org-ID fallback, preserving stack namespace isolation
(stacks-X namespaces all share OrgID=1 but must remain distinct).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Live: Simplify comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Dockerfile: Improve Go build layer caching
body:
**What is this feature?**

Replace full directory copies before go mod download with explicit go.mod/go.sum-only copies for each workspace module. This ensures the dependency download layer cache only invalidates when dependency files actually change, not on any .go source edit.

Add BuildKit cache mounts for /go/pkg/mod and /root/.cache/go-build to persist module downloads and compiled objects across builds, significantly reducing rebuild times for incremental changes.
  
**Why do we need this feature?**
On a Macbook Pro M4 these improvements reduce image build time after source modifications from over 7 minutes to around 30 seconds.

This is especially useful when running Grafana in a docker-compose environment.

**Who is this feature for?**

Users who repeatedly build Grafana's docker image during development.

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

title: Provisioning: use folder metadata title for existing folders when moved and reconciled in Full Sync
body:
**What is this feature?**

This change makes folder metadata authoritative for folder titles during provisioning **full sync**.

When `provisioningFolderMetadata` is enabled and a folder has a `_folder.json` with a non-empty `spec.title`, full sync now consistently reconciles that title into Grafana, including for folders that already exist in the in-memory folder tree. 

In practice, this means:

- `_folder.json.spec.title` takes precedence over the directory name when present;
- Directory renames do not rename the Grafana folder if the metadata title stays the same.

**Why do we need this feature?**

We need this so folder metadata remains the source of truth after the first sync.

This change closes that gap by ensuring folder metadata is applied consistently for full sync modes. It gives predictable behaviour, keeps Grafana aligned with repository metadata, and preserves stable folder naming even when directory names change independently from `_folder.json`.

**Who is this feature for?**

Users of GitSync

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Part of https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/936

title: Dashboards: fix missing render-readiness observer on render-authenticated /render/d/... route
body: **What is this PR?** This PR fixes a render-readiness wiring gap for `/render/d/...` callback flows. In the callback flow used by renderer: - route resolves to `DashboardRoutes.Normal` - auth context is `authenticatedBy === 'render'` Before this change, render-target detection only treated these as render targets: - `DashboardRoutes.Report` - `DashboardRoutes.Embedded` So render-authenticated `Normal` could skip `initializeReportRenderReadinessObserver()`. --- **Why do we need this?** This is a correctness bug in render readiness classification: - `/render/d/...` is a render flow. - route-only gating excluded the render-authenticated `Normal` path. - missing observer can increase reliance on timeout/network heuristics. In boundary scenarios, this increases timeout/500 risk. --- **Verification summary** Local integration smoke (same renderer/environment settings) used a data-less dashboard (`render-readiness-ab`) with `numbers + sleepEachRow` queries: **before fix around ~13s,** --- **after fix around ~7s** --- If users configure rendering timeout near the observed baseline latency (for example, 13s), the baseline frequently returns HTTP 500 at the timeout boundary, while the candidate fix completes with HTTP 200 more consistently. This reduces follow-up failures in downstream automation/reporting flows that expect an image response. --- **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?** Fixes #120354 --- Please check that: - [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Alerting: Add concurrency cap to buildinfo requests in useRuleSourcesWithRuler
body:
**Problem**

When a user opens the alert rule creation page, useRulesSourcesWithRuler() fires one discoverDsFeatures request per external datasource simultaneously with no concurrency limit. For customers with hundreds of Prometheus datasources, this creates a thundering herd that overwhelms the backend. The isLoading state is also broken (only tracks the last-triggered lazy query), so the UI either blocks incorrectly or shows stale partial results.

**Fix**

- Replaced the unbounded forEach fan-out in useRuleSourcesWithRuler with a batched Promise.all loop (max 10 concurrent requests) so datasource discovery no longer floods the backend. Moved isLoading to local state so the UI updates progressively as each batch completes, and added a cancelled cleanup flag to prevent stale state updates on unmount.
- Adds unit tests covering the current hook behaviour (baseline) and regression tests that reproduce the two bugs fixed above: uncapped concurrency and an incorrect isLoading state that resolved too early.
- Added keepUnusedDataFor: 3600 to both discoverDsFeatures and discoverAmFeatures RTK Query endpoints, extending the cache TTL to one hour to avoid redundant buildinfo requests across page navigations.

**Reproduction steps**

Note: No changes are visible to the user.

*Prerequisites*
- Grafana running at http://localhost:3000
- `alertingDisableDMAinUI` feature flag set to false in conf/custom.ini
- 50 Prometheus datasources pointing at Mimir (http://localhost:8080, test/test) with manageAlerts: true 
- Mimir devenv block running (make devenv sources=mimir_backend)

*Steps*
1. Go to http://localhost:3000/alerting/new/alerting
2. In section 2 "Define query and alert condition", click "Advanced options" (top-right toggle) to enable advanced mode
3. In the query editor, ensure only one data source query exists (delete any extra query rows beyond query A)
4. A "Grafana-managed / Data source-managed" radio toggle appears between the "+ Add query" button and the "Expressions" section
5. Click "Data source-managed"
6. Open DevTools → Network tab, filter by buildinfo
7. The CloudRulesSourcePicker mounts and useRulesSourcesWithRuler fires — you'll see 50 buildinfo requests going out in batches of ≤ 10 simultaneously

Alternatively (simpler — no toggle needed):
- Go directly to http://localhost:3000/alerting/new/recording (the page starts in data-source-managed mode and fires all 50 buildinfo requests immediately on load)

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Relates to escalation [#i-2026-01-08-cagip-p2-esc-grafana-is-very-slow-since-upgrade-in-12-2-2](https://grafanalabs.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A7FT0ADPF)

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: rodrigopk

reviews.body: review comments
Code changes LGTM!

commits.message:
Add concurrency cap to buildinfo requests in useRuleSourcesWithRuler

- Replaced the unbounded forEach fan-out in useRuleSourcesWithRuler with a batched Promise.all loop (max 10 concurrent requests) so datasource discovery no longer floods the backend. Moved isLoading to local state so the UI updates progressively as each batch completes, and added a cancelled cleanup flag to prevent stale state updates on unmount.
- Adds unit tests covering the current hook behaviour (baseline) and regression tests that reproduce the two bugs fixed above: uncapped concurrency and an incorrect isLoading state that resolved too early.
- Added keepUnusedDataFor: 3600 to both discoverDsFeatures and discoverAmFeatures RTK Query endpoints, extending the cache TTL to one hour to avoid redundant buildinfo requests across page navigations.

title: Public Dashboards: Prevent unintended CRUD operations from different orgs
body:
**What is this feature?**

A subset of public dashboard REST endpoints were only validating the role of the user making the request while ignoring which org the request was made from. This allowed users in one org to improperly access public dashboards in another, without actually having access to the source dashboard. 

Note that this is difficult to perform accidentally, as it requires Editor+ permissions and knowledge of the target dashboard uid and public dashboard uid ahead of time. 

**Why do we need this feature?**

Remove unintended cross-org behavior, orgs are meant to be completely isolated from one another. 

**Who is this feature for?**

On-prem Grafana installations with multiple orgs. 

**Other**

Example with improper public dashboard deletion, using basic auth as an admin in OrgB to attempt to delete a public dashboard in OrgA:
```
$ curl -X DELETE http://orgb:orgb@localhost:3000/api/dashboards/uid/orrskxl/public-dashboards/bffx4thvyzh8gf
{"statusCode":403,"messageId":"publicdashboards.wrongOrg","message":"Public dashboard does not belong to this organization"}%         
```      
        
Same thing but as OrgA admin:                                                                                            
```
$ curl -X DELETE http://orga:orga@localhost:3000/api/dashboards/uid/orrskxl/public-dashboards/bffx4thvyzh8gf`
// succeeds
```

title: kvsql/GC: Add setting to control wait between batches
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Add a new setting to Unified Storage Garbage Collector (SQLKV) to control the wait time between batches.

```
[unified_storage]
garbage_collection_batch_wait=100ms
```

**Why do we need this feature?**

With a default batchsize of 500 and wait time of 1 second between batches, the GC is taking 1000 seconds (almost 20 minutes!) to complete a loop.

We already have a setting for batch size, now this PR is adding a setting to control also the batch wait time.

**Who is this feature for?**

@grafana/grafana-search-and-storage 

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Part of https://github.com/grafana/search-and-storage-team/issues/682

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

**What is this feature?**

Repository controller does not reconcile repositories with the soft-deleted label.

title: add redirect for resource endpoints

This PR adds a redirect which will based on a feature boolean via goff redirect traffic from the existing legacy `/api/.../resources` endpoint for datasources to the new `/apis/..../resources/` endpoint. 

If disabled it will go through the existing working endpoint. (The current scenario)

If enabled it will hit the local k8 endpoint. (This would be the OSS usecase once we have migrated the frontend)

if enabled and [this pr is merged](https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11269) and the resources endpoint is enabled it will send a http request to the MT aggregator and get redirected to MT Datasource pods (The Cloud state eventually)

[Part of the MT Datasource endpoint migration](https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/issues/8975)

title: Provisioning: add graceful shutdown to jobs operator
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Update job controller to have a grateful shutdown. This is achieved by using a workgroup to wait for all job workers and ongoing job to exit after receiving the context done signal. In-flight jobs would be stopped the same way they are now, in places where they are checking the context Done signal. Another K8s pod timeout will kill the controller if this whole shutdown is still not working, but that should be a last resort.

**Why do we need this feature?**

This will make sure that the ready probe is set to false at shutdown start and that the operator has a clean exit status by not letting goroutines still running. 

**Who is this feature for?**

Provisioning feature.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

title: unified-storage: add ability to `ListHistory` within group/resource/namespace for trash source
body:
Prior to this commit, the KV storage backend implementation of `ListHistory` would assume that the request was for a single resource within a group/resource/namespace (i.e., non empty `Name`). This was true for both the history source as well as the trash source. As a result, requests for the entire group/resource/namespace would not return an error but an empty response.

In this PR, we change the implementation to only require a particular resource name when listing for the `history` source. For `trash`, we allow listing for the entire group/resource/namespace. This is used by the Restore Dashboards feature, where we want to get a list of recently deleted dashboards.

We chose to diverge slightly from the SQL backend implementation as far as _sorting_ is concerned: while the SQL backend always sorts by RV (sometimes ASC, sometimes DESC), the KV store backend will always sort by name first, then RV (ASC), the natural sorting within the datastore. This should not impact correctness, as ordering within `ListHistory` is not part of the `StorageBackend` contract, or the Kubernetes list API.

The behaviour is a little subtle as it combines existing [Kubernetes API](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/using-api/api-concepts/#semantics-for-get-and-list), along with three parameters that control the results: source (`History` or `Trash`), whether a `ResourceVersion` was passed, and the `Match` parameter.

This table summarizes the expected behaviour. The PR adds tests for the different scenarios:

| Source | ResourceVersion | Match | Behaviour |
|--------|-----------------|-------|-----------|
| History | 0 | Exact | Invalid — return error early |
| History | 0 | NotOlderThan | All events for given resource *after* the latest deletion (i.e., live events). Delete events are excluded by construction (they have RV ≤ latestDeleteRV). |
| History | n > 0 | Exact | Event (at most 1 by construction) where RV == n. May be a delete event. |
| History | n > 0 | NotOlderThan | Events where RV ≥ n. May include delete events. |
| Trash | 0 | Exact | Invalid — return error early |
| Trash | 0 | NotOlderThan | Latest delete event for each name. Excludes resources that are currently live. |
| Trash | n > 0 | Exact | At most one delete event (by construction) where RV == n |
| Trash | n > 0 | NotOlderThan | Latest deletion for each name where RV ≥ n |

**Note**: sorting in the KV backend's `ListHistory` is currently ASC when listing the trash, and DESC when listing the history of a resource. We can't use ASC for both yet because the frontend relies on the DESC sorting for the dashboards Versions page. This dependency should not exist, but this will be handled in future work.

Ticket: https://github.com/grafana/search-and-storage-team/issues/676

title: Snapshots: Add permissions validation to K8s API
body:
## Summary
- Add RBAC-based authorization for snapshot resources in the K8s API, replacing the default `ServiceAuthorizer` with a composite authorizer that dispatches snapshot requests to a dedicated `SnapshotAuthorizer`
- The `SnapshotAuthorizer` maps K8s verbs (`get`, `list`, `create`, `delete`) and subresources (`dashboard`, `deletekey`) to existing Grafana snapshot RBAC actions (`snapshots:read`, `snapshots:create`, `snapshots:delete`)
- Add RBAC permission checks to the custom API routes (`/create`, `/delete/{deleteKey}`, `/settings`) so they are also gated by the appropriate snapshot permissions
- Add integration tests verifying that users without snapshot permissions are denied access (403) on all operations: GET, LIST, DELETE, POST /create, DELETE /delete/{deleteKey}, and GET /settings

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/120187

## Special notes for your reviewer

title: Packaging: Remove `grafana-cli` and `grafana-server` commands; use `grafana cli` or `grafana server` instead
body:
Closes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/120211

- Removed old circle CI scripts that reference `grafana-server`, as they are unused.
- Updated `packaging/wrappers` scripts, which wrapped `grafana-server` and `grafana-cli` to use `grafana cli` and `grafana server`.
- Added deprecation warnings to `grafana-server` and `grafana-cli` wrapper scripts. In the future, users should use `grafana`, which has the same utility additions.
- Removed `grafana-cli` and `grafana-server` from `pkg/build` and various makefile targets.
- Removed `musl` from the deb dependencies. Not exactly related to this PR but this dependency is no longer needed now that we are building with CGO_ENABLED=0.

# Release notice breaking change

`grafana-cli` and `grafana-server` commands are removed; users should instead use `grafana cli` and `grafana server`. While `deb` and `rpm` installers will continue to have the `grafana-cli` and `grafana-server` wrapper scripts available, users are advised to switch to the `grafana` script, as `grafana-cli` and `grafana-server` will be removed in a future update.
author: kminehart

title: [release-12.4.2] Plugins: Forward AWS SDK credential chain env vars to external AWS plugins
body:
Backport 08bdf00c50e9385502fcacc60da93bbe2cb152cc from #119772

---

## What is this feature?

Forward AWS SDK credential chain environment variables from the Grafana process to external AWS plugins. This restores AWS authentication (assume role, SigV4) for plugins like Redshift, Athena, Amazon Prometheus, and OpenSearch on container platforms.

## Why do we need this feature?

In Grafana 12.4.0, commit f041563df3a made external plugins no longer receive host environment variables by default — a breaking change for AWS authentication in container environments (EKS with IRSA, ECS Fargate). The AWS SDK credential chain relies on env vars like `AWS_ROLE_ARN`, `AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE`, and `AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI` to resolve credentials. Without these, the SDK falls back to EC2 IMDS, which fails.

## Who is this feature for?

Users running external AWS plugins on EKS with IRSA or ECS Fargate who upgraded to Grafana 12.4.x and experienced broken assume role / SigV4 authentication.

## Which issue(s) does this PR fix?

Fixes #119235, #119603

## Special notes for your reviewer

This PR forwards 8 AWS SDK credential chain env vars to plugins in `[aws] forward_settings_to_plugins`. It's more targeted than PR #118870's global approach — only trusted AWS plugins receive the vars. Includes test coverage with 5 table-driven test cases.

title: Alerting: Improve notification timeline UX in triage drawer
body:
**What is this feature?**

## Summary

Feedback collected on the notification timeline in the alert activity triage drawer.

### Naming

- [x] Rename "Instance Timeline" to "History" to be consistent with the naming used elsewhere in the UI

### Filtering

- [x] **Keep as-is.** The All/State changes/Notifications filter helps when there are many state changes but few notifications (or vice versa). Will revisit based on real usage feedback.
- [x] Move filter tabs (All / State changes / Notifications) to the same row as the "History" heading and make them only as wide as the text requires.

### Dot colors and consistency

- [x] Flip the current color scheme: use colored dots for state transitions (matching the chart above) and grey dots for successful notifications. The state change is the primary event, the notification is a consequence : color should go where the important thing happened. Failed notifications keep the error/warning icons. This aligns with Option 2 from the original feedback.

### Visual weight of notification rows

- [x] Reduce visual prominence of notification rows. Currently they have colored borders and backgrounds, which makes them stand out more than state change rows. Consider removing the border or using a neutral style, and reserving color emphasis for failure cases only.

### Icon consistency

- [x] Unify failure icons to a single icon (`exclamation-circle`) across the timeline. Currently we use `exclamation-circle`, `exclamation-triangle`, and `times-circle` in different places.

### Notification count in summary

- [x] Remove the notification count (e.g. "2 notifications") from the summary row when all deliveries succeed. When there are failures, the failure details are already shown. Consider showing the count only when there are multiple notifications and some have failed.

### Contact point should be clickable

- [x] Make the contact point name in the summary row a link to the contact point page.

### Expanded row content

- [x] We show integration type + index instead of repeating the contact point name for every integration.

### Multiple contact points

- [x] Notifications for the same alert instance sent to different contact points appear as separate notification groups in the timeline.

### Quick actions from the drawer

- [x] Add ability to silence a currently active notification or declare an incident directly from the drawer, so the user doesn't lose context by navigating away. 

## Why

**Why do we need this feature?**
Improves the UX in the timeline view.

**Who is this feature for?**

Alerting users.

**Special notes for your reviewer:**



https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f14f9f4e-99c7-4092-af66-39782919d9f9





Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: soniaAguilarPeiron

Niceeee, LGTM!

commits.message:
Alerting: Improve notification timeline UX in triage drawer

Clarify the delivery model by renaming "notifications" to "deliveries",
showing integration types and indices in summary rows, and surfacing
which specific integration failed instead of generic counts. Also enforce
a minimum drawer width of 700px for better readability on narrow screens.

Alerting: Revert delivery terminology back to notification in timeline

Keep consistent terminology with the rest of the codebase (API, data
model, detail pages). The real UX improvement is showing integration
types and indices, not renaming the term.

Alerting: Strengthen test assertions for notification timeline

Use strict getByText assertion for receiver name and add coverage
for the new integration summary text in the collapsed row.

Alerting: Fix outcome label test to match current format

Check for /failed/i instead of /^Delivered$/ so the test actually
verifies no outcome label appears when all notifications succeed.

Alerting: Improve notification timeline visual indicators

Address review feedback on summary row styling:
- Replace yellow/warning background with firing/resolved colors
- Move failure info right after status label for prominence
- Remove redundant Failed badge, show error message directly in red
- Replace timeline dots with icons for failure states (exclamation-triangle
  for partial failures, times-circle for all failed) with tooltips
- Use StateTag component for Firing/Resolved status badges

Alerting: Rename Instance Timeline heading to History

Aligns the triage drawer section heading with the terminology
used elsewhere in the alerting UI.

Alerting: Move timeline filter tabs inline with History heading

Lift filter state from InstanceTimeline to InstanceTimelineSection
and place the RadioButtonGroup on the same row as the heading, so
tabs are compact and don't stretch to full width.

Alerting: Color state change dots, grey for notification dots

State change dots now use semantic colors (red for firing
transitions, green for resolved) matching the chart above.
Successful notification dots are grey since they are secondary
events. Failed notifications keep error/warning icons.

Alerting: Remove borders from notification summary rows

Keep the subtle background colors (firing/resolved) but remove
the colored borders to reduce visual weight of notification rows
in the timeline.

Alerting: Unify failure dot icons to exclamation-circle

Use the same exclamation-circle icon for both all-failed and
some-failed notification dots instead of two different icons.

Alerting: Hide notification count when all deliveries succeed

Only show the notification count in the summary row when there
are failures, since the count adds no value when everything is
green.

Alerting: Make contact point name clickable in timeline

The contact point name in the notification summary row now links
to the notifications list filtered by that receiver, opening in
a new tab to preserve drawer context.
Revert unintended whitespace changes in logs panelcfg.cue

Alerting: Fix timeline dot alignment and timestamp column width

Use tabular-nums for timestamp digits so all rows have the same
width, and align dot paddingTop with the timestamp column to keep
dots vertically consistent across entry types.

Alerting: Use yellow dot for Pending state transitions

Pending transitions now use a warning-colored (yellow) dot to
match the pending state color in the chart, instead of red.

Alerting: Refactor timeline layout to CSS Grid for consistent vertical alignment

Replace flex-based column layout with CSS Grid to ensure timestamp,
dot, and content cells are always vertically aligned regardless of
content height differences between state change and notification rows.

Alerting: Label notification rows as 'Firing notification' / 'Resolved notification'

Makes notification rows immediately distinguishable from state change
rows in the timeline by adding 'notification' to the status badge text.

Alerting: Simplify notification summary rows in timeline

Show only status type, delivery failures (if any), and contact point
link. Remove integration icons, integration summary, and notification
count from collapsed rows as this info is redundant and available in
the expanded view.

Alerting: Count unique integrations for delivery summary, add @ icon for contact point

Delivery counts now reflect unique integrations rather than individual
attempts (including retries). Also adds an @ icon before the contact
point name to clarify what it represents.

Alerting: Reverse expanded notification rows to show newest first

Consistent with the timeline's own ordering where most recent entries
appear at the top.

Alerting: Fix notification badge to show lowercase 'notification'

Override StateTag's text-transform: capitalize so 'notification' stays
lowercase inside the pill badge, showing 'Firing notification' instead
of 'Firing Notification'.

Alerting: Simplify silence link by passing rule UID in labels directly

Instead of manually concatenating the MATCHER_ALERT_RULE_UID matcher
to the URL, pass it as part of the labels to makeLabelBasedSilenceLink.

Alerting: Use explicit error check instead of negated success check

Since outcome can only be 'success' or 'error', checking for 'error'
directly is more readable than negating 'success'.
Alerting: Make timeline filter prop optional with default 'all'
Alerting: Rename receivers to uniqueReceivers for clarity

Alerting: Sanitize contact point URL and add rel to silence link

Apply textUtil.sanitizeUrl() to the contact point href for defensive
URL sanitization, and add rel="noopener noreferrer" to the silence
LinkButton that opens in a new tab.

Alerting: Use dateTimeFormat for timeline timestamps to respect user timezone

Replace raw Intl.DateTimeFormat with dateTimeFormat from @grafana/data
so timeline timestamps honor the user's configured Grafana timezone
instead of always using the browser's local timezone.

Alerting: Extract computeIntegrationOutcomes into a testable pure function

Move the integration deduplication and outcome counting logic out of
the NotificationStatusGroup component into a standalone exported
function with unit tests covering retries, mixed outcomes, and
edge cases.

Alerting: Fix stale test assertions in notification timeline tests

Update assertions that referenced UI text from a previous iteration:
- Replace '1 notification' checks with role-based queries for the
  notification toggle button
- Remove '· Slack #1' assertion (integration info is only in expanded view)
- Fix delivery label assertion to match actual '1 delivered, 1 failed'

Alerting: Deduplicate timeline dot styles with shared base class

Extract common dot size and shape into a dotBase style, compose
with color-only variants via cx() to remove repeated properties.
Alerting: Use consistent rel attribute on target=_blank links

Alerting: Add missing test coverage for notification timeline

Cover previously untested paths: error message rendering (with and
without error text), multiple contact points receiver count, and
Pending state transitions.

Alerting: Fix silence link missing rule UID matcher

The previous simplification of passing __alert_rule_uid__ directly in
the labels object didn't work because getMatcherQueryParams filters out
private labels (those wrapped in __). Restore manual URL append to
ensure the rule UID matcher is included in the silence link.

title: Unified: prioritize migration log in status reader
body:
## Summary

**Migration log takes priority over config for Dual Writer Modes**
- Previously, config mode 0-3 would always return `DualWrite` regardless of migration log state. Now the migration log is prioritized — if a successful migration entry exists, the resource moves to Unified (mode 5)

This allows resources to transition to unified storage as soon as the migration completes, without waiting for a config change. We cache the query results for 5 seconds by default, so the migration controller needs to keep the tables locked for at least that amount of time after data migration is finished.

**New metrics**
- `migration_status_reader_bootstrap_failures_total` — incremented when `EnsureMigrationLogTable` fails at startup.
- `dualwriter_status_reader_null_total{resource}` — incremented when status reader is nil (shouldn't happen).
- `dualwriter_status_reader_errors_total{resource}` — incremented when `GetStorageMode` returns an error.

**Status reader**
- Added result caching (`gocache`) to `migrationStatusReader` to avoid repeated DB queries

**Dualwrite service config fallback**
- Removed per-resource caching (`sync.Map`, `gocache`) from dualwrite service — caching now lives in the status reader

Logs:
```
logger=storage.unified.migrations t=2026-03-18T11:18:53.072884+01:00 level=info msg="Resolved storage mode from migration log" resource=dashboards.dashboard.grafana.app mode=unified
logger=storage.unified.migrations t=2026-03-18T11:18:52.18037+01:00 level=info msg="Resolved storage mode from migration log" resource=folders.folder.grafana.app mode=unified
```

## Test plan
- [x] `go test ./pkg/storage/unified/migrations/ -run 'Test(MigrationStatusReader|StorageMode)'`
- [x] `go test ./pkg/storage/legacysql/dualwrite/`
- [x] `go build ./pkg/server/` — wire_gen.go updated for new `prometheus.Registerer` parameter

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: RafaelPaulovic
