title: IAM: remove BasicRole from RoleBinding subject kinds
body:
## Summary
Assigning roles to basic roles is not something we provide with RBAC. The recommended approach has always been to grant roles to teams. This PR removes the possibility from the cue files.

- Remove `BasicRole` from the `#Subject.kind` union in both `RoleBindingSpec` and `GlobalRoleBindingSpec` CUE definitions and regenerated Go types
- Remove the `BasicRole` case from `GetRoleBindingTuple` in `tuple_helpers.go`
- Update proto comments to reflect the reduced set of subject kinds (`User/Team/ServiceAccount`)
- Remove all test cases that exercise `BasicRole` as a rolebinding subject kind

- [x] `go test ./pkg/services/authz/zanzana/...` passes

title: Elasticsearch: Raw query editor metrics orderby bug
body: When using the raw DSL query editor with a terms bucket aggregation, if the user does not specify an order field in their raw query, Grafana would parse no orderBy setting. This caused addTermsAgg to skip applying any order clause, resulting in inconsistent or missing results when running metric queries.
author: bossinc

commits.message: If order by is not defined, add it with metrics queries

title: Snapshots: Fix panel share using dashboard url
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

When viewing a dashboard snapshot, copying a panel share link from the panel menu now correctly generates a URL pointing to the snapshot rather than the original dashboard.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Previously, the share link URL was built using the original dashboard's UID as the path identifier. Since snapshot URLs use a separate access key (e.g. /dashboard/snapshot/{snapshotKey}), the generated link would resolve to the original dashboard — or fail entirely if the user didn't have access to it. The snapshot key was never being stored on the scene or passed through to the URL builder.

**Who is this feature for?**

Users who share dashboard snapshots and want to link others directly to a specific panel within that snapshot.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/116029

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

Steps to reproduce the bug:
1. Open any dashboard
2. Open the share menu and create a snapshot (Share → Snapshot)
3. Navigate to the snapshot URL
4. Reveal the panel menu (the ... icon)
5. Click Share → Share link
6. Copy the generated link and open it in a new tab
7. Observe the failure or link incorrectly pointing to the dashboard rather than the snapshot

Steps to verify the fix:
1–5. Same as above
6. Copy the generated link — confirm the URL contains /dashboard/snapshot/{snapshotKey} matching the snapshot you are viewing
7. Open the link in a new tab — the snapshot loads correctly at the expected panel
author: CollinFingar

reviews.body: LGTM. Tested and works as expected

title: Playlist: Restore playlist nav
body: ## What is this feature? Restores the playlist navigation buttons, and adds a user-facing option to show/hide them in the playlist start modal. ## Why do we need this feature? Playlist nav buttons were removed for dynamic dashboards. After many complains from customers and community users, this PR put them back with also the possibility of hiding them if necessary. ## Who is this feature for? Grafana users who use playlists and want control over the dashboard UI during playlist playback. ## Which issue(s) does this PR fix? ## Special notes for your reviewer - [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a notable improvement, it's added to What's New. **Changes summary:** - `DashboardControls.tsx`: restore controls visibility during playlist nav; respect `hidePlaylistNav` state to conditionally show prev/next buttons - `PlaylistSrv.ts`: expose `hidePlaylistNav` in playlist state - `StartModal.tsx`: add checkbox to toggle playlist navigation buttons visibility (behind `dashboardNewLayouts` feature flag); update label text - `DashboardControls.test.tsx`: extend test coverage for `hidePlaylistNav` undefined and true states - `grafana.json`: updated i18n translations via `make i18n-extract` | Desktop | Mobile | |--------|--------| | | |
author: juanicabanas

reviews.body:
LGTM. Tested and works as expected.
I'm not sure about the placement of the option in the Starting modal, since these are not part of the `dashboard controls`. It's more about playlist controls.
It may be better to put it above the `dashboard controls` as an independent option.
Can you show a screenshot? Is the back and forward part of a button group with stop in the middle?

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

title: Alerting: Treat not found error when fetching plugins as not installed
body:
**Problem**
Plugin detection in `usePluginBridge` was propagating not found errors after we migrated to using RTK Query, so when OnCall / IRM / Incident plugins were not installed the 404 response was surfaced as an error object, causing error banners to be shown on the contact points edit form for users who don't have any of these plugins installed. 

**Fix**
- Added a 404 check inside usePluginBridge
  - If the plugin settings API returns a 404, treat it as { installed: false } (not installed) instead of propagating the error
  - Non-404 errors (e.g., 500) are still propagated as before
- Updated the tests

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/119311

This PR reverts the `typesVersions` addition to `@grafana/alerting` from #120020.

The root cause (grafana-assistant-app using `moduleResolution: "node"`) has been fixed upstream by switching to `moduleResolution: "bundler"` in https://github.com/grafana/grafana-assistant-app/pull/5338.

Removing `typesVersions` keeps `@grafana/alerting` consistent with how the other `@grafana/*` packages are defined.

title: V2 Dashboards: Fix startup crash when v2 returns null annotations or variables
body:
**What is this feature?**

The v2 API accepts null for annotations and variables in the dashboard spec. When such a dashboard is loaded, the frontend crashes on startup because it calls array methods (.some(), .map(), etc.) on null.
author: kristinademeshchik

title: Testing: Fix flaky ProfileButton test by mocking news feed
body:
Mocks `loadFeed` in the `ProfileButton` test so it returns an empty items array instead of making a real network request to `grafana.com/blog/news.xml`.

The test is flaky because it renders the real `NewsContainer` without mocking the fetch layer. When the fetch completes before the test finishes, `feedToDataFrame` passes RSS `pubDate` values (e.g. `"Mon, 09 Mar 2026 14:04:35"`) to `dateTime()` without a format string, which triggers a moment.js deprecation `console.warn`. `jest-fail-on-console` (enabled in CI) then fails the test.

Whether the fetch completes in time is non-deterministic — the test passes when the network is slow and fails when it's fast.
author: tomratcliffe

title: AuthZ: Grant datasources:query permission to render service
body:
## Summary

- Fix 403 errors when the image renderer queries datasources through the multi-tenant query service
- Add `datasources:query` to the hardcoded permission allowlist for `TypeRenderService` identities in `getRendererPermissions()`

## Root cause

When the render service captures alert screenshots, it authenticates as `TypeRenderService` and queries datasources via `POST /apis/query.grafana.app/v0alpha1/.../query/name`. The authz mapper translates this to the `datasources:query` action, but `getRendererPermissions()` only allowed `datasources:read` — not `datasources:query` — resulting in 403 Forbidden.

Fixes grafana/grafana-enterprise#11145

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify image renderer can successfully capture alert screenshots with aggregated query service enabled
- [ ] Confirm no 403 errors in logs for render service datasource queries
- [ ] Existing authz tests pass

title: Dynamic dashboards: Add assistant prompt popover on panel click
body:
## Add assistant prompt popover on panel click in view mode

Requires https://github.com/grafana/grafana-assistant-app/pull/5337

Currently there is no easily discoverable way to interact with the assistant from dashboard view mode without openeing the assistant sidepane.

### Solution


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a5ece0dd-6b11-4f1d-ab19-64ecde9d6815


Floating prompt card appears on panel click in view mode, sends prompts with panel context to the assistant sidebar.

- Behind `dashboardAssistantPopover` feature toggle (experimental, off by default)
- Requires signed-in user with assistant access
- Only on panels with data queries
- AI sparkle icon on panel hover, animated gradient border on selection

### New interaction reports

- `dashboards_assistant_popover_displayed` -- panelCount, pluginIds
- `dashboards_assistant_popover_prompt_submitted` -- panelCount, promptLength
- `dashboards_assistant_popover_closed` -- action (escape/click_outside)

## How to test

Enable: `dashboardNewLayouts`, `dashboardAssistantPopover`, `extensionSidebar`, `externalServiceAccounts`. Assistant plugin installed, user signed in.

1. View mode, hover data panel -- sparkle icon appears
2. Click panel -- gradient border + prompt card below
3. Submit prompt -- assistant sidebar opens
4. Escape or click outside -- card closes
5. Text panel (no queries) -- nothing happens
6. Edit mode -- normal edit behavior, no popover
7. Toggle off / sign out -- no changes visible

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

Guard enableSelection/disableSelection against redundant setState calls

Prevents unnecessary re-renders when selection state is already in the
desired state. The assistant hook calls enableSelection on every render
cycle, and without the guard each call created a new state object that
triggered extra re-renders via useSceneObjectState, interfering with
useSidebar's internal dock state.

Move enableSelection/disableSelection from assistant hook to splitter

The assistant hook's useEffect was causing state changes on editPane
via enableSelection(), which triggered re-renders through
useSceneObjectState that interfered with useSidebar's internal dock
state in jsdom. Moving the effect back to the splitter component
(where it originally lived) keeps it in the same render cycle as
useSidebar, avoiding the race condition.

Enable selection on activate when dashboard is editing

Prevents the splitter's useEffect from triggering a setState on
editPane during the first render, which was causing re-renders that
interfered with useSidebar's dock state in CI.

Fix last duplicate isLoading property in HelpModal test

Clear selection and disable selection mode on editPane deactivation

Prevents the animated border and assistant popover from persisting
when navigating away from a dashboard and back.

title: Dashboard: Import - Fix Datasource not mapped in panels inside collapsed rows
body: When a dashboard has a collapsed row, its child panels are stored in `panel.panels[]` (not `dashboard.panels[]`). `applyV1Inputs` only iterated `dashboard.panels[]` shallowly, so child panels never had their `${DS_...} UIDs` resolved. **How to reproduce:** 1. Find or create a dashboard JSON with a collapsed row containing panels (e.g. collapse a row in a dashboard and export it - I used http://localhost:3000/d/Repeating-rows-uid/repeating-rows?orgId=1&from=now-6h&to=now&timezone=browser&var-server=A&var-server=B&var-pod=1&var-pod=2) 2. Go to Dashboards → Import via the v1 K8s path (disable `dashboardNewLayouts`) 3. Upload the JSON 4. On the import form, map the datasource to a real datasource and click Import 5. Open a panel inside that collapsed row *Before Data source not found or shows the raw ${DS_...} UID * After this PR Data source is mapped correctly Before **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**: Fixes # **Special notes for your reviewer:** Please check that: - [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Table: Add nested field overrides behind flag
body:
Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/99450, https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/109936

This PR updates field overrides and the Table panel to add support for field overrides in nested fields. Before this PR, nested fields could not be targeted for overrides.

### Summary of changes

- makes the required updates in the `applyFieldOverrides` method to recursively apply field overrides when a field of type `nestedFrame` is encountered.
  - ⚠️ this might be considered a breaking change when it is enabled, because it will lead to nested fields getting default options for the first time.
- makes the required updates in the Matchers UI / Field Overrides editor to show the appropriate nested fields in the UI when adding overrides by type and by name.
  - an extra prop was added to `Combobox` to support custom empty messages (which was a feature of `Select` we still used in MatchersUI)
  - because we touched the `useSelectOptions` hook to build the list of fields for a custom field picker to return ComboboxOptions, I replaced the usage of that field in Canvas and Geomap to Comboboxes from Selects.
- added a new gdev dashboard, some additional e2es, and updated the table e2e utils to be better set up to correctly test nested tables by using locators with strict hierarchy.

### Testing

- To test a normal nested table, I've updated the Kitchen Sink gdev dashboard's nested table to have more styles mirroring the kitchen sink patterns in the adjacent table.
- To test something more hardcore, there's a new gdev dashboard in this PR which turns the kitchen sink into a nested table, with all kinds of features exercised like word wrapping, all sorts of cell types, tooltip from field, styling from field, data links and actions, etc.
- To test this Markdown Dynamic Height and Pagination stuff, I used the Markdown Table dev dashboard, and then added a "Group to Nested Table" transform. Try grouping by both the `id` and `markdown` fields.

### Screenshots

#### Before

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5db32070-6a1c-4fca-ab40-e875534b0755

#### After

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b52cf308-ac15-4854-a3ca-876a83fa9fe1
author: fastfrwrd

reviews.body:
Code looks good, assuming there's nothing major uncovered in manual QA we should be good to merge.

Lots of nits on a PR of this size, and plenty more we could do, but this looks good enough to merge! 🚀

### BUGS: 1. When the feature toggle is disabled but the nested table transform is used we still see the header for `Target fields` but no selector shows up. 2. Resizing via column header dragging doesn't set override in nested frame, but on the top level scope. It does change the width, but the changes are lost when the table is saved/refreshed/ or another row is opened. But if I change the override scope to nested then it does work as expected. 3. When selecting nested frames convert field type doesn't appear to work if the transformation is AFTER the `group to nested table` transform. I know we were assuming the table transform would be last, but ideally we shouldn't show the nested fields in transformations where the behavior isn't supported. 4. Table footer > Calculation doesn't work on nested fields 5. Disabling the feature toggle doesn't stop overrides from being applied with the scoped defined when the flag was added. Probably fine to deal with later? ### NITS: 1. Thresholds doesn't do anything on table frames (don't address in this PR) 2. ~4px extra space on the right compared to other transforms 3. Overall this transformation could use a bit of UI clean up.

title: QueryEditor: Fix card flicker
body:
## Description
<!--- A high level description of what this PR is doing -->
It's _really_ hard to see in local, but in ops and other instances, there's a pronounced card flicker 1) on load (the cards render, go away, then come back), and 2) on any action (delete, hide, duplicate, etc). 

Another benefit is that it fixes this reported bug! ⬇️ 
Resolves https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/119992

## Changes
<!--- Describe your changes in detail -->
* Remove the `isLoading` check and loading component render. The card data lives in `queryRunner.state.queries` and is always synchronously available. It has no dependency on whether queries are currently executing, so the `isLoading` state probably isn't necessary.

N/A

title: CI: Enable Go module caching for backend unit tests
body:
## Summary

Enable Go module caching on both the `grafana` (OSS) and `grafana-enterprise` jobs in the backend unit tests workflow.

### Problem

The backend unit tests workflow uses `actions/setup-go` **without** `cache: true`. This means every shard job (8 OSS + 8 Enterprise = 16 jobs) downloads the full Go dependency tree from scratch on every run.

### How `actions/setup-go` caching works

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A["Compute cache key from:\n- runner.os\n- runner.arch\n- Go version\n- hash of dependency files"] --> B{"Exact key match\nin GitHub cache?"}
    B -->|Yes| C["Restore GOMODCACHE + GOCACHE\nfrom cache archive"]
    C --> D["cache-hit = true\n(no downloads needed)"]
    B -->|No| E{"Partial prefix\nmatch?"}
    E -->|Yes| F["Restore best partial match\n(some downloads needed)"]
    E -->|No| G["No cache restored\n(full download of ~400 modules)"]
    F --> H["Post-job: save updated\ncache under new key"]
    G --> H
```

### Fix

Add `cache: true` with `cache-dependency-path: "**/*.sum"` to both `setup-go` steps. The glob matches all 49 `go.sum` files and `go.work.sum` across the 39-module Go workspace, ensuring the cache key accurately reflects the full dependency state.

This matches the pattern already applied to the integration test workflow in #119968.

See [actions/setup-go: Caching in multi-module repositories](https://github.com/actions/setup-go/blob/main/docs/advanced-usage.md#caching-in-multi-module-repositories) for the recommended pattern.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify cache hit behavior on a follow-up push to this PR (second run should show `cache-hit: true` in the `Setup Go` step logs)
- [ ] Compare `Setup Go` step duration between this PR and a recent `main` run
- [ ] Confirm no `go: downloading ...` output during test execution when cache is warm

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

title: feat(import): support constant variables in V2 import and strip export metadata
body:
## Summary

- **V2 constant variable support**: The V2 dashboard import path was missing constant variable handling. When importing a V2 dashboard with `ConstantVariable` kinds, the user had no way to customize their values. This adds extraction, rendering, and application of constants in the V2 flow — matching the capability that existed in the V1/classic import path.
- **V1 export metadata cleanup**: The K8s V1 import path was not stripping `__inputs`, `__elements`, and `__requires` metadata before saving. The legacy backend import endpoint explicitly removed these keys, so this aligns the new path with the old behavior.

### Changes

| File | What |
|---|---|
| `inputs.ts` | `extractV2Inputs` now detects `ConstantVariable` and populates `inputs.constants`; `applyV2Inputs` applies user-provided constant values; new `stripExportMetadata` helper |
| `ImportDashboardFormV2.tsx` | Renders constant text input fields (matching V1's `ImportForm`) |
| `ImportOverviewV1.tsx` | Strips `__inputs`/`__elements`/`__requires` before saving |
| `inputs.test.ts` | Tests for V2 constant extraction, application, and preservation |

## Test plan

- [ ] Import a V2-exported dashboard containing `ConstantVariable` — verify the import form shows text inputs for each constant
- [ ] Change constant values during import — verify the imported dashboard uses the new values
- [ ] Import a V2 dashboard without constants — verify no regressions
- [ ] Import a classic/V1 dashboard — verify `__inputs`/`__elements`/`__requires` are not persisted in the saved JSON

title: CI: Fix Go module cache key for integration tests
body:
## Summary

Add `cache-dependency-path: "**/*.sum"` to all 6 `actions/setup-go` steps in the integration test workflow.

### Problem

The integration test workflow uses `actions/setup-go` with `cache: true` but without `cache-dependency-path`. This causes the cache key to only hash the root `go.sum` file, missing the 48 other `go.sum` files and `go.work.sum` across the 39-module Go workspace.

`actions/setup-go` computes a cache key like:

```
setup-go-<runner.os>-<runner.arch>-go-<version>-<hash-of-dependency-files>
```

Without `cache-dependency-path`, only the root `go.sum` is hashed. This leads to:

- **Stale hits**: A sub-module's `go.sum` changes (new dependency) but the root `go.sum` doesn't. The cache key matches an old archive missing the new modules, so Go re-downloads them anyway.
- **False misses**: The root `go.sum` changes (e.g. `go mod tidy`) but actual dependencies are the same. The key doesn't match, so the entire GOMODCACHE is re-downloaded from scratch across all 36+ shard jobs.

### How `actions/setup-go` caching works

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A["Compute cache key from:\n- runner.os\n- runner.arch\n- Go version\n- hash of dependency files"] --> B{"Exact key match\nin GitHub cache?"}
    B -->|Yes| C["Restore GOMODCACHE + GOCACHE\nfrom cache archive"]
    C --> D["cache-hit = true\n(no downloads needed)"]
    B -->|No| E{"Partial prefix\nmatch?"}
    E -->|Yes| F["Restore best partial match\n(some downloads needed)"]
    E -->|No| G["No cache restored\n(full download of ~400 modules)"]
    F --> H["Post-job: save updated\ncache under new key"]
    G --> H
```

**Before (root `go.sum` only):** The dependency hash misses changes in 48 sub-module `go.sum` files and `go.work.sum`, causing frequent stale hits or false misses.

**After (`**/*.sum`):** The dependency hash covers all 49 `go.sum` files and `go.work.sum`, so the cache key accurately reflects the full workspace dependency state.

### Fix

Set `cache-dependency-path: "**/*.sum"` so the cache key hashes all 49 `go.sum` files **and** `go.work.sum`, accurately reflecting the full workspace dependency state and improving cache hit rates.

See [actions/setup-go: Caching in multi-module repositories](https://github.com/actions/setup-go/blob/main/docs/advanced-usage.md#caching-in-multi-module-repositories) for the recommended pattern.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify cache hit behavior on a follow-up push to this PR (second run should show `cache-hit: true` in the `Setup Go` step logs)
- [ ] Compare `Setup Go` step duration between this PR and a recent `main` run
- [ ] Confirm no `go: downloading ...` output during test execution when cache is warm

title: Alerting: Additional triage improvements
body: **What is this feature?** Adds a persistent labels sidebar to the alerting triage workbench and improves the collapse/expand experience for grouped rows. The sidebar shows a state filter (Firing / Pending) and a full label breakdown with per-value instance counts and click-to-filter functionality. The sidebar is collapsible. A "Collapse all / Expand all" toolbar button is also added to the workbench. **Why do we need this feature?** The triage view previously showed label statistics only in an inline summary section that was limited and hard to navigate. Users had no quick way to filter by alert state or by a specific label value directly from a persistent UI element. The previous `AllLabelsDrawer` required opening a drawer, and the label buttons in the summary section were awkward. Additionally, there was no way to collapse or expand all groups at once when the list was long. **Who is this feature for?** On-call engineers and SRE teams using the Grafana alerting triage workbench to triage and investigate firing/pending alerts at scale. **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**: Fixes # **Special notes for your reviewer:** Key changes: - **New `LabelsColumn` component** (`triage/scene/filters/LabelsColumn.tsx`): a collapsible left sidebar with a state filter (firing/pending toggles with live counts) and a full label breakdown with search/filter input. - **New `LabelsContent` component** (`triage/scene/filters/LabelsContent.tsx`): renders each label's values as collapsible rows with unique value counts and click-to-add-filter behaviour. - **Removed `AllLabelsDrawer`** and the inline `LabelStatsSection` from `SummaryStats.tsx` in favour of the sidebar. - **`SummaryStats` simplified**: removed instance count query and display, keeping only rule counts. - **Collapse/Expand all**: `WorkbenchContext` now exposes `expandGeneration`/`collapseGeneration` counters; `GenericRow` subscribes to these to expand or collapse. A toolbar button in `Workbench.tsx` toggles all rows. - **Row styling improvements**: `GroupRow` and `FolderGroupRow` now have card-style borders/rounded corners; `GenericRow` supports an `indentBorder` prop for leaf rows (instances). - **Label value matching in the sidebar**: clicking a value in the sidebar adds an exact-match filter. Please check that: - [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Scopes: Add ScopeNavigationOptions contract type for depth and rootScope
body:
## What this PR does / why we need it

Adds a `ScopeNavigationOptions` struct to the scope API types defining the `depth` and `rootScope` query parameters for the `/find/scope_navigations` and `/find/scope_dashboard_bindings` connect endpoints.

This type documents the API contract for external backend implementations (e.g., some other Scopes client) per [hyperion-planning#523](https://github.com/grafana/hyperion-planning/issues/523). It is **not** a `runtime.Object` — the Grafana connect handlers parse query params directly from the request URL and do not yet consume these fields.

### Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `depth` | `int32` | Current nesting level in the rendered navigation tree. 0/omitted = top-level (non-nested) navigation. 1 = first level of sub-scope expansion. 2 = sub-scope within a sub-scope, etc. |
| `rootScope` | `string` | The top-level navigation scope the user started from. Stays constant when navigating into sub-scopes. Tells the backend which scope initiated the navigation session. Omitted for top-level requests. |

### Related PRs

- #119551 — Frontend: depth and rootScope param plumbing through ScopesDashboardsService

## Test plan

- [x] `gofmt` passes
- [ ] CI passes (backend-only change, no frontend impact)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: DynamicDashboards: Improve copy/paste of custom grid panels
body: While testing https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/119305, I noticed console warnings that occured after pasting a panel across tabs/rows that used different layout types (e.g., `GridLayout` → `AutoGrid`): **Problem:** When pasting a panel, [`getPanelFromClipboard`](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/public/app/features/dashboard-scene/scene/layouts-shared/paste.ts#L80) deserializes the clipboard data into a grid item that becomes the `VizPanel`'s parent. If the target layout requires a different grid item type, the conversion functions (`getAutoGridItemFromClipboard` / `getDashboardGridItemFromClipboard`) pass that same `VizPanel` to a new grid item constructor, triggering the warning because it already has a parent. **Solution:** This PR updates `getPanelFromClipboard` to return a plain object `{ body, key, variableName }` instead of a `SceneObject`. The deserialized grid item stays internal and the `VizPanel` is detached via `clearParent()` before being returned. Callers now always construct the correct grid item type directly — no `instanceof` checks or conversion branches needed. **Opportunity for improvement:** When pasting into a `DefaultGridLayout` from a same-type clipboard, the panel previously kept its original grid position, potentially overlapping existing panels. It now always uses the position computed by `findSpaceForNewPanel`, consistent with cross-layout pastes.

title: Alerting: Fix flaky test
body:
Since we weren't returning the actual config from the test server, an update call was being made when in the real code it would have verified the hash had not changed and sent no update.

The flakiness was caused by the updated config having a new `CreatedAt` timestamp. If the test was fast, the updated config was identical. If the test was slow, the update config has a newer `CreatedAt` value.

title: [release-12.4.1] A11y MenuItem: Fix voice over announce 1 of 1 for Keyboard shortcuts
body:
Backport 379a592d39f13565af88026c602e4af68dc7dc3b from #118170

---

**What is this feature?**

Fixes VoiceOver announcing "1 of 1" for the Keyboard shortcuts item in the Help dropdown, while other items correctly announce "X of Y" (e.g. "4 of 6").

Callers can still override via the `role` prop. Existing usage is unchanged: all `MenuItem` with `url` in this repo are inside `Menu`, so no orphan menuitems are introduced.

**Why do we need this feature?**

The Help menu mixes link items (`<a href="...">`) and the Keyboard shortcuts button. Previously, only the button got `role="menuitem"`; links kept the default (no role, exposed as "link"). VoiceOver counts list position by role, so it treated the single menuitem as its own list of one ("1 of 1") and the links as a separate list.

**Who is this feature for?**

all users

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/117832

body:

---

**What is this feature?**

Adds `aria-expanded` attribute to expand/collapse buttons across the Grafana UI to announce state changes to screen readers, per WAI-ARIA disclosure pattern.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Screen readers were not announcing expanded/collapsed state changes when users activated expand/collapse buttons. The `aria-expanded` attribute programmatically communicates state to assistive technologies, meeting WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) requirements.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/117828

title: unified-storage: make isSnowflake deterministic and fix test flake
body:
Previously, the `isSnowflake` function was non-deterministic and kind of brittle: it would compare the given `rv` with a timestamp one hour in the future. If the caller passed a microsecond RV 1h01nsec in the future, it would be considered a snowflake even though the actual integer number is still a lot smaller than an actual snowflake ID.

In this commit `isSnowflake` becomes deterministic by checking if the given `rv` has 19 digits (every valid snowflake in the time range we care about has 19 digits). If it doesn't it's considered a legacy microsecond RV.

This was causing some flakes in the KV store's GC tests where a timestamp of "1 hour from now" was being used and led to RVs being considered snowflake IDs _sometimes_.

For reference, these were the flakes:

```
=== RUN   TestIntegrationGarbageCollectionGroupResource/can_garbage_collect_a_deleted_resource
    storage_backend_gc_test.go:170:
                Error Trace:    /Users/rc/src/grafana/grafana/pkg/storage/unified/resource/storage_backend_gc_test.go:170
                Error:          Not equal:
                                expected: 0
                                actual  : 2
                Test:           TestIntegrationGarbageCollectionGroupResource/can_garbage_collect_a_deleted_resource

=== RUN   TestIntegrationGarbageCollectionGroupResource/pagination_does_not_delete_resources_that_were_recreated
    storage_backend_gc_test.go:472:
                Error Trace:    /Users/rc/src/grafana/grafana/pkg/storage/unified/resource/storage_backend_gc_test.go:472
                Error:          Not equal:
                                expected: 2
                                actual  : 7
                Test:           TestIntegrationGarbageCollectionGroupResource/pagination_does_not_delete_resources_that_were_recreated
```

---

**What is this feature?**

Adds clearer and more opinionated resource requirements for Grafana based on deployment size

**Why do we need this feature?**

The current docs are only the bare minimum to run Grafana server (which in and of itself is not very resource intensive) and is not sufficient for any sort of resource planning or estimation. his addition creates a basic framework to estimate deployment size and associated required resources

**Who is this feature for?**

Users planning to deploy Grafana

title: [release-12.4.1] fix(folders): read dual-writer mode dynamically to prevent stale legacy stats
body:
Backport d790eae51f38657644331905dc6e18f3e5465bcb from #119639

---

## Summary

Fixes #119488 — "Folders: Can't delete empty folder in 12.4.0"

After upgrading to 12.4 from a prior version, users cannot delete folders that appear empty. The root cause is a **timing issue** between the federated client creation and auto-migration:

1. During wire DI, the `LegacyStatsGetter` captured `DisableSQLFallbackDashboards` as a static boolean — always `false` because Mode5 hasn't been set yet
2. Auto-migration runs later during service startup and sets Mode5, but the boolean was already captured
3. When deleting a folder, `validateOnDelete` → `GetStats()` always appended legacy SQL dashboard counts (stale records from pre-migration that were never cleaned up)
4. This caused "folder is not empty" even though the dashboard was already deleted from unified storage

The fix replaces the static booleans with a `*setting.Cfg` pointer, reading the dual-writer mode at query time so auto-migration changes are picked up immediately.

## Changes

- **`federated/stats.go`**: Replace `DisableSQLFallbackDashboards`/`DisableSQLFallbackFolders` booleans with `*setting.Cfg` and dynamic mode-checking methods
- **`federated/client.go`**: Update `NewFederatedClient` to accept `*setting.Cfg` instead of two booleans
- **`client.go`**: Simplify call site, remove eagerly-computed boolean logic
- **`federated/stats_test.go`**: Update existing tests + add `DynamicModeChange` test verifying the fix

## Test plan

- [x] All existing `TestIntegrationDirectSQLStats` tests pass
- [x] New `DynamicModeChange` test verifies that changing cfg mode after `LegacyStatsGetter` creation is reflected in subsequent `GetStats` calls
- [ ] Manual verification: upgrade from 12.3 → 12.4, create folder+dashboard in 12.3, delete dashboard then folder in 12.4
author: app/grafana-delivery-bot

title: annotations: add support for metadata.generateName
body:
Adds support for `metadata.generateName` to the new Annotations App Platform API and validates that either `metadata.name` or `metadata.generateName` are provided when creating annotations.

_Note: This will not impact the names generated for annotations served from legacy sql storage as they are named according to the pattern `a-<database_id>`, with the name being derived from the ID in the database._

title: Alerting: Alert list view filter sidebar
body: **What is this feature?** Introduces a persistent filter sidebar for the alert rule list v2. Previously, advanced filters (state, type, health, rule source, contact point, labels, namespace/group, etc.) lived inside a popup panel that had to be explicitly opened. This PR moves all those controls into a dedicated `RulesFilterSidebar` component that is always visible alongside the rule list, with filters applying immediately on change. The sidebar is gated behind the existing `alertingFilterV2` feature toggle. **Why do we need this feature?** The popup filter UX requires extra clicks to access and apply filters, and lacks visual affordance that filters are active. A persistent sidebar makes filters always discoverable and provides a faster, more efficient filtering workflow. **Who is this feature for?** Grafana users who manage or monitor alert rules using the alert rule list view (v2). **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**: Fixes # **Special notes for your reviewer:** - The feature is behind the `alertingFilterV2` feature toggle — enable it to see the sidebar. - `RulesFilter.v2.tsx` has been significantly trimmed: all advanced filter field components have been moved to `RulesFilterSidebar.tsx`. - `RuleList.v2.tsx` renders the sidebar conditionally next to the main content area. Please check that: - [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Scopes: Add depth and rootScope parameters to scope navigation requests
body: ## What this PR does / why we need it Adds `depth` and `rootScope` parameters to scope navigation API requests, enabling the backend to return contextual results for nested scope navigation. - **`depth`**: Current nesting level in the rendered navigation tree (0 = top-level, omitted; 1 = first sub-scope expansion, 2 = nested sub-scope, etc.) - **`rootScope`**: The top-level scope the navigation tree was built for — always `forScopeNames[0]`, not `navigationScope` (which is UI state only set when on a dashboard under a sub-scope). Omitted on the root request, present on all sub-scope expansion requests. Ref: [hyperion-planning#523](https://github.com/grafana/hyperion-planning/issues/523) ### Changes - **`endpoints.gen.ts`**: Regenerated from updated OpenAPI spec — `depth` and `rootScope` params added to `scope_navigations` and `scope_dashboard_bindings` endpoints; stale `name` path param removed from arg types. - **`ScopesApiClient.ts`**: `fetchScopeNavigations` accepts optional `{ depth, rootScope }` and forwards to RTK Query. Both params use truthy checks — `depth: 0` and empty `rootScope` are omitted. - **`ScopesDashboardsService.ts`**: Top-level navigation requests omit both params. Sub-scope expansion passes `depth = path.length - 1` and `rootScope = forScopeNames[0]`. - **Tests**: `ScopesApiClient` — 3 new tests covering forwarding, depth-0 omission, and no-options case. `ScopesDashboardsService` — assertions updated to expect depth/rootScope; new test verifying rootScope comes from `forScopeNames` regardless of `navigationScope`. `dashboardsList` — 6 assertions updated. ### Demo https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d2ba14dc-f56d-4dec-a047-42be7faf3a84 ### Backward compatibility Both parameters are optional. When omitted (top-level requests), behavior is identical — falsy values are stripped before the HTTP request is made. ### Note on `endpoints.gen.ts` The scope API client has no codegen registration in the enterprise pipeline — `sync-from-enterprise.sh` was broken from the start because `yarn generate:api-client` was never run for scope. The file was hand-generated and maintained manually. grafana-enterprise#11420 fixes this permanently by registering scope in `generate-enterprise-apis.ts`. Until that lands, `endpoints.gen.ts` is maintained by hand with depth/rootScope matching the backend contract from grafana-enterprise#11210. Additionally, a subsequent enterprise commit (`ad792dafa`) accidentally stripped depth/rootScope from the OpenAPI snapshot — so `TestIntegrationOpenAPIs` passes but does not reflect the backend contract from #11210. That's a separate fix. ### Related PRs - grafana-enterprise#11210 — Backend: OpenAPI spec with depth + rootScope params (**merged**) - grafana-enterprise#11420 — Fix scope codegen registration (**open**) - #119893 — Go contract type (`ScopeNavigationOptions`) (**merged**) - #119566 — Frontend: remove stale `name` field (**merged**) ## Test plan - [x] All 417 scopes unit + integration tests pass - [x] E2E mock routes handle additional query params - [x] Manual verification: `scope_navigations?scope=...&depth=1&rootScope=...` visible in network tab on sub-scope expansion; root request omits both params - [x] CI passes 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: ericrshields

reviews.body: If the `preLoadSubScopeChildren` for a step is true, it will load with the correct depth, but it does not pass a rootScope. I suppose we should always consider the rootScope whichever scope is loading at depth=0, rather than using navigationScope (which is more used to maintain the UI state). navigationScope is only defined if you are on a dashboard under a subScope.

commits.message:
Scopes: Forward depth and rootScope to scope navigation API
Wire both params into fetchScopeNavigations: sub-scope expansion passes
  depth = path.length - 1 and rootScope = forScopeNames[0]; top-level calls
  omit both. Truthy checks ensure depth 0 and empty rootScope are stripped
  before the HTTP request.

Scopes: Remove unused name field from scope find endpoint generated types

The name path parameter is present in the OpenAPI spec but unused by the
find endpoints. Codegen re-adds it each run; remove it manually until the
spec is corrected upstream (same fix as #119566).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: chore: migrate grafana-app-sdk off of CLI flags and to config.cue blocks
body:
**What is this feature?**

Introduced in grafana-app-sdk recently is a change to define configuration options for apps via a new config cue block, rather than using CLI flags. This migrates all projects off the CLI flags and onto a config cue block.
- PR: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-app-sdk/pull/1130
- Release: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-app-sdk/releases/tag/v0.49.0
- Docs: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-app-sdk/blob/main/docs/code-generation.md#kind-code-generation

**How was it tested?**
Ran `make gen-apps` and all generated code remains intact without unexpected changes.

**Why do we need this feature?**

The CLI flags in the SDK are going to be removed in a future release, so this is in preparation for that.

title: Plugins: Update catalog to support community managed plugins
body: **What is this feature?** - Updates the Plugin Catalog to show managed plugins in grafana cloud, based on the info returned by gcom. - Updates Plugin Catalog to show managed community plugins in a different way (only cloud): - Show community plugins as managed in the catalog - Only allow updates to the latest available versions of a given major **Why do we need this feature?** This feature is necessary to allow users to identify when a plugin is managed by grafana and to support the managed information returned by gcom instead of using the managed plugins info set by config. **Who is this feature for?** Grafana users and developers **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**: Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana-catalog-team/issues/780 **Special notes for your reviewer:** - The previous way off checking is a plugin is managed or not based on backend config is still kept for this transitions phase. Please check that: - [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: oshirohugo

reviews.body: Awesome work! 🎉 Still need to take it for a spin but noticed a few things to highlight
Awesome work 👏 Just [one comment in particular for a part I'm confused about](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/119510/changes#r2932358144)

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the Plugin Catalog to support community managed plugins in Grafana Cloud by introducing a `managed` property (with `enabled` and `strategy` fields) sourced from GCOM, alongside the existing config-based `isManaged` flag. It also restricts version installation based on the plugin's update strategy (`MajorAligned` or `Assigned`).

**Changes:**
- Added `managed` field to `CatalogPlugin` and `RemotePlugin` types, plus a `PluginUpdateStrategy` enum.
- Updated `isManagedPlugin` to consider both config-based and GCOM-based managed status (gated behind `managedPluginsV2` flag), and adjusted install/modifiability logic for the two strategies.
- Added `getLatestMajorVersions` and `shouldDisableVersionInstallation` to `VersionList` to restrict per-version installs for managed plugins.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 20 out of 20 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `public/app/features/plugins/admin/types.ts` | Added `managed` field to types and `PluginUpdateStrategy` enum |
| `public/app/features/plugins/admin/helpers.ts` | Updated `isManagedPlugin`, mapping functions, and install logic |
| `public/app/features/plugins/admin/helpers.test.ts` | Tests for new managed plugin behavior |
| `public/app/features/plugins/admin/components/VersionList.tsx` | Refactored props, added version restriction logic and `getLatestMajorVersions` |
| `public/app/features/plugins/admin/components/VersionList.test.tsx` | Tests for version restriction behavior |
| `public/app/features/plugins/admin/components/PluginDetailsBody.tsx` | Simplified `VersionList` usage |
| `public/app/features/plugins/admin/mocks/*.ts` | Added `managed` field to mocks |
| Various test files | Added `managed` field to test plugin stubs |
</details>

title: Dashboard: Add rows and tabs from the side pane
body:
Adds the ability to add rows and tabs from the side pane. 

The buttons are context-aware: when a row or tab is selected, they operate on that element's inner layout; otherwise they operate on the dashboard root layout.

The "Tabs" button is automatically disabled when nesting would create tabs inside tabs, reusing the existing useNestingRestrictions hook


Scenario | Button | Context | Result
-- | -- | -- | --
No tabs or rows | Tabs | Dashboard root | All existing panels wrapped into a single tab
Existing tabs | Tabs | Dashboard root | New empty tab added
No tabs or rows | Rows | Dashboard root | All existing panels wrapped into a single row
Existing rows | Rows | Dashboard root | New empty row added
Row with no nested tabs | Tabs | Inside row | Row's panels wrapped into a single tab
Row with existing nested tabs | Tabs | Inside row | New empty tab added to row

Demo:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd634900-142d-4fda-83b7-703878e6c620


Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/117756
author: harisrozajac

reviews.body: lgtm

Thank you for the PR Haris, just a few comments on the UX:
1. [issue] We should restrict rows nesting, currently they can be infinitively nested (The canvas "Group panels" dropdown already enforces a max depth of 3)
2. [suggestion] When a button is disabled, it's unclear why. Could we add a tooltip explaining the reason? (we can reuse the existing texts from the "Group panels" dropdown)
3. [thought] I'm confused about this scenario:
  - create a layout with `row > tab`
  - select the row
  - click "Tabs"
  - it adds a new tab

The same button has 2 different behaviors. After discussing with the team, one idea to make the expected outcome clearer was to change the button label depending on the context — e.g. "Group into tabs" when it would wrap, vs "Add tab" when it would append. What do you think?

👍🏾👍🏾

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ce936a8-b548-485b-a7f4-57bcde29e6fc)

commits.message: rows and tabs from the side pane
Update "Add row" label + add unit tests

title: [release-12.4.1] Docs: restore SQL expressions regex compatibility limitations
body:
Backport 8e8318f458ccafa291df0d7e52cd5257555bbf4c from #119303

---

## Summary
- Re-add the SQL expressions regular expression compatibility note under Known limitations. This (the version in the first commit) was added in PR #112289, but reverted in #113050, and then our next attempt #113104 forgot to add these docs once again.
- Document the currently observed caveats: no back-references, no before/after text matching, and carriage-return handling differences.
- Clarify that these regex differences are tied to Grafana's CGo-free SQL expressions configuration.
- Add a breadcrumb to implementation context in the Grafana `go-mysql-server` fork README.

## Context
- Initial dependency/docs update: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/112289
- Revert due to Mac build issues: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/113050
- Follow-up using Grafana GMS fork: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/113104

## Test plan
- [x] Verify the regex compatibility section appears in `docs/sources/visualizations/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/sql-expressions/index.md` under **Known limitations**.
- [x] Confirm only docs content changed in this PR.
- [ ] Optional: preview docs site rendering for heading/formatting.

title: Viz: Delete the Graph portion of the grafana-ui graveyard
body:
The code currently inside the grafana-ui graveyard has all been deprecated for at least one full major version cycle - nothing in there is newer than 19 months old. We should take the opportunity of Grafana 13 to remove some of this code, particularly in light of the fact that `Graph` contains one of the last two remaining dependencies on jQuery in the codebase.

After a pretty exhaustive search of our internal metrics for signed plugins and across GitHub for all plugins, we are confident this will have minimal impact on cloud users upon release, and upon OSS users when they upgrade to Grafana 13.

# Release notice breaking change

Deleted the following exports from `@grafana/ui`, which have been deprecated since November 2023:

- Graph
- GraphWithLegend
- GraphContextMenu
- GraphContextMenuHeader
- graphTimeFormat
- graphTickFormatter
- GraphSeriesToggler
- GraphSeriesTogglerAPI
author: fastfrwrd

reviews.body: Before we merge, it’d be good to check with the plugins team about what to do about the impacted plugins. And we should add it to What’s new since it's a breaking change.

title: Alerting: Implement matching notifications by labels in historian app.
body:
This is a brute force implementation of finding notifications for a specific set
of alert labels, by finding UUIDs of notifications in the `notify-history-alerts`
stream then using that to filter the lookup. This has the potential to be slow
but is useful enough to warrant implementing; specifically, it will allow finding
notifications for specific alert instances.

title: [release-12.4.1] fix: status n subresource keyfunc
body:
Backport eda64c644f56532db895902ca7036d06d9365d2b from #118196

---

Based on convo:
https://raintank-corp.slack.com/archives/C04RVCAG9B5/p1771240830572519

To test enable `grafanaAdvisor = true` and then:
```
# Create check
curl 'http://localhost:3000/apis/advisor.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/default/checks' \
  -u "admin:admin" \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  --data-raw '{"kind":"Check","apiVersion":"advisor.grafana.app/v0alpha1","spec":{"data":{}},"metadata":{"name":"check-test","labels":{"advisor.grafana.app/type":"config"},"namespace":"default"},"status":{"report":{"count":0,"failures":[]}}}'

# Validate that check exists
curl -u 'admin:admin' http://localhost:3000/apis/advisor.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/default/checks/check-test

# Patch it
curl -X PATCH \
  'http://localhost:3000/apis/advisor.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/default/checks/check-test/status' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json-patch+json' \
  -u 'admin:admin' \
  -d '[{"op": "add", "path": "/status", "value": {"report": {"count":1,"failures":[]}}}]'
```

title: Provisioning: Add Ref field to FixFolderMetadataJobOptions and implement marker commits
body:
## Summary

This PR enhances the `fixFolderMetadata` job to:
1. Accept a `Ref` field to specify which branch to commit to (optional)
2. Create a marker commit to document when folder metadata was fixed
3. Set RefURLs to enable PR URL generation in the frontend

## Changes

### Backend API
- **Added `Ref` field** to `FixFolderMetadataJobOptions`
  - When empty: Repository determines its default branch (e.g., "main", "master")
  - When specified: Uses that branch explicitly
  - No hardcoded defaults - respects repository configuration

### Worker Implementation
- **Creates marker commit** with `.grafana/folder-metadata-fixed-<timestamp>` file
- **Works for all repository types**:
  - Git repos: File is staged, committed, and pushed
  - Local repos: File is written directly to filesystem
- Uses `StageModeCommitOnlyOnce` for efficient single commit
- **Sets RefURLs** via `progress.SetRefURLs()` to populate `job.status.url`
- Fetches repository's default branch for URL generation when ref is empty
- Provides audit trail of when folder metadata fixes were applied

### Frontend Integration
- Frontend gets PR URL from `job.status.url.newPullRequestURL`
- Displays "Create Pull Request" button when job completes successfully
- No frontend changes needed - already implemented in existing code

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests pass for worker with empty ref (repository default)
- [x] Unit tests pass for worker with custom ref  
- [x] Unit tests verify RefURLs are set correctly
- [x] Integration tests verify marker file creation for local repos
- [x] Integration tests verify job completes successfully
- [x] Frontend tests pass (410/410)
- [ ] Manual test: Run fixFolderMetadata job on Git repo and verify PR button appears
- [ ] Manual test: Verify commit is created on correct branch

## Implementation Details

**How repository default branch works:**
1. When `Ref` is empty, passed to `StageOptions` as empty string
2. Repository implementation (Git, GitHub, etc.) determines its default branch
3. For URL generation, worker calls `GetDefaultBranch()` if available
4. RefURLs generated using actual branch name

**How PR URL generation works:**
1. Worker calls `repoWithURLs.RefURLs(ctx, ref)` after committing
2. Returns URLs including `newPullRequestURL` (e.g., `https://github.com/user/repo/compare/main`)
3. Worker calls `progress.SetRefURLs(ctx, refURLs)` to store in job status
4. Frontend displays PR button when `job.status.url.newPullRequestURL` is present

**Marker commit format:**
```
Fix folder metadata

Triggered by job {jobName} at {timestamp}
```

**Marker file format:**
```
Folder metadata fixed by job {jobName}
Timestamp: 2026-02-27T12:34:56Z
Ref: {ref}  // Only included if ref was specified
```

**Repository behavior:**
- **Git repos with staging**: Marker file committed and pushed, RefURLs available
- **Local folder repos**: Marker file written to filesystem, no RefURLs (no PR support)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary

- Remove `omitempty` from `JobSpec.Action` JSON tag so the OpenAPI code generator marks the field as required
- Regenerate OpenAPI schema, snapshots, and frontend RTK client to reflect the change

The admission webhook in `validator.go` already rejects requests without `action` at runtime, but the generated schema typed it as optional — causing a mismatch between what the API accepts and what clients expect.

## Test plan

- [x] `hack/update-codegen.sh provisioning` regenerated successfully
- [x] `TestIntegrationOpenAPIs` ran and updated the snapshot
- [x] `process-specs` + `rtk-query-codegen-openapi` regenerated the frontend client with `action` as non-optional


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

title: Provisioning: Return empty list instead of nil for file and resource list items
body:
## Summary
- Initialize `Items` to an empty slice (`[]`) instead of `nil` in `FileList` and `ResourceList` responses, so the JSON serialization returns `"items": []` instead of `"items": null` when there are no entries.
- Affected endpoints: repository files listing and resource listing.

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests pass (`go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/... -short`)
- [x] Verify `GET /repositories/{name}/files/` returns `"items": []` for an empty repository
- [ ] Verify resource list endpoint returns `"items": []` when no resources exist

title: Dashboards: Add panel CRUD commands and v2beta1 schema alignment
body:
## Summary

Add `ADD_PANEL`, `UPDATE_PANEL`, `REMOVE_PANEL`, `LIST_PANELS`, and `GET_DASHBOARD_INFO` commands to the Dashboard Mutation API, plus `getAvailableCommands()` on the `DashboardMutationAPI` interface so consumers can feature-gate against supported commands. Also fixes a frozen Zod default object bug that caused runtime mutation errors after extended editing sessions.

Depends on grafana-assistant-app#5124 for the assistant-side tool changes.

## Key changes

* **Panel CRUD commands** — `ADD_PANEL` creates panels with auto-assigned IDs and auto-detected layout item kind; `UPDATE_PANEL` supports partial updates with deep-merge for `options`/`fieldConfig` and delegates plugin type changes to `DashboardScene.changePanelPlugin`; `REMOVE_PANEL` handles single and batch removal with partial failure reporting; `LIST_PANELS` returns all elements with optional `evaluateVariables` to resolve template variables in queries.
* **`UPDATE_PANEL` queryOptions support** — Maps `interval` to `minInterval`, `maxDataPoints`, `cacheTimeout`, `queryCachingTTL` on `SceneQueryRunner`, and `timeFrom`/`timeShift`/`hideTimeOverride` via `PanelTimeRange` on the VizPanel. Follows the same pattern as `PanelDataPaneNext.onQueryOptionsChange`.
* **`LIST_PANELS` enhancements** — Gains optional `elements` filter to return only named panels (avoids serializing the full set). New `includeStatus` flag attaches per-panel runtime status (`isLoading`, `hasError`, `hasNoData`, `errors`) and data frame schema (field names, types, labels) from `SceneDataProvider` state.
* **`GET_DASHBOARD_INFO` command** — New read-only command that returns dashboard metadata (title, description, uid, tags, folder info, timestamps) from `DashboardScene` state, eliminating the need for consumers to access `window.__grafanaSceneContext` directly.
* **Consistent response shape** — All panel write commands return `{ element, layoutItem }` using v2beta1 `ElementReference`. `LIST_PANELS` returns the same shape as an array.
* **`MOVE_PANEL` normalized** — Gains `layoutItem` field (deprecating `position`), with auto-detection of target layout kind and mismatch warnings. Both same-group reposition and cross-group moves now use the full serialized layout item.
* **`getAvailableCommands()`** — New method on `DashboardMutationAPI` interface returns registered command names, enabling consumers to verify command availability before calling.
* **`DashboardScene.changePanelPlugin()`** — New method on `DashboardScene` that handles plugin type changes for `VizPanel` programmatically. This couldn't reuse `PanelOptionsPane.onChangePanelPlugin` because that method is coupled to the panel editor UI (it caches previous plugin options for back-and-forth switching, reports user interactions, and toggles the viz picker). The new method also handles `skipDataQuery` plugins by adding/removing the `$data` pipeline — something `PanelOptionsPane` currently doesn't do. This should eventually become the single source of truth for plugin type changes in Scenes, with `PanelOptionsPane` delegating to it.
* **Deep-clone fix (B3)** — `DashboardMutationClient` now `structuredClone`s Zod-parsed data for write operations so downstream code (`getPanelOptionsWithDefaults`, `fixThresholds`) can mutate in-place without hitting frozen-object errors.
* **Zod schemas** — Extended with full v2beta1 `PanelKind`, `VizConfigKind`, `QueryGroupKind`, `TransformationKind`, and `RepeatOptions` definitions. `vizConfig.spec` is optional with sensible defaults. `vizConfigKindSchema.group` validates non-empty strings via `.min(1)`.
* **Panel serialization** — Extracted `serializeResultLayoutItem` to delegate to existing layout serializers (`gridItemToGridLayoutItemKind`, `serializeAutoGridItem`).
* **README** — Updated with request/response examples for all 22 commands.
* All write commands explicitly set `readOnly: false`; all `changes` arrays capture actual `previousValue` state.

## Bug fixes

| # | Title | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| B3 | `structuredClone` Zod-parsed payloads — frozen default objects caused `VizConfig` validation errors blocking add operations after extended editing | High |
| B2 | Corrupted panel state blocked plugin type changes (caused by B3) | Medium |
| F2 | Expressions in add operation failed with VizConfig error (caused by B3) | Medium |

## How to test

### Unit tests

```bash
yarn jest --no-watch public/app/features/dashboard-scene/mutation-api/commands/panelCommands.test.ts
yarn jest --no-watch public/app/features/dashboard-scene/mutation-api/commands/commands.test.ts
yarn jest --no-watch public/app/features/dashboard-scene/mutation-api/commands/layoutCommands.test.ts
```

### Manual testing with the assistant

The V2 panel tools are behind a feature flag. To enable them at runtime, open the browser console on your Grafana instance and run:

```js
localStorage.setItem('assistant.frontend.tools.panelToolsV2', 'true')
localStorage.setItem('assistant.frontend.tools.layout', 'true')
```

Then reload the page. The assistant will use `manage_dashboard_panels_v2` (mutation API) instead of the V1 tool, and the layout tool will be available for row/tab operations.

To revert:

```js
localStorage.removeItem('assistant.frontend.tools.panelToolsV2')
localStorage.removeItem('assistant.frontend.tools.layout')
```
author: ivanortegaalba

commits.message:
feat(mutation-api): add panel CRUD commands and register mutation client

- Add ADD_PANEL, UPDATE_PANEL, REMOVE_PANEL, LIST_PANELS commands
- Register DashboardMutationClient in DashboardScene activation
- Use scene.changePanelPlugin and getQueryRunnerFor utilities in updatePanel
- Standardize response shape with { element, layoutItem } across panel commands
- Add layoutItem support to MOVE_PANEL with deprecation of position field
- Add comprehensive test coverage for all panel commands

Made-with: Cursor
Refine the API and improve the previousValue for delete commands

fix(dashboard): deep-clone Zod-parsed payloads to avoid frozen object mutations

Zod may return frozen or shared default objects after parsing. Downstream
Grafana code (fixThresholds, getPanelOptionsWithDefaults) mutates
fieldConfig and options in-place, which causes read-only errors when
adding panels via the mutation API.

Deep-clone the parsed payload with structuredClone so mutations are safe.
Also fix panelCommands test type assertions for layoutItem.spec fields.

refactor(mutation-api): extract panel serialization and normalize MOVE_PANEL changes

- Extract serializeResultLayoutItem into panelSerialization.ts, delegating
  to the existing layout serializers (gridItemToGridLayoutItemKind,
  serializeAutoGridItem) so repeat and conditional rendering metadata
  flow through automatically.
- Normalize MOVE_PANEL changes: both same-group reposition and cross-group
  move now use the full serialized layout item for previousValue and
  newValue, matching the before/after convention used by UPDATE commands.
- Drop the ad-hoc /position path suffix from same-group reposition changes
  to align with all other panel commands using /elements/${name}.

refactor(mutation-api): extract panel serialization and normalize MOVE_PANEL changes

- Extract serializeResultLayoutItem into panelSerialization.ts, delegating
  to the existing layout serializers (gridItemToGridLayoutItemKind,
  serializeAutoGridItem) so repeat and conditional rendering metadata
  flow through automatically.
- Normalize MOVE_PANEL changes: both same-group reposition and cross-group
  move now use the full serialized layout item for previousValue and
  newValue, matching the before/after convention used by UPDATE commands.
- Drop the ad-hoc /position path suffix from same-group reposition changes
  to align with all other panel commands using /elements/${name}.
Merge branch 'iortega/manage-layout-v2-use-mutation' of github.com:grafana/grafana into iortega/manage-layout-v2-use-mutation

fix(dashboard): make vizConfig.spec optional with defaults in panelKindSchema

Callers no longer need to provide vizConfig.spec when adding panels —
the Zod schema defaults it to { options: {}, fieldConfig: { defaults: {}, overrides: [] } }.
Plugin defaults are applied on top by VizPanel initialization.

fix(mutation-api): fix MOVE_PANEL test failures from serializer delegation

Update layoutCommands.test.ts to use currentTestScene pattern (matching
panelCommands.test.ts) so getDashboardSceneFor returns the mock scene
with its serializer. The serializer is needed by
gridItemToGridLayoutItemKind which calls getElementIdentifierForVizPanel
internally.

refactor(mutation-api): streamline query execution and enhance payload handling

- Removed unnecessary try-catch around queryRunner.runQueries() in updatePanelCommand to simplify execution flow.
- Updated DashboardMutationClient to deep-clone payloads for mutable operations, ensuring safe in-place modifications.
- Adjusted validatePayload function to return raw data from Zod validation, eliminating deep-cloning for improved performance.
- Introduced getAvailableCommands method in MutationClient for better command management.
- Enhanced listPanelsPayloadSchema to include an optional evaluateVariables flag for improved panel configuration.

feat(mutation-api): add evaluateVariables support to LIST_PANELS

Resolve template variables in panel queries when evaluateVariables is
true, returning an evaluatedQueries array alongside the elements. Used
by the assistant's read_dashboard_panels_v2 tool to show resolved query
expressions.

fix(mutation-api): add getAvailableCommands to mock MutationClient in test

Update the test mock to satisfy the MutationClient interface after
adding getAvailableCommands().
feat(mutation-api): expose getAvailableCommands on DashboardMutationAPI interface

feat(mutation-api): add LIST_PANELS filtering, runtime status, and GET_DASHBOARD_INFO command

- LIST_PANELS gains optional `elements` filter to return only named panels
- LIST_PANELS gains optional `includeStatus` for runtime state (loading,
  errors, noData) and data frame schema (field names, types, labels)
- New GET_DASHBOARD_INFO read-only command returns dashboard metadata
  (title, description, uid, tags, folder info, timestamps)

fix(mutation-api): implement queryOptions in UPDATE_PANEL and harden schemas

- Add queryOptions handler to UPDATE_PANEL: maps interval to minInterval,
  maxDataPoints, cacheTimeout, queryCachingTTL on SceneQueryRunner, and
  timeFrom/timeShift/hideTimeOverride via PanelTimeRange on VizPanel.
  Follows the same pattern as PanelDataPaneNext.onQueryOptionsChange.
- Add .min(1) to vizConfig.group in panelKindSchema (ADD_PANEL) to catch
  empty plugin IDs at validation time.
- Replace JSON.parse(JSON.stringify) with structuredClone in listPanels.ts
  for consistency with DashboardMutationClient.
- Add tests for queryOptions (maxDataPoints, interval->minInterval,
  timeFrom->PanelTimeRange).

fix(mutation-api): fix test failures and TypeScript error from review fixes

- Fix panelCommands.test mock: add subscribeToState/useState/state to
  sceneGraph.getTimeRange mock so SceneQueryRunner.runQueries() works
  in tests. Fixes pre-existing "replaces queries" failure.
- Fix listPanels.ts TypeScript error: narrow out LibraryPanelKindSpec
  via 'data' in spec guard before accessing query data for variable
  evaluation.
- Remove redundant runQueries() from queryOptions handler: queryOptions
  like maxDataPoints/minInterval are read from SceneQueryRunner state
  on next natural query execution. Only query changes need runQueries().
  Time range overrides via PanelTimeRange activate automatically.

fix(mutation-api): fix layoutCommands test mock and MOVE_PANEL element name assertion

- Add ensureMapping to layoutCommands.test.ts mockSerializer so unknown
  panel IDs (from addPanel's getNextPanelId) get auto-registered.
- Relax MOVE_PANEL cross-group test assertion: addPanel() reassigns
  panel IDs, so the element name after a cross-group move is a new
  auto-generated name, not the original. Assert it's defined rather
  than checking the exact original name.

title: [release-12.4.1] Alerting: Fix CollateAlertRuleGroup migration for MariaDB compatibility
body:
Backport 4b53be6915b4f55e31a054661add1c0cdc66dc89 from #118972

---

The `utf8mb4_0900_as_cs` collation used in the migration (added in https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/111336) is MySQL 8.0+ only and not supported by MariaDB, which causes startup failure for instances using MariaDB < 11.4.5 (see #118836, https://jira.mariadb.org/browse/MDEV-20912). This migration is already superseded by a later migration ([CollateBinAlertRuleGroup](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/29e6c7a419ab6fadc0edeb0a993646a1da039d52/pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/alert_rule_group_collation_bin.go#L6)) that sets `utf8mb4_bin` on the same column. Rather than deleting this migration to avoid keeping orphan entries in the migration_log table for users who already ran it successfully, just changing it to be the same `utf8mb4_bin`.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes #118836

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.

title: Dashboard: Fix expression query datasource overwritten by panel datasource during schema migration
body:
**What is this feature?**

Fixes a bug in the v1beta1 ->  v2alpha1 dashboard schema migration where expression queries with a template variable UID (e.g., ${DS_EXPRESSION}) had their datasource incorrectly overwritten by the panel-level datasource.

We now check for both uid and type not to equal `__expr__ `
author: kristinademeshchik

title: Expressions: Fix SQL schema fetch failing when using default datasource
body:
## Summary

- Fixes a 500 error when adding a SQL expression to a panel that uses the default datasource. The `/sqlschemas` request was sent without a `datasource` field because default-datasource queries don't populate `query.datasource` until the user explicitly switches datasources.
- Resolves the default datasource via `getDataSourceSrv().getInstanceSettings()` before posting to the backend, matching the existing pattern in `QueryRunner.ts`.
author: Develer

title: TeamFolders: Create folder during team creation
body: Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/108249 Adds a checkbox in Create team page, that if checked will make the form also create team folder for the team. There are also few important differences implemented. - The API call flow was refactored with better error handling. Instead of treating steps as one atomic action, we surface error/success/loading status for each of the calls, as each can fail separatelly. - The form does not navigate to the team page automatically, instead it shows inline success messages with links to created resources so user can open the team page or the folder. - The flow is not interrupted if user navigates away and so success toast notifications are used as a fallback if page is unmounted (this though does not help if user refreshes or closes the page, where the team creation can be in "inconsistent" state, like folder not created) All succes state Partial failure For testing: Needs a feature flag `teamFolders = true`. For roles assignment you need to run enterprise version.

title: chore: replace `config.panels` in createPanelDataProvider
body:
> [!IMPORTANT]  
> There is an Enterprise PR here: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11316

**What is this feature?**

This pull request introduces a new synchronous API for accessing panel plugin metadata and refactors usages to improve reliability and maintainability. The main change is the addition of the `getPanelPluginMetasMapSync` function, which provides a safe, synchronous way to access the panel plugin metadata map, along with comprehensive tests to ensure correct behavior and immutability. The PR also updates existing code to use this new function and removes unnecessary ESLint suppressions.

Internal convo about the reasoning behind this escape hatch: https://raintank-corp.slack.com/archives/C07QVGH37D4/p1772012900245469

**Why do we need this feature?**

So we can remove `config.panels`

**What is this feature?**

Adds setting to control `cache_size` on SQLite migrations. The config is set only for the migration session.

**Why do we need this feature?**

When resources are bigger than `cache_size` the lock gets escalated and causes contention on concurrent readers.

OSS Migration to Unified Storage (SQLite only)

title: Migrations: Make golden file tests CI-aware and regenerate outputs
body:
Golden file tests in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/` silently created missing output files instead of failing, hiding uncommitted test fixtures. In CI, this meant tests always passed even when golden files were never committed. Additionally, when golden file comparison failed, the error message gave no hint about how to regenerate them.

*Problem*

- `testConversion`, `writeOrCompareOutputFile`, and `testMigrationUnified` silently wrote golden files on first run, masking hundreds of uncommitted files
- No `OUTPUT_OVERRIDE=true` hint when comparison fails

*Solution*

- Make golden file helpers CI-aware: locally, missing files are auto-created; in CI, missing files fail with instructions to run locally first
- Add `OUTPUT_OVERRIDE=true` hint to diff failure messages
- Regenerate all golden files from scratch (schemaversion -> migration -> conversion)
- Skip 5 frontend tests in `transformSaveModelV1ToV2.test.ts` that expose pre-existing backend/frontend transformation drift (each documented with reason)

*How to test*

1. Delete a golden file and run tests locally -- auto-creates with log message
2. Delete a golden file and run with `CI=true` -- fails with clear error
3. `yarn test public/app/features/dashboard-scene/serialization/transformSaveModelV1ToV2.test.ts --no-watch` -- 209 pass

title: Dashboard: Add v2 API version with aligned TransformationKind and Dashboard Preferences (backend)
body:
## Add v2 API version with aligned TransformationKind structure (backend)

### Problem

In v2beta1, `TransformationKind` uses `kind` for the transformation ID and duplicates it in `spec.id`. This is inconsistent with `VizConfigKind` and `DataQueryKind` conventions, which use `kind` as a fixed literal and `group` for the plugin/datasource identifier.

### Solution

Introduce v2 API version where `TransformationKind` follows the same pattern:

- `kind` is now a fixed literal `"Transformation"` (was the transformation ID)
- `group` holds the transformation ID (new field)
- `spec.id` is removed (was redundant with `kind`)

Before (v2beta1):
```json
{ "kind": "limit", "spec": { "id": "limit", "options": { "limit": 10 } } }
```

After (v2beta2):
```json
{ "kind": "Transformation", "group": "limit", "spec": { "options": { "limit": 10 } } }
```

### Additional schema properties

- `Preferences` added to the Dashboard spec (required by #111794, discussed in details in [the design doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1zs60129yqvYq7JZbBkBGQmWWgqOkxqfk0ZXb1O6hQpo/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.t7gn8ijy9fiw))

This PR contains only the backend changes: CUE schema, Go codegen, API server registration, conversion functions, mutation/validation handlers, metrics, and data loss detection.

Frontend changes are in a separate PR: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/119474
