### After

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1918cc7e-174d-4179-9604-8765d8fa7347
author: fastfrwrd

reviews.body: Functional review: _noice_ I can't find a single thing wrong with this fix. Either I'm losing my touch or you nailed it :P Taking a look at the code now. ### Observations outside of the scope of this PR: #### Stacking tooltips We shouldn't be able to open more then one tooltip at once: Although clicking anywhere else besides the filter icon does cause them all to despawn, so this probably belongs at the bottom of the priority barrel. #### Potential future enhancement - apply filter to all It'd be cool to add filters within nested tables that apply to other nested tables in the same level of the hierarchy, but the current functionality on this PR is not unexpected as the default behavior. (i.e. add an "apply to all" button to the filter tooltip) #### Random request: I would love a way to clear all the filters in the table without having to click into each column icon again.

Great work, great coverage.

I remember seeing an issue recently where someone was reporting that they were seeing filters that were not applicable in one column after adding more in another (in a non-nested table), I'll try to track that down as it looks like we can close that out with this fix.

commits.message: Table: Restore cross-filtering and make it work with nested tables
change __tail property to a dedicated array we pass around
combine applyFilter and computeCrossFilterRows to avoid duplicate passes over data for filtering
avoid a duplicate applyFilter run for nested tables
fix issue preventing nested tables from filtering, update tests

title: DynamicDashboards: Enable variables, annotations and links duplication
body: ### Goal and approach This PR resolves https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/120079 by enabling the duplication of variables, annotations, and links in the dashboard edit pane. The naming after duplication is as follows: - Variable names are suffixed with `_copyx` where x is the current number of variables in the parent set - Annotation names are suffixed with `- Copy` - Link titles are suffixed with `- Copy` ### Approach Extending the existing `EditableDashboardElement.onDuplicate` pattern already used by [panels](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/public/app/features/dashboard-scene/edit-pane/VizPanelEditableElement.tsx#L120-L124), rows and tabs. ### Key changes 1. `VariableEditableElement` — Added `onDuplicate` with the same clone pattern, plus an early-return guard refactor on `onDelete`. Tracks the new duplicate action via the refactored telemetry helper (see 4 below). 2. `AnnotationEditableElement` — Similar as above. 3. `LinkEditEditableElement` — Added `onDuplicate` that clones the link and opens the edit pane for it. 4. `interactions.ts` — Replaced `deleteVariableButtonClicked` with `variableActionButtonClicked(action, props)` to support both 'duplicate' and 'delete' actions through a single telemetry function. The reported event name stays backward-compatible. 5. Integration tests — New test files for `VariableEditableElement` and `AnnotationEditableElement` covering both `onDuplicate` and `onDelete`. Extended existing `LinkAddEditableElement` tests with a `onDuplicate` case. ### 🧪 Manual testing - Open a dashboard in edit mode - Select a variable, annotation, or link in the edit pane outline - Click on the action dropdown button in the edit pane header and click "Duplicate" - Verify a clone appears at the end of the list - Verify the original is unchanged - Verify undo/redo for variables, annotations, or links - For variables, check the browser network tab to confirm a `duplicate_variable_button_clicked interaction` is reported
author: grafakus

reviews.body: A duplicated variable needs to have a unique name to be usable in the dashboard.
LGTM!
I have not re-reviewed but removing my changes requested 😛 so it does not block the PR

commits.message: DynamicDashboards: Enable variables, annotations and links duplication

title: Frontend: Negotiate folder app API v1 vs v1beta1 at runtime
body:
Folder app RTK calls now use runtime version negotiation (v1 vs v1beta1) via GET /apis/folder.grafana.app/, aligned with dashboard API discovery.

**Changes**
- **createBaseQuery** supports optional async getBaseURL() (fixed baseURL unchanged for other clients).
- **folderApiVersionResolver** picks preferred v1/v1beta1, else v1 if served, else v1beta1; caches on success, retries discovery after failure.
- Folder baseAPI uses **getFolderAPIBaseURL**(); API_VERSION / BASE_URL stay v1beta1 for codegen/tooling.
- **setupTests** sets folderAPIVersionResolver to v1beta1 so existing MSW v1beta1 handlers keep working.

title: IAM: Add users filtering and improved RBAC mapper for users API
body:
## Summary

This PR improves the IAM Users API with authorization checks, hidden user filtering, and better error handling.

### Key changes

- **Hidden users filtering (`store_wrapper.go`)**: New `StoreWrapper` implementing `ResourceStorageAuthorizer` that filters hidden users on all CRUD operations. Uses `configprovider.ConfigProvider` for dynamic config. Hidden users are invisible (404 on Get, filtered from List) and protected from Create/Update/Delete.

- **`BeforeUpdate` interface change (`wrapper.go`)**: Extended `ResourceStorageAuthorizer.BeforeUpdate` signature to `(ctx, oldObj, obj)` so authorizers can inspect the old object. This prevents bypassing hidden-user checks by renaming the login field. All implementations updated.

- **`WithPreserveIdentity` option (`wrapper.go`)**: New functional option that preserves the caller's identity context instead of replacing it with the service identity. Used by the user store wrapper so validation callbacks run with the original requester context.

- **Profile field update restriction (`validate.go`)**: Added `onlyAllowedFieldsChanged` check using reflection — non-service, non-Grafana-admin users (e.g. OrgAdmins) can only change the `Role` field. Service identities and Grafana admins can update all profile fields.

- **DB conflict error mapping (`legacy/user.go`)**: `toUserConflictError` converts UNIQUE constraint violations on `user.email`/`user.login` into proper 409 Conflict API errors as defense-in-depth.

- **RBAC mapper improvements (`mapper.go`)**: Explicit verb-to-action mapping for IAM users using `org.users:*` actions instead of default `users:*`.

- **Single-org gating (`register.go`)**: Users API is only enabled when `cfg.RBAC.SingleOrganization` is true, checked via `configprovider.ConfigProvider`.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `StoreWrapper` (hidden user filtering)
- [x] Unit tests for `onlyAllowedFieldsChanged` and updated `ValidateOnUpdate` 
- [x] Unit tests for `Wrapper` with `WithPreserveIdentity` option
- [x] Updated `BeforeUpdate(ctx, oldObj, obj)` signature in all authorizer test files
- [x] Integration tests for hidden user filtering, field selectors, and user CRUD (`TestIntegrationUsers`)
- [x] All tests pass with `-tags=pro`

Relates to https://github.com/grafana/identity-access-team/issues/1879
author: mgyongyosi

reviews.body:
Great work! 🎉 

Left a couple of comments that I think should be accounted for then 🚢

commits.message:
IAM: Add hidden users filtering and improved RBAC mapper for users API

- Add StoreWrapper for user resource that filters hidden users on Get/List
- Wire up StoreWrapper in the users API group registration
- Expand RBAC verb mapping for users to use explicit action translations
- Add integration tests for hidden users filtering behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

IAM: Fix duplicate user validation and storewrapper context propagation

The storewrapper replaced the request context with a service identity
(OrgID=0) before invoking createValidation/updateValidation callbacks.
Since these callbacks wrap k8s admission webhooks (including the
duplicate email/login checks), the validation ran with OrgID=0 causing
SearchOrgUsers to return no results, silently passing duplicates through
to the DB which then returned a 500 instead of 409.

Fix 1 (storewrapper): Add validationWithUserContext and
updateValidationWithUserContext helpers that rebind validation callbacks
to the original user context before passing them to the inner store.

Fix 2 (legacy store): Add toUserConflictError as defense-in-depth that
converts SQLite UNIQUE constraint failures on user.email/user.login into
proper 409 Conflict API errors in CreateUser and UpdateUser.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enforce hidden-users restrictions on write operations

BeforeCreate, BeforeUpdate, and BeforeDelete in the user StoreWrapper
now return HTTP 403 when the target user's login is in the hidden-users
list, returning a generic "operation not permitted" message to callers
and logging the hidden-user detail server-side via a structured logger.

Integration tests are updated to create the user before marking it
hidden (so BeforeCreate does not block setup), then verify all four
guarded paths (get→404, list filtered, update→403, delete→403) and
add a dedicated sub-test that confirms create is blocked once a login
is in the hidden list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

IAM: Add WithPreserveIdentity option to storewrapper

Introduces a WithPreserveIdentity() functional option on storewrapper.New()
so the users storage path passes the original caller identity through to the
inner store instead of replacing it with a service identity. This ensures
admission validation (e.g. duplicate email/login checks) runs with the correct
OrgID. Adds unit tests for the new option.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Splash Screen: Add dismissal persistence with versioned resurfacing
body:
## Summary

- Adds `useShouldShowSplash` hook using `UserStorage` API for server-side persistence (with automatic localStorage fallback for anonymous users)
- Splash only shows when the stored `dismissedVersion` differs from `config.version`
- Bumping `version` in `splashContent.ts` automatically resurfaces the splash for all users

## New file

- `useShouldShowSplash.ts` - encapsulates storage read/write and versioned comparison

## Test plan

- [ ] Enable `splashScreen` feature flag, verify splash appears on first visit
- [ ] Dismiss the splash, refresh - verify it does not reappear
- [ ] Bump `version` in `splashContent.ts`, refresh - verify splash reappears

### Inspecting/clearing stored value

Run in the browser console while logged into Grafana to check or clean storage:

```javascript
// Check current value
fetch('/apis/userstorage.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/default/user-storage/grafana-splash-screen:' + grafanaBootData.user.uid)
  .then(r => r.json())
  .then(data => console.log(data.spec))
  .catch(e => console.log('No stored value yet', e));

// Delete stored value (to re-trigger splash)
fetch('/apis/userstorage.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/default/user-storage/grafana-splash-screen:' + grafanaBootData.user.uid, {
  method: 'DELETE',
}).then(r => console.log(r.status));
```
author: Develer

reviews.body: looks good!

commits.message: feat(splash-screen): add dismissal persistence with versioned resurfacing
feat(splash-screen): use useUserStorage hook for dismissal persistence

title: OpenFeature: Add client-side response cache for flag evaluations
body:
Flag evaluations via the OFREP provider hit the features service on every call. Under load (e.g. `kubernetesTeamSync` evaluated on every auth event) this causes 429 errors from the features service.                                         
                                                                                  
This adds a caching http.RoundTripper middleware that caches successful responses by URL+body hash (so responses don't leak between tenants) with a configurable TTL (default 1 min, set via `cache_ttl` in `[feature_toggles.openfeature]` section of config.ini).                       
Non-2xx responses are not cached.
author: undef1nd

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds client-side HTTP response caching for OpenFeature (OFREP) flag evaluations to reduce repeated calls to the features service under load and mitigate 429 rate-limiting.

**Changes:**
- Add `cache_ttl` setting (default 1m) to OpenFeature config and plumb it into HTTP client creation.
- Implement an `http.RoundTripper`-based middleware that caches successful (2xx) responses by URL + request-body hash.
- Add unit tests for the cache behavior; update Go module dependencies to include the cache/map + test libs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| pkg/setting/setting_openfeature.go | Introduces `CacheTTL` setting read from `feature_toggles.openfeature.cache_ttl`. |
| pkg/services/featuremgmt/openfeature.go | Passes configured `CacheTTL` into the features HTTP client options. |
| pkg/infra/features/client.go | Wires `CacheTTL` into HTTP client middleware construction. |
| pkg/infra/features/cache.go | New caching RoundTripper + middleware implementation. |
| pkg/infra/features/cache_test.go | Tests cache hit/miss/expiry and tenant separation behavior. |

Versions 3.4.8-3.4.10 contain patch-level dependency bumps to `@grafana/e2e-selectors`, keeping the E2E testing toolchain current. No breaking changes or API modifications were introduced.

Developers running E2E tests via Playwright in the Grafana repository.

- TypeScript typecheck passes.

title: Dashboards: Fix 500 on GET non-existent dashboard via v1beta1 API (backport #111571 to 12.2.x)
body:
## Summary

Backport of #111571 to 12.2.x for grafana/support-escalations#21192.

- **Bug**: `GET /apis/dashboard.grafana.app/v1beta1/namespaces/default/dashboards/<non-existent-uid>` returns **500 Internal Server Error** instead of **404 Not Found** on Grafana 12.2.x
- **Root cause**: The k8s API authorizer (`authorizeDashboard`) resolved RBAC scopes (`dashboards:uid:<uid>`) during the authorization phase. When the dashboard didn't exist, the scope resolver returned `"could not resolve dashboards:uid:<uid>: Dashboard not found"` — an untyped Go error that the k8s API server surfaced as 500.
- **Fix**: Skip per-resource scope resolution for `GET` operations at the authorizer level. Per-resource authorization (404 for not found, 403 for unauthorized) is already handled downstream in the unified storage layer's `read()` method (`pkg/storage/unified/resource/server.go`).
- The legacy API (`/api/dashboards/uid/<uid>`) correctly returns 404 — this bug is specific to the v1beta1 k8s-style API.
- This entire legacy authorizer was removed in 12.3+ via #111571 (replaced by `ServiceAuthorizer`), so **12.3+ is not affected**.

## Validated behavior after fix

| Scenario | Expected | Verified |
|----------|----------|----------|
| Non-existent dashboard (viewer) | 404 | Yes |
| Existing dashboard, no permission (viewer) | 403 | Handled by downstream `server.read()` |
| Existing dashboard (admin) | 200 | Yes |
| Unauthenticated | 401 | Yes |
| Legacy API parity (`/api/dashboards/uid/`) | Same codes | Yes |

## Test plan

Added in support ticket
author: konsalex

commits.message:
Dashboards: Fix 500 error for GET non-existent dashboard via v1beta1 API

Backport of #111571 to 12.2.x.

The k8s API authorizer for dashboards was resolving RBAC scopes
(dashboards:uid:<uid>) during the authorization phase. When the
dashboard didn't exist, the scope resolver returned an untyped error
that surfaced as a 500 Internal Server Error instead of 404.

Fix: skip per-resource scope resolution for GET operations at the
authorizer level. Per-resource authorization (404 for not found,
403 for unauthorized) is already handled downstream in the unified
storage layer's read() method.

This entire legacy authorizer was removed in 12.3+ via #111571,
replaced by the ServiceAuthorizer which defers all user-level
authorization to the downstream storage layer.

Fixes grafana/support-escalations#21192

body:

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

-->

**What is this feature?**

Implements `GetByLogin` and `GetByEmail` on `UserK8sService`, completing the lookup path when `kubernetesUsersRedirect` is enabled. Both methods use a field selector list against the k8s users API.

`GetByLogin` mirrors legacy behaviour: when the input contains `@` it tries to find the user by email first, then falls back to `spec.login`.

The user service routes `GetByLogin` and `GetByEmail` to the k8s service whenever the feature flag is on and a requester is present in the context. Callers without a requester continue to use the legacy store.

**Why do we need this feature?**

To migrate the user service to the new APIs.

**Who is this feature for?**

all users

title: Provisioning: Add folder rename support
body:
**What is this feature?**

Adds the ability to rename provisioned folders directly from the Grafana UI via a new rename drawer on the folder browse page. Also fixes two bugs in folder provisioning forms: the rename form hardcoded `canPushToConfiguredBranch={false}` (showing an editable branch field even when the repo only supports direct writes), and the delete form appended a trailing slash at the call site instead of normalizing the path once in the shared hook.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Provisioned folders could not be renamed through the UI — users had to manually edit files in their repository. 

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1029

title: PluginMeta: fallback to bootdata if api results are empty
body:
**What is this feature?**

When the PluginMeta API returns an empty result (e.g. due to a failed fetch or an API error), the panel and app plugin meta services now fall back to the bootdata from config.panels / config.apps instead of leaving the plugin meta state empty. A warning is logged both to the console and to Grafana's monitoring logger (Faro) when this fallback occurs, including the plugin type (panel or app) for context. 

Additionally:
  - Introduces a shared logging.ts module for PluginMeta warning reporting
  - Consolidates panel/app state mutations behind `setPanelsAndAliases()` and `setApps()` helpers to reduce duplication                                                                                                
  - Fixes the `angularMapper` to return { detected: false } instead of an empty object 
  - Updates test fixtures to use keywords: [] instead of keywords: null for consistency with the mapper output

**Why do we need this feature?**

If the PluginMeta API call fails or returns no items, Grafana currently ends up with an empty plugin registry, meaning no panels or apps are available to the user. This is a silent, hard-to-diagnose failure that degrades the entire UI. By falling back to bootdata (which is always available from the initial page load), Grafana can continue functioning with the plugin metadata it already has, while the warning log and Faro telemetry ensure the team is alerted to investigate the root cause.

This also makes us more confident to securely rollout `useMTPlugins`.

**Who is this feature for?**

Grafana maintainers

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

title: Zanzana: Fix inverted folder parent tuples in reconciler
body:
## Summary

Fix swapped arguments to `NewFolderParentTuple` in the reconciler's `TranslateFolderToTuples`, which inverted the entire folder hierarchy in Zanzana's tuple store
- The reconciler passed `(parent, child)` but the function expects `(child, parent)`, causing children to not inherit permissions from parents
- This is the root cause of widespread shadow check mismatches (`expected=true, actual=false`) for folder and dashboard access checks that rely on folder inheritance

After deploying, the next reconciler run will automatically correct all folder parent tuples via its diff+sync mechanism. No manual migration needed.

## Test plan

- [x] Fixed existing `TestTranslateFolderToTuples` assertions to match correct tuple direction
- [x] Added regression test comparing reconciler output to mutation path convention (`NewFolderParentTuple(child, parent)`)
- [x] All reconciler tests pass (`go test ./pkg/services/authz/zanzana/server/reconciler/`)
- [x] Schema validation tests pass (`TestTranslatedTuplesAreSchemaValid`)
- [x] Shadow client tests pass (`go test ./pkg/services/authz/zanzana/client/`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: mihai-turdean

commits.message:
Zanzana: Fix inverted folder parent tuples in reconciler

The reconciler's TranslateFolderToTuples passed arguments to
NewFolderParentTuple in the wrong order (parent, child) instead of
(child, parent), inverting the entire folder hierarchy in Zanzana's
tuple store. This caused folder permission inheritance to break —
users with permissions on parent folders could not access resources
in child folders, producing shadow check mismatches
(expected=true, actual=false).

The mutation path in server_mutate_folder.go and all tests used the
correct argument order, but the reconciler's periodic full sync would
overwrite correct tuples with inverted ones.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Datasource Permissions: Allow sending in query param `ds_type` and add database migration
body: In the future, datasource uids will only be unique per datasource type, not per all datasources. This PR: - Adds a db migration to set datasource uid to the permissions table (if it is a datasource uid scoped permission) - Adds the optional query parameter `ds_type` to datasource permission endpoints. This becomes required (for reads & writes) _IF_ there ends up being uid collisions for that uid. --------------------- Implementation notes: - This PR allows /api/access-control/{resource}/... endpoints to specify a request validator that is checked before every request. This is added because the resource validator is not used on GETs (and also doesnt have the query params). In [this PR](https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11501), logic is added to require the type to be provided if we get a collision (for gets too) - It also allows endpoints to specify a datasource type resolver. The request validator sets it in the context, to avoid a double look-up. This is to keep the datasource logic out of other parts of the code. - The datasources team hasn't yet implemented the check for collision on uids, so I added the error we expect for now and a TODO. - I had looked into a few ways of implementing this - but ended up here as the best way to do it (even though it is weird to have a query param on write endpoints, i think that is the least awful way of doing this here, particularly since these endpoints will be deprecated soon). The other ways I looked at it were: - Allowing ds_type as a query param on GET, and then adding it as a body in the post. However, this would mean that the open api spec (and then what terraform imports) would have datasource type as a field for all of resource permission endpoints, not just the datasource ones. - Copying over generic functions and registering the datasource api endpoints separately so the body fields could be different but the duplicated code would be rough to maintain (especially w/ the api redirects). ---------------------------- Testing: MySQL migration: Sqlite migration: Postgres migration:

title: unified-storage: fix bulk rv generation in kv store
body:
It breaks backwards compatibility with the sql backend. 

To maintain this compatibility, we can't store snowflakes with the node bits set. We can only populate the timestamp and the counter bits. With the current approach, we store all the bulk imported items with the same millisecond timestamp and just increment the counter. With the node bits we can import up to 2^22 (~4 mil) items before an RV collision, which is more than enough for bulk import. 

But to be able to convert back and forth in backwards compatibility mode, we get a microsecond a rv, store the millisecond part in the timestamp, and store the microsecond remainder in the counter bits (1000, which fit comfortably in the 2^10 counter bits (4096)). This PR changes the logic to increment the millisecond when spilling over instead.
author: gassiss

title: QueryEditor: Extract UI toggles hook (1/3)
body:
## What
Resolves https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/119626

Pure refactor — no user-visible behaviour change. Prepares the context for multi-selection in [#120139](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120139).

**`useQueryEditorUIToggles` hook**

Extracts `isQueryOptionsOpen`, `focusedField`, `showingDatasourceHelp`, and `transformTogglesState` out of `QueryEditorContextWrapper` into a dedicated hook, reducing the size of the wrapper component.

**Selection model migration**

Migrates selection state from scalar `string | null` to `string[]` arrays: `selectedQueryRefIds` and `selectedTransformationIds`. The "primary" card is always the last element, so single-select behaviour is unchanged.

Updates `useSelectedCard`, `QueryEditorContextWrapper`, `testUtils`, and all related tests accordingly.

## Test plan

- All existing tests pass unchanged
- Single-select behaviour is unaffected — the primary selected card is always the last element of the array
author: alexjonspencer1

reviews.body: Looks great now!

  showingDatasourceHelp, and transformTogglesState out of QueryEditorContextWrapper
  into a dedicated hook, reducing the size of the wrapper component.

- Migrates selection model from scalar string|null to string[] arrays:
  selectedQueryRefIds and selectedTransformationIds. The "primary" card is
  always the last element, so single-select behaviour is unchanged. This
  prepares the context for multi-selection in the next PR.

- Updates useSelectedCard, QueryEditorContextWrapper, testUtils, and all
  related tests accordingly. Pure refactor — no user-visible behaviour change.

title: Transformations: Remove content.ts from main bundle
body: ## Description Moves transformer documentation content out of the main bundle into a lazily-loaded webpack chunk. Previously, `content.ts` (~69 KB of markdown) was pulled into the main bundle on every page load via static imports across 30+ transformer editor files. ## Changes * Converted `getTransformationContent` to `async`, dynamically importing `./content` with `/* webpackChunkName: "transformer-docs" */` * Removed `help: getTransformationContent(id).helperDocs` from all transformer registry items — this was the static import chain causing the eager bundle inclusion * Updated both help display components to fetch docs lazily on drawer open via `useEffect`, with cancellation flags to prevent stale state on rapid transformer switching (though unlikely...) * Removed the `hasHelp` guard on the help button — since `help` is no longer set at registration time it would have hidden the button for all transformers ## Risks N/A ## Demo #### Before **Directly added to initApp.ts, not good!** #### After **Now lives in its own separate file, "transformer-docs.c4306f2ac31c678346dd.js"** ## Testing Instructions * Add any transformation in panel edit → click `?` → verify `transformer-docs.[hash].js` appears in DevTools Network * Click `?` on a second transformation → confirm no new network request (cached) * Verify help drawer content is correct and switching transformations works ## Reviewer Checklist - [ ] Tests are added where applicable - [ ] Issue is linked to PR - [ ] Code pulled and tested - [ ] Code is meaningfully improved if applicable
author: alexjonspencer1

reviews.body:
Wow, huge improvement! 🚀🚀🚀

Did you use @leeoniya's skill agent for this?

commits.message: chore: remove content.ts from main bundle
fix: re work tests + new testing file

title: IAM: Support action sets for alerting managed routes
body:
**What is this feature?**
This PR achieves two goals:
- make new actions alerting introduced recently to be forward compatible with a new IAM framework
	- action names follow the pattern `<api-group>/<resource>:<k8s-action>`
	- scope follows the pattern `<api-group>/<resource>:uid:<metadata.name>`
- renames managed routes actions to align with resource name. This will make action sets and actions look similar (i.e. action set `alert.routes:edit` will include `alert.routes:read`, `*:write`, `*:delete` )
- updates permission service to support action sets for managed routes. For example, for `edit` permission the service will store one permission instead of 3, and automatically expand to actions when needed.

**Why do we need this feature?**
- support action sets

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

The actions are new and not used in production yet. Therefore, it's safe to rename.


Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: yuri-tceretian

reviews.body: Looks good to me, since actions are not in production yet, should be ok.

make resource match the scope to enable action sets

title: [release-12.3.7] Dashboards: Get annotations and dashboard endpoint performance improvements
body:
Backport 1da06fc1a997d89aebc04cfa6134fd91fce2e9fd from #119378

---

<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

This PR contains general performance improvements for **Get Annotation** and **Create Dashboard** endpoints

**Enterprise PR**: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11257

Here's a quick list of changes included in this PR:

- **GetUserPermissions** – DB time was low but total time high due to xorm’s reflection over many rows → run the query with QueryContext and scan directly into a slice (no xorm Find()).
- **getCachedUserPermissions** – Repeated appends caused many reallocations when merging three permission slices → pre-allocate one slice with the total length, then copy the three into it.
- **ExpandActionSetsWithFilter** – Growing the result with many appends and reusing a single struct was slow and fragile → count the final size first, allocate once, then fill without mutating the input.
- **Dashboard search (MySQL)** – FORCE INDEX (IDX_dashboard_title) could hurt when filters are very selective → add force_dashboard_title_index (default true) so it can be turned off to let the optimizer choose the index.
- **Permission lookups** – Queries by role/scope were not indexed → add migration for index on permission(role_id, scope).

**Why do we need this feature?**

With many dashboards (e.g. 10k+), **latency grew linearly with the number of permissions** because most time was spent in Go after the DB responded: xorm reflection over many rows, repeated slice reallocations when merging/expanding permissions, and a forced MySQL index that could hurt selective queries. The changes reduce that post-DB work (direct row scanning, single allocations, optional index) so latency scales with DB time instead of permission count.

**Who is this feature for?**

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

Fixes #

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: filewalkwithme

commits.message:
Dashboards: Get annotations and dashboard endpoint performance improvements (#119378)

* Avoid reallocation of large array on getCachedUserPermissions

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* perf: add setting for MYSQL FORCE INDEX IDX_dashboard_title

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* permissions: optimize ExpandActionSetsWithFilter allocation

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* wip

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* Revert "wip"

This reverts commit 1f5479fce4b33e354d1bd7187ff59da9a0014a90.

* perf(accesscontrol): avoid xorm reflection in GetUserPermissions for large result sets

The DB query for user permissions was fast (~2ms) but the total time reached
40ms with ~12k rows because xorm's Find() uses reflection to map each row
into a struct.

- Run the permission query via engine.DB().QueryContext() and scan rows
  directly with rows.Scan(&action, &scope) into []Permission.
- Add getUserPermissionsRaw() so this read-only path no longer uses
  WithDbSession + Find().
- Pre-allocate the result slice (cap 4096) to limit reallocations.
- Keep build_query and query spans for profiling.

Query span time should now align with actual DB time instead of adding tens
of ms from reflection over 10k+ rows.

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* migrations: add (role_id, scope) index on permission table

Add index to speed up GetUserPermissions-style queries that join permission
with role and filter/select by role_id and scope.

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* Revert "migrations: add (role_id, scope) index on permission table"

This reverts commit dfdf35db5ce00d4e0d6968fba91fc5373767b502.

* Revert "perf(accesscontrol): avoid xorm reflection in GetUserPermissions for large result sets"

This reverts commit 91c2038916bf7cf1a7f9dbb860ff79cc1f07bfc9.

* perf(accesscontrol): avoid xorm reflection in GetUserPermissions for large result sets

Run the permission query via engine.DB().QueryContext() and scan rows
directly into []Permission to avoid xorm Find() reflection over 10k+ rows.

- Add getUserPermissionsRaw() and replacePlaceholdersForPostgres() so
  the raw query path works with SQLite, MySQL and Postgres (convert ?
  to $1, $2 for Postgres).
- Pre-allocate result slice (cap 4096); keep build_query/query spans.

Made-with: Cursor

* run make gofmt

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* Reapply "migrations: add (role_id, scope) index on permission table"

This reverts commit 006e7e5643a0c3a81daaedb7269c9a111493fece.

* fix(accesscontrol): check rows.Close() return value for errcheck

Made-with: Cursor

* accesscontrol: use sqlx Rebind for GetUserPermissions instead of replacePlaceholdersForPostgres

Use GetSqlxSession().Query() which rebinds ? to driver-specific placeholders
(e.g. $1,$2 for Postgres), removing the need for replacePlaceholdersForPostgres
and the migrator/xorm imports in the accesscontrol database package.

Made-with: Cursor

* Pre-allocate permission array with 512 bytes length

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

* accesscontrol: use sqlx Query for GetTeamsPermissions instead of Find

Run the teams-permissions query via GetSqlxSession().Query() and scan rows
directly into the result map, avoiding xorm Find() reflection. Remove the
teamPermission struct and Permission() helper.

Made-with: Cursor

* accesscontrol: initialize teamPermissions map for GetTeamsPermissions

Co-authored-by: Gabriel MABILLE <gamab@users.noreply.github.com>

* accesscontrol: run permission queries on DB session retry path

Add xorm.Session.QueryRows as a thin wrapper around queryRows so callers
can stream raw SQL results on the same connection/transaction as the
session.

Use WithDbSession + QueryRows for GetUserPermissions and GetTeamsPermissions
instead of GetSqlxSession().Query, restoring sqlite lock retries and
transaction-bound sessions from context.

Made-with: Cursor

---------

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Gabriel MABILLE <gamab@users.noreply.github.com>
(cherry picked from commit 1da06fc1a997d89aebc04cfa6134fd91fce2e9fd)
Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

title: Alerting: Declare dedicated structs for external Alertmanager APIs
body:
Create dedicated structs for external Alertmanager APIs. Currently, we reuse structs with storage model of Grafana alertmanager  and it causes many issues
The new stucts have custom marshaling logic that keeps the semantic of remarshaling yaml to json without losing secret fields.  

Note: It become obvious that this re-marshaling struct does not need to be fully structured. It's main purpose is to go from YAML to JSON. Validation on Grafana side is redundant and counter-productive. So, in a follow up PR I plan to replace `config.Config` with `map[string]any`
author: yuri-tceretian

commits.message: declare dedicated structs for external alertmanager APIs

title: Suggested Dashboards: Integrate grafana assistant in the suggested dashboards modal
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Adds an "Customize with Assistant" button to the suggested dashboards modal (both the Provisioned and Community tabs), gated behind the `suggestedDashboardsAssistantButton` feature flag.

**Changes I made**

  - **Assistant button in suggested dashboards:** The existing `showAssistantButton` prop on `DashboardCard` is now passed as true in both `DashboardLibrarySection` and `CommunityDashboardSection` when the feature flag is enabled.
  - **Adapted prompts & context:** For suggested_dashboard kind, the assistant gets a different prompt — "adapt this dashboard" rather than "create from template", and the context title drops the Template prefix to avoid triggering the template-dashboard tool. Instructions clarify that the dashboard is  already rendered and should not be saved.
  - **Analytics**: Introduced in `SuggestedDashboardInteractions` an action: 'use_dashboard' | 'assistant' and i`sSuggestedDashboardAssistantButtonEnabled` for experiment analysis. The assistantSource param flows through communityDashboardHelpers into the URL.

  **Known limitations**

  - **Context window: Fixed!** When the assistant adapts a suggested dashboard, it relies on Grafana MCP tools to inspect the rendered panels. For complex dashboards,  reading all panels at once can exceed the LLM's context window. The assistant instructions attempt to mitigate this by directing the model to use a lightweight dashboard summary first, but this is a best-effort prompt instruction, not a hard constraint (node exporter failed 😢 ).  -- this was fixed,  see context ([slack](https://raintank-corp.slack.com/archives/C09DXGH5W6T/p1773410021924469?thread_ts=1773404551.048619&cid=C09DXGH5W6T))
 
  - **Race condition:** The assistant is opened (and auto-sends) immediately after navigation to the dashboard page. If the dashboard scene hasn't fully initialized by the time the LLM starts issuing MCP tool calls, the tools may return incomplete or empty results. This can cause the assistant to misread the dashboard structure and produce incorrect adaptations. Fixing this properly (detecting scene readiness before auto-sending) will be tackled in a a follow-up PR.

 Note: Design is not ready, as soon as this [PR](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/120198) is merged I will adapt it.

[Kooha-2026-03-13-11-28-08.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7f01e82-87fd-4b0b-90e7-eeaa26202fc9)

title: CSP: Add configurable form-action directive via $FORM_ACTION_ADDITIONAL_HOSTS
body:
**What is this feature?**

Adds a new `[security] form_action_additional_hosts` config setting and corresponding `$FORM_ACTION_ADDITIONAL_HOSTS` CSP template variable. This allows operators to specify additional hosts (beyond `'self'`) permitted in the CSP `form-action` directive. The default CSP templates are updated to use `form-action 'self' $FORM_ACTION_ADDITIONAL_HOSTS;`, so `'self'` is always included and the variable is purely additive.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Some deployments need forms to POST to external hosts (e.g. external identity providers for SAML/OAuth flows). Previously, this required replacing the entire CSP template. This setting makes it configurable without touching the template, following the same pattern as `allow_embedding_hosts` for `frame-ancestors`.

**Who is this feature for?**

Operators who need to allow form submissions to external hosts while keeping CSP enabled. In the frontend service, this is a per-tenant setting fetched from the settings service.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes #

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

- The `ReplacePolicyVariables` function signature changed from a bare `[]string` to a `CSPHostLists` struct to cleanly carry both `FrameAncestorHosts` and `FormActionAdditionalHosts`
- When `form_action_additional_hosts` is empty, `$FORM_ACTION_ADDITIONAL_HOSTS` resolves to an empty string (not `'self'`) — `'self'` lives in the template itself
- Supported in both the standard Grafana codepath and the frontend service codepath with per-tenant override support

Please check that:
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
author: joshhunt

commits.message:
CSP: Add configurable form-action directive via $FORM_ACTION_ADDITIONAL_HOSTS

Add support for customising the CSP form-action directive through a new
`[security] form_action_additional_hosts` config setting. The setting
specifies additional hosts beyond 'self' (which should be included
directly in the CSP template).

The new $FORM_ACTION_ADDITIONAL_HOSTS template variable is replaced with
the configured hosts, or empty string when unset.

Supported in both the standard Grafana codepath and the frontend service
codepath, where it can be overridden per-tenant via the settings service.

title: Provisioning: parameterise folder GVK in internal functions
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

Make folder version a parameter on relevant code points:
- `NewFolderManager` now accepts a `folderGVK` parameter instead of using the `FolderKind` constant directly.
- `NewFolderManifest` and `ParseFolderResource` accept a `schema.GroupVersionKind` parameter.
- `DualReadWriter` uses `r.folders.FolderGVK()` for constructing folder resources.
- Adds `FolderGVKForVersion()` helper.
- All call sites pass `FolderKind` — **no behavior change**.

**Why do we need this feature?**

This is the first step to make folder version configurable per environment:
- OSS and enterprise should use v1.
- Hosted grafana should use v1beta1 until rollout completion.

**Who is this feature for?**

Provisioning feature.

title: fix: add @openfeature/web-sdk as direct dep of @grafana/runtime
body:
**What is this feature?**

This installs `@openfeature/web-sdk` as a direct dependency of `@grafana/runtime` to satisfy the peer dependency requirement in `@openfeature/ofrep-web-provider` and ensure it is available.

_This also updates the other `@openfeature/*` packages to their latest versions to fix compatibility issues between the previously installed versions and the most recent `@openfeature/web-sdk` version._

**Why do we need this feature?**

`@openfeature/ofrep-web-provider` declares `@openfeature/web-sdk` is required as a peer dependency. `@openfeature/react-sdk` is already a direct dependency of `@grafana/runtime`, which itself has a dependency of `@openfeature/web-sdk`, and so `@openfeature/web-sdk` is normally already available for `@openfeature/ofrep-web-provider`. _However_, in certain package manager situations, such as Yarn Classic, it would appear that `@openfeature/web-sdk` is isolated to just `@openfeature/react-sdk` and so `@openfeature/ofrep-web-provider` fails to run as it does not have `@openfeature/web-sdk` available as a dependency.

**Who is this feature for?**

Developers.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

N/A

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

N/A

title: Dashboards: Get annotations and dashboard endpoint performance improvements
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

3. In a new feature or configuration option, an update to the documentation is necessary. Everything related to the documentation is under the docs folder in the root of the repository.

4. If the Pull Request is a work in progress, make use of GitHub's "Draft PR" feature and mark it as such.

5. If you can not merge your Pull Request due to a merge conflict, Rebase it. This gets it in sync with the main branch.

6. Name your PR as "<FeatureArea>: Describe your change", e.g. Alerting: Prevent race condition. If it's a fix or feature relevant for the changelog describe the user impact in the title. The PR title is used to auto-generate the changelog for issues marked with the "add to changelog" label.

7. If your PR content should be added to the What's New document for the next major or minor release, add the **add to what's new** label to your PR. Note that you should add this label to the main PR that introduces the feature; do not add this label to smaller PRs for the feature.

-->

**What is this feature?**

This PR contains general performance improvements for **Get Annotation** and **Create Dashboard** endpoints

**Enterprise PR**: https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/pull/11257

Here's a quick list of changes included in this PR:

- **GetUserPermissions** – DB time was low but total time high due to xorm’s reflection over many rows → run the query with QueryContext and scan directly into a slice (no xorm Find()).
- **getCachedUserPermissions** – Repeated appends caused many reallocations when merging three permission slices → pre-allocate one slice with the total length, then copy the three into it.
- **ExpandActionSetsWithFilter** – Growing the result with many appends and reusing a single struct was slow and fragile → count the final size first, allocate once, then fill without mutating the input.
- **Dashboard search (MySQL)** – FORCE INDEX (IDX_dashboard_title) could hurt when filters are very selective → add force_dashboard_title_index (default true) so it can be turned off to let the optimizer choose the index.
- **Permission lookups** – Queries by role/scope were not indexed → add migration for index on permission(role_id, scope).

**Why do we need this feature?**

With many dashboards (e.g. 10k+), **latency grew linearly with the number of permissions** because most time was spent in Go after the DB responded: xorm reflection over many rows, repeated slice reallocations when merging/expanding permissions, and a forced MySQL index that could hurt selective queries. The changes reduce that post-DB work (direct row scanning, single allocations, optional index) so latency scales with DB time instead of permission count.

**Who is this feature for?**

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

Usage: "Fixes #<issue number>", or "Fixes (paste link of issue)"

-->

commits.message:
Avoid reallocation of large array on getCachedUserPermissions

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

perf: add setting for MYSQL FORCE INDEX IDX_dashboard_title

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

permissions: optimize ExpandActionSetsWithFilter allocation

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

perf(accesscontrol): avoid xorm reflection in GetUserPermissions for large result sets

The DB query for user permissions was fast (~2ms) but the total time reached
40ms with ~12k rows because xorm's Find() uses reflection to map each row
into a struct.

- Run the permission query via engine.DB().QueryContext() and scan rows
  directly with rows.Scan(&action, &scope) into []Permission.
- Add getUserPermissionsRaw() so this read-only path no longer uses
  WithDbSession + Find().
- Pre-allocate the result slice (cap 4096) to limit reallocations.
- Keep build_query and query spans for profiling.

Query span time should now align with actual DB time instead of adding tens
of ms from reflection over 10k+ rows.

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

migrations: add (role_id, scope) index on permission table

Add index to speed up GetUserPermissions-style queries that join permission
with role and filter/select by role_id and scope.

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

Revert "migrations: add (role_id, scope) index on permission table"

This reverts commit dfdf35db5ce00d4e0d6968fba91fc5373767b502.

Revert "perf(accesscontrol): avoid xorm reflection in GetUserPermissions for large result sets"

This reverts commit 91c2038916bf7cf1a7f9dbb860ff79cc1f07bfc9.

perf(accesscontrol): avoid xorm reflection in GetUserPermissions for large result sets

Run the permission query via engine.DB().QueryContext() and scan rows
directly into []Permission to avoid xorm Find() reflection over 10k+ rows.

- Add getUserPermissionsRaw() and replacePlaceholdersForPostgres() so
  the raw query path works with SQLite, MySQL and Postgres (convert ?
  to $1, $2 for Postgres).
- Pre-allocate result slice (cap 4096); keep build_query/query spans.

run make gofmt

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

Reapply "migrations: add (role_id, scope) index on permission table"

This reverts commit 006e7e5643a0c3a81daaedb7269c9a111493fece.

fix(accesscontrol): check rows.Close() return value for errcheck

Made-with: Cursor

accesscontrol: use sqlx Rebind for GetUserPermissions instead of replacePlaceholdersForPostgres

Use GetSqlxSession().Query() which rebinds ? to driver-specific placeholders
(e.g. $1,$2 for Postgres), removing the need for replacePlaceholdersForPostgres
and the migrator/xorm imports in the accesscontrol database package.

Made-with: Cursor

Pre-allocate permission array with 512 bytes length

Signed-off-by: Maicon Costa <maiconscosta@gmail.com>

accesscontrol: use sqlx Query for GetTeamsPermissions instead of Find

Run the teams-permissions query via GetSqlxSession().Query() and scan rows
directly into the result map, avoiding xorm Find() reflection. Remove the
teamPermission struct and Permission() helper.

Co-authored-by: Gabriel MABILLE <gamab@users.noreply.github.com>

accesscontrol: run permission queries on DB session retry path

Add xorm.Session.QueryRows as a thin wrapper around queryRows so callers
can stream raw SQL results on the same connection/transaction as the
session.

Use WithDbSession + QueryRows for GetUserPermissions and GetTeamsPermissions
instead of GetSqlxSession().Query, restoring sqlite lock retries and
transaction-bound sessions from context.

title: Provisioning: use incoming file count for bootstrap quota guard
body:
**What is this feature?**

- Switch bootstrap quota validation from global resource count to repository file count so the guardrail reflects incoming sync scope.
- Add regression tests to ensure quota blocking is driven by fileCount and not resourceCount

**Why do we need this feature?**

The old bootstrap check used a signal that could represent namespace-wide/global resource state rather than the incoming repository payload. That made quota blocking confusing and potentially incorrect during setup. We need the guardrail to be tied to repository input so users can trust the warning and understand why setup is blocked.

**Who is this feature for?**

Git sync users

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

<!--

- Automatically closes linked issue when the Pull Request is merged.

-->

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:

title: Alerting: Preserve scroll position on Alert Activity refresh
body:
## Summary
- Keep existing table rows visible during data refresh on the Alert Activity (Triage) page instead of replacing them with skeleton placeholders
- Show skeletons only on initial load (when no data exists yet); on subsequent refreshes, display a `LoadingBar` at the top of the scroll container — matching the `PanelChrome` pattern used throughout Grafana
- Remove `isPending` from the loading calculation as it was defeating the purpose of React's `useTransition` API (hiding old UI instead of keeping it visible during transition)

Before

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/00bc590b-809b-4fc4-9343-7b572f300b27



After

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a5a2690-a7a6-4856-a794-1c348dba773e




## Test plan
- [ ] Navigate to Alert Activity page (`/alerting/alerts`) with firing alerts
- [ ] Verify initial load shows skeleton placeholders as before
- [ ] Scroll down and expand some group rows
- [ ] Click the refresh button — rows should stay in place with a thin loading bar at the top
- [ ] Enable auto-refresh (e.g. 5s interval) — rows should remain stable across refresh cycles
- [ ] Verify scroll position and expanded/collapsed row state persist across refreshes
- [ ] Change a filter — verify old rows remain briefly visible with loading bar, then update to new results


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: dhalachliyski

reviews.body: Nicee!

Alerting: Preserve table context on Alert Activity page refresh

Keep existing rows visible during data refresh instead of replacing
them with skeleton placeholders. Show skeletons only on initial load
(no data yet) and use a LoadingBar for subsequent refreshes, matching
the PanelChrome pattern. This preserves scroll position, expanded row
state, and the user's reading context across manual and auto-refreshes.

Also removes isPending from the loading calculation as it was defeating
the purpose of React's useTransition API.

title: Feature Toggles: Support setting via configOverrides command-line flag
body:
**What is this feature?**

Previously, it was possible to enable boolean feature flags on the commandline with the `configOverrides` flag, like so:

```console
$ grafana server --configOverrides "cfg:feature_toggles.enable=myFlag ..."
```

**Why do we need this feature?**

However, the `enable` form is deprecated (as of #114047), and the supported form (which allows disabling boolean features and setting non-boolean values) didn't work:

```console
$ grafana server --configOverrides "cfg:feature_toggles.myFlag=myValue ..."
<silently ignores the flag setting>
```

The current behaviour is that `configOverrides` is only able to set an item when a default value is present in `defaults.ini`. This is undesirable for the `feature_toggles` section, which presumes the ability to set arbitrary names to arbitrary values.

I had to address this to solve #121271, and this PR extends that same approach to `configOverrides` handling.

**Who is this feature for?**

Grafana operators

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: RBAC: Add K8s action format option to resource permissions
body:
## Why the change

New resources should use K8s-native format for action names, scopes, and fixed role names (e.g., `dashboard.grafana.app/dashboards:get_permissions`) instead of the legacy format (e.g., `dashboards.permissions:read`). This change adds a `K8sActionFormat` option to `resourcepermissions.Options` that allows new resources to opt into the K8s format while leaving existing resources unchanged.

Alerting is planning to add a new resource permission for alert routes. It'd be good to use forward-compatible RBAC actions and scopes. With this PR we make it possible. 

## Summary
- Add `K8sActionFormat bool` field to `Options` struct
- Add private helper methods on Service: `getAction(verb)`, `getScope(parts...)`, `getActionSetName(permission)`, `getRoleName(suffix)`
- Add validation in `New()` that K8sActionFormat requires APIGroup to be non-empty
- Update API endpoint registration to use service helpers for action names and scope
- Update fixed role registration to use helpers for role names, actions, and scopes
- Update action set registration and `mapPermission` to use `getActionSetName`
- Update command/query construction to use `getScope` for the Resource field
- Add test cases in service_test.go and api_test.go for K8s action format mode
author: gamab

reviews.body: LGTM. I tested it with my PR https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/121372 and https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/121253. It works as expected

commits.message:
RBAC: Add K8s action format option to resource permissions

Add a `K8sActionFormat` option to `resourcepermissions.Options` that switches
action names, scopes, and fixed role names to K8s-native format for new resources.

Changes:
- Add `K8sActionFormat bool` field to Options struct
- Add private helper methods on Service: getAction, getScope, getActionSetName, getRoleName
- Add validation that K8sActionFormat requires APIGroup to be non-empty
- Update api.go to use service helpers for action names and scope
- Update declareFixedRoles to use helpers for role names, actions, scopes
- Update action set registration and mapPermission to use getActionSetName
- Update command/query construction to use getScope for the Resource field
- Add test cases for K8s action format mode

Made-with: Cursor
Switch to use options as a method holder

Account for PR feedback

Co-authored-by: Yuriy Tseretyan <yuriy.tseretyan@grafana.com>

title: Alerting: Move inhibition rules alert to sidebar
body: **What is this feature?** This PR removes the inhibition rules banner from the alert manager config page, and replaces the same banner in the alert rule detail page with a new "Inhibited" status when the rule is being affected by an existing inhibition rule. **Why do we need this feature?** The previous generic banner was too broad and appeared in contexts where it wasn't actionable where users editing configuration don't benefit from a generic "inhibition rules are in effect" notice. It was also shown on every Grafana-managed rule detail page regardless of whether that specific rule was involved in any inhibition relationship, creating noise and reducing trust in the signal. The new approach surfaces inhibition information only when and where it is relevant: on the detail page of a rule that is actually affected by an inhibition rule. **Who is this feature for?** Alerting users **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**: Fixes https://github.com/grafana/alerting-squad/issues/1154 **Special notes for your reviewer:** Please check that: - [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.

title: Security: Upgrade minimatch to fix CVE-2026-27903 and CVE-2026-27904
body:
## Summary
- Upgrades minimatch from 10.2.2 to 10.2.5 to remediate two ReDoS vulnerabilities (CVSS 7.5 HIGH each):
  - **CVE-2026-27903**: matchOne() combinatorial backtracking via multiple non-adjacent GLOBSTAR segments
  - **CVE-2026-27904**: nested *() extglobs generate catastrophically backtracking regular expressions
- minimatch is a transitive dependency used by glob, @npmcli/*, and @tufjs/models
- Only `yarn.lock` changes — fix versions are within existing semver ranges (`^10.0.3`, `^10.1.1`, `^10.2.2`)

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify `grep 'minimatch@npm:10.2.2' yarn.lock` returns empty (vulnerable version removed)
- [ ] Verify `yarn install --immutable` passes with updated lockfile
- [ ] CI passes

title: Unified: add goleak goroutine leak detection to resource tests
body:
## Summary
- Add `goleak.VerifyTestMain` to `pkg/storage/unified/resource` to catch leaking goroutines
- Fix real leaks:
  - Add `Stop()` to `Pruner` interface and call it in `kvStorageBackend.Stop()`
  - Add cancellable context to `kvStorageBackend` and `backend` to stop all background goroutines on `Stop()`
  - Fix GC goroutine initial jitter sleep to respect context cancellation
  - Fix fake test backends (`fakeBackend`, `panicBulkBackend`, `mockStorageBackend`) to return context-aware channels from `WatchWriteEvents` instead of nil
  - Fix `sleepAfterSuccessfulWriteOperation` and `pollingNotifier.Watch`, cancelled at shutdown or request cancellation. 
- Add proper `t.Cleanup` calls to stop backends/servers in tests
- Use `t.Context()` instead of `context.Background()` in test watchers
- Third-party goroutines (go-cache, OTel, database/sql, dskit, hashicorp LRU) are on the ignore list


Ref: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/121007#discussion_r2993404143

## Test plan

Storage: add goleak goroutine leak detection to resource tests

Add goleak.VerifyTestMain to detect leaking goroutines in the
resource package test suite. Fix several real leaks discovered:

- Add Stop() to Pruner interface and call it in kvStorageBackend.Stop()
- Add context.WithCancel to kvStorageBackend to stop background goroutines
- Fix server.initWatcher goroutine to respect s.ctx cancellation
- Add t.Cleanup to stop backends and servers in tests

Third-party goroutines (go-cache janitor, OTel span processor,
database/sql, dskit runtime config, hashicorp LRU) are added to
the ignore list since they are not under our control.

title: Provisioning: Fix folder rename ref-not-found on new branch workflow
body:
## What

When renaming a provisioned folder with the "new branch" workflow, `WriteFolderMetadataUpdate` reads the existing `_folder.json` from the target ref (the new branch name). Since the branch doesn't exist yet, `repo.Read()` → `resolveRefToHash()` fails with `ErrRefNotFound`, returning a 404.

## Why

The subsequent `repo.Update()` call would have been fine — it calls `ensureBranchExists()` which creates the branch from the configured branch. But the `ReadFolderMetadata` call happens first and fails.

## Fix

Fall back to reading from the configured branch (empty ref) when `ErrRefNotFound` is returned. This is safe because `ensureBranchExists` creates the new branch from the configured branch, so content is identical at creation time. Matches the precedent already in `CreateFolder` (dualwriter.go:194).

## Tests

- Unit test: `WriteFolderMetadataUpdate` fallback path and double-failure path
- Integration test: `UpdateFolderMetadata` end-to-end with new branch ref
author: Clarity-89

commits.message:
fix: handle ErrRefNotFound in WriteFolderMetadataUpdate for new branch workflow

When renaming a folder with the 'new branch' workflow, the frontend
sends a ref that doesn't exist yet. WriteFolderMetadataUpdate tried to
read the existing _folder.json from that ref, which failed with
ErrRefNotFound before repo.Update (which calls ensureBranchExists)
ever ran.

Fall back to reading from the configured branch (empty ref) when the
target ref doesn't exist. This is safe because ensureBranchExists
creates the new branch from the configured branch, so content is
identical at creation time. Matches the precedent in CreateFolder.

Provisioning: Add integration tests for folder rename on new branch workflow

Add end-to-end integration test exercising the ErrRefNotFound fallback
in WriteFolderMetadataUpdate through the full HTTP → files endpoint → git
backend stack.

Tests:
- rename folder on new branch succeeds (core fix path)
- second rename on same branch updates existing branch (non-fallback path)
- rename nested folder on new branch succeeds (path handling)

title: Provisioning: Switch provisioningFolderMetadata toggle to OpenFeature
body:
**What is this feature?**

Switches the `provisioningFolderMetadata` feature toggle from boot data (`config.featureToggles.provisioningFolderMetadata`) to OpenFeature (`useBooleanFlagValue` from `@openfeature/react-sdk`) across all 5 frontend component files that read it. Updates 2 test files to use `setTestFlags` from `@grafana/test-utils/unstable` instead of `testWithFeatureToggles`.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Reading from boot data means the toggle cannot be controlled per-cluster via goff. Switching to OpenFeature enables evaluated feature toggle support, allowing the toggle to be managed dynamically at the cluster level rather than requiring a full config deployment.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/1046

title: Zanzana: make datastore creation pluggable via StoreProvider interface
body:
## Summary

Introduces a `StoreProvider` interface in the zanzana store package that abstracts OpenFGA datastore creation, enabling alternative backend implementations to be injected via Wire
- Refactors `NewEmbeddedZanzanaServer` and `NewZanzanaServer` to accept a pre-built `storage.OpenFGADatastore` instead of creating stores internally
- Threads `StoreProvider` through `ProvideEmbeddedZanzanaServer`, `ProvideZanzanaService`, and `ModuleServer` via Wire dependency injection
- Adds `store_type` and `[zanzana.store.grpc]` configuration settings to support future alternative datastore backends

## Test plan

- [x] `go test ./pkg/services/authz/...` — existing zanzana tests pass
- [x] `make build-go` — full enterprise build succeeds
- [x] `make gen-go` — wire regeneration succeeds for both OSS and enterprise

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: mihai-turdean

commits.message:
Zanzana: make datastore creation pluggable via StoreProvider interface

Introduce a StoreProvider interface that abstracts how OpenFGA datastores
are created for the Zanzana authorization server. This allows alternative
datastore backends to be injected via Wire dependency injection.

Changes:
- Add StoreProvider interface with NewEmbeddedStore and NewStandaloneStore
  methods, plus a default SQL-based implementation
- Refactor NewEmbeddedZanzanaServer and NewZanzanaServer to accept a
  pre-built storage.OpenFGADatastore instead of creating one internally
- Thread StoreProvider through ProvideEmbeddedZanzanaServer,
  ProvideZanzanaService, and ModuleServer via Wire
- Add ZanzanaGRPCStoreSettings config and store_type setting to support
  future alternative backends
- Regenerate wire_gen.go

title: Suggestions: Limit series and data points in preview cards
body: Suggestion and preset preview cards render the full dataset into a tiny thumbnail, which causes slow rendering/UI issues with large datasets. This adds three new `cardOptions` properties to cap the data before it reaches the panel renderer: - `maxSeries` - limits the number of data frames - `maxRows` - limits rows per frame - `transformPreviewData` - arbitrary transform applied after the caps, used for LTTB downsampling on time series #### Per-panel limits (based on visuals) | Panel | maxSeries | maxRows | transform | |-------|-----------|---------|-----------| | timeseries (line/area) | 8 | - | LTTB (150 pts) | | timeseries (bar) | 8 | 30 | - | | candlestick | 8 | - | LTTB (150 pts) | | bar chart | - | 20 | - | | bar gauge | 6 | - | - | | stat | 6 | - | - | | pie chart | 10 | 10 | - | [LTTB](https://github.com/sveinn-steinarsson/flot-downsample) preserves the visual shape of a time series while reducing point count, so preview cards look like the real chart. Before After

title: annotations: postgres store implementation
body:
This PR creates a PostgreSQL store implementation for the annotations app platform API. Key changes include:
- Weekly time-based partitioning with configurable retention TTL
- Schema migrations using `github.com/pressly/goose/v3`
- Tags and scopes stored as de-normalized arrays with GIN indexes to enable efficient array containment queries
- Caching for tag queries (defaulting to 60s TTL)

_Note: I'd like to add some integration tests to cover this. Debating whether to add them in this PR or as a follow-on as the diff is already >1k lines._

title: Panels: Fix crash in joinByField when no frames match the join field
body:
**What is this feature?**

`joinDataFrames()` crashes with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')` when no input frames contain the join field. This happens when `joinByField` is configured with a field name that doesn't exist in any of the input data frames (e.g., when queries return empty/no-data responses), causing the internal `allData` array to be empty.

## Root cause

PR #120919 added a "cheap join" optimization that calls `allHeadersSame(tables)` at the top of `join()` without guarding against empty input:

```js
function allHeadersSame(tables) {
  let vals0 = tables[0][0]; // crashes when tables is empty
```

Before that PR, the `join()` function handled empty tables gracefully — the loops simply wouldn't execute and it would return empty data.

## Fix

Add an early return guard in `joinDataFrames()` before the mode switch that dispatches to `join()` / `joinTabular()`. When `allData` is empty, it returns a zero-length frame directly. This single guard protects all three join modes (outer, inner, outerTabular).

## Fixes

- Fixes #113663

title: Correlations: (FE) Ensure correlations that were written by a manager are read only in the UI
body:
**What is this feature?**

In app platform, two properties work in tandem to mark if a resource is read only due to being provisioned.

If there is no `managedBy` annotation and no `allowsEdits` annotation, the resource is editable
If there is a `managedBy` annotation and no `allowsEdits` annotation, the resource is not editable

This brings in that logic to have it match with the provisioned flag the frontend uses to mark a correlation readonly.

Be sure the `kubernetesCorrelations` feature flag is enabled and dualWriterMode is set to 1 in custom.ini with

```
[unified_storage.correlations.correlations.grafana.app]
dualWriterMode = 1
```

Correlations are set up in the default datasources.yaml file ([source](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/devenv/datasources.yaml#L264)) and you will see them show up in the UI as "read only" with this change. Before this change, they could be edited and those edits would not persist when the server was restarted.

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

Fixes #120095

title: unified-storage: only return AlreadyExists error when resource can no longer be reverted
body:
Previously, it was possible for the KV backend (with or without the rv manager) to return `ErrResourceAlreadyExists` even though no resource of that name actually exists. This happens in cases where clients are trying to create the same resource concurrently: one could see the other writer's temporary record. However, that record could later be reverted by the optimistic lock checks: as a result, a client receiving `ErrResourceAlreadyExists` could reasonably assume the resource exists even though it actually doesn't. This is similar to a [dirty read](https://jepsen.io/consistency/phenomena/p1) phenomenon in a transactional system.

In this PR, `WriteEvent` deals with the uncertainty of finding a previous create record for a resource: if that resource is old enough (more than 30s old), it's considered "permanent" (or committed). Similarly, if a corresponding event is found, it's also considered committed. With these changes, we should no longer return `ErrResourceAlreadyExists` for records that could still be deleted due to conflicts.

One caveat to mention about this approach is that bulk imports currently insert directly to the datastore, without creating a corresponding event. As a consequence, if a client attempts to create a resource right after import, they would get an optimistic lock error instead of a `ErrResourceAlreadyExists`. After 30s, however, they should start seeing the expected error.

Ticket: https://github.com/grafana/hyperion-planning/issues/553

title: SuggestedDashboards: Filter provisioned dashboards that were removed
body:
<!--

Thank you for sending a pull request! Here are some tips:

1. If this is your first time, please read our contribution guide at https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

2. Ensure you include and run the appropriate tests as part of your Pull Request.

**What is this feature?**
