title: Zanzana: use ListObjects for direct resource phase in BatchCheck
body:
## Summary
- Replaces per-item `Check` calls in the `direct_resource` phase of `BatchCheck` with `ListObjects` + set-membership lookups. Most users have direct access to a small number of resources, so enumerating them all once and resolving via O(1) set lookups is significantly cheaper than issuing one `Check` per batch item — especially when most items are denied (denials have high dispatch counts).
- Adds test coverage for typed resources (teams, users, service accounts), mixed generic/typed batches, and folder subresource access via `set_edit`.

## Test plan
- [x] All existing `TestIntegrationServerBatchCheck` tests pass
- [x] All existing `TestIntegrationServerBatchCheck_SubBatching` tests pass
- [x] New tests for typed resource subresource access (team, user, service account)
- [x] New test for mixed generic and typed resources in same batch
- [x] New test for folder subresource access via `set_edit`
author: chaudyg

commits.message:
Zanzana: use ListObjects for direct resource phase in BatchCheck

Replace per-item Check calls in the direct_resource phase with
ListObjects + set-membership lookups. Most users have direct access to a
small number of resources, so enumerating them once is cheaper than
issuing one Check per batch item (denials are especially expensive due to
high dispatch counts).

Zanzana: use streamed ListObjects and enforce context deadline

Always use StreamedListObjects to avoid the silent 1000-result
truncation from OpenFGA's non-streaming ListObjects. Apply a context
timeout (ListObjectsDeadline - 1s, default 2s) so our context fires
before OpenFGA's internal deadline, ensuring truncation surfaces as
an error instead of silently returning partial results.

title: fix(dashboard): schema validation errors for interface{} fields and API version race
body:
Two fixes in `dashboardSchemaFetcher` to not block code editor in a dashboard. Without those, the validation errors would block applying changes through the editor:

1. Add fixAnyValueProperties() for DynamicConfigValue.value, MatcherConfig.options, and DataTransformerConfig.options — the OpenAPI generator emits { type: "object" } for Go interface{} fields, but these accept any JSON type (string, number, boolean, array). 

2. Await dashboardAPIVersionResolver.resolve() before reading the API version to prevent schema/API version mismatch when v2 negotiation is re-enabled.

Reproduction is available in this dashboard https://ops.grafana-ops.net/d/yalckbr/dashboards-core-homepage
author: dprokop

commits.message:
fix(dashboard): schema validation errors for interface{} fields and API version race

Two fixes in dashboardSchemaFetcher:

1. Add fixAnyValueProperties() for DynamicConfigValue.value, MatcherConfig.options,
   and DataTransformerConfig.options — the OpenAPI generator emits { type: "object" }
   for Go interface{} fields, but these accept any JSON type (string, number, boolean,
   array). Distinct from fixOpaqueMaps which keeps the object constraint.

2. Await dashboardAPIVersionResolver.resolve() before reading the API version to
   prevent schema/API version mismatch when v2 negotiation is re-enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: OpenFeature: Improve namespace validation observability in features service
body:
The `rejecting request with namespace mismatch` log was missing context, making it hard to debug which stacks were failing and why.                                                  
                            
  Changes:                                                                                     
  - Log `authNamespace`, `evalCtxNamespace`, and slug on mismatch
  - Distinguish body read failures from actual namespace mismatches, previously both logged the same message                                                
  - Add `auth_namespace` and `eval_ctx_namespace` as span attributes 
  - Parse eval context body once upfront and then pass only namespace to `validateNamespace`/`validateNamespaceIfPresent`                                                     
  - Add logging to `proxyAllFlagReq`                    
  - Fix wrong logger used in `proxyAllFlagReq.ModifyResponse`
author: undef1nd

## Pull request overview Improves observability around namespace validation for OpenFeature (OFREP) evaluation requests in the features service, adding more context to logs/traces and separating body read/parse failures from namespace mismatches. **Changes:** - Introduces `evalContext` parsing (`namespace`, `slug`) and re-buffers the request body for downstream handlers. - Adds span attributes (`auth_namespace`, `eval_ctx_namespace`) and enhances mismatch logging with auth/eval namespaces and slug. - Fixes `proxyAllFlagReq` to use the correct logger and adds error logging when proxy creation fails. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg/registry/apis/ofrep/register.go | Adds eval context parsing, improved tracing/logging for namespace validation, and new body-read failure handling. | | pkg/registry/apis/ofrep/http_routes.go | Updates embedded Grafana HTTP routes to read eval context once and improve mismatch logging/tracing attributes. | | pkg/registry/apis/ofrep/proxy.go | Uses `b.logger` consistently and logs proxy creation failures. | | pkg/registry/apis/ofrep/register_test.go | Adds unit tests for `readEvalContext` and updates namespace validation tests for new signatures/flow. | | pkg/registry/apis/ofrep/http_routes_test.go | Updates tests for `validateNamespaceIfPresent` to use `readEvalContext` and new return values. |

deletes dozens of `@alpha` tags that I know for a fact aren't really "alpha" anymore, since they have been in place and unchanging for many years.

### Background

When doing some AI analysis across the codebase, tools like Claude were taking us at our word about some of these `@alpha` designations and including notes like this one (which I have deleted: https://github.com/grafana/plugin-tools/pull/2541/changes/027719dd94f6bb462bddcfbb4ad480d6e6b91db8). It was also proving confusing in reviews around the `FieldType.nestedFrames`, which has been in the product for many years and is not going anywhere at this point.

I didn't delete _every_ old tag, because there are many places in the code that I'm not personally familiar with, but I took a big swing at the parts I do know about and can reasonably say many of these things aren't alpha. I can open a targeted and more broken-up version of this if we want.

title: Dashboard: Use preferredVersion from discovery endpoint for API version negotiation
body:
_To enable console debug log_: `  localStorage.setItem('grafana.debug.dashboardAPI', 'true')`


  - Frontend version resolver now reads `preferredVersion` from the `GET /apis/dashboard.grafana.app/` discovery
   response instead of hardcoded version selection logic
  - Allows backend operators to control which API version the frontend uses via the `preferred_api_version` ini
  config (backend support: #121256)
  - Fixes a race condition where `getV1()`/`getV2()` were called before version discovery completed, always
  falling back to beta versions

  ## Test plan
  - [x] Manual: set `preferred_api_version = dashboard.grafana.app/XXX` in `[grafana-apiserver]` config,
  verify frontend uses XXX paths
  - [x] Manual: verify home dashboard loads with correct resolved version (no beta fallback)
author: dprokop

reviews.body: Looks good to me! 💯

commits.message:
Dashboard: Use preferredVersion from discovery endpoint for API version negotiation

The frontend version resolver now reads preferredVersion from the
GET /apis/dashboard.grafana.app/ discovery response instead of using
hardcoded logic. This allows backend operators to control which API
version the frontend uses via the preferred_api_version ini config,
which is critical for proxy/interceptor compatibility during the
v1/v2 stable graduation rollout.

Also ensures API version discovery completes before synchronous
getV1()/getV2() calls in the dashboard loading path.

title: datasources: querier: optional raw output mode
body:
(part of https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/120663)

currently the query service's output is done using a `rest.Responder`. we want to switch writing the response manually through the `http.ResponseWriter`, this has the advantage that we control exactly what bytes we output and exactly what http response code we produce.

this PR introduces this change using a feature flag ( `datasourcesQuerierRawOutput` ), when disabled (the default) it uses the old way, and when enabled, it uses the new way.

when using the new way, we also emit a http-header ( `X-Ds-Querier-Raw=1`), so one can check in the browser's dev-tools if we got the response through the new path.
author: gabor

reviews.body: yeah I feel like this generally makes sense as a general direction to go in!
Seems good to me!

commits.message: datasources: querier: optional raw output mode

title: feature flags: use UTC timestamps in csv
body:
the `pkg/services/featuremgmt/toggles_gen.csv` file contain timestamps. these are formatted using local time,
so for example these two invocations produce different results:
```
TZ=Europe/Berlin make gen-feature-toggles
```
and
```
TZ=Europe/Istanbul make gen-feature-toggles
```

this causes unintended changes in `pkg/services/featuremgmt/toggles_gen.csv` in PRs that add new feature-flags, the timestamps will differ by 1 day based on what hour they were added.

this PR converts the timestamp to UTC, so it will always use UTC timestamps.
author: gabor

commits.message: feature flags: use UTC timestamps in csv

title: Alerting: Replace provisioning banner with inline badge in rule detail view
body: **What is this feature?** In the alert rule detail view, the full-width "provisioned" info banner (`ProvisioningAlert`) is replaced by the compact `ProvisioningBadge` component. The badge is rendered inline in the page title row, to the right of the rule name and to the left of the rule's state indicator. **Why do we need this feature?** The banner takes up significant vertical space and interrupts the page layout. title: Rendering: Prohibit usage of empty/default render auth token in JWT mode
body:
**What is this feature?**

Adds some guards when using `renderAuthJWT` mode, to avoid allowing usage of the empty/default renderer_token.

In dev mode, warns only, but in prod mode does not let Grafana start.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Because the renderer_token signs JWTs, we should not be allowing the usage of default credentials.

**Who is this feature for?**

Operators

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**:

N/A

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

Please check that:
- [X] It works as expected from a user's perspective.
- [X] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle.
- [X] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.


# Release notice breaking change

When using the `renderAuthJWT` feature toggle, you now need to set a `[rendering]renderer_token` that is not empty or the default value, otherwise Grafana will not start.

When updating this value, you also need to make the same change in your Image Renderer configuration. Read more at https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/image-rendering/#security
author: macabu

title: Dashboard: Switch frontend API to v2 and align TransformationKind
body:
Depends on #118646 (backend).

Transformations compatibility layer described in https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ft-dyqwdE_0mi7DvT1E2RFXNa8ZEYpiJbZkjfRSU5Oo/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.i450k8t7bja3

The backend now serves v2 stable with the aligned `TransformationKind` structure (fixed `kind: "Transformation"` literal, transformation ID in `group`). The frontend needs to be updated to use the new API version and handle the new shape.

- v2 schema exports: v2/index.ts now exports v2 stable generated types instead of re-exporting v2beta1
- Transformation compatibility layer (transformationCompat.ts):
    - `normalizeTransformation()` — read path: converts v2beta1 or v2 wire to v2 stable internal representation
    - `toWireTransformation()` — write path: converts v2 stable to wire format matching resolved API version
    - `convertSpecToWireFormat()` — walks full dashboard spec converting all transformations at save boundary
- Read path (layoutSerializers/utils.ts): normalizes transformations from API responses
- Write path (v2.ts saveDashboard): converts spec to correct wire format before API call
- Mutation API: updated schema and panel update to use v2 stable TransformationKind shape (kind: "Transformation", group holds the ID)

### Breaking change handled
- v2beta1: `{ kind: "limit", spec: { id: "limit", disabled, filter, options } }`
- v2 stable: `{ kind: "Transformation", group: "limit", spec: { disabled, filter, options } }`

 ### Deployment compatibility

  - Old backend + New frontend: Uses v2beta1 wire format via compat layer
  - New backend + Old frontend: Backend still serves v2beta1
  - New backend + New frontend: Uses v2 wire format


## Test plan 
### Manual: New backend (v2) + New frontend
  - [ ] Load dashboard with transformations - renders correctly
  - [ ] Add/modify/remove transformation -> save -> reload -> persists
  - [ ] Network tab shows `/apis/dashboard.grafana.app/v2/`

### Manual: Old backend (v2beta1 only) + New frontend
  - [ ] Load dashboard with transformations - renders correctly
  - [ ] Save dashboard - request payload has v2beta1 shape (`kind: "limit"`, `spec.id`)
  - [ ] Network tab shows `/apis/dashboard.grafana.app/v2beta1/`

  ### Manual: Edge cases
  - [ ] Dashboard with no transformations - unaffected
  - [ ] Disabled transformations survive roundtrip
  - [ ] Transformation filters survive roundtrip
  - [ ] Multiple transformations preserve ordering
author: dprokop

reviews.body:
⚠️  @dominik, while I was reviewing your PR, I had a realization 🤔 

If we now change the format for v2 export. Won't we have the same issue that we had [here](https://grafanalabs.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0AGZKNT2G6) with Terraform, Crossplane and create through v2beta1 legacy endpoint? If we rename from v2beta1 to v2 route will be fine, but if we change the format, we need to adapt in all those places.

Great job! All seems to be working as expected!

I tested loading/adding/editing/saving transformations  from non scenes, scenes, with DD and there we no issues 👍
![](https://media.giphy.com/media/v1.Y2lkPWVjZjA1ZTQ3Y2dkOGlqaTZzNXhtcjJuM2Njbm44cXg5Y3dhdXJoZmhwa3U2dnpmaSZlcD12MV9naWZzX3NlYXJjaCZjdD1n/2S9Hio4GAGUEZXxUg4/giphy.gif)

There are a few places where we still refer explicitly to `v2beta1` that should be updated:

- [dashboard-scene/scene/DashboardScene.tsx#L1175](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/feat/align-transformation-kind-frontend/public/app/features/dashboard-scene/scene/DashboardScene.tsx#L1175)

Dashboard: Switch frontend to v2 stable API with transformation compat layer

- Export v2 stable types from schema v2/index.ts
- Add transformation compatibility layer (normalizeTransformation, toWireTransformation,
  convertSpecToWireFormat) to handle both v2 and v2beta1 wire formats
- Update read path to normalize transformations from either wire format
- Update write path to convert spec to wire format at the API save boundary
- Update mutation API schema to v2 stable TransformationKind shape
- Refactor DashboardScene transformation counting to use normalizeTransformation
- Add tests for the compatibility layer

title: PanelChrome: exclude table column headers from triggering panel selection in edit mode
body:
Clicking on a table column header (to sort) or dragging the resize handle would unintentionally select the panel in the dashboard edit pane. Adding `[role="columnheader"]` to the existing pointer-down exclusion list fixes both cases, consistent with how canvas/svg elements are already excluded for graph panels.

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/120077
author: oscarkilhed

title: Elasticsearch: Remove core datasource
body:
This removes the Elasticsearch datasource as a core plugin as part of the process of converting it to an externalized plugin. The externalized version will be bundled with future versions of Grafana; this removal prepares us to test the bundling process.

# Release notice breaking change

This is a breaking change in that in its current state in that the Elasticsearch datasource has long been part of every Grafana release. However, we will not merge this PR without additional changes that will bundle the [externalized Elasticsearch datasource](https://github.com/grafana/grafana-elasticsearch-datasource) with Grafana builds going forward. This should not be a breaking change in practice, but it is somewhat experimental, so we're making the change along with a major Grafana version update just in case.

## Test plan
- [x] `make build-go` passes
- [x] CI pipeline passes
- [x] Verify no runtime errors when starting Grafana without the core elasticsearch plugin

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: njvrzm

(public/app/plugins/datasource/elasticsearch/), generated schema types,

title: Datasources: Disable deprecated numeric id using APIs by default
body:
we have certain data source APIs that we deprecated in `Grafana 9`. they refer to the data sources by their numeric `id` attribute. in this PR we disable them by default. to re-enable them, the feature-flag `datasourceLegacyIdApi` can be set to true.

NOTE: if you look at the diff, the following context will help: we already had a similar feature flag, named `datasourceDisableIdApi`, that worked in the opposite way. by-default it did not do anything, and when set to `true`, it disabled these API endpoints. in this PR, we removed this feature-flag and added the new one that works in the opposite way:
- old feature-flag, `datasourceDisableIdApi`:
    - `false` (default) => no change
    - `true` => the deprecated APis are disabled
- new feature-flag, `datasourceLegacyIdApi`:
    - `false` (default) => the deprecated APIs are disabled
    - `true` => no change

we also remove the swagger and markdown documentation for these API endpoints.

(NOTE: i had to disable the `check-separation CI job` that forbids merging frontend&backend changes in the same PR. this PR has no "real" frontend-changes. my changes are backend-only. but, because i changed the feature-toggle and api-stuff, the tools we need to run, that generate other files, will generate frontend files too)

# Release notice breaking change

Starting in Grafana v13.0, data source API endpoints using numeric id attributes are disabled by default.
To temporarily re-enable them, set the `datasourceLegacyIdApi` feature flag to `true`.
These endpoints have been deprecated since v9.0 and will be permanently removed in a future release, together with the feature flag.
author: gabor

reviews.body:
Because this is a breaking change, let's make sure its added to the changelog, not sure if there's anything special we have to do besides adding the label, its been a minute since the last time I looked at that. 

Also just an idea feel free to take/leave: Do you think we should add the word "legacy" or "deprecated" to the feature toggle name to help alert users to this? Otherwise they might think this is a new experimental feature we're adding vs removing old endpoints?

Also I think Ryan's suggestion to clean up docs made sense as a blocker! Let me know when you need another review happy to approve then!

commits.message: datasources: disable deprecated APIs by default
